title: Aalbessen (Roode) te Konfijten (Candied Red Currants)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound red currants
- 1 pound sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Cook this with little water, then shake the well-washed fruits into it and let both come to a boil, continuously skimming off the foam
2. Now pour off the juice, let it cook down well once more, then pour it over the berries already placed in bottles, and tie the bottles shut as soon as they are cold

title: Aalbessenstruif (Currant Fritter)
body:
## Ingredients

- a beer glass currant juice
- stale white bread, grated
- a cup melted butter
- 4 to 5 eggs
- sugar and cinnamon to taste
- grated lemon zest
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a beer glass currant juice, stale grated white bread, a cup melted butter, 4 to 5 eggs, sugar and cinnamon to taste, grated lemon zest and some lemon juice
2. Stir well together
3. Let it thicken on a baking sheet
4. Then bake it like any other fritter

title: Aalbessenvlade (Currant Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 beaten eggs
- bread sugar
- cinnamon
- 1 beer glass red currant juice

## Directions

1. Stir 6 beaten eggs, bread sugar and cinnamon through 1 beer glass red currant juice
2. Set on the fire and cook while constantly stirring until it becomes thick
3. Pour quickly onto a small dish on which it is to be served

title: Aardappelballetjes (Duchess Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cooked potatoes
- 2 dl milk
- 100 g flour
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Press the potatoes through a ricer finely or grind them through the mill, mix them with some salt and nutmeg.
2. Bring the milk with the butter or margarine to a boil, stir in the flour while stirring and keep stirring until the dough releases as a ball from the pan.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the eggs one by one and mix the batter with the finely made potatoes.
4. Form small, round balls, place these on a flour-dusted plate to prevent sticking.
5. Fry 10-12 balls at a time in hot frying fat light brown and cooked (2-3 minutes).
6. Let them drain on brown paper and serve with tenderloin, chateaubriands, etc.
7. Sieve the fat after use.

title: Aardappelbeignets (Potato Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cold potatoes
- 1-2 eggs
- 100 g old Gouda cheese
- nutmeg
- salt
- 1 egg for coating
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Grind the potatoes, mix them with the beaten eggs, half of the grated cheese, some salt and nutmeg.
2. Form flat cakes, roll these through fine breadcrumbs mixed with the remaining cheese.
3. Treat them further exactly as potato croquettes (recipe 416).

title: Aardappelcroqueties (Potato Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cold cooked potatoes
- 1 dl milk
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- salt
- nutmeg
- finely chopped parsley
- egg white
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Make stiff mashed potatoes in the usual way, stir through the egg yolk and some finely chopped parsley and let it cool.
2. Form small balls with two spoons, roll these through fine breadcrumbs, shape them evenly with your hands by hand, but make sure there are no cracks in them.
3. Roll them through the remaining egg white, which has been loosened with 2 tablespoons of water, but make sure this touches the croquettes everywhere.
4. Roll them once more through breadcrumbs and fry 4-5 croquettes at a time in hot frying fat, brown and crispy.
5. Make sure the croquettes are completely under the fat.
6. Remove them with a skimming spoon or croquette basket and let them drain on brown paper.
7. Serve with beefsteak, tenderloin, etc.
8. Sieve the fat after use.

title: Aardappelen in de schil koken (Potatoes Boiled in Their Skins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Brush the potatoes clean thoroughly, remove the eyes, set them to cook with enough boiling water so that they are submerged one-third of the way.
2. Let them cook until they can be easily pierced with a fork, though not too easily.
3. Drain them as dry as possible if necessary, let them dry on the fire without a lid, shaking occasionally.
4. Serve them with unmelted butter or margarine and some fine salt, or peel them when cooked and shake them with some butter or margarine and fine salt.

title: Aardappelen met uien (Potatoes with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- onions
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. In a dish, lay first a layer of sliced raw potatoes with salt and pepper, or nutmeg
2. Then a layer of onions cut into slices
3. Then again slices of potatoes, until the pan is full
4. Add butter and milk
5. Let it stew well with fire below and above
6. When the sauce cooks down, add more

title: Aardappelen smoren (Braised Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg potatoes
- water
- 60 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel small round potatoes and remove the eyes; cut large ones into quarters and wash them.
2. Set them to cook with the butter or margarine, salt and little water in a well-closed pan on low heat (approximately 30 minutes).
3. If the liquid has not evaporated, it can be drained and reserved for a sauce.
4. Let the potatoes dry without a lid, shaking occasionally.

title: Aardappelen stomen (Steamed Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg potatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, remove the eyes, wash them, and check again that all eyes are removed.
2. Lay them on the perforated bottom of the steaming pan.
3. Let them steam until cooked (approximately 45 minutes), sprinkle them with some salt and let them dry by removing the pan with water underneath.
4. Shake them from time to time until they are crumbly.

title: Aardappelen stoven in een sausje (Potatoes Braised in Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg cooked potatoes
- 1/2 l milk
- 10 g flour or 8 g Duryea cornstarch
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- finely chopped parsley or nutmeg

## Directions

1. Slice the potatoes or leave small potatoes (e.g. new ones) whole.
2. Make a thin sauce, add some salt and carefully braise the potatoes in it, but make sure the slices do not break.
3. Mix them just before serving with very fine parsley or grate some nutmeg over the plate.

title: Aardappelen zonder water te koken (Potatoes Cooked Without Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Let the potatoes drain well on a colander
2. Place them in an iron pot with the necessary salt
3. Close the lid of the pot so tightly that no steam can escape
4. Hang them over the fire until they are cooked
5. They will cook in the steam trapped in the pot and will be very crumbly

title: Aardappelensoep (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces potato starch
- beef or veal bouillon
- salt
- bread

## Directions

1. Take 5 ounces of potato starch and mix that with the necessary amount of beef or veal bouillon, as preferred
2. Add a little salt
3. When ready, add bread cut into cube-sized pieces

title: Aardappelgebak (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces cooked potatoes
- 2½ ounces melted butter
- 10 eggs with whites

## Directions

1. Rub the cooked potatoes very fine.
2. Add 2½ ounces melted butter and 10 eggs with whites, adding the butter last.
3. Bake in a pudding or other mold with heat above and below.

title: Aardappelkoek (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes, not too well cooked
- 2 eggs
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter

## Directions

1. Rub cooked potatoes fine.
2. Mix with 2 eggs, milk, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Stir well together and fry in butter over moderate heat in a cake pan.
4. Be careful when turning.

title: Aardappelpannekoekjes (Potato Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g cooked potatoes
- 2.5 dl milk
- 15-20 g flour
- 15-20 g butter or margarine
- 1-2 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- 60 g butter or margarine for frying

## Directions

1. Make the potatoes fine, mix them with the milk, flour, melted butter or margarine, egg yolks and finally the stiffly beaten egg white as airy as possible.
2. Fry 8 small pancakes from this batter in a small frying pan with the butter or margarine light brown and cooked.

title: Aardappelpodding (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ned. pound cooked potatoes
- 2.5 ounces melted butter
- 5 eggs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- cinnamon snippets
- candied citrus peel
- flour
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cream

## Directions

1. Make 1 Dutch pound cooked potatoes fine
2. add 2.5 ounces melted butter, 5 eggs, sugar, nutmeg, cinnamon, cinnamon snippets and candied citrus peel
3. stir well together
4. put in the mold
5. let it cook
6. serve with a sauce of flour, butter, sugar, cinnamon and cream

title: Aardappelpudding (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Ned. pond cooked potatoes
- 2.5 ounces melted butter
- 5 eggs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- snippets
- sukade

## Directions

1. Mash 1 pound of cooked potatoes finely
2. Add 2.5 ounces melted butter, 5 eggs, sugar, nutmeg, cinnamon, snippets and sukade
3. Mix well together
4. Put it in a mold and let it cook
5. Serve with a sauce of flour, butter, sugar, cinnamon and cream

title: Aardappelpurée (Mashed Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cooked potatoes
- approximately 4 dl milk
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD: Push warm cooked potatoes through a ricer or squeeze them through a potato press. If you do not have these tools, warm the potatoes in the boiling milk, for example 5 minutes, and mash them with a potato masher or make them fine with a fork.
2. Mix the purée with the milk, pepper, butter or margarine, salt and nutmeg to taste.
3. Let the purée become warm on the fire, stirring well.
4. SECOND METHOD: Make the potatoes fine as indicated above, mix them with the milk, half of the melted butter, salt, pepper and nutmeg to taste.
5. Ensure that the purée is not too dry, place it in an ovenproof dish, smooth the top, sprinkle some breadcrumbs through a sieve over it, place small pieces of the remaining butter here and there, clean the edge of the dish and let a brown crust form in a moderately warm oven.

title: Aardappelpurée in schelpen (Mashed Potatoes in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cold potatoes
- 2.5 dl milk
- 1 egg yolk
- 80 g grated cheese
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- 50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Make mashed potato purée in the usual way, but stir only 15 g of butter or margarine through it, mix it with the egg yolk, chopped parsley and grated cheese.
2. Grease the shells with some butter or margarine, fill them three-quarters full with the purée and finish them as described in recipe 102.

title: Aardappelpurée met eieren (Mashed Potatoes with Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cooked potatoes
- 4 dl milk
- 1 egg
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make the mashed potato purée as described in the previous recipe, but always ensure that the milk used is boiled before it is added to the purée.
2. Stir the beaten egg yolk through it, mix the purée with the stiffly beaten egg white, put everything in an ovenproof dish and finish the purée as in recipe 409 (second method).

title: Aardappelsla (Potato Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg freshly cooked potatoes
- 1-2 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 leeks or onions
- 3/4 dl salad oil
- 1/2 dl vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- mustard
- very finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Make the hard-boiled eggs fine, mix them with the oil, vinegar, pepper, salt, mustard, very finely chopped leeks or onions and parsley.
2. Carefully mix the potatoes sliced into this sauce, let the salad marinate for at least an hour and serve with fried fish, cold meat, etc.

title: Aardappelsoep (Potato Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 potatoes
- 3/4 l water
- 1/4 l milk
- salt
- 30 g butter or margarine
- celery greens
- 3 carrots
- piece of Spanish pepper
- piece of mace
- finely chopped parsley
- Maggi's Aroma if desired

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, cut them into small pieces, wash them, put them on to cook with salt, the milk, the chopped and washed carrots and the celery, the mace and Spanish pepper.
2. Let them cook until they have become completely soft (approximately 3/4 hour), pour the soup through a sieve and rub the potatoes through.
3. Gradually add the water to the thick purée while stirring, make the soup warm again and add the butter and parsley. Finish the soup with a few drops of Maggi's Aroma.

title: Aardbeien op Brandewijn (Strawberries in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g strawberries
- 200 g sugar
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 0.5 l brandy

## Directions

1. Wash the strawberries and let them drain well. Treat them as described in recipe 1031.

title: Aardbeien-ijs I (Strawberry Ice Cream I)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 g strawberries
- 500 g sugar
- 1/2 liter water or milk
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1/4 liter cream
- a little carmine

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in the warm water or warm milk and let the sugar syrup cool.
2. Pick the strawberries, wash them and rub them through a hair sieve.
3. Add the obtained purée to the sugar syrup, add the cream, the sieved lemon juice and as much carmine as needed to obtain a nice color.
4. Place the mixture in the rinsed ice mold and let it freeze in the manner as indicated.

title: Aardbeien-ijs II (Strawberry Ice Cream II)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg strawberries
- 1/4 liter water
- 1/2 kg sugar
- juice of 1 to 2 lemons
- a few drops of red coloring or 2 sheets of red gelatin

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in the water.
2. Rub the cleaned strawberries through a hair sieve and add them with the lemon juice to the sugar syrup.
3. Add some red coloring and count on the ice becoming lighter in color by freezing.
4. Treat it further as other ice cream and serve if desired garnished with whipped cream.

title: Aardbeiencompôte (Strawberry Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ kg strawberries
- approximately 2½ dl water
- 100 g sugar
- some lemon juice
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the strawberries and remove the stem.
2. Let the sugar cook down with the water to a thin syrup, add the strawberries, bring them to a boil, remove them with a skimming spoon from the liquid and lay them in the compote dish.
3. Mix the liquid with lemon juice to taste, thicken it with some mixed sago and pour it over the strawberries.

title: Aardbeienjam (Strawberry Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg strawberries (cleaned)
- 2¼ kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- elastic bands
- string
- labels
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the strawberries carefully, wash them, let them cook without sugar, stirring occasionally
2. Add the sugar and let them continue cooking until they reach the required thickness (test by dropping a drop in cold water—it should not spread but sink whole)
3. Remove the foam with a flat spoon
4. Add some lemon juice, fill the cleaned jars or bottles as quickly as possible, close them with damp parchment paper or cellophane
5. Fasten this with string or elastic band and label the cleaned jars or bottles with name and date

title: Aardbeiensaus (Strawberry Sauce) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g small, perfect strawberries
- 4 dl water
- 20 g sago or potato flour
- 75 g sugar
- lemon juice
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Pick the strawberries, wash them and let them drain briefly in a colander.
2. Bring them with the water, lemon zest and sugar almost to a boil, remove them with a skimming spoon from the liquid, place them in a glass dish and bind the liquid with the mixed sago.
3. Remove the lemon zest, add some lemon juice and pour the sauce over the strawberries.
4. Present the sauce with maizena, semolina pudding etc.

title: Aardbeiensaus (Strawberry Sauce) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g strawberries
- 4 egg yolks
- 125 g bastard sugar
- 4 dl white or Rhine wine
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Pick the strawberries, wash them and press them through a horsehair sieve.
2. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar, add the wine, lemon juice and strawberry puree and let the sauce be bound, stirring, on a gentle fire or in a bain-marie.
3. Present it with warm semolina, rice pudding etc.

title: Abrikozen (Preserved Apricots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 apricots
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Treat the apricots entirely as peaches.

title: Abrikozenbeignets (Apricot Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- salt
- 3 to 3.5 dl warm milk
- 20 g yeast
- approximately 400 g apricots
- approximately 60 g sugar
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with enough warm milk so it no longer clouds, stir out the lumps, add the remaining milk, salt and the mixed yeast and let it rise covered (approximately 3 to 4 hours).
2. Soak the apricots, after washing them, for 24 hours in cold water, cook them in this water for 5 minutes, let them drain on a colander and dust them with the sugar, which should infuse while the apricots are still warm.
3. Treat the beignets further as indicated for apple beignets.

title: Abrikozencompote (Apricot Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 apricots
- 100 g sugar
- 2.5 dl water
- 3/4 dl Rhine wine (optional)
- sago (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the apricots carefully, peel them if desired (which is easiest done by holding them in hot water for a few minutes), cut them in half and remove the pits.
2. Let the water with the sugar reduce to a thin syrup.
3. Place no more than 8 half fruits at a time in it and simmer gently for 2 minutes, but ensure they do not break.
4. Remove them with a skimming spoon, bind the liquid with a little mixed sago or let it reduce and add the wine.

title: Abrikozencompôte (Apricot Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 apricots
- 100 g sugar
- approximately 2½ dl water
- ¾ dl Rhine wine (optional)
- some sago (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the apricots carefully, peel them if desired (which is easiest done by holding them in hot water for a few minutes), cut them in half and remove the pits.
2. Let the water cook down with the sugar to a thin syrup.
3. Lay no more than 8 half fruits at a time in it and cook them gently for 2 minutes, but make sure they don't break.
4. Remove them with a skimming spoon, thicken the liquid with a little mixed sago or let it cook down and add the wine.

title: Abrikozenjam (Apricot Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.75 kg apricots (+ 3 kg without pits)
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- labels

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots, remove the pits, put them with as much water as necessary to prevent burning during the first moments in a pan.
2. Let them cook down to a thick pulp.
3. Add the sugar, bring them to a boil with the lid on and let the jam cook without the lid, stirring occasionally, until it reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369).
4. Treat it further entirely as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Abrikozenpudding (Apricot Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g dried apricots
- 1/2 liter water
- 150-175 g sugar
- peel of 1/2 lemon or orange
- 25 g gelatin or 20 g powdered gelatin

## Directions

1. Rinse the apricots with lukewarm water, soak them for 24 hours in the water with the peel
2. Bring them to a boil and let them cook gently (5-10 minutes)
3. Dissolve the sugar and gelatin (which has been soaked 10-15 minutes in cold water and may include 1 sheet of red gelatin) in the mixture
4. Rub the mass through a horsehair sieve, let it become jelly-like while stirring occasionally and pour it into a stone mold rinsed with cold water
5. Turn the pudding out onto a damp dish after cooling and serve with vanilla sauce

title: Abrikozensaus (Apricot Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 apricots
- 4 egg yolks
- 100 to 125 g bastard sugar
- 4 dl white or Rhine wine
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Cook the apricots in some water until they are soft, press them through a horsehair sieve.
2. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar, add the wine, lemon juice and apricot puree and let the sauce be bound, stirring, on a gentle fire or in a bain-marie.
3. Present it with warm almond cake, cabinet pudding etc.

title: Advocaat (Advocaat Liqueur)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large or 14 small eggs
- 400 g sugar
- 1 stick vanilla
- 1 l brandy

## Directions

1. Whip with a whisk in a well-enameled pan the eggs with the vanilla pulp and the sugar until this is dissolved as well as possible.
2. Add the brandy gradually and let the advocaat au bain-marie or on a gentle fire, always stirring with the whisk, become warm and lightly thickened, but make sure it does not curdle.
3. Pour it into well-cleaned carafes or bottles and close them tightly.

title: Ajam Besegnek (Indonesian Chicken Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- Salt
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 3 tablespoons ground onions
- 5 garlic cloves
- 2 lomboks
- 1 tablespoon laos
- 1 teaspoon djienten
- 2 teaspoons ketoembar
- 1 teaspoon trassi
- 1 tablespoon goela djawa
- 2 to 3 tablespoons tamarind or assem water
- 1 dl water
- 2 dl coconut milk or milk with some salt
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Roast the cleaned and salted chicken until cooked
2. Debone it
3. Pound the spices finely with the ground onions
4. Fry them, add water, tamarind water, sugar, coconut milk or milk with salt
5. Pour this sauce over the chicken pieces and let them stew in it

title: Allumettes (zoete) (Sweet Matches)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g hard butter or margarine
- 50 g flour
- some water
- salt
- approximately 1 egg white
- 100 g powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846).
2. Roll it out to a thickness of 1.25 cm to a layer approximately 10 cm wide.
3. Brush this with egg white glaze (see recipe 820), cut cookies 1.5 cm wide from it and bake these in a warm oven light brown and cooked through (approximately 15 minutes).
4. Remove them from the baking sheet immediately.

title: Almond Tart (Amandeltaart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g almonds
- marmalade or jam
- butter or margarine
- sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Soak the almonds in warm water, peel them and grind them finely.
2. Knead them with the butter or margarine, sugar and flour into a firm ball and divide it into two equal parts.
3. Press one half into a greased tart pan, making sure the layer of dough is as thin as possible. (When using a small tart pan, divide the dough into 3 parts instead.)
4. Bake it in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked through (15 to 20 minutes).
5. Let the cake cool, turn it out and bake the second half in the pan.
6. Let this also cool and place both parts on top of each other with some jam or marmalade between them.
7. Sprinkle the tart with sifted powdered sugar or tie a colored ribbon around it or garnish the tart with water glaze and candied fruits.

title: Amandelbroodjes (Almond Breads)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces flour
- 5 ounces sugar
- 3 ounces ground almonds
- 3 ounces candied peel
- 1 ounce snippets
- 15 eggs, of which 5 with white
- Cinnamon and mace to taste

## Directions

1. Make a dough
2. Form into small breads
3. Bake

title: Amandelgebak (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces coarsely ground almonds
- 6 eggs, of which 4 with the white
- 1 ounce bread sugar
- Lemon peel grated thinly
- Butter and rusks for the form

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar with the eggs well first, then stir the almonds through it well
2. Beat together very quickly so that it rises in the pot
3. Put it in the form, which has first been greased with butter and sprinkled with rusks
4. The lid, which one puts on top, must first be warm
5. Let it bake slowly
6. Watch it from time to time

title: Amandelmelk (Almond Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound peeled sweet almonds
- 1 can water
- sugar

## Directions

1. A quarter pound of peeled sweet almonds is finely pounded, soaked on a can of water for an hour, subsequently pressed through a cloth, and sugar added to taste

title: Amandelmelk (Almond Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 almonds
- 5 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Treat the almonds as in recipe 947. Pound the ground almonds well for 10 minutes in the stone mortar. Then mix the mass, while stirring with the pestle, with the water. If necessary, sieve the mass through a rinsed linen cloth.

title: Amandelnootjes (Almond Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g almonds
- 125 g sugar
- 70 g flour
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Wash the unpeeled almonds, dry them and grind them fine.
2. Mix them with the stiffly beaten egg white, the sifted flour and the sugar and shape this dough into small rolls about 1 cm thick.
3. Lay these on a greased baking sheet and bake them in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done (15-20 minutes). Remove them from the baking sheet and let them cool.

title: Amandelpers (Almond Paste)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g sweet almonds mixed with 4 bitter almonds
- 125 g sugar
- 1 egg
- some grated lemon zest or vanilla pulp

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds.
2. Grind them twice with the sugar, egg and flavoring through the almond mill.
3. Store the almond paste in well-sealed jam jars.
4. Almond paste becomes better if it stands for some time, for example at least 2 to 3 weeks.

title: Amandelroom (Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces sweet almonds
- 1 can good milk
- 5 ounces sugar
- 2 egg whites
- oil
- paper

## Directions

1. Peel the almonds
2. pound them finely with a little water
3. whip the milk with the egg whites and sugar
4. place the mixture on a gentle fire and let it cook
5. when nearly reduced to three-quarters, add the almonds
6. let them cook together briefly and strain through a sieve
7. let it cool
8. place it in a dish and arrange almonds around the edge that have been rolled lightly in oil and roasted on paper

title: Amandelroom (Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces sweet almonds
- one can good milk
- five ounces sugar
- water
- egg whites from 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Take six ounces sweet almonds and peel them
2. Pound them finely with a little water
3. Beat the milk with the egg whites from 2 eggs and the sugar
4. Place the mixture on a gentle fire and let it cook
5. When it has almost reduced to three quarters, add the almonds
6. Let everything cook together briefly and strain through a sieve
7. Let it cool
8. Place on a plate and arrange almonds around the edge

title: Amandelstruif (Almond Fritter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces ground almonds
- 5 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls flour
- some crushed cinnamon
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Add 5 eggs to 5 ounces ground almonds
2. Stir very finely with 2 spoonfuls flour
3. Add some crushed cinnamon and milk
4. Bake in the frying pan
5. Sprinkle sugar over when serving

title: Amandeltaart (Almond Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 150 g almonds
- 150 g sugar
- 50 g potato starch or cornstarch
- 25 g flour
- salt
- jam

## Directions

1. Whip the eggs with a pinch of salt and the sugar in a bain-marie until thick, white and fluffy (approximately 15 minutes).
2. Add the potato starch or cornstarch and flour through a sieve, add most of the ground almonds last and bake the cake in a greased and dusted tart pan in a fairly warm oven until light brown and done (approximately 3/4 to 1 hour).
3. Cover with a buttered piece of paper if needed, check if it is done by pricking it with a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
4. Turn it out onto a cake rack, let it cool, spread the top and sides with some jam and garnish the cake with the reserved ground almonds.

title: Amandeltaart zonder boter (Almond Tart Without Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces almond paste, finely ground
- One and a half ounces grated bread sugar
- 4 eggs
- Zest of one lemon, grated

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients together in a tall pot
2. Stir constantly for a quarter hour
3. Before it sets, pour into a pan or molds
4. Bake until done

title: Amandeltaart Zonder Boter (Almond Tart Without Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces almond paste, finely ground
- 1.5 ounces grated bread sugar
- 4 eggs
- Lemon peel grated

## Directions

1. Put all together in a high pot and stir continuously for a quarter hour
2. Before it stops, put it in the pan or forms and set it to bake

title: Amandelvlade (Almond Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces almond paste, of which 1 ounce bitter
- 2 ounces sugar
- 20 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Stir the almond paste, of which 1 ounce bitter, together well with sugar and egg yolks
2. Bake it on a baking sheet under a cover with fire until done

title: American Punch (Amerikaansche punsch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of twelve lemons
- One can red wine
- One can rum
- Five ounces sugar
- Half a can water
- Half a can strong tea water

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar with half a can of water and skim
2. Mix together the lemon juice, red wine, rum, sugar syrup, and strong tea water
3. Stir everything well

title: Ananaspudding (1st Recipe) (Pineapple Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 85 g Duryea maizena
- 75 g sugar
- 2 egg whites
- 5 slices pineapple

## Directions

1. Mix the maizena with some cold milk into a thin paste, without lumps.
2. Bring the remaining milk to a boil, add the sugar and, stirring vigorously, the mixed maizena and let the pudding cook through while stirring for 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Then mix it away from the fire with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Make sure that in the meantime the pineapple is pitted and cut into small pieces, stir the pineapple through the pudding, but keep some pieces aside to mix through the sauce.
5. Pour the mixture into a mold rinsed with cold water and let it become cold and firm.
6. Bring the pineapple liquid to a boil, add sugar to taste and bind it with some mixed sago.
7. After cooling, stir the remaining pineapple pieces through it and serve the sauce with the pudding.
8. Color the sauce rose if desired by dissolving 1/2 sheet red gelatine in it.

title: Ananaspudding (2nd Recipe) (Pineapple Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 1/2 tin pineapple with 1 1/2 to 2 dl liquid
- 75 g brown sugar
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 12 g white gelatine or 10 g powdered gelatine

## Directions

1. Cut the pineapple into pieces and remove pits if necessary.
2. Dissolve the gelatine (which has soaked 10 to 15 minutes in cold water) in the pineapple liquid.
3. Let this mixture cool somewhat and add it to the cream, which has been beaten stiff with the sugar.
4. Mix this with the stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. Stir the pineapple pieces and the scraped vanilla through it at the same time and let the mass stand, stirring from time to time, until it becomes jelly-like and the pineapple pieces no longer sink.
6. Then pour it into a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white and let the pudding become cold and firm.
7. Turn it out onto a damp plate.
8. Garnish the pudding with jelly made from pineapple liquid (see fruit jelly recipe 566) and with pineapple pieces.

title: Ananassorbet (Pineapple Sorbet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can pineapple
- 5 to 6 apricots
- 100 g sugar
- a little lemon juice
- a little carmine
- 1 dl maraschino or Champagne

## Directions

1. Take 3 to 4 pieces of pineapple from the can, remove the pits and cut them into very small cubes.
2. Rub the remaining pineapple with the apricots through a hair sieve and mix this purée with the lemon juice, the liquid in which the sugar is dissolved and as much carmine until a nice red color is obtained.
3. Let the mass freeze in the usual manner.
4. Add some maraschino or Champagne, let the mixture freeze a little longer and present it as soon as it has become stiff, after having stirred the pineapple pieces through it.

title: Anchovy Sauce (Ansjovisaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies
- Water
- Water or bouillon
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the anchovies
2. Let them freshen in water
3. Chop them finely
4. Cook them with water or bouillon
5. When done, press them through a sieve
6. Add pepper, vinegar, and butter
7. Bind the sauce by beating in a couple of egg yolks

title: Andere (Another Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongue
- Pepper
- Salt
- Cloves
- Red wine
- Lemon
- Onions

## Directions

1. Take the cooked tongue and split it lengthwise open
2. Lay it in a dish and let it simmer for a good hour with the addition of pepper, salt, crushed cloves, a glass of red wine, some lemon and some finely chopped onions

title: Andijvie (Endive)
body:
## Ingredients

- endive
- sorrel
- salt
- nutmeg
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil well and drain
2. Add sorrel, salt, nutmeg, flour and butter to taste
3. Let it stew for an hour

title: Andijvie (Endive)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 heads of endive or 1 1/2 kg
- 2 1/2 deciliters milk (or liquid from the endive)
- 25 g flour or 15 g Duryea maizena
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut off the withered leaves, a piece of the stem, and if necessary the hard green points of the leaves.
2. Cut the heads, starting from the stem, into thin strips.
3. Wash them repeatedly in plenty of water until the water is completely clear, then place them with the attached water and let them cook until tender (1/2 hour).
4. Let them drain in a colander and if desired, catch the liquid to make a sauce from it.
5. Prepare the sauce in the manner indicated for cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328 or recipe 329).
6. Add the endive with some salt and braise for about 5 minutes.
7. Grate some nutmeg over it on the plate.

title: Andijvie (Pickled Endive)
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow endive heads
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut endive to the size of a finger joint
2. Wash clean and let drain
3. Line the pot or barrel with a pillowcase
4. Place endive in the pot, salting it slightly less than for string beans
5. Place a cloth and fitted board with stone on top
6. Every 14 days, clean the cloth and stone
7. To eat, soak and stew as if fresh endive

title: Andijvie in een vuurvast schoteltje (Endive au Jus)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 heads of endive or 1 1/2 kg
- 1 1/2 deciliters bouillon or lean gravy with water
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the heads thoroughly in the usual manner.
2. Wash them under running water and tie them closed with string.
3. Cook them in salted water until tender (approximately 3/4 hour).
4. Let them drain in a colander, remove the strings, and place the heads in a heat-proof dish.
5. Add the bouillon or gravy with water, sprinkle breadcrumbs over it.
6. Distribute the butter or margarine in pieces on top, clean the edge of the dish, and let it brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Andijvie in het zout (Endive in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 kg endive
- 1 or 3 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Clean the endive, cut it into thin strips, wash it until the water runs completely clear, let it drain as dry as possible on a colander.
2. Finish it as indicated in recipe 1017.

title: Andijvie uit het zout met aardappelen (Endive from Salt with Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg endive
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the endive as indicated for salted vegetables.
2. Let it drain in a colander.
3. Peel the potatoes, cut them into quarters, wash them and place them in the bottom of the pan.
4. Add enough boiling water so they are about half submerged.
5. Add the endive, salt if needed, and the fat, and let everything cook together on low heat until done and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
6. Add more water if needed, but ensure the stamppot does not become too moist.
7. Quickly mash everything together with a wooden spoon or masher.

title: Andijvie uit het zout met witte bonen en aardappelen (Endive from Salt with White Beans and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg endive
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 250 g white beans
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the soaked beans in the usual way (recipe 493).
2. Cook the endive as indicated for salted vegetables.
3. Let it drain in a colander.
4. Peel the potatoes, cut them into quarters, wash them and place them in the bottom of the pan.
5. Add enough boiling water or bean broth so they are about half submerged.
6. Add the endive, beans, fat and salt, and let everything cook together on low heat until done and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
7. Add more water if needed, but ensure the stamppot does not become too moist.
8. Quickly mash everything together with a wooden spoon or masher.

title: Andijviesalade (Endive Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- endive, preferably curly endive
- cooked beet in slices
- onions, finely cut
- warm sour sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the endive as finely as possible.
2. Place it in a salad bowl over slices of cooked beet and finely cut onions.
3. Toss it as ordinary head lettuce.
4. Eat it with warm sour sauce.

title: Andijviesla (Endive Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches endive or curly endive
- 1 egg
- 3 spoonfuls salad oil
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar or herb vinegar
- optionally a splash of Maggi's Aroma
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- optionally sliced leek, onion, or chives

## Directions

1. Remove the outer wilted leaves of the endive, cut off the hard green points and a piece of the stalk and then cut it into fine strips.
2. Wash them, let them drain as dry as possible in a colander or shake them in a salad spinner.
3. Prepare the endive as described in the previous recipe.
4. Note: See note under recipe 479.
5. Vary the endive salad by occasionally mixing it with very finely cut cooked beet.

title: Andijviestruiken in het zout (Endive Heads in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 kg endive
- 1 or 3 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Clean the endive heads in their entirety, wash them and boil them in plenty of boiling water for 5 minutes.
2. Remove them with a skimming spoon from the pan, let them cool completely spread out.
3. Finish them as indicated in recipe 1017.

title: Anijsmelk (Aniseed Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 l milk
- 80 g sugar
- 12 1/2 g aniseed
- 20 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Tie the aniseed in a cheesecloth, put it in the milk and prepare it as indicated for vanilla milk (recipe 900).

title: Ansjovisbroodjes (1st Recipe) (Anchovy Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices casino bread
- 6 anchovies
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- 1/8 teaspoon mustard
- 1/2 tablespoon vinegar
- 1/2 tablespoon salad oil
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as indicated in recipe 17.
2. Cut very thin slices of casino bread, remove the crusts, cut them lengthwise in half and fry them with butter in a skillet until light brown and crispy.
3. Boil the eggs hard (8 minutes), peel them, separate the white and yolk.
4. Rub the yolks fine, mix them with the oil, vinegar, mustard and some parsley.
5. Spread this mixture on the bread pieces on one side, place 2 rolled-up half anchovies on each slice and garnish the breads with finely chopped egg white, finely chopped parsley and capers.

title: Ansjovisbroodjes (2nd Recipe) (Anchovy Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices casino bread
- 6 anchovies
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as indicated in recipe 17.
2. Rub 3 anchovies through a horsehair sieve, mix the obtained purée with the butter beaten until creamy.
3. Taste whether this anchovy butter is not too salty, if so stir in more butter.
4. Cut very thin slices of casino bread, remove the crust and cut them in different shapes, e.g. triangles, diamonds, pear-shaped figures, etc.
5. Fry them with butter in a skillet until light brown and crispy or toast them on a baking sheet in the oven until brown.
6. Let them cool.
7. Spread them on one side with the anchovy butter so that it lies higher in the middle than at the edges (so-called domed).
8. Cut the remaining anchovies into very thin strips, place these over the anchovy butter and further garnish the breads with finely chopped egg white, finely chopped egg yolk and parsley.

title: Ansjovisbroodjes (3e recept) (Anchovy Sandwich, 3rd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices casino bread without crust
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 4 anchovies
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- ravigotte sauce
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as described in recipe 17
2. Cut the slices of bread lengthwise in half and fry them light brown
3. Cut the hard-boiled egg into slices
4. Lay a slice of egg on each slice of bread; place half a rolled anchovy on top and garnish the sandwich by piping ravigotte sauce (see recipe 370) along it
5. Curled parsley can be placed along the plate

title: Ansjovisbroodjes (3rd Recipe) (Anchovy Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices casino bread without crust
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 4 anchovies
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- ravigote sauce
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as described in recipe 17.
2. Cut the slices of bread lengthwise in half and fry them light brown.
3. Cut the hard-boiled egg into slices.
4. Place a slice of egg on each slice of bread; on top a rolled-up half anchovy and garnish the bread by piping ravigote sauce (see recipe 370) along it.
5. Curled parsley can be placed along the plate.

title: Ansjovissalade (Anchovy Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies
- Cold water
- Milk (if needed)
- Hard-boiled egg whites
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Lemon slices
- Parsley
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the anchovies in cold water, or with milk if necessary, to remove excess salt
2. Treat them as you would herring, but leave half of them whole
3. Let them drain and arrange the half-fishes in diagonal diamonds overlapping each other
4. Serve the dish garnished with slices of hard-boiled eggs, lemon slices and parsley
5. Dress as you would herring salad

title: Ansjovissalade (Anchovy Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- cold water
- milk (if needed)
- hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- lemon slices
- parsley
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the anchovies in cold water, with milk if needed, to remove excess salt.
2. Treat them as herring, but leave half whole.
3. Let them drain and lay the half fish in slanting diamonds over each other.
4. Serve the dish decorated with slices of hard eggs, lemon slices, and parsley.
5. Dress as herring salad.

title: Ansjovissandwiches (Anchovy Sandwiches)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 thin slices casino bread
- 6 anchovies
- lemon juice
- pepper
- salad oil

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as described above.
2. Soak them for 10 minutes in salad oil mixed with some lemon juice and pepper.
3. In the meantime, cut 6 very thin slices of casino bread, remove the crust, cut them in half and place the anchovy between two slices.
4. Press the slices firmly together.

title: Appelbeignets (Apple Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 3 to 3.5 dl beer or sparkling water
- salt
- 6 sour apples (Goudreinettes or Bellefleurs)
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Heat the frying fat in a deep iron pot.
2. Core and peel the apples, cut them into not-too-thick slices.
3. Mix the flour with some beer or sparkling water and salt quickly, stir out the lumps and add enough beer or sparkling water so the batter reaches the required thickness.
4. Coat some apple slices in the batter, place them in the hot fat and fry the apple fritters light brown and cooked through.
5. Remove them from the fat with a larding needle or knitting needle.
6. Let them drain thoroughly on gray paper, dust with powdered sugar and serve warm.

title: Appelbollen (Apple Balls) (6-8 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- some water
- some salt
- approximately 6 sour apples
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 80 g sugar
- 1 teaspoon fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846). Peel the cored apples and place them on the dough, which has been rolled out one last time into a large, thin sheet. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the sugar and fine cinnamon and fill the openings of the apples with this mixture. Wrap the dough around them, seal it underneath after making a few folds. Brush the apple balls with egg yolk beaten with water and bake them in a hot oven until light brown and cooked (approximately 15 minutes). Remove from baking sheet and let cool.

title: Appelcompôte (Apple Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 sour apples
- 8 candied cherries or some marmalade
- 50 g sugar
- peel of ½ lemon
- 1 leaf of white or red gelatin
- approximately 2½ dl water with some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Core the apples, peel them, halve them, wash them and cook them gently in the liquid that has been brought to a boil with the lemon juice in a flat, wide pan (for example a braising pan).
2. Just before they are done, add the sugar and gently moisten the apples with the liquid.
3. Lay each apple that is done with a flat spoon in a compote dish.
4. Dissolve the soaked gelatin in the liquid and pour it through a horsehair sieve along or over the apples.
5. Lay a candied cherry or some marmalade on the holes.

title: Appeldieetspijs (Miisli) (Apple Diet Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g appels (+ 2 stuks)
- 10 g havervlokken (één eetlepel)
- 25 g gecondenseerde melk (één eetlepel)
- 10 g gemalen noten (één eetlepel)
- sap van 1/2 citroen

## Directions

1. Week 12 uur van tevoren de havervlokken in drie eetlepels water.
2. Voeg hierbij de gecondenseerde melk en het citroensap.
3. Rasp de goed schoongemaakte appels (zie blz. 354) met schil en klokhuis fijn.
4. Voeg ze vlug bij het mengsel om te voorkomen dat de appels verkleuren.
5. Strooi er véér het gebruik de gemalen noten over.

title: Appelen met Aardappelen (Apples with Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg tart apples (or half sweet, half tart)
- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- 40 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes, wash them, cut them into quarters and place them in enough boiling water to half cover them.
2. Add the peeled and cut apples, from which the cores have been removed, and the fat.
3. Let everything cook together on a gentle fire until tender (approximately 1/2 hour) and mash it together.

title: Appelen met Aardappelen en Spek - Hete Bliksem (Apples with Potatoes and Bacon - Lightning Hot)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg tart apples (or half sweet, half tart)
- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- 400 g lean smoked bacon
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the mash as described in the previous recipe, but cook the washed bacon 1/2 hour in advance.

title: Appelen met Aardappelen en Witte Bonen (Apples with Potatoes and White Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg tart apples (or half sweet, half tart)
- 1 kg potatoes
- 250 g white beans
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in the usual manner (recipe 493) until tender.
2. Peel the potatoes, cut them into quarters, wash them, place them at the bottom of the pan and add enough water (or if available: bean water) to half cover them.
3. Peel the apples, cut them into quarters, wash them, add them together with the beans and fat to the potatoes.
4. Let everything cook together on a gentle fire until tender and almost dry and mash or stir it quickly together with a wooden spoon or with a masher.

title: Appelensop (Apple Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour apples
- butter
- slices of day-old white bread
- sugar
- cinnamon
- white wine
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the sour apples and cut them into slices.
2. Melt some butter in a pan.
3. Lay a layer of slices of day-old white bread upon it, then a layer of apple slices with sugar, cinnamon, and butter, and so on until the pan is full.
4. Pour in some white wine and water.
5. Let it stew until done with fire under and above.
6. Some add rye bread and use bouillon or the liquid from cooked salted meat instead of water and wine.

title: Appelgebak (Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bowls thick applesauce
- 10-15 cents worth grated white bread
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 ounce melted butter
- cinnamon
- sugar
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 2 bowls thick applesauce and stir through 10-15 cents worth grated white bread, 8 egg yolks, 1 ounce melted butter, cinnamon and sugar
2. Beat the egg whites until foamy and add
3. Bake for more than 3 quarters of an hour in a mold

title: Appelgelei (Apple Jelly)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg sour apples (preferably Goudreinettes)
- Juice and peel of 2 lemons
- 3/4 of the weight of the liquid from the apples after cooking in sugar
- Jam jars
- Parchment paper
- String
- Labels

## Directions

1. Wash the apples carefully, cut them into sixths.
2. Place them with the lemon peel, the juice and enough boiling water so they are just covered and let them cook until completely soft.
3. Pour the mass into a clean cloth and let it stand without stirring until all the liquid has completely drained out.
4. Weigh the liquid or measure it (1/2 liter = 500 g), set it with 3/4 of its weight in sugar and let it cook down, stirring occasionally, until the jelly reaches the required thickness.
5. Treat further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Appelkoekjes (Apple Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- best sour apples
- yeast batter with a dash of brandy
- boiling butter or lard, or half of each

## Directions

1. Take best sour apples, core them, and cut into slices.
2. Roll the slices in a somewhat thick yeast batter to which a dash of brandy has been added.
3. Put them in a flat pan with boiling butter or lard (or half of each) and let them brown well.

title: Appelkoekjes (Apple Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Best sour apples
- Wheat flour
- Yeast batter
- A splash of brandy
- Butter or lard for frying, or half of each

## Directions

1. Take best sour apples, core them and cut them into slices
2. Dip them in a somewhat thick yeast batter to which a splash of brandy has been added
3. Put them in a flat pan with boiling butter or lard, or half of each
4. Let them become nicely brown

title: Appelkoekjes (Apple Fritters, 30 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 2 eggs
- 1 dl milk
- salt
- 5-6 sour apples
- 1/2 dl rum
- 20 g sugar
- 80 g frying fat or 3/4 dl salad oil

## Directions

1. Core the apples, peel them, slice them, and if desired soak them in rum mixed with sugar.
2. Stir the flour with the baking powder, egg yolks, salt, and gradually all the milk through each other until a smooth batter without lumps is obtained.
3. Fold this as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Dip some apple slices in this batter and fry them with the frying fat or salad oil in the frying pan until light brown and cooked through.
5. Sprinkle them with sugar mixed with fine cinnamon and serve as hot as possible.

title: Appelmoes (Apple Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and remove the cores
2. Stew them with some butter, sugar and cinnamon, mashing the apples
3. Bind the sauce with egg yolks

title: Appelmoes (Applesauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sour apples
- approximately 100 g white sugar
- some boiling water

## Directions

1. Cut the apples into quarters or sixths and immediately place them in cold water to keep them pale.
2. Wash them, set them immediately on with some boiling water (the amount depends on the thickness desired) and let them cook quickly without a lid until soft (10-15 minutes).
3. Stir from time to time to prevent burning.
4. Rub them through with a wooden spoon or press the sauce through a horsehair sieve in small portions at a time, as hot as possible.
5. Stir in the sugar and optionally an egg, with the white and yolk beaten separately.

title: Appelpudding (Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- puree of the best sour apples, well firmly cooked
- crumb of stale white bread
- melted butter
- eggs
- currants
- sugar
- cinnamon
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take puree of the best sour apples, well firmly cooked
2. Add crumb of stale white bread, melted butter, eggs, currants, sugar, cinnamon and grated lemon peel
3. Mix well together
4. Cook it in a mold until done

title: Appelpudding (Apple Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg tart apples
- 1/2 liter water
- 100-125 g sugar
- peel of 1 lemon
- 20 g red and white gelatin or 17 1/2 g powdered gelatin

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into small pieces, rinse them and cook them in the water that has been brought to a boil with the lemon peel
2. Pour everything through a horsehair sieve, rub the apples through it and mix the puree with the sugar and gelatin (which has been soaked 10-15 minutes in cold water and if necessary dissolved with some lemon juice and then with as little boiling water as possible)
3. Let the mass become jelly-like while stirring occasionally and pour it into a stone pudding mold rinsed with cold water
4. Turn the pudding out onto a damp dish after cooling, garnish it with fruit jelly and nuts and serve with vanilla sauce

title: Appelschoteltje (Apple Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 golden reinette apples
- approximately 2 dl Rhine wine
- 100 g sugar
- 40 g butter or margarine
- some fine cinnamon and chopped snippets or some marmalade

## Directions

1. Cream the butter or margarine, add some marmalade or some fine cinnamon and chopped snippets and 25 g sugar
2. Cut the peeled apples, carefully cleaned of brown spots, in half
3. Remove the cores, make shallow diamond-shaped incisions on the surface and fill the apples with a little of the mixture
4. Place them in a heat-proof dish, pour over the wine in which the remaining sugar is dissolved and let the apples become tender covered in a moderately warm oven (20 to 30 minutes), but ensure they remain whole

title: Appelspijs Gebakken (Baked Apple Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful flour
- water
- 8 eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- small cut lemon peel
- currants
- apples, cut into large cubes
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir a handful flour with water as for a pancake
2. Add 8 eggs, sugar, cinnamon, small cut lemon peel, currants and apples cut into large cubes
3. Stir the mixture well together
4. Bake in a deep pan with butter for 1.5 hours
5. Serve with sugar and cinnamon

title: Appelstruif (Apple Fritter)
body:
## Ingredients

- applesauce
- rusk
- cinnamon
- currants
- a little butter
- grated lemon zest
- 6 to 7 beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Mix rusk into applesauce in proportion to the sauce, with some cinnamon, currants and a little butter
2. Add grated lemon zest and 6 to 7 beaten eggs
3. Stir everything well together
4. Bake with fire above and below until done

title: Appeltaart (Apple Pie)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 175 g butter or margarine
- 80 g sugar
- 2 eggs
- 12 sour apples (750 g)
- 100 g sugar
- 100 g currants
- 50 g almonds
- grated zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Knead butter or margarine, flour, sugar and 1½ eggs together into a firm ball
2. If too soft, let rest for a quarter hour in a cool place
3. Roll out into a large round sheet and press into a greased springform pan so that the bottom is completely covered and the sides are covered 4 cm up
4. Slice the peeled apples and mix with sugar, well-washed currants with stems removed, chopped almonds and grated lemon zest
5. Fill the form with this mixture
6. Lay thin strips of remaining dough in a diamond pattern over the top
7. Roll the edge slightly so the strips are firmly attached, or make an extra rim of dough, flute it and finish the edge with it
8. Brush the dough with some of the remaining beaten egg
9. Bake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked through (approximately 1 hour)

title: Arme ridders (Poor Knights)
body:
## Ingredients

- Stale white bread, rolls or French bread, without crust
- Cold milk
- Beaten eggs
- Fine breadcrumbs
- Fat or brown butter for frying
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut bread into finger-thick slices in round shapes
2. Soak well in cold milk
3. Roll in beaten eggs and fine breadcrumbs
4. Fry in fat or in a pan with plenty of brown butter on both sides until brown
5. Sprinkle with sugar
6. Serve warm with chocolate custard or similar

title: Artisjokken (Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- water
- wine
- white bread
- sugar
- butter
- nutmeg
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut short near the stem
2. Remove the hard leaves and clean the rest of all roughness
3. Place in cold water
4. Cook for one and a half hours with plenty of salt
5. Remove the stringy parts
6. Serve with a sauce made from a little wine, finely grated white bread, sugar, water, butter and grated nutmeg, stirred with a couple of egg yolks

title: Artisjokken (Artichokes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large or 4 small artichokes
- 2.5 dl lemon sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the stem from the artichokes, take out the ugly leaves and cut off the withered tips from the remaining ones.
2. Wash them, halve the large ones and bind them crosswise with twine.
3. Cook them in boiling salted water until the leaves come away (approximately 1 hour or less).
4. Let them drain on a skimming spoon, remove the inner part (the still rolled flower petals) and serve them with lemon sauce or Hollandaise sauce.

title: Aspergepunten met Mayonnaise (Asparagus Tips with Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 asparagus tips
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Place the asparagus tips, which are cooked through, on the plate and cover them, once they have cooled, with mayonnaise.

title: Asperges (Asparagus)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 bunches asparagus
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Break off the hard ends of the asparagus.
2. Scrape the part where the head sits and peel the lower part.
3. Wash the asparagus and arrange it in well-cleaned bottles.
4. Pour on enough water so that the asparagus is just covered, add salt if desired.
5. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize for 1½ hours.
6. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.

title: Asperges in Bruine Saus (Asparagus in Brown Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg asparagus
- 2.5 dl lean jus with water
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the asparagus until done as described in recipe 463
2. Prepare the brown sauce in the ordinary manner and add some sugar and nutmeg
3. Gently stir the cooked asparagus through it
4. Serve it with beef tenderloin

title: Asperges in een Vuurvast Schoteltje au Gratin (Asparagus Gratin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can half-length pointed asparagus or 2 kg fresh cooked asparagus
- 75 g old grated cheese
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- approximately 2.5 dl asparagus liquid
- 0.5 dl cream
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 to 4 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Let the asparagus drain well in a colander and carefully collect the liquid
2. Make a very thick sauce from this liquid with butter or margarine and flour in the ordinary manner
3. Add the cream and egg yolks and approximately 50 g old cheese
4. Peel the hard-boiled eggs and cut them into slices
5. Lay a layer of asparagus in the bottom of the heatproof dish, then the eggs, then a layer of sauce, and so on, but ensure that the last layer consists of sauce
6. Sprinkle a little salt over the canned asparagus each time, sprinkle the reserved cheese over the dish and let a brown crust form on it in the oven

title: Aspergesla (Asparagus Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg braising asparagus
- optionally 1 egg
- 3 tablespoons salad oil
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the asparagus as indicated for 'braised asparagus' (recipe 463), but do not cook it too soft. Let it drain in a colander and treat it further as described in recipe 479.

title: Aspergesoep (Asparagus Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l asparagus liquid or water in which the ends of the asparagus have been cooked
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- (1 egg)
- 1 dl cream or milk
- piece of mace
- (some cooked asparagus tips)

## Directions

1. Let the mace steep in the asparagus liquid or in the water in which the hard ends of the asparagus have been cooked
2. Sieve the liquid and pour it gradually while stirring into the melted butter or margarine, mixed with the flour and from which all lumps have been carefully rubbed out
3. Let the soup cook while stirring for 10 minutes
4. Warm the asparagus tips in it and carefully pour the soup while stirring and beating into the egg, which has been beaten in the soup tureen with the milk or cream

title: Aspic (donkere) (Dark Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dl bouillon of beef or water with Maggi's bouillon cubes (4 pieces)
- brown color from meat juices, burnt sugar (caramel), soy, marmite, bay leaf, clove, carrot, onion, parsley, 1 dl vinegar

## Directions

1. For preparation see previous recipe.

title: Aspic (lichte) (Light Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For daubes: 9 dl bouillon of veal, fish or chicken, mace, carrot, parsley, onion, cayenne pepper, 35 g gelatine or 30 g powdered gelatine, 2 egg whites and eggshells, 1 dl vinegar
- For garnishing aspic: 9 dl bouillon, mace, carrot, parsley, onion, cayenne pepper, 55 g gelatine or 50 g powdered gelatine, 2 egg whites and eggshells, 1 dl vinegar

## Directions

1. Make bouillon in the usual way with the spices, strain the bouillon and let it cool.
2. Whip the egg whites briefly with the eggshells together.
3. Add them with the (soaked in cold water) gelatine to the bouillon and bring the mixture to a boil, whisking vigorously with a whisk.
4. Remove from heat and let it stand without stirring until it becomes completely clear.
5. Pour the aspic through a doubled cloth, add vinegar to taste and let it cool.

title: Atjar Ketimoen (Cucumber Pickles)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large cucumbers
- 2 spoons finely ground onions
- 1 spoon finely chopped garlic
- 1 teaspoon ginger powder
- 1 teaspoon laos powder
- 1 spoon fine turmeric
- 1 lombok without seeds
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 bottle vinegar
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut off the bitter ends, divide them into four parts lengthwise and then divide these lengthwise into eighths
2. Let the pieces stand overnight under salt with a heavy weight, such as a stone, on top
3. The next day remove the pieces, let them drain well and dry them with a clean cloth
4. Pound the spices with the onions — except the lombok — finely and fry them
5. Add the vinegar and the lombok and let this cook together briefly
6. Put the cucumber pieces in a well-cleaned stoppered bottle and pour the mass over them
7. Let the contents cool
8. Close the bottle airtight and let it stand for a few days, after which the atjar is suitable for use

title: Augurken (Gherkins in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- gherkins
- brine
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Handle as described for cucumbers, but let them stand in the brine for only one night

title: Augurken in het zout (Cucumbers in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg large cucumbers
- 2 liters water
- 200 g salt

## Directions

1. Clean the cucumbers as described in recipe 1005.
2. Place them in brine made from the water and salt and check them in the same manner as vegetables in salt.

title: Augurken Saus (Gherkin Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely chopped shallots
- flour
- butter
- boiling water
- fresh gherkins
- bay leaf
- tarragon
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Let some finely chopped shallots with flour and butter take on a light color by stirring on the fire.
2. Make a bound sauce with boiling water.
3. Peel fresh gherkins, cut them in half, remove the middle, cut them into small cubes and cook them with bay leaf, tarragon, crushed pepper and salt in vinegar until soft.
4. Add a small amount of Liebig's Meat Extract and let cook through.

title: Augurkjes in azijn (Pickled Gherkins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg gherkins
- 2 bottles pickling vinegar
- 2 Spanish peppers
- 5 g dried ginger root
- 5 g bay leaves
- 10 g horseradish
- 2 sprigs fennel
- some salad oil

## Directions

1. Clean the gherkins by cutting off the crown and stems and further clean them by brushing or wiping with a damp cloth.
2. Set them aside for one night sprinkled with salt. Let them drain as dry as possible in a colander the next day.
3. Fill well-cleaned bottles or pots with them and arrange the spices between them.
4. Pour vinegar over them so that the gherkins are amply covered and pour a layer of salad oil on top.
5. Close the bottles or pots with damp parchment paper and provide them with a label with name and date.

title: Bahmi (Chinese Vermicelli Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 pieces of mie = approximately 500 g
- 4 spoons ground onions
- 2 garlic cloves
- 1 teaspoon ginger powder
- 200 g pork cut into small cubes
- 100 g peeled shrimp
- 100 g sliced ham
- pepper
- 3 bunches celery
- 2 pieces leek
- 3 eggs
- 3 spoons water
- salt
- soy sauce
- lemon
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash the mie and let them soak in warm water for 3 hours and then drain in a colander
2. Slice them into pieces
3. Make broth from the meat cut into small cubes with some salt, about 4 dl
4. Remove the meat
5. Cook the cleaned and finely chopped vegetables in the broth until almost done
6. Pound the spices with the ground onions finely
7. Fry them
8. Then add the meat and the sliced ham and let this brown
9. Add this mass to the vegetables; add the mie and the shrimp and let this all stew in the broth (approximately 15 minutes)
10. Fry an omelet in the known manner
11. Slice this into strips
12. Put the bahmie in a heated dish and garnish it with omelet strips
13. Serve soy sauce or ketjap and eighth pieces of lemon with it

title: Baked Beans met Smac en Appel (Baked Beans with Smac and Apple)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g white or brown beans cooked in the usual way and drained
- approximately 2 onions
- 1 tin of tomato purée or less to taste
- 50 g margarine
- 2 cloves
- a pinch of thyme
- approximately 4 dl bouillon made from 2 cubes
- 1 tin smac
- 2 star apples
- 1 large sour apple

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped onion and margarine, add tomato purée and spices, the sour apple cut into chunks and after about 5 minutes the bouillon.
2. Make a fragrant sauce from all these ingredients.
3. Add the beans and let them stew for at least 1/2 hour.
4. Then put them in a deep ovenproof dish.
5. Divide the smac into two pieces.
6. Wash the apples, do not peel them but divide them into narrow wedges with the core removed.
7. Cut both pieces of smac into slices that remain connected at the bottom.
8. Insert a piece of red apple between every 2 slices.
9. Lay these 'hedgehogs' on top of the beans and let everything heat through thoroughly in the oven.

title: Bakpoeder (Baking Powder)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g cream of tartar
- 50 g double carbonate of soda
- 50 g fine rice flour

## Directions

1. Mix the three powders together and keep them in a tin box or in a well-sealed jam jar.

title: Bananenbeignets (Banana Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 6 ripe bananas
- 3 to 3.5 dl beer or sparkling water
- salt
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Heat the frying fat.
2. Peel and halve the bananas, then cut into pieces.
3. Prepare the batter as for apple beignets.
4. Coat some banana pieces in the batter, place them in the hot fat and fry them light brown and cooked through.
5. Let them drain thoroughly on gray paper.
6. Dust with powdered sugar and serve warm.
7. Optionally serve with raspberry sauce.

title: Banketletter (Almond Letter Pastry)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g hard butter or margarine
- 125 g flour
- approximately 1.5 dl water
- salt
- 125 g sweet almonds mixed with 4 bitter almonds
- 125 g sugar
- 1 egg
- some grated lemon zest or vanilla pulp
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846), roll it out the last time to a thickness of 1/4 cm and a width of 10 to 12 cm.
2. Make almond paste (see recipe 850).
3. Form small rolls from this almond paste on a lightly floured sheet approximately 2.5 cm thick.
4. Lay these next to each other in the middle of the dough, fold the dough around it and seal the ends with some water.
5. Lay the dough with the sealed side down on the baking sheet and shape it into the desired letter form.
6. Brush the letter with egg yolk that has been beaten with some water and bake it in a hot oven light brown and cooked through (approximately 20 minutes).
7. Remove it from the sheet and let it cool.

title: Barioles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 spoons flour
- 3 spoons sugar
- a piece of butter the size of half an egg
- half a grated fresh lemon peel or some orange blossom
- 4 egg yolks
- a glass of good cream

## Directions

1. Take 2 spoons flour, 3 spoons sugar and a piece of butter the size of half an egg and let this melt
2. Add half a grated fresh lemon peel or some orange blossom and 4 egg yolks
3. Mix everything together, adding a glass of good cream
4. Put this mixture in small pastry mold forms
5. Bring them briefly in the oven and serve them warm

title: Begijnenrijst (Begine Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter milk
- 150 or 225 g rice
- 1/2 g saffron
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the porridge as rice porridge from the previous recipe, but add the saffron to the milk.

title: Berliner bollen (Berlin Balls) (Makes ~40)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 600 g flour
- 6 eggs
- 125 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 50 g yeast
- approximately 1 dl lukewarm milk
- some salt
- some jam or marmalade

## Directions

1. Make a firm batter that can be rolled out from the flour, eggs, melted butter or margarine, sugar, mixed yeast, milk and salt.
2. Let this rise, covered, in a lukewarm place (approximately 1 hour).
3. Roll it out on a flour-dusted board with a flour-dusted rolling pin to a thickness of approximately 1 cm.
4. Cut out an even number of not-too-large circles with rings or glasses.
5. Brush the outside of the circles with some egg white, indent half of the circles slightly in the middle and place some marmalade or jam in this hollow.
6. Stick two circles firmly together at the edges.
7. Let the balls rise, covered with a somewhat damp cloth, for another 20 minutes to ½ hour.
8. Heat lard, frying fat or salad oil in a deep iron pot until blue steam rises, then place it on a gentle heat, add 4 to 5 balls at a time and fry them, turning occasionally, light brown and cooked (approximately 10 minutes).
9. Let the balls drain well on porous paper and sprinkle them with powdered sugar or roll them through sugar and cinnamon after cooling.

title: Berry Wine (Bassenwijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- 26 to 27 pounds berries per anchor
- Equal amount of common baster sugar
- Rainwater

## Directions

1. Drain the berries and rub them through a sieve
2. Take a properly sulfured and aired vessel
3. Put the juice in the vessel with the sugar dissolved in rainwater
4. Press out the seeds several times and add that juice to the rest until the vessel is full
5. Lay the vessel slightly higher at the back than at the front and let it ferment
6. After fermentation, wrap a cloth around the stopper and drive it in, not too tight, gradually tightening it each day
7. Let the vessel lie in the cellar until Christmas or New Year, when the wine is clear enough to tap
8. Handle it further as with ordinary wine

title: Beschuit met Bessensapsaus (Rusk with Berry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large rusks
- 3 dl berry juice
- 3 dl water
- 75 g sugar
- 10 g sago
- piece of cinnamon stick or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare the berry juice sauce in the usual manner (see recipe 748) without sago
2. Soak the rusks one by one in the berry juice sauce
3. Lay them in a shallow dish
4. Pour the remaining sauce, bound with sago, over them

title: Beschuit Podding (Rusk Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- crushed rusk
- eggs
- butter
- currants
- raisins
- candied citrus peel
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as bread pudding, but use crushed rusk instead of bread

title: Beschuitkoek (Rusk Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large rusks
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 l milk
- 40 g sugar
- 1/2 stick vanilla
- 40 g butter or margarine
- rum (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar and vanilla pulp, add the lukewarm milk gradually.
2. Melt some butter or margarine in a large frying pan, lay the rusks in it, pour a large part of the sauce over it and fry the cake on low heat until light brown on the bottom.
3. Add some of the remaining liquid from time to time until all of it has been absorbed into the rusks.
4. Turn it onto a butter-greased lid, let it slide from the lid into the pan and fry the other side light brown.
5. Sprinkle powdered sugar over the cake and if desired pour some rum over it.

title: Bessengelei (Berry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove berries from their stems
2. Squeeze the berries
3. Add sugar, using 3 ounces sugar for every 5 ounces of juice
4. Cook, carefully watching for foam, until thick enough that it clings to a spoon
5. Pour layer by layer into jars
6. Cover the jars with paper soaked in brandy

title: Bessengelei (Berry Jelly)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Berries, washed
- Sugar, 1 kg per liter of berry juice

## Directions

1. Wash the berries and place them with very little liquid on a gentle fire and let the berry juice extract.
2. Squeeze or press out the berries.
3. Measure the juice and use 1 kg sugar per liter.
4. Cook no more than 1 liter of juice at a time in a large pan.
5. As the juice boils, sprinkle the sugar in very slowly while stirring so that the sugar dissolves quickly and the liquid barely stops boiling.
6. Let the jelly boil for 2 minutes and pour it, still thin and liquid, into well-cleaned jam jars.
7. Treat further as other jelly.

title: Bessengelei (Red Currant Jelly, First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg red currants
- 1 kg sugar (approximately)

## Directions

1. Wash the currants, put them with very little liquid on a low heat and let the currant juice come out.
2. Squeeze or press out the currants.
3. Measure the juice and use 1 kg sugar for every liter.
4. Cook no more than 1 liter of juice at a time in a large pan.
5. When the juice boils, sprinkle the sugar in very slowly.
6. Stir meanwhile so that the sugar dissolves quickly and the liquid hardly comes off the boil.
7. Let the jelly boil for 2 minutes and put it, still thinly liquid, in well-cleaned jam jars.
8. Treat them further as other jelly.
9. Note: Instead of fresh fruit juice, one can also use currant juice from bottles.

title: Bessengelei, Second Recipe (Berry Jelly, Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg red and white berries or black berries
- 3/4 of the weight of the fruit juice in sugar
- Jam jars
- Parchment paper
- String
- Cellophane
- Elastic bands
- Labels

## Directions

1. Wash the berries well.
2. Let them steep with some water and allow the mass to drain through a horsehair sieve.
3. Weigh the juice or measure it (1/2 liter = 500 g) and cook until about a quarter has evaporated.
4. Add 3/4 of the original weight in sugar and cook until the jelly reaches the required thickness.
5. Treat further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Bessenjam (Red or White Currant Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg red or white currants (cleaned)
- 2¼ kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- elastic bands
- string
- labels

## Directions

1. Wash the currants, remove them from the stems
2. Put them in a pan, bring them to a boil with the lid on and let the fruit cook without the lid, stirring occasionally
3. Add the sugar and let the jam cook until it reaches the required thickness (see rules on page 369)
4. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983)

title: Bessensap (Berry Juice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2/3 red berries
- 1/3 black berries
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the fruit and add enough water so that they are half submerged.
2. Let them cook on a small fire until they have become soft (10 to 15 minutes) and then press them through a fruit press or through a folded linen cloth.
3. Put the juice in a clean pan, let it boil briefly and carefully skim off the foam.
4. Fill the well-cleaned and boiled bottles with it and close them immediately with boiled corks.

title: Bessensappudding met griesmeel (Berry Juice Pudding with Semolina)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 7½ dl berry juice
- 2½ dl water
- 90 g semolina
- 175 g sugar
- piece of cinnamon stick or peel of ½ lemon

## Directions

1. Bring the berry juice with the water and flavoring to a boil.
2. Sprinkle in the semolina and sugar slowly, stirring constantly, and let the pudding cook while stirring until done (5 to 10 minutes).
3. Remove the cinnamon stick or lemon peel, pour the mixture into a stone mold rinsed with cold water and let it cool and become firm.
4. Turn the pudding out onto a moist plate and serve it with vanilla sauce.

title: Bessensapsaus (Berry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 dl berry juice
- 2 1/2 dl water
- 75 g sugar
- 20 g sago or potato starch
- a piece of lemon peel or cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Bring the berry juice with the water, the very thinly pared lemon peel or the cinnamon stick to a boil
2. Mix the sago or potato starch with a little cold water into a thin paste
3. Pour this into the boiling liquid while stirring and let the sauce simmer while stirring briefly
4. Add the sugar afterwards
5. Remove from heat and adjust the flavour
6. Serve the sauce warm with cooked rice; cold with semolina or cornstarch pudding etc.

title: Bessenvla (Berry Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 eggs
- 2 dl berry juice
- 1 piece cinnamon stick
- 75 g brown sugar

## Directions
