1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar for a long time until they become thick, white and airy.
2. Add the fruit juice in small portions while stirring, add the cinnamon and let the custard bind au bain-marie while stirring vigorously.
3. After cooling, mix with the stiffly beaten egg white and serve with biscuits.

title: Beuling (Blood Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- unmelted lard
- pork blood
- cream
- salt
- spices
- clean intestines

## Directions

1. Chop onions very finely and cook them with unmelted lard
2. When well melted, add more unmelted lard cut into cubes, pork blood, cream, salt and spices
3. Stuff the beuling into clean intestines, tie them and cook in water for ten minutes
4. To serve, roast them, but prick the intestine so it does not burst

title: Biefstuk (Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Beat the meat well and rub it in with salt and pepper
2. Put a small piece of butter in the pan and let it brown well
3. Lay the beefsteak in and prick it until it is browned enough on one side
4. Turn it over; you do not need to prick it anymore
5. The pan cannot be too hot

title: Bierpap (Beer Pap)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 jug strong beer
- 3 or 4 eggs
- a little flour
- sugar to taste
- roasted bread or hardtack
- some ground cloves

## Directions

1. Beat 3 or 4 eggs with a little flour together
2. Stir that into the strong beer until everything is well cooked through
3. Add sugar to taste
4. Pour the mixture over roasted bread or hardtack
5. Sprinkle with some ground cloves

title: Bieten (Braised Beets)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 beets
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 0.5 dl vinegar
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- sago (15 g sugar optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the beets well in lukewarm water, let them cook in plenty of boiling water until the skin can be easily removed (in winter 3-4 hours, in summer 2-2.5 hours or less).
2. Let them drain in a colander, remove the skin and cut them into thin slices or cubes or grate them on a cucumber grater.
3. Peel the onion, wash and mince it.
4. Put the minced onion in the braising pan with a spoonful of water.
5. Add the butter or margarine, vinegar, cloves and beet and braise for another 20-30 minutes.
6. Before serving, remove the cloves and thicken the liquid with some mixed sago.
7. If the beets are not sweet enough, add the sugar.

title: Bietensla (Beet Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 beet
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the piece of beet, cut it into small pieces and treat it as described in recipe 9.

title: Bijoutaart (Jewel Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 120 g flour
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 5 eggs
- jam
- 2.5 dl milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 25 to 30 g sugar
- 25 g flour
- 1/2 vanilla stick
- chopped almonds
- icing
- candied fruits

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake like the German sand cake (see recipe 863).
2. Cut it after cooling into different layers, which are alternately covered with jam and pastry cream, prepared in the usual way (see recipe 816).
3. Spread the side of the cake with jam and roll it through the chopped almonds lightly browned in the oven.
4. Garnish the top with icing, almonds and candied fruits.

title: Bijststaart (Rice Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound best rice
- 1 quart sweet milk
- 6 egg yolks, beaten well
- 1 package almond press
- 4 tablespoons loaf sugar
- grated lemon zest
- ½ pound melted butter
- egg whites beaten to foam

## Directions

1. Cook ½ pound rice and 1 quart sweet milk until done.
2. When almost cold, beat together 6 egg yolks, almond press, 4 tablespoons loaf sugar, and grated lemon zest.
3. Stir in ½ pound melted butter.
4. Just before baking, lightly fold in the egg whites beaten to foam.
5. Bake slowly with heat on top, in hot ash.

title: Biscuit de Savoye (Savoy Biscuit)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 120 to 150 g powdered sugar
- 60 g flour
- 60 g Duryea cornstarch
- some vanilla pulp

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the powdered sugar until a smooth mass.
2. Add the vanilla pulp and gradually the sifted flour and cornstarch.
3. Mix this batter with a little of the very stiffly beaten egg white and then add it as lightly as possible to the remaining stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Put the batter in a greased cake pan, dusted with powdered sugar and flour, and bake the cake in a fairly warm oven until light brown and done (20 to 40 minutes).
5. Cover if necessary during baking with a greased piece of paper.
6. Test if it is done by inserting a knitting needle, which should then come out dry.
7. Let the cake cool on a cake rack and garnish it after cooling with icing and French fruits.

title: Biscuitgebakjes met Jam en Cocosnoot (Sponge Cakes with Jam and Coconut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 100 g flour
- 20 g cornstarch
- 150 g sugar
- the grated zest of one lemon
- marmalade or jam
- 50 g grated coconut

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs + 20 minutes with the sugar until a foamy mass.
2. Then stir through the sifted flour and the grated lemon zest.
3. Line tall cake molds with greased paper, fill them 1/4 with the mixture and let them bake in -- 20 minutes in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done.
4. Let the cakes cool, brush them with jam, sprinkle them with grated coconut and place a small fruit on top.

title: Biscuits (Sponge Fingers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g sugar
- 4 egg whites
- 5 egg yolks
- grated lemon zest
- 90 g flour
- 10 g cornstarch

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks completely smooth with the sugar and the grated lemon zest.
2. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, combine it with a few light strokes with the yolks and stir through this batter as quickly as possible the sifted flour.
3. Put the batter in a piping bag with a smooth nozzle and pipe oblong cookies onto a baking sheet with paper on it.
4. Sprinkle them with a little sugar and bake the biscuits in a hot oven done (at most 15 minutes), but ensure they remain as light in color as possible.
5. Remove them from the paper and let them cool.

title: Bishop (Bisschop)
body:
## Ingredients

- One bottle red wine
- One spoon bishop
- Sugar to taste
- Half a bottle water

## Directions

1. Take care that it does not boil
2. Stop the spout of the kettle with a plug to prevent the spirit from evaporating

title: Bisschopsbrood (Bishop's Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 200 g flour
- 200 g sugar
- 60 g raisins
- 60 g currants
- 30 g candied citrus peel
- 25 g almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the whole eggs and sugar for 15 minutes with a whisk.
2. Add the washed and dried currants and raisins, from which the stems have been removed, the chopped candied citrus peel and the chopped almonds.
3. Stir in the sifted flour last, as lightly as possible.
4. Pour the batter into a greased loaf pan and bake in a moderately warm oven until light brown (approximately 1 hour).
5. Check if it is done by inserting a knitting needle; it should come out dry.
6. Turn the cake out onto a cake rack and let it cool.

title: Bisschopsbrood (Bishop's Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- flour equal to the weight of 4 eggs (approximately 200 g)
- sugar equal to the weight of 4 eggs (approximately 200 g)
- 60 g raisins
- 60 g currants
- 30 g candied citrus peel
- 25 g almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the whole eggs and the sugar for 15 minutes with a whisk.

title: Bisschopskoekjes (Bishop's Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 2.5 ounces butter
- grated lemon peel
- mace (foelie)
- 13 drams flour

## Directions

1. Beat 8 eggs with 2.5 ounces butter until foamy
2. Add grated lemon peel and mace
3. Mix in 13 drams flour
4. Put the batter in molds greased with butter
5. Bake gently until done

title: Bitterkoekjes (Bitter Almond Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g bitter almonds
- 200 g sweet almonds
- 600 g sugar
- + 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Peel the blanched almonds, dry them and grind them with 150 g sugar twice through an almond mill fine.
2. Mix them with the remaining sugar and enough egg white until a fairly loose dough is obtained.
3. Pipe through the piping bag fitted with a smooth funnel onto grey paper placed on a baking sheet, small round balls.
4. Flatten them slightly with a damp cloth and bake them in a fairly hot oven until light brown and done (+ 1/2 hour).
5. Let them cool on the paper and remove them carefully.

title: Bitterkoekjesmelk (Bitter Almond Cake Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 and one-half cans milk
- 1 ounce finely ground bitter almond cakes

## Directions

1. For three and one-half cans milk take 1 ounce finely ground bitter almond cakes and let it cook until it is bound

title: Blanc-manger (in Various Colours) (6 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/2 liter milk
- 20 g white gelatin or 17 1/2 g powdered gelatin
- 80-100 g sugar
- 150 g almond macaroons (10-12 pieces)
- 1/2 drop carmine
- some coffee extract

## Directions

1. Prepare the pudding as indicated for blanc-manger with almond macaroons
2. Measure the content of the rinsed pudding mold and divide the pudding mixture into 3 parts, each of which will give an equally thick layer in the mold
3. Pour 1/3 of the mass in, let this mass become cold and stiff
4. Pour then a third part of the mixture again, which is colored rose, but make sure that it is so lukewarm that the lower part cannot melt and that the 2 layers stick together
5. Let this become stiff
6. Add to the last part some coffee extract and another half sheet of dissolved gelatin and pour this, also lukewarm, onto the congealed second part
7. Let the pudding become cold and stiff, turn it out onto a damp dish and serve without sauce
8. Note: If one wishes to color the pudding with red gelatin instead of carmine, it is best to dissolve the gelatin in 3 parts separately and in one part instead of 3 1/2 sheets of white gelatin: 1 sheet of red and 2 sheets of white gelatin. In the last part more gelatin must be dissolved, as otherwise it would be too diluted by the coffee extract and thus not become stiff

title: Blanc-manger (Simple) (6 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/2 liter milk
- 20 g white gelatin or 17 1/2 g powdered gelatin
- 80 g sugar
- 1 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Slowly bring the milk with the cream and the vanilla stick, which especially must not be split, to a boil
2. Add the sugar
3. Soak the gelatin for 15 minutes in plenty of cold water, then dissolve it, after wringing out well, in the liquid taken off the fire
4. Remove the vanilla stick
5. Stir from time to time until the mass begins to become jelly-like
6. Pour it into a mold rinsed with cold water and let it become cold and stiff
7. Turn the pudding out onto a damp dish
8. Note: This pudding does not need a sauce, vanilla wafers and strips are served with it. However, a fragrant raspberry sauce tastes quite good with this cream pudding

title: Blanc-manger (with Almond Macaroons) (6 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/2 liter milk
- 20 g white gelatin or 17 1/2 g powdered gelatin
- 80-100 g sugar
- 150 g almond macaroons (10-12 pieces)

## Directions

1. Wrap the almond macaroons in a piece of cheesecloth and let them steep in the milk
2. Remove them from the milk
3. Dissolve the sugar in the milk and pour the mass through a sieve in which a piece of cheesecloth has been laid
4. Prepare the pudding further as described in the previous recipe

title: Blanke Soep (White Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or a couple of chickens
- water
- salt
- rice or stale white bread pieces
- lemon juice
- nutmeg
- egg sauce

## Directions

1. Cook from veal or a couple of chickens
2. When the meat or chickens are cooked, remove them from the broth and keep them warm
3. In the broth cook rice or pieces of stale white bread until done with the addition of salt, lemon juice and nutmeg
4. The veal or chickens are eaten with egg sauce

title: Blankmangé (Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- two and a half ounces rice
- one and a half ounces bread sugar
- one can sweet milk
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. In a new pan, cook the rice, bread sugar and sweet milk under constant and strong stirring until it is thick
2. Grate lemon zest through it

title: Blankmangé (Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces rice
- 1.5 ounces bread sugar
- 1 quart sweet milk
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook in a new pan with constant and vigorous stirring 2.5 ounces rice, 1.5 ounces bread sugar and 1 quart sweet milk until it is thick
2. Grate lemon peel through it

title: Bleekselderij au jus (Pale Celery au Jus)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bunches celery
- salt to taste
- 2 dl lean jus with water or bouillon
- 50 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the celery until tender, as described in the previous recipe.
2. Let it drain in a colander, place it in a fireproof small dish and pour the bouillon or lean jus with water over it.
3. Place small pieces of butter or margarine here and there, sprinkle some breadcrumbs over it and let the dish stew in the oven for approximately 15 minutes.

title: Blinde Vinken (Stuffed Veal Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oblong-shaped veal cutlets of approximately 75 g
- 75 g veal mince or half veal, half pork mince
- 1 egg
- Old bread
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- ½ lemon
- Approximately 80 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash or scrape the cutlets and rub them with salt.
2. Make the mince with the egg yolk, some soaked old bread, pepper, salt and nutmeg.
3. Divide it into four portions and spread it over the cutlets, making sure it does not stick out at the edges.
4. Roll up the cutlets, tie them closed with a very thin string, roll them in egg white that has been beaten with one tablespoon of water and then roll them in fine breadcrumbs.
5. Make the butter or margarine light brown in the frying pan, fry the blinde vinken carefully until brown in it and braise them in the finished jus, with lemon slices without pips, over a gentle fire for another 45 minutes to 1 hour, but ensure the crust does not come loose.
6. Remove the strings before serving.

title: Bloedworst (Blood Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- warm pork blood
- pork tongue
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- clove powder
- rye flour
- ox intestines
- lukewarm water

## Directions

1. Stir the warm pork blood until the skin and fibers are removed
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Cut the pork tongue and some bacon into small cubes
4. Mix with the blood, adding salt, pepper, clove powder and some rye flour
5. Stuff into ox intestines
6. Hang them in lukewarm water and remove as soon as they boil to prevent bursting

title: Bloemkool (Cauliflower)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cauliflower
- 1/2 l milk
- 50 g flour or 30 g Duryea cornstarch
- 50 g butter or 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower by cutting out the stalk, green leaves and small leaves in the cabbage.
2. Cut the cabbage into large pieces or leave the cauliflower whole.
3. Make an incision in the remaining stalk to let it cook more easily, wash the cabbage and in the caterpillar season soak it for half an hour in salted water.
4. Set it on with enough boiling water to just cover it, add salt and let it cook until a fork easily penetrates the stalk (in summer 15-20 minutes, in winter 30-40 minutes).
5. Remove the cabbage with a skimming spoon from the pan, let it drain well and place it in a warmed dish.
6. Pour the sauce prepared in the usual way (see recipe 328 or recipe 329) over it and grate some nutmeg over.

title: Bloemkool in een vuurvast schoteltje (Cauliflower au Gratin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cauliflower
- 1/2 l milk or cauliflower water
- 30-40 g flour or 20 g Duryea cornstarch
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 15 g butter or margarine extra
- 60 g old cheese

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower and cook it tender, as described in the previous recipe.
2. Remove the cabbage with a skimming spoon from the pan, let it drain and place it in an ovenproof dish.
3. Pour the sauce prepared in the usual way (see recipe 328 or recipe 329) and through which the greater part of the grated cheese has been stirred over it.
4. Sprinkle the remaining cheese over, pour the melted butter over it and let it color very lightly brown in the oven.

title: Bloemkool in een vuurvast schoteltje (Cauliflower au Gratin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cauliflower
- 1/2 liter milk or cauliflower water
- 30 to 40 g flour or 20 g Duryea maizena
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 15 g butter or margarine extra
- 60 g aged cheese

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower and cook it until tender, as described in the previous recipe.
2. Remove the cauliflower from the pan with a skimming spoon, let it drain, and place it in a heat-proof dish.
3. Pour the sauce, which is prepared in the usual manner (see recipe 328 or recipe 329) and into which most of the grated cheese has been stirred, over it.
4. Sprinkle the remaining cheese over it, drizzle with the melted butter, and let it brown lightly in the oven.

title: Bloemkoolsalade (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower stalks, sliced
- cauliflower florets
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower with stalks cut into slices and florets laid on top.
2. Dress with salt, pepper, vinegar, and oil.

title: Bloemkoolsaus (Cauliflower Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh butter
- Half a spoon flour
- Milk, cream or bouillon
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- A couple egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix half a spoon flour into fresh butter
2. Add milk, cream or bouillon as preferred, along with nutmeg and salt
3. Finally stir a couple egg yolks through

title: Bloemkoolsaus (Cauliflower Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring to a boil while stirring.
2. Rub out all lumps carefully.
3. Add the milk while stirring, in small amounts at a time, and let the sauce cook while stirring until done (10 minutes).
4. Add salt as needed.
5. Serve over cauliflower or use it, either thinner or thicker, for stewing various vegetables.
6. Make a thick sauce with leafy vegetables, as moisture will draw out from the vegetables.
7. Make the sauce thinner with vegetables that release no moisture, for example salsify.

title: Bloemkoolsoep (Cauliflower Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- bouillon
- egg
- water

## Directions

1. Cut cauliflower into small pieces, cook them briefly
2. Then let them cook a little longer in bouillon, but do not let them become too soft
3. Now pour them off and place them in the soup tureen
4. Stir an egg in a little cold water

title: Bloemkoolsoep (Cauliflower Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- bouillon
- 1 egg
- potato starch
- herbs to taste

## Directions

1. Cut cauliflower into small pieces and cook them
2. Let them cook further in bouillon, but do not let them become too soft
3. Drain them and place them in the soup tureen
4. Stir an egg in some cold water with a spoon of potato starch
5. Add this with desired herbs to the boiling bouillon
6. Stir well and pour it over the cauliflower

title: Bloemkoolsoep (Cauliflower Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l cauliflower water (optionally with some leftover cauliflower pieces) or 3/4 l cauliflower water and 1/4 l milk
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg (optional)
- 1 dl cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine and then bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour, add the cauliflower water with the milk in small amounts while stirring and let the soup cook until done (approximately 10 minutes).
2. Warm the cauliflower pieces in it and if desired carefully pour the soup into the egg that has been beaten in the soup tureen, optionally with the cream or milk.
3. Serve small cubes of bread fried in butter or margarine with it.

title: Boekweitegort met Karnemelk (Buckwheat Porridge with Buttermilk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l buttermilk
- 200 g buckwheat groats
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the buttermilk to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Prepare the porridge further, as described in the previous recipe.

title: Boekweitegort met Melk (Buckwheat Porridge with Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l milk
- 200 g buckwheat groats
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
2. Sprinkle in the buckwheat groats while stirring and let the porridge cook while stirring until done (15 to 20 minutes).
3. For stiff buckwheat porridge, use 250 g per 1.5 l.

title: Boekweiten Gort (Buckwheat Groats)
body:
## Ingredients

- Buttermilk
- Groats

## Directions

1. For each can of buttermilk, take 2½ ounces of groats
2. Cook to the required thickness with constant stirring

title: Boerekool (Boerenkool - Kale)
body:
## Ingredients

- kale
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the kale tender and let it drain
2. Chop finely
3. Press out firmly
4. Stew with a piece of butter, a little bouillon, pepper, salt and nutmeg

title: Boerekool Anders (Kale - Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- kale
- potatoes
- smoked bacon
- lard
- water
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat the kale as before
2. Add peeled potatoes and a piece of smoked bacon
3. Stew with a little lard, water, nutmeg and a little salt
4. Shake together continuously

title: Boerekoolsoep (Kale Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- kale
- butter
- veal or beef bouillon
- salt
- mace
- pepper
- crushed cloves

## Directions

1. Chop kale very finely and fry it with butter until soft
2. Now add it to veal or beef bouillon with salt, mace, pepper and crushed cloves
3. Let it continue cooking until it is done

title: Boerenjongens (Raisins in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g sultana raisins
- 250 g sugar
- 2½ dl water
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 1 liter brandy
- 200 g almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the raisins, wash them with hot water, let them drain well.
2. Melt the sugar in the water.
3. Add the cinnamon and the fruit.
4. Let the fruit come to a boil on the fire and become soft.
5. Transfer the fruit to the well-cleaned stoppered bottle (see page 369 3°); cook the sugar solution down a bit more, let it cool and pour it over the fruit.
6. Add the brandy when the mixture has cooled completely.
7. Seal the bottle hermetically.
8. Use the fruit only after 3 months.
9. Optionally, the peeled almonds can be added at the same time as the brandy.

title: Boerenkool met Aardappelen en Rookworst (Kale with Potatoes and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches or 1 1/2 kg kale
- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Strip the kale, that is, remove the leafy greens from the hard central ribs, wash it several times in plenty of cold water and cook it in salted water until tender (approximately 1 hour).
2. Drain the kale then

title: Boerenkool met groene erwten en aardappelen (Kale with Green Peas and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches or 1½ kg kale
- 1 kg potatoes
- 250 g green peas
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Follow the preparation method of the previous recipe (Kale with Potatoes and Smoked Sausage), but add the cooked and drained peas together with the potatoes.

title: Boerenmeisjes (Apricots in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g apricots or 300 g dried apricots
- 350 g sugar
- 2½ dl water
- some lemon peel
- 1 liter brandy

## Directions

1. Wash the fruit.
2. Wash the dried fruit with hot water.
3. Let them drain.
4. Make a sugar syrup.
5. Add the lemon peel and the fruit to it.
6. Let them boil for 1-2 minutes in the hot liquid.
7. Remove the pan from the fire.
8. Let the fresh fruit stand for 24 hours in a bowl with the hot sugar syrup.
9. Let the dried apricots first boil for 10 minutes and stand for 48 hours in the sugar syrup.
10. When the fruit is soft, transfer it to the clean stoppered bottle (see page 369 3°).
11. Cook the sugar solution down a bit more on the fire and let it cool.
12. Pour it over the fruit.
13. When the mixture has cooled completely, add the brandy and seal the bottle hermetically.
14. Store the apricots for 3 months before using them.

title: Boerenspruiten met aardappelen en rookworst (Turnip Greens with Potatoes and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches or 1½ kg turnip greens
- 2 kg potatoes
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the stamppot entirely as kale with smoked sausage (recipe 516).

title: Boerenspruiten met groene erwten en aardappelen (Turnip Greens with Green Peas and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches or 1½ kg turnip greens
- 2 kg potatoes
- 250 g green peas
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the stamppot entirely as kale with green peas and potatoes (recipe 517).

title: Boeuf (Beef Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- Upper part of beef rump
- Beef fat
- Butter
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Cloves
- Vinegar
- Water
- Salt
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Lard the upper part of a beef rump with beef fat, rolled beforehand in a mixture of butter, pepper, nutmeg and cloves.
2. Then place it for a couple of days in vinegar.
3. Braise the meat in a pan with a little vinegar and water, sufficient salt and a couple of bay leaves.

title: Boeuf (Beef)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg boeuf
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 20 to 25 minutes per 500 g.

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rump
- Kidney fat or bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Breadcrumbs, pounded
- Bacon slices
- Wine vinegar
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Lemon slices
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Lard a piece of the rump with kidney fat or bacon, rolled beforehand in salt, pepper and cloves, and dust the meat itself also with salt, pepper and pounded breadcrumbs.
2. Then place bacon slices at the bottom of a pan, put the meat on top of them, and add a glass of beer of wine vinegar with water, some bay leaves, slices of lemon and some butter.
3. Close the lid tightly and set the pan early in the morning on low heat.
4. When done, stir some fried flour into the pan to bind the sauce.

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the rump
- Kidney fat or bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Bacon slices
- A beer glass of vinegar
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Lemon slices
- Butter
- Flour (fried)

## Directions

1. Lard a piece of beef from the rump with kidney fat or bacon that has been previously rolled in salt, pepper and cloves
2. Sprinkle the meat itself with salt, pepper and ground rusks
3. Lay bacon slices at the bottom of a earthenware pan, place the meat on top
4. Add a beer glass of vinegar with water, some bay leaves, lemon slices and some butter
5. Close the lid tightly and place the pan on a low fire early in the morning
6. When cooked, stir in some fried flour through the pan to bind the sauce

title: Bolpens (Beef Paunch)
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 pounds beef
- 4 ounces salt
- 4 drams cloves
- 1 ounce pepper
- 4 nutmegs
- water
- vinegar
- fat

## Directions

1. Soak the paunch in water overnight
2. Mince or cut the beef not too finely
3. Mix thoroughly with salt and spices
4. Cut the paunch into square pieces and sew into pouches
5. Fill pouches with meat, not too tightly, and sew closed
6. Hang in cold water and boil continuously for 3 hours, ensuring they remain submerged
7. Let drain and press under a weighted board until cold
8. Place in an earthenware pot with half vinegar and half of the cooking liquid
9. Cover with a slate or pour a layer of fat over the top

title: Boluskoek (Bolus Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For the dough: 475 g flour, 120 g melted butter or margarine, 2 eggs, approximately 3 dl lukewarm milk, 30 g yeast
- For the filling: 200 g butter or margarine, 100 g sugar, 1 teaspoon fine cinnamon, 75 g candied orange peel, 1 dl syrup
- 50 g flour for dusting the board or table

## Directions

1. Make a firm batter from the flour, melted butter or margarine, the eggs, the lukewarm milk and the yeast mixed with lukewarm milk.
2. Let this rise, covered, in a lukewarm place for approximately 1 hour.
3. In the meantime, for the filling, stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the sugar, the fine cinnamon, the chopped candied orange peel and the syrup.
4. Roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board to approximately 1/2 cm thickness.
5. Cut strips of 2.5 cm width.
6. Pinch the strips together on both sides so that a groove is formed, which is filled with the filling, which should protrude slightly above the dough.
7. Roll the strips with the openings facing upward in a spiral shape (starting in the middle) into a greased iron pot, fireproof saucer or cake pan.
8. Bake the cake for 1 to 1.5 hours in a moderately warm oven and drizzle it occasionally with the liquid that comes out, or, if this is not sufficient, with melted butter or margarine mixed with some syrup.
9. Turn out the cake while warm, cut it into pieces after cooling.

title: Bonenfilosoof (Bean Philosopher)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g brown beans
- 1/2 kg cooked potatoes
- 200 g onions
- 40 g butter or margarine
- Marmite
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the washed and 24 hours soaked beans in the known manner until tender and mash them.
2. Lightly fry the chopped onions in the butter or margarine until light brown.
3. Make the cooked potatoes fine.
4. Mix beans, onions and potatoes together.
5. Moisten the mass with the bean cooking liquid.
6. Season the whole with salt and marmite (mixed with water).
7. Place the mass in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with a little water.
8. Finish the dish as indicated in recipe 85.

title: Bonenpurée (Bean Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g brown or white beans
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- nutmeg to taste
- chopped parsley if white beans are used

## Directions

1. Prepare it entirely as the pea purée from the previous recipe.

title: Bonenpurée (Bean Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g brown or white beans
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley if white beans are used

## Directions

1. Prepare it entirely as pea purée from the previous recipe.

title: Bonensla (Bean Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cooked salad beans
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat the cooked (if necessary rinsed) beans as described in recipe 9.

title: Bookworst (Book Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds beef
- 5 pounds lean pork
- 2 pounds bacon
- 2 and a half ounces coarse white pepper
- half ounce cloves
- 12 ounces salt
- wide intestines
- fine salt
- saltpeter

## Directions

1. Chop the meat and bacon very finely and work them well together
2. Stuff tightly into wide intestines
3. Hang to dry for one night
4. Take 4 ounces fine salt and saltpeter and rub them in
5. Smoke for three weeks, so they do not become too hard

title: Boonensoep (Bean Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- rainwater
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat entirely as described for green pea soup

title: Borstplaat (First Recipe) (Almond Brittle Candy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Make sugar moist with as much water that it just barely dissolves.
2. Bring the sugar solution to a boil and cook it until it has reached the required thickness.
3. Test this as follows: Stick in the sugar solution a small stick (for example the handle of a butter brush), which has been in cold water for some time, stick it afterwards again in the cold water and try whether the adhering.

title: Borstplaat (First Recipe) (Breast Candy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water
- essence or cacao or Nescafe

## Directions

1. Moisten sugar with just enough water so it is barely covered.
2. Bring the sugar solution to a boil and cook until it reaches the required thickness.
3. Test the consistency by dipping a wooden stick (e.g., a butter brush handle) that has been in cold water into the sugar solution, then return it to cold water and try to form a firm ball from the adhering sugar solution with your finger.
4. Remove the pan from the heat and add cold water drop by drop until the sugar solution no longer effervesces.
5. Let the mixture become opaque and cool while stirring occasionally.
6. Add a little essence, let the breast candy become thick and fluid, and pour it as quickly as possible into rings that have been in cold water for some time, then placed on smooth white paper.
7. When the breast candy is stiff, flip it with the rings, let the underside dry, and then remove the rings.

title: Borstplaat (Second Recipe) (Breast Candy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g sugar
- 1½ dl water
- essence or 20 g cacao or 1 tablespoon Nescafe

## Directions

1. Pour the water onto the sugar, dissolve it completely and let this solution boil until a drop falling from the spoon pulls a thin thread.
2. Remove from heat and stir until it becomes opaque.
3. Add the essence, let the breast candy become thick and fluid and finish it as indicated in the previous recipe.

title: Bosobottels te Konfijten (Candied Rose Hips)
body:
## Ingredients

- rose hips
- half kilogram sugar
- 4 ounces rose hips

## Directions

1. Cut the fruit lengthwise open, with a spoon remove the pits, wash, cook a quarter hour in plenty of water and drain
2. Cook half a kilogram sugar until it becomes syrupy, add 4 ounces rose hips, and when everything has cooled put it in bottles

title: Bot (Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- flounder
- water
- salt
- parsley root
- parsley leaves

## Directions

1. Set the fish in water for an hour
2. Cook with water and sufficient salt, but be careful of the foam
3. As water flounder, serve with parsley root and parsley leaves in bunches and dress with some fish water

title: Boterbiesjes (Butter Rounds)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 250 g butter or margarine
- 150 g powdered sugar or brown sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare them as sand cookies (recipe 772)

title: Boterbollen (Butter Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ ounces wheat flour
- ¼ quart milk
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix 2½ ounces wheat flour with ¼ quart milk and let simmer slowly with constant stirring.
2. When cooled slightly, slowly beat in 4 eggs.
3. Roll into small balls and fry in not too hot oil.

title: Boterkoeken (Butter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces flour
- 1 and a half ounces butter, melted in warm milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 5 drams crushed sugar
- A little cinnamon
- Dissolved yeast
- Fine sliced almonds
- Bread sugar

## Directions

1. Warm flour dry
2. Mix with butter melted in warm milk
3. Add egg yolks, crushed sugar, cinnamon and dissolved yeast
4. Cut dough into pieces and form into cakes the thickness of a couple of guilders
5. Pour melted butter over them
6. Sprinkle with fine sliced almonds and bread sugar
7. Bake on a sheet in the oven until done

title: Botersaus (Butter Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 80 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Melt 40 g of the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring to a boil while stirring.
2. Rub out all lumps carefully.
3. Add the liquid while stirring, in small amounts at a time, and let the sauce cook while stirring until done.
4. Add the remaining butter or margarine in small pieces at a time, after the sauce is removed from the heat.
5. Serve with haddock, cod, potatoes, hamburger rib, etc.

title: Bouillivlees (Boiled Meat for 2 days)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg boiling meat for example breast
- approximately 1 1/2 liters boiling water
- pair of bay leaves
- piece of mace
- small carrot
- sprig of parsley
- some salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, place it in boiling water with the spices and some salt.
2. Let it simmer gently for 5 hours, turning it several times during cooking and serve it with an egg or mustard sauce, for which the bouillon of the meat is used.
3. Slice the meat thickly before serving.

title: Bouillon (in een uur) (Beef Broth in One Hour)
body:
## Ingredients

- half Dutch pound beef
- veal if for a sick person
- lamb if for a sauce
- carrots
- onions
- vegetables as desired
- half beer glass water
- approximately one and a half cans boiling water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small pieces
2. Place in a pot with carrots, onions, and vegetables of choice
3. Add half a beer glass of water
4. Let simmer slowly on gentle heat for about a quarter hour
5. Once it begins to stick to the pot, pour approximately one and a half cans of boiling water over it, more or less depending on desired strength
6. Add salt
7. Let boil on high heat for a quarter hour
8. Filter the broth through a linen cloth

title: Bouillon met Zakeieren (Bouillon with Poached Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- bouillon made from 500 g beef shank meat
- 4 fresh eggs

## Directions

1. Make bouillon in the usual way, see recipe 35.
2. Strain it through a flannel or double-folded linen cloth so that all fibers are removed.
3. Prepare the poached eggs as indicated in recipe 402.
4. Place one egg in each bowl of bouillon.

title: Bouillon trekken (Beef Broth)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g beef shank meat (or beef knuckle) for soup
- 1 l water
- 5 to 10 g salt
- herbs (parsley, carrot, piece of mace, thyme for drinking broth)

## Directions

1. Rinse the meat, cut it into small pieces or make deep cuts on all sides.
2. Bring it in a tightly closed pan slowly to a boil and let the broth simmer on gentle heat (3 to 4 hours).
3. Pour it through a sieve lined with a rinsed flannel or muslin cloth.
4. For drinking broth, let the following herbs simmer along: parsley, carrot, a piece of mace and thyme, but use so little that the flavor does not overpower.
5. After straining the drinking broth, warm it again and serve in cups with a slice of lemon and a rusk or piece of toasted bread.
6. The leftover meat can be excellently used in various dishes, for example rice or barley with curry.

title: Bouillon van kalfsschenkelvlees (Veal Broth)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g veal shank meat
- 1 l water
- 5 to 10 g salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see beef broth (recipe 35).

title: Bouillon van kalfsschenkelvlees (Veal Shank Bouillon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g veal shank meat
- 1 liter water
- 5 to 10 g salt

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the method for beef bouillon (recipe 35).

title: Bouillon van Ossevleesch (Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of lean beef, preferably from the hind quarter
- water

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of lean beef, preferably from the hind quarter of the ox
2. Set it with water to cook, for instance for each Dutch pound use 4 bottles of water
3. Let it cook well for 4 to 5 hours
4. Pay special attention to skimming

title: Bout van een Zeuglam (Leg of a Suckling Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of suckling lamb
- salt
- pepper
- crushed rusk
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare as the ordinary leg of lamb
2. It can also be roasted after rolling it in fine salt, pepper and some crushed rusk
3. Place a small lump of butter at the bottom of the pan

title: Bowl (Fruit Bowl)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg fruit
- one kind of fragrant fruit, such as strawberries, or mixed, such as strawberries with peaches, or strawberries with pineapple
- various fresh fruit as desired: strawberries, raspberries, cherries, pears, grapes, pineapple, oranges, mandarins or peaches
- canned or bottled fruit
- 400 g brown sugar
- lemon juice
- 2 bottles Rhine wine or May wine or alcohol-free wine
- 1 bottle champagne or soda water

## Directions

1. Clean the fruit; remove the pits.
2. Cut them into neat small pieces if necessary.
3. Let them stand covered for several hours with the sugar.
4. Then add the wine and as much sieved lemon juice until the drink is flavored to taste.
5. Let the mixture stand in a cool place for several more hours.
6. Just before serving, add the chilled champagne or chilled soda water.
7. Present the bowl as cold as possible.
8. When using canned or bottled fruit, the liquid can also be used.

title: Bozekoekjes (Rose Water Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- One piece stale white bread, without crust
- Cooked rainwater for soaking
- 8 eggs with white
- 3 spoons rosewater

## Directions

1. Soak bread in cooked rainwater
2. Beat eggs with white finely
3. Mix in rosewater
4. Fry well in fat

title: Brabbel-koek (Batter Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons wheat flour
- 3 eggs
- A little cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 2 coffee cups cold water
- Boiling butter, or half butter and half lard

## Directions

1. Beat together the wheat flour, eggs, cinnamon, and sugar with 2 coffee cups cold water
2. Pour the batter through a funnel into boiling butter, or half butter and half lard
3. Let it become nicely brown

title: Bramen- en Bessengerecht (Blackberry and Berry Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g fruit (blackberries, blueberries, white and red currants)
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Pick through the berries and wash all fruits well. Mix them to taste with sugar (brown bastard sugar) and let the sugar infuse for an hour.

title: Bramensapsaus (Blackberry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 dl blackberry juice
- 1 1/2 dl water
- approximately 60 g sugar
- 20 g sago or potato starch
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as raspberry juice sauce
2. Use blackberry juice instead of lemon juice if desired

title: Bread Dish with Almonds (Broodschoteltje met amandelen)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 thin slices of old bread without crust (200 g)
- 4 1/2 deciliters milk
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 100 g almonds or other nuts
- 75 g sugar
- fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds, peel them, grind them in an almond mill until fine.
2. Soak the bread in boiling milk, rub it smooth, add the egg yolks, the sugar, the almonds and lastly lightly the stiffly beaten egg white to it.
3. Put everything in a fireproof dish, smooth the top, sprinkle some fine cinnamon and sugar over it and let the dish brown and rise in a moderately warm oven.

title: Bread Dish with Apples (Broodschoteltje met appelen)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- 1/2 kg tart apples
- 80 to 100 g sugar
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 2 teaspoons fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook applesauce in the usual manner (see recipe 531), but ensure it is not too dry and press it through a horsehair sieve.
2. Mix it with the sugar.
3. Cut the old bread, after removing the crust from all sides, into thin slices.
4. Spread these on both sides with some butter or margarine and layer them alternately with the applesauce in a fireproof dish.
5. Ensure that the top layer consists of bread and that the slices fit well against each other.
6. Sprinkle the fine cinnamon mixed with some sugar over it and let the dish brown in a moderately warm oven (approximately 20 minutes).

title: Bread Dish with Currants and Raisins (Broodschoteltje met krenten en rozijnen)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 thin slices of old bread without crust (approximately 200 g)
- 4 1/2 deciliters milk
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 50 g currants
- 50 g raisins
- 50 g candied citron
- 60 g sugar
- 175 g butter (or 50 g butter) or margarine
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD. Beat the eggs with the sugar, add the lukewarm milk to it. Wash the currants and raisins, which have been removed from the stems, chop the candied citron.
2. Spread the thin bread slices on both sides with some butter or margarine and layer them alternately with the mixture in the fireproof dish, but ensure that the last layer consists of bread.
3. Moisten each layer of bread with a portion of the milk and egg sauce.
4. Sprinkle some sugar mixed with cinnamon over the last layer of buttered bread.
5. Let a crispy crust form on the dish in the oven.
6. SECOND METHOD. Soak the bread in boiling milk and rub it completely smooth.
7. Add the egg yolks, the sugar, the well-washed currants and raisins and the chopped candied citron to it and lastly stir in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible.
8. Put the mixture in a fireproof dish, sprinkle some fine cinnamon mixed with sugar over it, place small lumps of butter or margarine here and there on it and let the dish brown and rise in a moderately warm oven.

title: Bread Dish with Oranges (Broodschoteltje met sinaasappelen)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- approximately 4 1/2 deciliters milk
- salt
- juice of 4 oranges
- zest of 1 orange
- 1 to 2 eggs
- approximately 100 g sugar
- 50 to 60 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Rub the bread, which has been carefully removed of crusts, completely smooth in the boiling milk to which some salt has been added.
2. Stir the butter or margarine until it has become thick, white and airy, add the egg yolks, sugar and grated orange zest to it and rub the sugar smooth.
3. Mix this mixture with the soaked bread, add the orange juice and lastly as quickly as possible the stiffly beaten egg white to it.
4. Let the mixture in a fireproof dish in a moderately warm oven become light brown and rise.

title: Bread Omelette (Broodomelette)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- 50 to 60 g sugar
- 80 g butter or margarine
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 2 deciliters milk
- 1/4 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon zest
- apricot jam or orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Soak the bread in boiling milk and rub it completely smooth.
2. Stir the flavoring, the egg yolks and the sugar through it and lastly as lightly as possible the stiffly beaten egg white.
3. Make an omelette of half of the mixture.

title: Bread Pudding (Broodpap)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liters milk
- 175 to 200 g old bread with or without crust
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil with the salt.
2. Cut the bread into small cubes and put it in the milk and let it cook for 5 to 10 minutes.
3. Serve brown sugar and if desired some fine cinnamon with the pudding.

title: Breakfast Cake (Ontbijtkoek)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 150 g bastard sugar
- 1½ to 2 dl milk
- ½ teaspoon crushed cloves
- ½ teaspoon fine cinnamon
- ½ teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon baking soda

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, add the spices, the baking soda and the milk and stir out the lumps from this stiff dough.
2. Check if the spice flavor is good and put the dough in a greased loaf pan.
3. Bake the cake in a moderately warm oven until brown and cooked through (approximately 1 hour).
4. Test if it is cooked by pricking it with a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
5. Let it cool in the pan and turn it out afterwards.

title: Broeder (Bread Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g flour
- approximately 4½ dl lukewarm milk
- 30 g yeast
- 200 g currants, raisins and mixed peel
- 2 eggs (optional)
- salt
- 20 g lard or beef fat

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with enough lukewarm milk so it does not fly about
2. Stir out the lumps from this batter and add the remaining milk, salt, well-washed currants and raisins with stems removed, the chopped mixed peel and the yeast mixed with lukewarm milk (optionally also the beaten eggs)
3. Let the batter rise, covered, in a warm place for three quarters of an hour to 1 hour
4. Let the lard or beef fat melt in an iron pot and grease the sides with it
5. Put the batter in and let the broeder bake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked through (approximately 1 hour)
6. Lay a lid on it if necessary
7. Check if it is cooked by pricking with a knitting needle, which should come out dry
8. Serve this dish with butter and sugar or syrup sauce
9. Alternatively, let the batter rise in the greased iron pan (3/4 hour) and then bake it

title: Broedertjes (Little Brothers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 and a half ounces flour
- 2 eggs
- Warm sweet milk
- Raisins
- Some almond paste
- Currants
- Candied peel
- Grated lemon zest
- Just over 1 ounce butter
- A piece of yeast
- Butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Make a batter, not too thin, with flour, eggs, warm sweet milk, raisins, almond paste, currants, candied peel, lemon zest, butter and yeast
2. Fry in a broedertje pan with butter

title: Brood met Appelen (Bread with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- a deep plate full of old white bread without crust, cut into cubes
- one and a half ounces butter
- one quarter can hot milk
- 4 eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- currants
- a deep plate full of peeled apples, cored
- lemon sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Take a deep plate full of old white bread without crust, cut into cubes
2. Fry them in one and a half ounces butter until golden
3. Pour one quarter can hot milk over them and let stand for half an hour
4. Add 4 eggs, sugar, cinnamon, currants and a deep plate full of peeled, cored apples
5. Bake all together in a butter-lined mold for 1.5 hours
6. Garnish with lemon sugar

title: Brood met Pruimen (Bread with Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums without pits, cut into cubes
- old white bread without crust, cut into cubes
- butter
- hot milk
- eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- currants

## Directions

1. Cut plums (without pits) into cubes
2. Prepare as for bread with apples, but using more bread

title: Brooddeeg (Bread Dough)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 7.5 g salt
- 10 g yeast
- 1 egg
- 50 g butter or margarine
- some sugar
- 2 dl lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the usual way and let it rise (see recipe 835).
2. Place the dough in tall cake molds that have been greased with butter or margarine, or make round pieces approximately 3 cm thick and place them on a greased baking sheet.
3. Let the batter rise for another 1/2 hour under a damp cloth.
4. Bake them in a hot oven for 20 to 30 minutes until light brown and cooked through.
5. Cut them warm in half, spread them, place the halves back together, and serve warm.

title: Broodkoek (Bread Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces white bread
- 4 cups sweet milk
- eggs
- 4 cups melted butter

## Directions

1. Cook 2 ounces white bread in 4 cups sweet milk into a pulp
2. When somewhat cooled, stir in beaten eggs and 4 cups melted butter
3. Bake in a tart pan with heat below and above

title: Broodomelette (Bread Omelette)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- 50 to 60 g sugar
- 80 g butter or margarine
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 2 dl milk
- 1/4 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon peel
- apricot jam or orange marmalade

## Directions

1. Soak the bread in the boiling milk and rub it completely fine.
2. Stir in the flavoring, the egg yolks and the sugar, and last of all fold in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible.
3. Make half of the butter or margarine light brown in the frying pan, add half of the batter and fry this light brown on both sides.
4. Place the cake on a warmed plate, spread it with some apricot jam or orange marmalade.
5. Fry the other half of the batter in the same way and place this on the first cake.
6. Sprinkle the omelette with powdered sugar and serve it warm.

title: Broodpodding (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- inside of stale white bread
- warm milk
- 6 fresh eggs
- 2 ounces currants
- 1.5 ounces raisins
- 1.5 ounces butter
- candied citrus peel
- cinnamon
- butter for greasing mold
- fine rusk for coating mold
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Crumb the inside of stale white bread
2. let it soak in warm milk
3. stir until smooth
4. beat in 6 fresh eggs
5. stir in 2 ounces currants, 1.5 ounces raisins, 1.5 ounces butter, candied citrus peel, snippets and cinnamon
6. pour the batter into a mold greased with butter and sprinkled with fine rusk
7. let the pudding cook vigorously for 1.5 hours
8. serve with wine sauce

title: Broodpuddinkjes (Bread Puddings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g old bread
- 100 g bastard sugar
- 75 g butter or margarine
- juice and zest of 1/2 lemon
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, add the sugar, lemon zest and juice and the grated or finely made old bread.
2. Fill sand-tart molds greased with butter and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs with this mixture.
3. Place the puddings on a baking sheet and bake them in a moderately warm oven until light brown.
4. Turn them out and serve with lemon sauce.

title: Broodsoep (Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- crumb of white bread
- water
- salt
- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take crumb of white bread and cook it very gently for an hour in a small iron pot with water, salt and a little butter, according to the quantity desired
2. When it is ready, stir two, three or more eggs through it, but take care not to let it cook with the eggs

title: Broodsoep (Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bouillon from beef bones
- Water
- Salt
- Bread
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Cook bouillon from beef bones with water and salt, taking care to skim well when it comes to a boil; let it simmer gently for four to five hours.
2. Pour the bouillon through a sieve, remove the fat and let it come to a boil again.
3. Cut some strips of bread into pieces, in proportion to the amount of bouillon, or preferably cut the bread into the soup tureen and pour the bouillon over it.
4. Add some Liebig's Meat Extract according to taste.
5. You can use stale bread very well for this.

title: Bruinbrood (Brown Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g unbolted flour
- 10 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 2.5 dl milk

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread in the same manner as described in the previous recipe.
2. Note: Replace the milk with water if desired.

title: Bruine Bonen (Brown Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g brown beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Rinse the beans and soak them for 24 hours in water with 20 g salt per liter. Pour away this water and cook the beans (approximately 1 hour) in fresh unsalted water until done.
2. Let the beans cook gently and ensure they always remain under the liquid.
3. Remove them with a skimming spoon from the pan and serve them with fried bacon and onions. Keep the cooking water for soup.

title: Bruine boter (Brown Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat butter in an earthenware pan until it turns brown
2. Warm some wine vinegar in the same pan
3. Mix them together

title: Bruine pikante saus (Brown Piquant Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl lean jus with water
- 50 g onion
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- small carrot
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- splash of Worcestershire sauce and soy sauce

## Directions

1. Mix the jus with enough water so that it is not too salty.
2. Add the spices and let them steep gently for 20 minutes.
3. Strain the liquid and prepare sauce in the usual manner (see cauliflower sauce).
4. Remove from heat and mix with Worcestershire sauce and soy sauce to taste.
5. Serve with beef tenderloin, chateaubriands, etc.

title: Bruine Ragout (Brown Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l dark bouillon or lean jus with water
- piece of Spanish pepper
- couple of bay leaves
- piece of onion
- couple of cloves
- piece of carrot
- 55 g flour
- 55 g butter or margarine
- 50 g ground veal
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms
- approximately 450 g pieces of ox tongue
- English sauce
- soya

## Directions

1. Brown the butter or margarine and flour, add the strained bouillon in which the spices have been infused and let the sauce simmer stirring until cooked (10 minutes).
2. Warm in it the cooked meat balls and pieces of ox tongue.
3. Remove the ragout from the heat and mix with the mushrooms, English sauce and soya and fill pastries, galettes etc. with it.

title: Bruine Ragout van Kalfsvleesch en Wild (Brown Ragout of Veal and Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato flour
- Fat or butter
- Laurel leaf
- Softly cooked onions
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Gherkins
- Meat pieces

## Directions

1. Fry potato flour brown in fat or butter
2. Let it cook for a quarter hour
3. Add a laurel leaf, softly cooked onions, vinegar, salt and sliced gherkins
4. Put the chopped meat pieces in
5. Keep it warm until serving

title: Bruine Ragout voor Runder- of Lamsvieesch (Brown Ragout for Beef or Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two spoons flour
- Fat or butter
- Chopped onions
- Water or bouillon
- Vinegar
- 1 spoon mustard
- Sugar
- Cloves
- Laurel leaf
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Brown two spoons flour half in fat or butter with some chopped onions until dark brown
2. Cook it with water or bouillon until a liquid sauce
3. Add vinegar, 1 spoon mustard, sugar, cloves, laurel leaf, pepper and salt

title: Bruine ragoutsaus (Brown Ragout Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl brown piquant sauce
- 50 g ground veal
- 1/2 can of mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in the previous recipe, but stir in the cooked ground meat balls and the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half.
2. Serve with beef tongue, beef tenderloin, etc.

title: Bruine Ragoût Soep (Brown Ragout Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bouillon
- mace, Spanish pepper, carrot, onion, cloves and bay leaves
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 50 g ground veal
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms
- pieces of cooked ox tongue (optional)
- English sauce and soya or Madeira to taste

## Directions

1. Make bouillon with the herbs in the usual manner
2. Strain it and add it while stirring to the butter or margarine and flour, which have been browned together
3. Let the soup simmer while stirring until cooked (10 minutes)
4. Add the cooked meat balls, the mushrooms and if desired pieces of ox tongue
5. Remove from heat and add English sauce and soya or some Madeira to the soup

title: Bruine Soep (Brown Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 Dutch pounds lean beef
- salt
- rice or barley
- veal meat for meatballs

## Directions

1. Cook 1.5 Dutch pounds of lean beef with some salt for a long time and strongly, skimming well
2. Add rice or peeled barley, and half an hour before serving, add veal meatballs

title: Bruine uiensaus (Brown Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 8 onions
- 1 ounce butter
- parsley, finely chopped
- bouillon
- good mustard

## Directions

1. Cut 6 or 8 onions into cubes and brown them with 1 ounce butter over a strong fire
2. Add finely chopped parsley and bouillon
3. Cook it slowly together
4. After removing from heat, stir in a spoonful of good mustard

title: Brussels lof (1e recept) (Belgian Endive - 1st Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Belgian endive
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour or 15 g cornstarch (Duryea maizena)
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the brown leaves from the plants or scrape the brown off.
2. Remove the spongy heart that sits in the stem and is very bitter, cut off a piece of the stem and cut the plants into strips.
3. Wash the endive until the water runs completely clear, let it cook with as little water as possible in a well-enameled pot (to prevent discoloration).

title: Brussels Lof (First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels lof
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour or 15 g Duryea corn starch
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the brown leaves from the sprouts or scrape off the brown.
2. Remove the spongy heart that is in the stalk and is very bitter, cut off a piece of the stalk and cut the sprouts into strips.
3. Wash the lof until the water runs completely clear, let it cook with as little water as possible in a well-enameled pan (to prevent discoloration) for 1/2 hour.
4. Let it drain in a colander and braise it in a sauce made of butter or margarine, flour and milk, prepared in the manner of cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328 or recipe 329), for another 10 minutes with salt.
5. Grate nutmeg over it on the plate.

title: Brussels Lof (Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels lof
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape the lof with a knife, remove the outer leaves if necessary and a small piece of the stalk, but make sure the sprout stays whole.
2. Wash it and let it cook in as little boiling water as possible with salt in a well-enameled pan (approximately 3/4 hour).
3. Let it drain in a colander and braise it for 10 minutes in a sauce made of butter or margarine, flour and milk, prepared in the manner of cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328).
4. Grate nutmeg over it on the plate.

title: Brussels Lof (with Rolled Butter)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels lof
- 80 g butter or margarine
- 2 eggs
- nutmeg
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the lof as indicated in recipe 444.
2. Remove it with a skimmer from the pan, let it drain as dry as possible and place it on a warmed flat plate.
3. Serve with the halved hard-boiled eggs, rolled butter (see recipe 365) and nutmeg.

title: Brussels Lof au Jus (In a Fireproof Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels lof
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the lof as described in the previous recipe.
2. Place it in a fireproof dish, add a little of the cooking liquid.
3. Sprinkle bread crumbs over it, distribute the butter or margarine in small pieces on top, clean the rim of the dish and let it brown lightly in a moderately warm oven.

title: Brussels Lof Schotel (Brussels Lof Casserole)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels lof
- 150 g ham or bacon
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- salt to taste
- 0.5 l milk
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 40 g flour
- 60 g aged Gouda cheese
- 20 g melted butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the lof (see recipe 444).
2. Cut the hard-boiled eggs into slices.
3. Make a cheese sauce in the usual manner (see recipe 336), but reserve 20 g of the grated cheese to finish the dish.
4. Wrap each drained lof sprout in a slice of ham or bacon and place them in a fireproof dish.
5. Lay the egg slices on top.
6. Sprinkle with a little table salt.
7. Pour the cheese sauce over it.
8. Sprinkle with the reserved grated cheese and pour the melted butter over it.
9. Let it brown lightly in the oven.

title: Brusselsche Kool (Brussels Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels cabbage
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- jus (optional)

## Directions

1. Treat as Brussels sprouts
2. Can also be stewed with jus

title: Butter Sauce (Botersaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two parts butter
- One part water

## Directions

1. Place two parts butter and one part water cold on the heat
2. Stir until it boils
3. This sauce is very thick

title: Béarnaisesaus (Bearnaise Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 140 g butter or margarine
- 10 white peppercorns
- 1/4 dl tarragon vinegar
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Crush the peppercorns, add them to the vinegar and let it boil until 1/2 dl remains, then pour through a horsehair sieve
2. Beat the egg yolks with salt until thick and foamy, add the vinegar and allow the sauce to thicken over a bain-marie, stirring constantly
3. Add butter or margarine in small pieces at a time to the sauce and keep beating
4. Continue until all the butter is used and the sauce is of good consistency
5. Serve with cooked calf's or beef tongue, turbot, sole fillets

title: Béchamelsaus (Béchamel Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l milk, cream or light bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- piece of mace
- pinch of thyme
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 1 egg yolk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Let the herbs steep in the liquid for 15 to 20 minutes
2. Strain and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
3. If desired, blend with the beaten egg yolk and serve with hard-boiled eggs, poached eggs or stuffed cucumber

title: Cake
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- 200 g flour
- 200 g sugar
- 4 eggs
- the scraped pulp of one vanilla pod, or the grated zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add alternately some sugar and one whole egg and stir until the mass has become thick, white and airy.
2. Add vanilla or lemon zest and sifted flour, but stir this in with a few strokes.
3. Grease a cake pan with butter or margarine, put the batter in it and bake the cake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done (3/4 to 1 hour).
4. Check if the cake is done by inserting a knitting needle, which should then come out dry.
5. Turn the cake onto a cake rack, let it cool and dust it with powdered sugar or garnish it with icing after cooling.

title: Caper Sauce (Kapper Saus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Six eggs
- One spoon flour
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Capers

## Directions

1. Let six eggs, one spoon flour, salt, and vinegar to taste cook, stirring constantly to prevent curdling
2. When it boils well, add a lump of butter and then the capers
3. Stir it thoroughly together

title: Capon au Grossel (Kapoen au Grossel)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- bouillon or water
- coarse salt
- bouillon or jus sauce

## Directions

1. Take a capon, clean it properly
2. Lard it
3. Cook it in bouillon or water in an iron pot
4. When it is done, serve it
5. Lay some pieces of coarse salt on it and pour a sauce of bouillon or jus over it

title: Capucijners (Bruine) (Brown Capuchins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg capuchins
- salt

## Directions

1. Shell the capuchins, wash them and let them cook in little boiling water with salt (approximately 1/2 hour).
2. Drain them quickly in a colander if needed and present them as warm as possible with gravy.

title: Capucijners (Witte) (White Capuchins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg capuchins
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Treat them as peas in recipe 472.

title: Capucijners Witte (White Capuchins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg white capuchins
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Treat them as peas in recipe 472

title: Caramelsaus (Caramel Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 dl milk
- 2.5 dl cooking cream
- 150 g sugar
- 2 eggs
- 3 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with the cream to a boil.
2. Put 125 g sugar in a fairly large pan and let it stand on a not too fierce fire until it has become caramel and begins to foam over the entire surface.
3. Then slowly pour, all in one stream, the boiling liquid into it, off the fire.
4. Add 25 g sugar.
5. Beat the eggs, add them slowly, stirring constantly and rubbing away lumps, to the maizena.
6. Add to this mixture carefully, stirring constantly, enough of the hot liquid until the eggs are thoroughly warmed.
7. Pour it back into the pan and let the sauce, stirring vigorously with a whisk, boil for 3-4 minutes on a gentle fire.
8. Present the sauce with warm puddings.

title: Caramelvla (Caramel Custard) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ l milk
- ½ vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- 175 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 25 to 30 g Duryea cornstarch

## Directions

1. Let the vanilla steep in the milk.
2. Put 150 g sugar in an iron pan with a tablespoon of water.
3. Let the sugar dissolve over low heat and then turn brown while stirring occasionally, but do not burn.
4. Add another spoonful of water and then the milk from which the vanilla has been removed.
5. Let the caramel dissolve completely in the milk.
6. Stir the egg yolks with the cornstarch and 25 grams sugar, if necessary also a few spoonfuls of milk into a smooth paste.
7. Pour the paste into the caramel milk and let the custard cook through while stirring with a whisk (3 to 4 minutes).
8. Let it cool while stirring occasionally, mix with the stiffly beaten egg white and serve the custard in a custard dish.

title: Caramelvla (Caramel Custard) - Second Recipe (for 6 people)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ dl cream
- 2½ dl milk
- 5 eggs
- ¼ vanilla stick
- 125 g sugar

## Directions

1. Slowly bring the milk with the cream and the vanilla to a boil.
2. Put 100 g sugar in a fairly large pan and make caramel from it.
3. Then slowly pour the boiling milk with cream into it, all at once but away from the heat.
4. Beat the egg yolks with 25 g sugar, carefully add, while stirring constantly, enough of the hot liquid so that the egg yolks are well warmed.
5. Pour them back into the pan and let the custard bind while stirring vigorously with a whisk over the heat, but ensure that it does not boil, as otherwise the risk of curdling becomes greater.
6. Let it cool and mix with the stiffly beaten egg white just before serving.
7. Serve with biscuits.

title: Champignon-saus (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- very young white mushrooms
- water
- lemon juice
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take very young white mushrooms and cut off the stems
2. Put water with lemon juice in an earthenware pan and add the mushrooms when it boils
3. Chop them very finely and press them lightly out in a linen cloth
4. Put a little butter with lemon juice in a pan
5. Add the mushrooms with twelve tablespoons of good bouillon
6. Add a little pepper

title: Champignons (Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled mushrooms
- warm water
- bouillon
- cooked butter
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- flour
- shallots
- milk
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pickled mushrooms must first soak in warm water for a couple of hours
2. Then stew in bouillon and cooked butter
3. Sprinkle nutmeg over the mushrooms
4. When nearly cooked, add finely chopped parsley
5. Alternatively, cut into pieces and stew with bouillon, butter, pepper, chopped parsley, a little flour and some shallots
6. Make a sauce of milk with beaten egg yolks, nutmeg, flour and butter, stirred on the fire until proper thickness
7. Pour the sauce over when serving
8. Add lemon juice

title: Champignons met Paprika (Mushrooms with Paprika)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 600 g mushrooms
- salt
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 onion
- 2 teaspoons paprika
- sour cream or yogurt
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms as described in recipe 477.
2. Cut them into quarters.
3. Braise a chopped onion in the butter or margarine.
4. Add paprika powder and the mushroom pieces.
5. Let them braise tender (approximately 20 minutes) with the salt, if needed with some water.
6. Add the sour cream and bind the sauce if necessary with some mixed cornstarch.

title: Champignonsaus (Donkere) (Dark Mushroom Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l brown piquant sauce
- 1 bunch of mushrooms

## Directions

1. Make brown piquant sauce as described in recipe 359
2. Blend the sauce off the heat with the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, and with some mushroom liquid
3. Note: Use a bit more butter or margarine and flour when preparing the sauce, as the mushroom liquid must also be bound

title: Champignonsaus (Lichte) (Light Mushroom Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter bouillon
- mace
- thyme
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 tin of mushrooms
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Let the spices steep in the bouillon, strain it and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Mix it away from the heat with the beaten egg yolk, the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, and some of the liquid from the mushrooms

title: Champignonsaus (Lichte) (Light Mushroom Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l bouillon
- mace
- thyme
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 bunch of mushrooms
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Let the herbs steep in the bouillon, then strain it and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Remove from heat and blend with the beaten egg yolk, the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, some of the mushroom liquid and lemon juice to taste
3. Serve with calf's tongue, ox tongue, cooked calf's meat, etc.

title: Champignonsoep (Mushroom Soup, 1st recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l light veal bouillon
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 bunch mushrooms
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 dl cream or milk
- nutmeg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring it to a boil while stirring, rub out the lumps, add the bouillon while stirring
2. Let the soup cook while stirring until done (+ 10 minutes), add the mushrooms with their liquid and some lemon juice and pour the soup while stirring vigorously into the egg yolk, which has been beaten in the soup tureen with the cream or milk and nutmeg
3. N.B. The mushrooms must not cook with the soup, as they would become tough

title: Champignonsoep (Mushroom Soup, 2nd recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l light bouillon or water with salt
- 200 g fresh mushrooms
- 40 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt
- lemon juice
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms as described in recipe 477
2. Braise them in 15 grams butter or margarine with salt and lemon juice (5 minutes)
3. Add the liquid and let the mushrooms cook another 15 minutes
4. Melt 35 grams butter or margarine, add the flour and bring it to a boil while stirring
5. Rub out the lumps and add the liquid
6. Let the soup cook while stirring occasionally for 10 minutes
7. Warm the mushrooms in it
8. Pour the soup while stirring vigorously into the egg yolk, which has been beaten in the soup tureen

title: Charlotte aux Pommes (Apple Charlotte)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 200 g old bread without crust
- 100 g butter or margarine
- approximately 800 g sour apples
- approximately 50 g sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons apricot jam
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Cut the bread, of which the crusts have been removed, into equal thin slices, coat them with some butter or margarine, dip the sides in the egg white and line a mold coated with butter or margarine along the bottom and side walls with them, but make sure they fit snugly together.
2. Cook applesauce in the usual way, but make sure it is as dry as possible.
3. Sieve it and mix it with the jam, sugar and egg yolks.
4. Fill the mold with this mixture, close the top with a matching piece of bread, coated with butter or margarine on both sides.
5. Put the pudding in the oven and let it turn occasionally until brown on all sides (1 to 1 1/2 hours).

title: Charlotte Russe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g biscuits
- 2 1/2 dl whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 50 g brown sugar
- 10 g white gelatine or 8 g powdered gelatine
- some coffee or vanilla extract or some maraschino
- 35 g candied fruits

## Directions

1. Cut the edges of the biscuits straight and set them, with the brown side outward, against the wall of a smooth pudding mold, but make sure they stand as firmly as possible against each other.
2. Clean the bottom of the pudding mold with a damp brush and brush it with some unbeaten egg white.
3. Beat the cream stiff with the brown sugar and mix it with the stiffly beaten egg whites and the flavoring.
4. Soak the sheet gelatine 10 to 15 minutes in cold water, then dissolve it with as little water as possible, let the gelatine cool briefly and stir it through the cream.
5. Let this mixture become jelly-like, stirring from time to time, and pour it inside the biscuits.
6. Cut the biscuits level with the surface of the cream and let the pudding become cold and firm.
7. Turn it out and garnish the top with candied fruits or with fruit jelly and fruits.
8. Tie a narrow ribbon around it.

title: Chartreuse (Chartreuse Liqueur)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l brandy
- 400 g white sugar
- 1/2 g saffron
- peel of 2 lemons (very thinly peeled)
- 12 to 14 cardamom seeds

## Directions

1. Peel the cardamom seeds, take out the seeds from them, put them with all other required ingredients in a well-closing bottle.
2. Close it and let the liqueur stand for at least 4 weeks, stirring occasionally.

title: Chateaubriands (Thick Beef Steaks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beef steaks from the loin at 100 g each (thick, round cut)
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash the chateaubriands, dry them with a clean cloth or scrape them, rub them with pepper and salt and immediately bake them in the browned butter in the manner indicated for beef steak (cooking time 4-5 minutes per piece)
2. Finish the jus with milk

title: Chateaubriands (Thick Beef Steaks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 filet steaks of approximately 100 g (thick, round shape)
- Approximately 100 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- Pepper
- Milk

## Directions

1. Wash the chateaubriands, dry them with a clean cloth or scrape them, rub them with pepper and salt and fry them immediately in butter or margarine.

title: Chaufroid van kip (Chicken Chaud-Froid)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large chickens
- 100 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 100 g rice
- 3/4 l aspic
- truffles
- lettuce leaves
- For chaud-froid sauce: 4 dl bouillon, 50 g flour, 65 g butter or margarine, 3 leaves white gelatine or 1/2 spoon powdered gelatine, some cream

## Directions

1. Roast the chickens until done and cut nice pieces from breast and legs.
2. Let these cool.
3. Draw bouillon from the bones for the sauce and for the aspic.
4. Make a thick sauce from the butter or margarine and flour with the bouillon and dissolve in it the gelatine that has been soaked in cold water and well wrung out.
5. Finish the sauce with cream and let it cool, stirring from time to time.
6. Draw the chicken pieces through it.
7. Garnish the chicken pieces with small figures of truffle, parsley, egg white and tomato.
8. Glaze the entire surface with aspic, which begins to cool and thus becomes stiff as soon as it is poured over the chicken pieces.
9. Make a base of cooked rice on the plate.
10. Put some aspic on this.
11. Place the chicken pieces on it, which are entirely covered with chaud-froid sauce.
12. Chop the remaining aspic finely and garnish the plate with it.
13. Place a piece of truffle here and there and garnish the plate with fresh lettuce leaves.

title: Cheese Pâte à Choux (Soesjes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dl water with salt
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 60 g flour
- 2 eggs
- 50 g butter or margarine (for filling)
- 150 g grated cheese
- 1 egg yolk
- pepper
