- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the water with butter or margarine to a boil.
2. Stirring constantly, sprinkle in the sifted flour and continue stirring until the dough forms a ball that releases from the pan.
3. Remove from heat and add the eggs one by one.
4. Using a pastry bag, a butter syringe, or a teaspoon, place small mounds of this dough on a buttered baking sheet.
5. Bake the puffs in 10 to 15 minutes until light brown and cooked through in a hot oven.
6. For the filling: beat the butter or margarine until creamy, mix it with the egg yolk and stir in the grated cheese with some pepper and salt.
7. Cut or open the puffs and fill them with the cheese mixture.

title: Cherry Sauce (Kersesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherry flesh
- Red wine
- Sugar
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the flesh of cherries with red wine and sugar until soft
2. Press it through a sieve
3. Let it boil once more
4. Bind it with egg yolks

title: Chervil Sauce (Kervelsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chervil
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Chop chervil very finely and fry it with butter and flour until very soft
2. Pour in bouillon or water
3. Let it cook while stirring constantly until very thick

title: Chicken with Truffles (Kip met Truffels)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- bread crumbs
- butter
- parsley
- finely chopped truffles
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Roast the chicken on the spit
2. Stuff it with bread crumbs, butter, parsley and finely chopped truffles
3. Cover the chicken on the spit with a paper smeared with butter

title: Chicken à la Tartare (Hoenders à la Tartare)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- melted butter
- small onions
- parsley
- mushrooms
- pepper
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- bound sauce

## Directions

1. Cut young chickens in half
2. Singe them over the fire with some melted butter, two small onions, parsley and mushrooms, all finely chopped, and pepper and salt
3. Subsequently cover it with breadcrumbs and roast it over a bright fire
4. Serve with a bound sauce (see under sauces)

title: Chipolata (8 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ dl cream
- 2½ dl milk
- 6 eggs
- 80 g currants
- 80 g raisins
- 50 g almonds
- 30 g candied peel strips
- 30 g sugar
- 20 g white gelatin or 17½ g powdered gelatin
- 100 g biscuits
- 1 dl maraschino or persico

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the currants and raisins, rinse them with lukewarm water, cover with boiling water and cook for 10 minutes. Let them drain well in a sieve.
2. Chop or grind the peeled almonds finely, chop the candied peel strips.
3. Soak the biscuits in the liqueur.
4. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar, carefully add the cream mixed with the milk that has been brought to a boil, and let the mixture bind in a bain-marie while stirring well, but ensure it does not boil.
5. Soak the gelatin for 10-15 minutes in cold water, dissolve it in the liquid taken from the heat, let the mass cool and add the currants, raisins, almonds and candied peel strips.
6. Stir occasionally in the mixture until it begins to become jelly-like. Then mix it as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg whites and layer it alternately with the biscuits in a pudding mold smeared with unbeaten egg white, but ensure that the first and last layer do not consist of biscuits.
7. Turn out the pudding after cooling onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.

title: Chips (Potato Chips)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg potatoes
- fine salt
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes as thinly as possible, carefully remove the eyes and cut them into very thin slices.
2. Dry them firmly in a cloth.
3. Fry them in frying fat until cooked and golden yellow in small quantities at a time, because they stick together quickly and the fat cools down.
4. Let them drain briefly on brown paper and sprinkle them with fine salt.

title: Chocolade-ijs (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter vanilla ice cream
- 100 g Blooker's cocoa

## Directions

1. Prepare the ice cream as vanilla ice cream, but add the cocoa to the milk and let this mixture come to a boil.

title: Chocolade-room (Chocolate Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 ounces chocolate
- 5 ounces sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the cream, sugar and eggs together
2. place on fire, stirring constantly, and let it reduce to a quarter
3. add the finely powdered chocolate
4. strain through a sieve
5. serve when cold

title: Chocolademelk (Chocolate Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 l milk
- 100 g sugar
- 50 g Blooker's cacao

## Directions

1. Stir the cacao with the sugar together and gradually mix with a little cold milk to form a thin paste without lumps.
2. Bring the remaining milk to a boil, pour the mixed cacao into it while stirring and let the milk simmer for 5 minutes while stirring.
3. Pour it into the chocolate kettle.

title: Chocolademelk met Koffie en Slagroom (Chocolate Milk with Coffee and Whipped Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 45 g Blooker's cacao
- 75 g sugar
- 1 1/2 dl coffee extract
- 1/3 l whipped cream
- 1 tablespoon Nescafé

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the cacao and Nescafé together and mix with some cold milk.
2. Pour this while stirring into the boiling milk and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
3. Pour the cacao into chocolate cups and top with whipped cream.
4. Serve with dry biscuits.

title: Chocoladepudding met agar-agar (Chocolate Pudding with Agar-Agar)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- 70 g Blooker's cocoa
- 150 g sugar
- 1 stick agar-agar (of 14 g) or 2 small ones of 7 g each

## Directions

1. Rinse the agar-agar.
2. Pour enough boiling water over it so that it just covers and let it cook until it is completely dissolved (10 to 15 minutes).
3. In the meantime, prepare chocolate milk in the usual way from the quantities indicated above.
4. Pour the dissolved agar-agar through a sieve into it and place the mixture in a stone mold rinsed with cold water.
5. Let the pudding become cold and firm, turn it out onto a wet plate and serve with vanilla sauce.

title: Chocoladepudding met gelatine (Chocolate Pudding with Gelatin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 50 g Blooker's cocoa
- 120 g sugar
- 25 g white gelatin or powdered gelatin
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the cocoa and sugar with some cold milk thinly.
2. Bring the remaining milk with the vanilla slowly to a boil.
3. Pour in the mixed cocoa while stirring and let the milk cook through while stirring for 5 minutes.
4. Remove the vanilla.
5. Soak the gelatin for 15 minutes in plenty of cold water. (Powdered gelatin need not be soaked but can be dissolved immediately.)
6. Squeeze it out well and add it off the heat to the cooked mixture.
7. Stir from time to time, until the pudding begins to become jelly-like and then pour it into a mold rinsed with cold water.
8. Let the pudding become cold and firm.
9. Turn it out onto a wet plate and serve the pudding with vanilla sauce.

title: Chocoladepudding met maizena (Chocolate Pudding with Cornstarch)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 75 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 50 g Blooker's cocoa
- 100 g sugar
- half a vanilla stick or vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together the cornstarch, cocoa and sugar and mix them with a portion of the cold milk until a thin batter without lumps.
2. Bring the remaining milk with the vanilla slowly to a boil, pour in the batter while stirring and let the mixture, stirring vigorously, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Remove the vanilla, pour the pudding into a mold rinsed with cold water and let it become cold and firm.
4. Turn it out onto a wet plate and serve with vanilla sauce.

title: Chocoladesaus (Chocolate Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces chocolate powder
- 1 pint boiling milk
- 6 lod sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 2.5 ounces chocolate powder into 1 pint boiling milk until completely dissolved
2. Add 6 lod sugar
3. Let it boil a bit more
4. It should be bound

title: Chocoladesaus (Chocolate Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 15 g Duryea cornstarch
- 20 g Blooker's cocoa
- 60 g sugar
- 1/4 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Bring 3 1/2 dl milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla
2. Mix the cornstarch with the cocoa and the sugar together and blend them slowly with 1 1/2 dl milk into a thin paste without lumps
3. Pour this into the boiling milk while stirring, from which the vanilla has been removed
4. Let the sauce simmer briskly while stirring for 3-4 minutes
5. Stir occasionally while cooling to prevent a skin from forming
6. Present the sauce with semolina or cornstarch pudding etc.

title: Chocoladeschuimpjes (Chocolate Foam)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces chocolate
- Cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 ounces sugar

## Directions

1. Treat it the same as coffee foam, using 2.5 ounces chocolate

title: Chocoladetaart (Chocolate Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces butter
- ½ pound finely ground almonds
- 2 ounces sugar
- juice and zest of 1 lemon
- 10 egg yolks
- ½ pound powdered chocolate
- 8 egg whites
- 2 ounces melted loaf sugar
- 1 thimbleful egg white
- a few drops lemon juice
- powdered chocolate for glaze

## Directions

1. Mix together the butter, ground almonds, sugar, lemon juice and zest, and egg yolks.
2. Beat the 8 egg whites until stiff and fold in.
3. Pour the mixture into a prepared mold (see cherry cake).
4. Once cooled, glaze as follows: stir 2 ounces melted loaf sugar with 1 thimbleful egg white, a few drops of lemon juice, and powdered chocolate for a quarter hour until thick, working quickly until it becomes light and light brown.

title: Chocoladevla (Chocolate Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l milk
- 35 g Blooker's cocoa
- 40 g Duryea maizena
- 60 to 80 g sugar
- 1/4 stick vanilla (optional)
- 1 egg white or small meringues (optional)

## Directions

1. Bring 6 1/2 dl milk (with the vanilla) slowly to the boil. Stir the cocoa with the maizena and sugar together, mix them with the remaining milk to a thin paste without lumps. Pour this, stirring well, into the nearly boiling milk and let the custard, stirring, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes. Let the custard cool, stirring occasionally, remove the vanilla and pour the custard into a custard dish. If desired, garnish the custard with the stiffly beaten egg white, through which some sugar has been stirred, or with small meringues.

title: Chocoladevlade (Chocolate Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 chocolate cakes
- 3 cups water
- some cinnamon
- bread sugar
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook 3 chocolate cakes with 3 cups water, some cinnamon and bread sugar
2. Beat in enough eggs so that it is well bound
3. Pour quickly over

title: Chocolate Tart (Chocoladetaart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces butter
- Half pound finely pounded almonds
- Two ounces sugar
- Juice and peel of one lemon
- Ten egg yolks
- Half pound powdered chocolate
- White of eight eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together well in order: two ounces butter, half pound finely pounded almonds, two ounces sugar, juice and peel of one lemon, ten egg yolks, and half pound powdered chocolate
2. Beat the white of eight eggs
3. Add it to the mixture

title: Cichoreisalade (Chicory Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicory roots
- rainwater
- oil
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the chicory roots into pieces.
2. Cook them in rainwater.
3. Eat it with oil, vinegar, and a little salt.

title: Citroenbonen (Lemon Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g lemon beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), reckon as cooking time 1¼ to 1¾ hour and serve them with sour sauce.

title: Citroenen in het Zuur (Lemons in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 lemons
- fine salt
- 16 bay leaves
- half a pound of ginger
- 2 bottles of best wine vinegar
- 4 Spanish peppers
- 1 pound of white mustard seed

## Directions

1. Cut 10 lemons crosswise, add fine salt on both sides and lay them on a plate for 14 days, turning daily
2. Place the lemons raw in a pot with 16 bay leaves and half a pound of ginger between them
3. Boil 2 bottles of best wine vinegar with 4 Spanish peppers and 1 pound of white mustard seed, and pour this boiling over the lemons

title: Citroenkaas (Lemon Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g brown sugar
- 125 g butter
- juice and peel of 3 lemons
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the lemons, grate the peel, squeeze them out.
2. Beat in a well-tinned bowl or with 2 silver forks in a clean, enameled pan the eggs with the brown sugar and the grated lemon peel until a light mass.
3. Add the butter and the strained lemon juice and let the mass, beating well, become thick in a bain-marie and pour it directly into the cleaned jars, which have been treated as indicated in the 'rules' on page 369.

title: Citroenlimonade (Lemon Lemonade)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg sugar
- 2 l water
- 50 g citric acid
- 30 g lemon essence

## Directions

1. Bring the water to a boil and dissolve the sugar and the citric acid (off the heat) in it.
2. After cooling, stir in the essence and pour the lemonade into well-cleaned bottles.
3. Close them with a cleaned and boiled cork.
4. Dilute the lemonade with 3 parts water when using.

title: Citroenmarmelade (Lemon Marmalade)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 lemons
- water
- sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- labels

## Directions

1. Brush the lemons clean, cut them whole into thin slices, removing the pits.
2. Soak them in enough water to cover them completely for 24 hours.
3. Cook them in this liquid until the pieces have softened, weigh them with the liquid, add the same weight of sugar and cook them, stirring occasionally, until they reach the required thickness.
4. Treat them further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Citroenmelk (Lemon Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 l milk
- peel of 1 1/2 lemon
- 80 g sugar
- 20 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Wash the lemon well and peel it thinly so that no white remains on the peel.
2. Put this in the milk and prepare it as indicated for vanilla milk (recipe 900).

title: Citroenpudding (Lemon Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 150 g brown sugar
- juice of 2 lemons with some water or Rhine wine (together 2 dl liquid)
- 10 g white gelatin

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar in a well-enameled pan until thick, white and fluffy, add the lemon juice and let the mixture be bound in a bain-marie, stirring vigorously.
2. Remove from heat and blend with the gelatin (which has been soaked 10 to 15 minutes in cold water) and then dissolved with as little boiling water as possible.
3. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible and add the pudding mixture.
4. Let the pudding become gelatinous, stirring occasionally, and pour it into a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white.
5. Let it become cold and stiff, turn it out onto a damp dish and serve it without sauce, but with vanilla wafers or biscuits de Savoye.

title: Citroenrijst (Lemon Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g rice
- 6 deciliters water
- zest of 1 lemon
- juice of 2 lemons
- 200 g sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice until completely clear water comes off.
2. Let it drain well.
3. Place the rice on with the cold water and the thinly pared or grated lemon zest and let it cook slowly on a gentle fire until done, but not completely dry.
4. Stir in the lemon juice and sugar off the heat, remove the lemon zest, place the mixture in a stone pudding mold rinsed with cold water and let it become cold and firm.
5. Serve the pudding, after turning it out onto a wet plate, with vanilla sauce or place a somewhat thinner mixture in a shallow dish and pour the sauce over it.

title: Citroensaus (Lemon Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl water
- juice of 2 lemons, together approximately 3/2 liter
- peel of 1 lemon
- approximately 75 g sugar
- approximately 20 g sago or potato starch

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of one well-washed lemon on an enamelled or glass grater
2. Bring the water with the sugar and the lemon peel to a boil
3. Pour in the sago mixed with cold water and let the sauce simmer while stirring briefly
4. Add the sieved lemon juice
5. Present it with semolina or cornstarch pudding etc.

title: Citroensaus (Lemon Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- piece of mace
- bay leaf
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 35 to 45 g flour
- 35 to 45 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- juice of approximately 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Let the spices steep in the water or bouillon
2. Strain this, prepare sauce from it in the usual way
3. Carefully add the sauce to the beaten egg, mix it with the lemon juice and serve with cooked chicken, calf poulette, calf's or beef tongue, artichokes, etc.

title: Citroentaart (Lemon Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ ounces rice
- juice of 6 lemons
- zest of 1 lemon, cooked
- candied citron peel
- sugar
- tart crust

## Directions

1. Cook 2½ ounces of rice until completely done, without stirring.
2. Add the juice of 6 lemons, the zest of 1 lemon that has been cooked, candied citron peel, and sugar.
3. Put the mixture into a tart crust and bake for half an hour.
4. Allow to cool in the pan afterwards.

title: Citroenvla (1e recept) (Lemon Custard, 1st Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 80 g brown sugar
- juice of approximately 2 lemons, made up to 1/4 l with water

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks completely smooth in a well-enameled pan with the sugar. Set the pan in a bain-marie and let the mixture thicken under constant stirring. Remove this mixture from the heat at the right moment and above all do not let it come to the boil. Add the stiffly beaten egg white in small quantities at once, and let this, beating well, thicken each time. Remove the custard from the heat, let it cool briefly and mix the lemon juice through it with a wooden spoon. Serve immediately after cooling with biscuits.

title: Citroenvla (2e recept) (Lemon Custard, 2nd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 100 g brown sugar
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 dl white wine

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar in a well-enameled pan until they become thick, white and fluffy. Then gradually add the wine and lemon juice and let the mass thicken in a bain-marie with vigorous stirring, but do not let it boil. Mix this off the heat with the stiffly beaten egg white and serve with biscuits.

title: Citroenvlade (Lemon Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle white currant juice or Rhine wine
- juice of 2 lemons
- grated lemon zest
- a piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook the white currant juice or Rhine wine with the juice of 2 lemons and grated lemon zest, along with a piece of cinnamon
2. Let it cook a little longer while stirring well
3. Pour it quickly over

title: Citroenvlade (Lemon Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 bottle white wine or Rhine wine
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 grated lemon peel
- cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 bottle white wine or Rhine wine with the juice of 2 lemons and 1 grated lemon peel, with a piece of cinnamon
2. Let it cook a bit more while stirring well
3. Pour quickly over

title: Citronengelei (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lemons
- bread sugar
- a glass Rhine wine or white currant juice
- 12 loot melted fish glue

## Directions

1. Grate 3 lemons and squeeze the juice over them
2. Add bread sugar and a glass Rhine wine or white currant juice
3. Stir this into 12 loot melted fish glue
4. Let it come to a boil briefly
5. Pour into molds or small glasses
6. When cold, turn out onto the serving dish

title: Cobbler's Tart (Schoenlapperstaart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large sour apples
- 10 large rusks
- 1½ teaspoons fine cinnamon
- 80 g butter or margarine
- 75 g sugar
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook thick applesauce in the usual way (see recipe 531), sift it, mix it with grated rusks, the butter or margarine, the sugar, the egg yolks and the fine cinnamon.
2. Finally stir in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible, put the mixture in a greased and finely breadcrumbed tart pan and bake the tart in a moderately warm oven until light brown (½ hour to 45 minutes).
3. Let it steam out on a cake rack and serve it warm or cold.

title: Cocoskoek (Coconut Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g ground coconut
- 200 g white sugar
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 25 g flour
- 1 large egg
- vanilla sugar
- milk

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients together with enough milk to form a moist but cohesive dough.
2. Line a baking sheet with greased buttered parchment paper.
3. Spread the dough on it and bake it until very light brown (about 25 minutes).
4. After some cooling, turn the cake and remove the paper.
5. Cut the cake into pieces.

title: Columbuseieren (Columbus Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- mayonnaise made from 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 tablespoon capers
- fresh parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard, peel them and cut off a cap so they can stand
2. Prepare the mayonnaise in the usual way (recipe 367)
3. Stir the capers through
4. Stand the eggs upright on a small dish, pour the mayonnaise around them and garnish the dish with a tuft of parsley

title: Compot van Abrikozen (Apricot Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots, cut them in half and remove the pit
2. Set them in water on the fire, using half a pound of sugar for every pound of apricots
3. Cook them carefully and slowly until done, so they do not turn to mush
4. When cooked, remove the pan from the fire
5. As soon as they are cold, serve them with the liquid, which should not be too thin

title: Compot van Appelen (Apple Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- grated stale white bread
- glass of wine
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and bore out the cores
2. Cook the peels and cores in water for half an hour
3. Strain the liquid and set it with a quarter can of Rhine wine, a quarter kilo sugar, lemon peels and cinnamon
4. As soon as it boils, add the apples and let them cook until soft, being careful not to overcook them so they remain whole
5. Remove the cooked apples with a spoon
6. Strain the syrup and bring it to boil again with half a lemon until it becomes syrup-like
7. Pour this over the prepared apples

title: Compot van Appelen (Compote of Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- quarter can Rhine wine
- quarter kilo sugar
- lemon skins
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and core them
2. Cook the skins and cores for half an hour in water
3. Strain and set the obtained juice with a quarter can Rhine wine, a quarter kilo sugar, lemon skins and cinnamon
4. As soon as this boils, add the apples and let them cook until soft
5. This should not take long, as they must remain whole
6. Remove the cooked ones with a spoon

title: Compot van Peren (Pear Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 winter pears
- water
- 6 cloves
- cinnamon
- sugar
- lemon peels

## Directions

1. Peel 20 winter pears, leaving them whole with the stem
2. Set them in enough water to cover them all
3. Add 6 cloves, cinnamon, plenty of sugar and lemon peels
4. Cover the pan and let them cook for two to four hours
5. If they have reduced too much and are not yet soft, add some water
6. Place them on small dishes with the stems pointing downward and pour the syrup over them

title: Compot van Peren in Roode Wijn (Pear Compote in Red Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- red wine
- pears
- sugar
- lemon peels
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Heat red wine
2. Lay in whole, halved or quartered pears
3. Add sugar, lemon peels and cinnamon
4. Cook until thick and syrup-like
5. When serving, sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Compot van Pruimen (Plum Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh plums
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook fresh plums in water with sugar and cinnamon for half an hour
2. Strain off the juice
3. Cook the juice until it thickens
4. Pour it over the plums

title: Compot van Rozebottels (Rose Hip Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried rose hips
- cold water
- raisins
- sugar
- lemon peels
- cinnamon
- Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Soak dried rose hips in cold water beforehand
2. Set them on with an equal amount of raisins, sugar, lemon peels and cinnamon, in half water and half Rhine wine
3. Cook them until soft, but only reduce them so the juice still covers them, though it should not be too thin

title: Compôte van Pisang, Sinaasappelen en Mandarijnen (Compote of Banana, Orange and Mandarin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pisangs
- 2 oranges
- 3 mandarins
- sugar
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges and mandarins, carefully remove all white fibers, cut them crosswise into slices and remove the pits.
2. Remove the peel from the pisangs, cut them likewise into slices.
3. Lay the fruits layer by layer with sugar in a compote dish, pour the lemon juice over it, let them stand covered until all sugar is dissolved, but moisten the top layer from time to time with the liquid.

title: Confituurtaart (Jam Tart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g hard butter or margarine
- 200 g flour
- some water
- salt
- jam or preserves for filling

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846), roll it out one last time to a thickness of 3/4 cm and place it on a baking sheet. Roll out the remaining dough again, cut out half-moons and place them around the edge of the circle. Seal them with water to the base layer. Cut long, thin, narrow strips from the remaining dough and divide the tart into 6 or 8 sections with these. Seal the strips with water, brush everything with egg yolk beaten with water. Make small cuts in the bottom here and there to prevent it from rising too much and bake the tart in a hot oven until light brown and cooked (15-20 minutes). Fill the different sections after cooling with preserves or jam or place different types of fruits in them.

title: Consommé (Consommé)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g veal soup meat
- 250 g beef soup meat
- 1/2 veal foot
- salt
- or one chicken
- salt
- or 400 g veal soup meat with bone
- 400 g beef soup meat
- salt
- 2 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and cut it into small pieces, or wash the chicken and cut it as finely as possible.
2. Place this with 2 tablespoons water and salt in a consommé pot or in a canning jar; ensure it is filled 3/4 full, close it, place it in a pan with water that reaches just below the rim of the pot and let it cook 6-8 hours in a bain marie.
3. Taste the resulting broth, add salt if necessary and strain it through a piece of cheesecloth into a bowl.
4. Keep the consommé as cool as possible, skim off the fat before use and serve it cold or melted through bouillon.
5. Melt the remaining fat to prevent spoilage and never keep the rest longer than 1 or 2 days.

title: Cromesquis de Kaas (Cheese Croquettes) (8 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- approximately 2 dl milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 150 g Parmesan cheese
- 100 g Gruyère cheese
- breadcrumbs
- parsley
- for the batter: 40 g flour
- 1 spoon salad oil
- 1 dl warm water
- 1 egg
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Prepare in the usual way a thick sauce of butter or margarine, flour and milk.
2. Add the egg yolks and the grated Parmesan cheese, cut the Gruyère cheese into small pieces and stir these in at the end with pepper and salt to taste.
3. Form small round balls or elongated rolls from this cheese mixture, roll them briefly through some breadcrumbs.
4. Mix for the batter the flour with the oil, add the warm water, the egg yolk and finally the stiffly beaten egg white.
5. Dip the cheese balls briefly through this batter and fry them immediately in frying fat or salad oil, from which blue steam rises, brown.
6. Let them drip on gray paper and place them on a dish on which a napkin is folded.
7. Garnish them with fresh sprigs of parsley.

title: Croustades van Zwezerik met Tomatensaus (Sweetbread Croustades with Tomato Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For the croustades: approximately 200 g rice
- 1 egg
- 50 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt
- egg white for coating
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat
- For the filling: 2 sweetbreads
- 20 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl bouillon
- 1 dl cream
- 1 to 2 egg yolks
- pepper and salt
- For the sauce: 3 to 4 large tomatoes
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 onion
- 1/2 large carrot
- 4 cloves
- 15 g flour
- approximately 25 g butter or margarine
- 2 dl bouillon
- some lean jus
- 1/2 dl cream

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in the usual way until done and while warm mix it with egg, butter or margarine, pepper and salt. Let this mixture spread on a plate cool down.
2. Form large pyramid-shaped croquettes from it, the underside of which is so broad that they can easily stand. Coat them twice and fry them in frying fat until brown. Let them drain well on grey paper, cut off the top cap, hollow them out and fill them with the sweetbread ragout.
3. Cook the sweetbreads in the usual way (see recipe 122), cut them into small pieces. Make a small sauce from butter or margarine, flour and bouillon, let it cook until done and add the cream and 1 to 2 egg yolks. Stir the sweetbread pieces through last. Place the caps on the sweetbread croquettes and serve them warm with the sauce, which is prepared as follows: crush the tomatoes, chop the onion and carrot and let this together with cloves and bay leaves cook gently in butter or margarine (approximately 15 minutes), but make sure it does not brown.
4. Add the flour and the bouillon, jus and cream and if the colour is not nice, some tomato purée. Let the sauce cook until done, press it through a sieve.

title: Croustadines (Fried Pasta Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 dl milk
- salt
- 100 g fine vermicelli
- 3 egg yolks
- ragout of 125 g veal for the filling

## Directions

1. Cook the washed vermicelli in boiling milk with salt (1/4 hour)
2. Stir the beaten yolks through the paste and fill buttered timbale molds or cups sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs with it
3. Bake them in a moderately warm oven light brown (1/2 hour to 45 minutes), turn them out and cut off the top cap with a sharp knife
4. Carefully hollow them out, fill them with ragout, replace the cap and let them heat through well in the oven

title: Croûtons met Kaas (Croutons with Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 40 to 50 g grated sharp cheese
- Cayenne pepper
- 4 slices of casino bread

## Directions

1. Remove the crusts from the slices of bread and cut them in two.
2. Toast them evenly brown and let them cool.
3. Cream the butter or margarine, add the egg yolk, the grated cheese and pepper to taste and spread this mixture on the bread, so that it lies higher in the middle than at the sides (so-called bombéed).

title: Crème au Beurre (Butter Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 50 to 100 g powdered sugar
- strong coffee extract, cocoa powder, fruit essences, rum or maraschino, plant coloring

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy and add the sifted powdered sugar.
2. Then add a little strong coffee extract, cocoa powder, some fruit essence, rum or maraschino and if necessary a drop of plant coloring.
3. Let the crème au beurre cool well before using it for piping.
4. In very warm weather, a sheet of dissolved gelatin, which has been soaked in cold water first, can be added to the mass.
5. Note: A less fatty and cheaper crème au beurre can be made by adding the same amount of pastry cream (see recipe 816) to the crème au beurre in small quantities at a time.

title: Crème Javanaise (Javanese Cream) (6-8 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 1 vanilla pod
- 4 egg yolks
- 15 g gelatin or 12½ g powdered gelatin
- 150 g sugar
- ¼ l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 150-200 g almond cookies
- For the sauce: 1 small jar apricot jam, some maraschino or kirsch

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with the sugar and the vanilla to a boil and pour enough of it to the beaten yolks so that they become thoroughly hot.
2. Return everything to the pan and let the custard bind on low heat, beating with a whisk, but do not boil.
3. Dissolve the soaked gelatin in it.
4. Let the pudding mixture cool and combine it with the stiffly beaten cream and stiffly beaten egg whites and let this custard become fairly stiff.
5. Toast the almond cookies on a baking sheet in the oven until they become brown.
6. Let them cool and pound them finely in a mortar.
7. Take a timbale mold, smear it with unbeaten egg white.
8. Put a layer of the custard in it and sprinkle some pounded almond cookies on top.
9. Continue this way until the mold is filled, ensure that the last layer consists of custard.
10. Let the pudding set firmly, turn it out and sprinkle the remaining almond cookie crumbs over it until it looks breaded.
11. Serve with an apricot sauce, which is prepared as follows: warm the jam with some water.

title: Custardsaus (Custard Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 15 g custard powder
- 40 g sugar

## Directions

1. Bring almost all the milk to a boil
2. Mix the custard with the sugar together and blend them with a little of the cold milk into a thin paste without lumps
3. Pour this into the boiling liquid while stirring
4. Let the sauce simmer while stirring for two minutes on a gentle heat
5. Let it cool, stirring occasionally, to prevent a skin from forming

title: Custardsaus (Custard Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l milk
- 15 g custard powder
- 40 g sugar

## Directions

1. Bring almost all of the milk to a boil.
2. Stir the custard with the sugar together and mix them with a little of the cold milk until a thin paste without lumps.
3. Pour this, while stirring, into the boiling liquid.
4. Let the sauce, stirring, boil for two minutes on a gentle fire.
5. Let it, stirring occasionally, cool down to prevent a skin from forming.

title: Custardvla (Custard Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l milk
- 50 g sugar
- 30 g custard

## Directions

1. Mix the custard with the sugar and combine this with a portion of the cold milk into a smooth paste without lumps
2. Bring the rest of the milk to a boil and pour the smooth paste, stirring, into the almost boiling liquid
3. Let the custard, stirring, simmer for two minutes on gentle heat
4. Let it cool, stirring occasionally, to prevent a skin from forming

title: Deense Rijst (Danish Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter milk
- 175 g rice
- 75 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 150 g bitter biscuits
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook rice porridge in the usual manner (see rice porridge recipe).
2. Mix this with the sugar, the egg yolks and the stiffly beaten egg white.
3. Layer the mixture alternately with the bitter biscuits in a fireproof dish, making sure the last layer consists of rice porridge.
4. Let the dish rise and brown in a moderately warm oven and serve as soon as it comes from the oven.

title: Deense Rijstsoep (Danish Rice Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g rice
- 1/2 lemon peel
- 1 1/2 liter water
- salt
- 100 g raisins (or half raisins, half currants)
- 2 1/2 dl berry juice
- juice of one lemon
- 125 g sugar

## Directions

1. Set the washed rice on with the water, salt and lemon peel and let it cook gently for half an hour.
2. Add the sugar, berry juice, lemon juice and well-washed raisins.
3. Let the soup cook gently for another quarter hour.
4. Remove the lemon peel.

title: Dikke Koek (Thick Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces wheat flour
- 4 eggs
- 12 loods currants
- 12 loods butter
- 2 loods orange peel snippets
- Warm milk
- Salt
- Yeast
- Butter and ground rusks for the pan

## Directions

1. Mix the wheat flour, eggs, currants, butter, and orange peel snippets
2. If there is not enough moisture, add some warm milk
3. Add salt and yeast, beat everything together and let it rise
4. When risen, put this batter in a Turk's head or tart pan that has been previously greased with butter and sprinkled with ground rusks
5. Set the pan in hot ashes or over a moderate fire, lay fire orderly on top and let it bake
6. To know if it is done, stick a knife in it; as long as flour sticks to it, it is not ready

title: Dikke melk (Thick Milk - Old Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dl thick milk
- some cream

## Directions

1. Place the thickened milk in a pillowcase, hang the pillowcase up and place a bowl underneath to catch the watery liquid
2. Remove the mass from the pillowcase as soon as all the water has run out
3. Mix it to taste with some sweet cream and serve it with sugar and cinnamon

title: Diplomaatpudding (Diplomat Pudding) (10 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 150 g sugar
- ½ vanilla pod
- 5 egg yolks
- 100 g currants
- 100 g raisins
- 100 g candied citron
- 100 g biscuits or fruit in syrup
- 40 g gelatin or 35 g powdered gelatin
- 2½ dl whipped cream

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar.
2. Remove the stems from the currants and raisins, rinse them with lukewarm water, cover with boiling water and cook for 10 minutes. Let them drain well in a sieve.
3. Chop the candied citron finely.
4. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla, soak the biscuits in it briefly once it has cooled. Pour it while stirring to the yolks, let the mass bind slightly while stirring well, but do not boil.
5. Soak the leaf gelatin for 10-15 minutes in cold water, then dissolve it in the liquid taken from the heat and let it cool somewhat.
6. Add the mixed dried fruit to it.
7. Add the mass to the stiffly beaten cream, let it become jelly-like stirring occasionally and layer it alternately with the biscuits in a mold rinsed with unbeaten egg white, but ensure that the first and last layer do not consist of biscuits.
8. Turn out the pudding after cooling onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.
9. Note: Instead of with mixed dried fruit and biscuits, the pudding can also be prepared with fruit in syrup. The biscuits can be soaked in rum or maraschino instead of in the milk.

title: Dop Erwten (Pickled Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- butter brine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Handle as roman beans
2. When stewing, add some sugar

title: Doperwten (Garden Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg very young peas or 2 kg older peas
- salt
- 300 g butter
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Shell the peas, wash them and cook them in little boiling water with salt until tender (20 to 25 minutes, very young peas shorter), or steam them.
2. Drain them quickly in a colander if needed and stir them as warm as possible through the butter, which has been placed in the dish.
3. Sprinkle some very fine parsley over them.

title: Doperwten (Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg very young or 2 kg old peas
- salt
- 300 g butter
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Shell the peas and wash them
2. Cook them in little boiling water with salt until done (20 to 25 minutes, very young peas shorter), or steam them
3. Let them drain quickly in a colander if necessary
4. Stir them as warm as possible through the butter that has been placed in the bowl
5. Sprinkle some very fine parsley over them

title: Doperwten (Shelled Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 kg shelled peas
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Shell the peas, check carefully that there are no damaged ones, wash them, fill well-cleaned bottles with them up to 3 cm below the rim, add salt if desired.
2. Pour on enough water so that the peas are just covered.
3. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize for 1 to 1½ hours.
4. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.
5. Note: It is best to sterilize the shelled peas again for one hour the next day, as they spoil easily, or blanch them before they go in the bottles.

title: Doperwtensoep (Split Pea Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g old shelled peas or a packet of frozen peas
- 1 l water with 4 Maggi bouillon cubes or light bouillon
- some parsley and chervil
- 1 dl cream or milk
- 40 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the washed peas until done in the water with the cubes or the bouillon and the parsley and chervil
2. Pour the liquid through a sieve, rub the peas through and bring it to a boil while stirring
3. Let the soup cook until it is evenly bound, skim off the foam if necessary and add the butter or margarine and the cream or milk
4. Serve with diced brown fried bread

title: Drabbelkoeken (Fried Batter Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 200 g white sugar
- 3 teaspoons fine cinnamon
- 1 egg
- + 2 1/2 dl milk
- melted veal fat

## Directions

1. Make a stiff batter from the flour, sugar, fine cinnamon, egg and milk.
2. Heat melted veal fat in a small iron pot until blue smoke rises from it.
3. Pour part of the batter through the special funnel, which is continuously moved back and forth in the fat, and fry the drabbelkoek until brown and done.
4. Remove it from the pan and press it briefly in a small round bowl to get a nice shape. Let it drip on porous paper.

title: Dragonsaus (Tarragon Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter bouillon or water with salt
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 tablespoon tarragon

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Stir the washed and finely chopped tarragon through away from the heat
3. Serve with cooked fish

title: Drie in de Pan (Three in the Pan)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 1 egg (optional)
- 3 dl lukewarm milk
- 15 g yeast
- 75 to 100 g currants, raisins and mixed peel
- 60 g lard
- salad oil, butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg and add to the flour with enough lukewarm milk so the flour does not fly about
2. Stir out the lumps from this batter
3. Add the remaining lukewarm milk, well-washed currants and raisins with stems removed, the chopped mixed peel, salt and the yeast mixed with lukewarm milk
4. Let the batter rise, covered, in a warm place for half an hour to three quarters of an hour
5. Fry round cookies from the batter using lard, salad oil, butter or margarine (or half lard, half butter) until light brown and cooked through
6. Test if they are cooked by pricking with a steel fork, which should come out dry
7. Serve with syrup or brown sugar

title: Duif (Pigeon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pigeons
- at least 100 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the pigeons thoroughly inside and outside, rub them with salt.
2. Make the butter or margarine light brown in a pan, place the pigeons in it, baste them on all sides and roast them brown and cooked through (approximately 1/2 hour).
3. Turn them several times during this period and add some water to the butter or margarine if needed when it threatens to become too dark in color.
4. Place the pigeons on a platter, pour the gravy, which has been finished with some water, over them and tie small manchettes around the legs.
5. Serve with stewed fruit or salad.

title: Duitse Biefstuk (German Steak)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g stewing beef, chopped
- 100 g butter or margarine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 1 onion
- 4 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Wash the pieces and chop or grind them very finely.
2. Add the milk and the spices and mix the mass until it forms a coherent whole.
3. Form steaks from this and fry them brown in the butter, finish the gravy (see beef steak recipe 253).
4. Fry the chopped onion and garnish the steaks with it.

title: Duitse zandtaart (German Sand Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 eggs
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- 150 g sugar
- vanilla or grated lemon zest
- icing
- French candied fruits

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks completely smooth with the sugar, add the flavoring.
2. Sift the flour, add the hard butter or margarine in small, equal pieces.
3. Whip the egg whites as stiffly as possible, stir in the yolks with a few strokes and then, as lightly as possible, the chopped butter or margarine and flour.
4. Put the batter immediately into a greased tart pan dusted with powdered sugar and flour.
5. Place the pan in a hot oven, check carefully after 5 minutes to see if the cake is not browning too much, otherwise lay a buttered piece of gray paper on it and bake it light brown and done (25 to 30 minutes).
6. Test if it is done by inserting a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
7. Turn the cake out onto a cake rack, let it cool and garnish it if desired with icing and French candied fruits.

title: Duiven aan 't spit (Doves on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- doves
- bacon
- vine leaves
- butter

## Directions

1. Ensure the doves are well cleaned and singed.
2. Open them and place under each a strip of bacon with which to lard the dove, and a vine leaf.
3. Baste with butter and roast for only half an hour.

title: Duiven als patrijzen (Doves as Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- doves
- water
- vinegar
- shallots
- morels
- celery
- parsley root
- bay leaves
- juniper berries
- pepper
- cloves
- brown butter

## Directions

1. Allow two parts water and one part vinegar with shallots, morels, celery or parsley root, bay leaves, juniper berries, pepper and cloves to boil for half an hour and then cool.
2. Place the doves in this and let them soak for a whole day.
3. Lard them and roast slowly in brown butter while constantly basting.
4. Heat the liquid in which the doves soaked and pour under them during roasting as often as needed.

title: Duivesoep (Pigeon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- water
- salt
- rice
- ground mace
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Treat as chicken soup, with or without vegetables

title: Een Vliegende Podding (A Flying Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces flour
- 2.5 ounces suet
- 1 ounce raisins
- 4 eggs
- nutmeg
- mace
- 0.25 can milk
- flour for dusting cloth

## Directions

1. Stir 2.5 ounces flour, 2.5 ounces suet, 1 ounce raisins, 4 eggs, nutmeg and mace well together with 0.25 can milk
2. dust a cloth or napkin with flour
3. put everything in it
4. bind it up
5. let it cook for 1 hour

title: Eendebout (Duck Leg)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck leg
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Well washed and cleaned, sprinkle inside and outside with salt and pepper as desired.
2. Place a piece of butter in the pan and let it brown well.
3. Place the duck leg in it, pouring water over from time to time, and roast until done in an hour and a half.

title: Eendvogel (Duck)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 100 g butter or margarine
- salt
- chopped sour apples or water (optional)

## Directions

1. Press out the tear glands at the tail until no more fat comes out, or cut them out.
2. Wash the bird, salt it inside and outside.
3. Make the butter or margarine light brown in a roasting pan or stew pot, place the duck in it, baste it on all sides and roast it until light brown and cooked through (approximately 1 1/2 hours).
4. Add some water to the butter or margarine if needed when it threatens to brown.
5. Test if the duck is cooked by pricking with a fork between the joints; it should pass through easily.
6. Make the gravy in the usual way with water.
7. Note: To remove the strong flavor of duck, one may sometimes fill it with chopped sour apples or with water and sew it closed.

title: Eendvogelsoep (Duck Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- water
- salt
- rice
- ground mace
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Treat as chicken soup above
2. You can also prepare it with vegetables

title: Eenvoudige Bruine Bonensoep (Simple Brown Bean Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g brown beans
- 2 1/2 l water
- salt to taste
- 4 leeks
- 1 bunch celery greens
- 60 g butter, margarine, or beef fat

## Directions

1. Wash the beans and soak them for 24 hours in cold water
2. Bring them to a boil in this water and let them cook on low heat for 1 1/2 hours
3. Add the cleaned and washed vegetables and salt, and let the soup cook slowly for another 1 1/2 hours
4. Rub the beans fine so that the soup is evenly bound and stir in the butter, margarine, or fat

title: Egg Sauce (Eiersaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Vinegar
- Two to three eggs
- Flour
- Butter
- Mace

## Directions

1. After the vinegar has boiled, add two to three well-beaten eggs
2. Stir well while pouring to prevent curdling
3. Add flour, a piece of butter to taste, and some mace, which greatly enhances the flavor

title: Egg Sauce (Eiersaus) - Alternative
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tablespoons flour
- Vinegar
- Water
- Three eggs
- Lemon peel
- Onion
- One ounce butter

## Directions

1. Stir two tablespoons flour with vinegar and water
2. Add three well-beaten eggs, lemon peel, onion, and one ounce butter
3. Stir everything together well

title: Eierbroodjes (Egg Bread Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small bread rolls
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 10 g flour
- 10 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl milk or broth
- some parsley
- 4 mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- if desired some lemon juice
- 15 g butter or margarine to brush

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 102.
2. Cut the peeled eggs into small dice and stir these with pepper, salt, mustard and

title: Eierbroodjes (Egg Bread Slices)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 slices of casino bread without crust
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 1/2 dl milk
- 15 g butter or margarine
- 15 g flour
- chopped parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Halve the slices and fry them light brown on both sides in the butter or margarine
2. Chop the hard-boiled eggs finely
3. Mix them with plenty of salt
4. Make a sauce in the usual way from butter or margarine, flour, milk and the very finely chopped parsley
5. Stir the finely chopped eggs through
6. Spread this mixture on the slices so that it rises somewhat higher in the middle
7. Place the bread slices on a warmed dish on which a folded napkin lies
8. Garnish the dish with a tuft of curly parsley
9. Present the bread slices with spinach or serve at the coffee table

title: Eierbroodjes (Egg Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kleine broodjes (small rolls)
- 2 hardgekookte eieren (hard-boiled eggs)
- 10 g bloem (flour)
- 10 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- 1 dl melk of bouillon (milk or bouillon)
- wat peterselie (parsley)
- 4 champignons (mushrooms)
- zout (salt)
- peper (pepper)
- mosterd (mustard)
- desverkiezend wat citroensap (lemon juice, optional)
- 15 g boter of margarine om te besmeren (butter or margarine for brushing)

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 102.
2. Cut the peeled eggs into small cubes and stir these with pepper, salt, mustard and optionally lemon juice through the sauce.
3. Add the chopped mushrooms and prepare the rolls further as described in the previous recipe.

title: Eieren en Surprise (Surprise Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- flour and egg white
- chocolate cream

## Directions

1. Carefully blow out twelve eggs
2. Close one hole (with flour and egg white) and let it dry well
3. Fill the empty shell with chocolate cream as described later
4. After closing the second hole, place the eggs in boiling water to thicken for a considerable time, but do not let them boil
5. Clean them well and serve in a folded napkin

title: Eieren koken (Boiled Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the eggs and place them carefully, either with a spoon or with an egg net, in ample boiling water.
2. Cook soft eggs 3 minutes, medium-hard eggs 5 minutes and hard eggs 8 minutes.
3. Cook duck eggs 12 minutes.
4. For very fresh eggs, cook 1 minute longer than usual.
5. Remove them from the water and present them hot with salt.

title: Eieren met béchamelsaus (Eggs with Béchamel Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 dl béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 346 and pour it onto a warmed small dish.
2. Place the hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters on it and garnish the dish with fresh sprigs of parsley.
3. Serve this dish as described in the previous recipe.

title: Eieren met kerrysaus (Eggs with Curry Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 4 dl curry sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 339.
2. Pour it through a sieve onto a warmed small dish and place the hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters on it.
3. Serve this dish at coffee time or with cooked rice at lunch.

title: Eieren met mosterdsaus (Eggs with Mustard Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 dl mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 332.
2. Place the hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters on it and serve that dish as you would eggs with curry sauce.

title: Eieren met tomatensaus (Eggs with Tomato Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 dl tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 340 and pour it over the eggs cut in half, which have been placed on a warmed small dish.

title: Eieren op den Rooster (Eggs on the Gridiron)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- chopped onions and parsley
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper
- a little bread crumb

## Directions

1. Make a paper case and place a piece of butter inside
2. Add chopped onions and parsley
3. Warm on the gridiron so the butter is well melted and warm
4. Break an egg into the case with a little pepper, salt and a few bread crumbs
5. Place on a moderate fire with a glowing coal on both sides of the case
6. Take a glowing ash shovel and hold it above the egg until it is cooked
7. 6, 8 or 10 make a small dish

title: Eieren à la Pauvre Femme (Eggs Poor Woman Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- twelve eggs
- bread, cut into cubes
- butter for frying
- mustard sauce or spices

## Directions

1. Melt a little butter on a plate
2. Break twelve eggs onto it and place in warm ashes
3. Cut bread into cubes and fry in butter
4. Add the bread to the eggs
5. Place fire on top
6. When well cooked, pour a sauce with mustard or spices over it

title: Eierkaas (Egg Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 dl milk
- salt
- Maggi's Aroma to taste
- 25 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with some salt, pour the milk in (for 1 small egg ¾ dl, for 1 large egg 1 dl) and pour the mixture into cups greased with butter or margarine.
2. Place these in a pan with water that is kept just below boiling, but must not boil, as the egg cheese would then become crumbly.
3. Let them stand in it until the egg has set, turn them out and cut them into cubes for garnish and filling of soup.
4. For pressure cooker users: Beat 3 eggs with ½ l liquid, salt and spices. Fill buttered molds with this. Place them on the rack in the pressure cooker and keep the egg custard under pressure for 5 minutes. Then remove the pan from the heat and let it stand for 5 minutes before opening it. In this short time, a beautifully smooth egg custard will have been made.

title: Eierklonten (Egg Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- salt
- finely chopped parsley
- nutmeg
- 1 liter water
- 0.5 teaspoon Liebig's Meat Extract
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs well with a fork together, then stir in finely chopped parsley, a little nutmeg, salt, and 1 liter of water in which you have cooked 0.5 teaspoon of Liebig's Meat Extract with some salt.
2. Place this mixture in a pot lined with butter and set it in boiling water. After the course of half an hour it becomes thick; however, do not let it become hard.
3. Then cut off small dumplings and place them in prepared soup. The above is calculated for 8 persons.

title: Eiermelk (Egg Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet milk
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. In 3 cans of sweet milk, stir in 12 to 13 yolks

title: Eiermelk (Egg Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cans sweet milk
- 12 to 13 egg yolks
- a little rose water
- roasted white bread, cut into cubes
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Stir 12 to 13 egg yolks and a little rose water into 3 cans sweet milk
2. Place on the fire and stir until it comes to a boil
3. Pour it over roasted white bread cut into cubes and add sugar to taste

title: Eierpannekoeken (Egg Pancakes, approximately 8 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 3-4 eggs
- salt
- 1/2 l milk
- 80-100 g butter or margarine
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with salt.
2. Add the flour and enough milk until the flour no longer floats.
3. Stir out the lumps from the batter and pour in the remaining milk.
4. Melt some butter in the frying pan, pour a spoonful of batter in and turn the pancake as soon as it has dried from below.
5. Pour some melted butter or margarine underneath and fry the pancake, turning it repeatedly and adding a small knob of butter each time, until golden yellow and cooked through on both sides.
6. Serve with white sugar.

title: Eiersaus of Zure Saus (Egg Sauce or Sour Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 2 eggs
- 3/2 deciliter vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce) and mix it with the vinegar
2. Pour it, stirring well, into the beaten eggs
3. Serve with calf's tongue, beef tongue, pork tongue, cooked flatfish, white beans, etc.

title: Eierschotel met tomatensaus (Egg Dish with Tomato Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hardgekookte eieren (hard-boiled eggs)
- 3 dl tomatensaus (tomato sauce)
- 75 g rijst of gort (rice or barley)
- zout (salt)
- peterselie (parsley)

## Directions

1. Cut the hard-boiled and peeled eggs lengthwise in half.
2. Place them with the flat side on a warmed plate.
3. Place the cooked rice (see recipe 569) as quickly as possible in egg cups rinsed with cold water and pour them between the eggs.
4. Cover the eggs with the tomato sauce (see recipe 340) and present the rest separately.
5. Place a sprig of parsley here and there.

title: Eiersla (Egg Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- mayonnaise
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs hard (8 to 10 minutes), place them in cold water, peel them and cut them into slices with the aid of the so-called egg slicer
2. Make sure the mayonnaise tastes sharp, otherwise stir in some more mustard, pepper and salt
3. Carefully mix the egg slices with some mayonnaise, place them on the plate and sprinkle some fine parsley over them

title: Eiersoep (Egg Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 4 lood butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 good spoon cream or sour cream

## Directions

1. Beat 2 eggs and 4 lood butter together into foam
2. Add 2 tablespoons flour, 1 good spoon cream or sour cream, and make it into a dough
3. Cut this dough into small pieces and throw them into boiling bouillon
4. Let them cook somewhat, then serve

title: Eiwitglazuur (Egg White Glaze)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g powdered sugar
- approximately one egg white
- a few drops of maraschino, kirsch or vanilla extract
- 1/2 drop carmine if desired

## Directions

1. Sift the powdered sugar, add small amounts of egg white until a glossy mass is obtained that is so stiff that it does not run off the spoon or spatula with which you stir but stands firm.
2. Add the flavoring and if desired the food coloring.
3. Use egg white glaze for fruit cakes. Brush the shell of puff pastry, pie dough or sand-tart dough first with egg white glaze, let it dry and then add the moist filling. This prevents the baked good from becoming moist and soft.
4. Use this egg white glaze to make decorations on water glaze or cooking glaze. Egg white glaze hardens quickly and thus holds its shape better.

title: Ejoopsalade (Pickled Escarole)
body:
## Ingredients

- escarole
- salt
- butter brine

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves
2. Boil briefly in water
3. Place in an earthenware pot
4. Add a little salt between each layer
5. Make a butter brine and pour over
6. Weight down

title: Engelsche Kaatjes of Zandkoekjes (English Cheese Cakes or Sand Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- ½ pound butter
- ½ pound brown sugar
- 1 grated lemon
- cinnamon
- currants and candied citron peel (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together cold.
2. Place in small molds and set in a cake or tart pan.
3. Bake slowly with heat above and below.

title: Engelsche saus (English Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 anchovies
- 1 ounce capers
- 3 egg yolks from hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- bouillon
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop the anchovies, capers and egg yolks from hard-boiled eggs very finely with a little pepper
2. Add some bouillon
3. Heat it and thicken it with a little butter and flour

title: Engelse kerstgebakjes (English Christmas Pastries - Mince-pies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 125 g butter or margarine
- water
- 100 g currants
- 100 g raisins
- 75 g citron peel
- 25 g candied peel snippets
- 2 sour apples
- 100 g calf's suet
- 80 g sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger
- 1/2 teaspoon crushed cloves
- juice of 2 lemons
- grated zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl, add the butter or margarine and cut it into the flour until it is as fine as breadcrumbs.
2. Add water quickly and lightly while stirring until the dough hangs together as a firm ball.
3. Roll it out on a floured board into a thin sheet and let the dough, which has become slack from handling, rest under a damp cloth for half an hour.
4. Remove the suet from its membrane, wash it, as well as the currants, raisins and peeled and cut into quarters sour apples.
5. Chop this all together with the citron peel until it becomes a fine mixture.
6. Stir in the sugar, ginger powder, lemon zest, lemon juice and crushed cloves.
7. Butter approximately 30 small tartlet molds, line them with a piece of dough and fill them with a little of the mixture.
8. Lay a layer of dough on top, press the edges firmly together and place the molds on a baking sheet in a moderately warm oven.
9. Let the pastries become light brown and done (approximately 20 minutes), turn them out and keep them in a tin for several days before serving.
10. Serve them sprinkled with powdered sugar and decorate the platter with a sprig of holly.

title: English Fruit Pie (Engelse Vruchtenpie)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g flour
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 25 g sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- juice of one lemon
- salt
- fruit that cooks quickly, such as fresh plums, apples, gooseberries or rhubarb or stewed fruit or preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Use hard butter or margarine for the dough (from the refrigerator in summer).
2. Sift the flour into a bowl, put the butter or margarine in it and chop it finely until it looks like breadcrumbs; beat the egg with the sugar and lemon juice and add this to the mixture.
3. Stir this together as lightly as possible, add as much water as is necessary to form a firm ball of dough.
4. Roll out the dough on a flour-dusted board with a flour-dusted rolling pin into a round sheet, larger than the heat-resistant dish.
5. Put the required fruit in the dish.
6. Sprinkle sugar between the raw fruit and fill the dish in the middle high with the fruit.
7. Make sure that if stewed fruit is used, it is cold.

title: English Pigeon Pie (Engelsche duivenpastei)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pigeons, cleaned
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Six hard-boiled egg yolks
- Pastry crust with lid
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Cut the legs, neck and wings off the cleaned pigeons
2. Cook everything together in a pan with bouillon, salt and pepper
3. Let them cook until nearly done
4. Place them in a pastry crust mold with a lid
5. Add six hard-boiled egg yolks beforehand
6. Stick the pigeon legs on top of the pastry lid
7. Brush everything with egg white
8. Bake in the oven until done

title: English Tart (Engelsche taart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Feuilleté crust
- Crust for pies
- Cherries, morello cherries or other preserves
- Egg white
- Fine pounded white candy sugar

## Directions

1. Make a crust from feuilleté and also a round crust as used for pies
2. Make the bands and standing edges from feuilleté
3. Brush with egg white
4. Lay in this open tart: cherries, morello cherries or other preserves
5. Lay diagonal strips of feuilleté over them
6. When it comes out of the oven, sprinkle with half-finely pounded white candy sugar

title: Erwtenpurée (Pea Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g green peas
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the peas in the usual way until done. Rub them, a small amount at a time, as warm as possible through a sieve, until only the skins remain. Mix this purée with as much of the liquid in which the peas were cooked until it has reached the thickness of mashed potatoes.
2. Stir in butter or margarine, salt and nutmeg to taste, heat it well and mix it with the finely chopped parsley. Serve the purée with sausages, pork chops, etc.

title: Erwtensoep (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 hectograms green peas or split peas
- 5 liters water
- celery and leek
- salt
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- pork knuckle or salted sausage (optional)

## Directions

1. Take 5 hectograms of green peas or if you prefer split peas, let them cook in 5 liters of water until they are well done and become fine.
2. Take celery and leek, adjusting the proportion according to which vegetable you prefer more flavor in. Wash them well in cold water after cleaning them thoroughly, chop them finely and let them cook for about a good hour in water until they are well done.
3. Rub the cooked peas through a sieve and add them with the cooking liquid to the vegetables, add salt to taste and some Liebig's Meat Extract. If you want to enhance the flavor with a little pork, you can use a pork knuckle or salted sausage which you let cook together with the peas.

title: Erwtensoep (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- rainwater
- salt
- salted pork
- sausage
- celery
- scorzonera
- onions

## Directions

1. Soak the needed quantity of green peas in rainwater the evening before
2. Hang the peas with the water in which they soaked and let it almost cook down
3. When the peas are cooked, mash them with a spoon into pieces and add pieces of salted pork and sausage, some celery and scorzonera; some also add onions
4. If you don't want shells in the soup, mash the peas when cooked through a strainer or use split peas

title: Erwtensoep (Pea Soup) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pork feet
- 500 g fresh sausage or 50 g butter or margarine
- 750 g green peas
- 4 l water
- 30 g salt
- 4 leeks
- 1 bunch celery greens
- 1 small celeriac
- 2 potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash the peas, soak them overnight in water, and cook them the next day in the same water gently until tender (approximately 2 hours)
2. Pour them with the liquid through a horsehair sieve and rub the peas through until only the skins remain
3. Cook the washed feet in this liquid for one hour, the washed and pricked sausage for half an hour (this last is then removed and reheated in the soup just before serving)
4. Add the cleaned, finely cut, and very well washed vegetables and potatoes and let everything cook together gently, stirring occasionally, until the soup is evenly bound and the meat leaves the bone
5. Fill the soup from time to time with a little water
6. Serve with rye bread

title: Erwtensoep (Pea Soup) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beef tail
- 100 g ground veal
- 4 l water
- 30 g salt
- 400 to 750 g split peas or pearl peas
- 4 leeks
- 1 bunch celery greens
- 1 small celeriac

## Directions

1. Brush the tail well in lukewarm water and let it cook gently with 4 l water for 2 hours
2. Add the washed peas and cook everything together for another 3 hours, filling the liquid from time to time
3. Remove the tail from the soup and cut off the pieces of meat
4. In the meantime, clean the vegetables, wash them, and cut them fine
5. Let them cook in the soup for approximately 1/2 hour
6. Stir from time to time to prevent burning and add water to the soup if needed
7. Add the meatballs, made in the usual way, 10 minutes before serving
8. Serve with toasted or baked bread cut into dice

title: Erwtensoep in den Zomer (Summer Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- young peas
- bouillon
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Cook young peas briefly, then cook them for half an hour until they are done
2. Add bouillon and a little brown sugar

title: Erwtensoep in den Zomer (Summer Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- young peas
- broth
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Cook young peas briefly, then cook them another half hour until done, adding broth and a little brown sugar

title: Erwtensoep met Rundvleesch (Pea Soup with Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- bouillon from lean beef
- vegetables
- salt

## Directions

1. Handle the peas as described above
2. When the peas are cooked, add bouillon made from lean beef, vegetables and salt to taste
3. Let everything cook together until the vegetables are well cooked

title: Erwtensoep met Rundvleesch (Pea Soup with Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- rainwater
- salt
- beef broth
- lean beef
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Treat the peas as above
2. When they are cooked, add broth made from lean beef, vegetables and salt according to preference, letting everything cook together until the vegetables are well done

title: Evenvoeltjes (Small Even-things)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 beaten eggs
- 2½ ounces wheat flour
- 2 ounces melted butter
- lukewarm milk
- yeast
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a batter of 10 beaten eggs, 2½ ounces wheat flour, 2 ounces melted butter, lukewarm milk, yeast, and a little salt.
2. Let it rise for a while.
3. Bake in a poffertje pan and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar when serving.

title: Fazant (Pheasant)
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Lard the pheasant and keep the tail whole; also cut off the head.
2. Roast it on the spit, but be careful to cover it with paper smeared with butter and baste it well.
3. When done, fasten the head back on, attach the tail and present it.

title: Fazant (Pheasant)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- 150 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Roast the pheasant as you would chicken (see recipe 309).
2. Allow 1 to 1½ hours roasting time.
3. Serve with peach or apricot compote.

title: Fazant (Pheasant)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- 150 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Roast the pheasant as you would chicken (see recipe 309).
2. Allow 1 to 1 1/2 hours as roasting time.
3. Serve with peach or apricot compote.

title: Fazantepooten in Papillotten (Pheasant Claws in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant claws
- finely chopped parsley
- buttered paper
- lemon sauce

## Directions

1. Cut off the claws from the remaining pheasant legs
2. Prepare these with finely chopped parsley and wrap them in buttered paper
3. Place them on the grill
4. Serve with a lemon sauce

title: Fazantepooten in papillottes (Pheasant Legs in Papillotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant legs
- parsley
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Cut the claws from the leftover pheasant legs.
2. Prepare these with finely chopped parsley and wrap them in buttered paper.
3. Place them on the grill and eat with a lemon sauce.

title: Feuilleté with Lard (Feuilleté met reuzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- One measure flour
- One quarter pound fine salt
- One whole egg
- Water
- One ounce lard

## Directions

1. Spread flour on the work surface and make a mound with a hole in the middle
2. Place in the hole: one quarter pound fine salt, one whole egg, water, and one ounce lard
3. Knead together
4. Let the dough rest for two hours
5. Roll out very thin
6. Pour melted lard (three ounces) over the rolled dough each time
7. Continue rolling with lard under the dough, as with butter feuilleté
8. Let rest, then roll four more times
9. Use as needed

title: Feuilletée or Frans bladerdeeg (Puff Pastry)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or hard margarine or calf suet
- 100 g flour
- some salt
- approximately 0.5 dl water

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl, add salt, place butter, margarine or fat in it and cut these with 2 knives into fairly coarse pieces.
2. Add, stirring as gently as possible, drippings of cold water until everything sticks together, but especially do not use too much water, as otherwise the dough would stick when rolling out.
3. Roll out the ball, turning it over each time, on a flour-dusted board or table with a flour-sprinkled rolling pin to a thin layer.
4. Carefully brush off excess flour that clings to it and fold the dough lengthwise into thirds. (Larger quantities can be folded into thirds lengthwise and widthwise.)
5. Let the dough, which has become soft from handling, rest in a cool place until it is stiff again (approximately 15 minutes) and repeat rolling out three times and folding three times.
6. Then fold it into quarters and let it rest as long as possible.
7. Roll it out to the desired thickness and give it the desired shape.

title: Filets van Tong (Sole Fillets)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large or 4 small cooking soles
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a deep cut in the middle of the fish down to the bone, do the same along the fins and remove the fillets with a sharp knife.
2. Wash them, roll them up, tie them shut with a thread or knot them and cook them in enough boiling water with salt until they just are covered (4 to 6 minutes).
3. Remove them with a skimming spoon from the liquid and present them with parsley, butter, shrimp, oyster or mussel sauce, poured over as hot as possible.
4. Note: The fishmonger delivers the sole also filleted upon request.

title: Filosoof (Minced Meat and Potato Casserole) - First Method
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat, minced or ground
- Potatoes, minced
- Onions, chopped
- Lean gravy with water (or milk or bouillon)
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Mince or grind the meat finely, make the potatoes fine.
2. Fry the chopped onions light brown.
3. Mix these with the meat and potatoes, moisten everything with the lean gravy with water (milk or bouillon) until it reaches the thickness of mashed potatoes.
4. Place it in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water, smooth the top, sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place a few pieces of butter on it, clean the edge of the dish and let the philosopher brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Filosoof (Minced Meat and Potato Casserole) - Fourth Method
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat, minced
- Onions, fried
- Gravy with water
- Potatoes, mashed
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make a ball from the minced meat with the fried onions and some gravy with water.
2. Place it in the middle of a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water.
3. Lay mashed potatoes, prepared in the usual way, around it and finish the dish as indicated in the first method.

title: Filosoof (Minced Meat and Potato Casserole) - Second Method
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat, minced or ground
- Potatoes, minced
- Fat
- Onions, chopped
- Gravy with water

## Directions

1. Mince or grind the meat finely.
2. Make the potatoes fine.
3. Fry the chopped onions light brown in the fat.
4. Layer everything layer by layer in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water, making sure the last layer is potatoes.
5. Moisten with the gravy with water and finish as indicated in the first method.

title: Filosoof (Minced Meat and Potato Casserole) - Third Method
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat, sliced
- Potatoes, sliced
- Onions, fried
- Gravy with water
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Layer the meat sliced, the potato slices and the fried onions layer by layer in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water.
2. Make sure the last layer is potatoes.
3. Moisten everything with the gravy with water and finish as indicated in the first method.

title: Flageolets (Flageolet Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flageolet beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), reckon as cooking time approximately 1½ hour and serve them with sour sauce with veal or beef tongue.

title: Flensjes (Crepes, 24 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs or 1 egg
- salt
- 120 g or 150 g flour
- 1/2 l milk
- 80 g butter or margarine
- powdered sugar or crushed white sugar
- applesauce, rhubarb compote, jam, or whipped cream (for second method)

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD: Beat the eggs with salt, add the flour (with 1 egg also 1 dl milk) and stir out the lumps with a wooden spoon or whisk. Add the milk gradually. Melt some butter or margarine in the frying pan or heat some salad oil, pour a little batter onto it but let it run as thin as possible. Fry the crepe on one side until light brown and cooked through on fairly high heat, roll it up with 2 forks. Lay the crepes crosswise on a plate, sprinkle powdered sugar or crushed white sugar over each layer.
2. SECOND METHOD: Prepare the batter as described above and fry the crepes in the same way. Do not roll them up, but lay them on a round plate with an inverted small plate on top, with the fried side facing down. Spread some applesauce, rhubarb compote, jam, or whipped cream over each crepe. Lay the last crepe with the fried side up, sprinkle some powdered sugar over it and cut the crepe cake thus obtained into wedges.
3. THIRD METHOD: Prepare the batter as described above, but use more flour. Fry the crepes on both sides until light brown and serve them with crushed sugar.

title: Forel (Trout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg trout
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, wash them and cook them in boiling water with salt, until a fin comes loose easily (+ 10 minutes), or thread a strong knitting thread through the tail and the head and pull the fish somewhat round before it is cooked.
2. Serve it with butter sauce, parsley sauce or tomato sauce.

title: Forel in aspic (Trout in Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg trout
- 1 l white aspic
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. For preparation see lobster in aspic.
2. The trout is first boiled until done, carefully freed of bones and skin and used only after cooling.
