title: Frambozen op Brandewijn (Raspberries in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g raspberries
- 200 g sugar
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 0.5 l brandy

## Directions

1. Sort the raspberries carefully, remove any worms if necessary and wash them. Treat the fruit further as described in recipe 1031.

title: Frambozen te Konfijten (Candied Raspberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- half the amount of sugar as for red currants

## Directions

1. Prepare as for red currants, but with only half the amount of sugar

title: Frambozen- en bessengelei (Raspberry and Berry Jelly)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg raspberries
- 1½ kg red and white berries
- 3/4 of the weight of the fruit juice in sugar
- Jam jars
- Parchment paper
- Cellophane
- Elastic bands
- String
- Labels

## Directions

1. Treat the jelly as indicated for berry jelly, second recipe.

title: Frambozen- en Bessenjam (Raspberry and Currant Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg raspberries
- 1½ kg red currants (cleaned)
- 2¼ kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- elastic bands
- string
- labels

## Directions

1. Pick the raspberries, check carefully that there are no worms in them
2. Wash them briefly in cold water
3. Wash the currants, remove them from the stems
4. Put the fruit in a pan, bring them to a boil with the lid on

title: Frambozen- en Bessenjam (Raspberry and Red Currant Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg raspberries
- 1.5 kg red currants (cleaned)
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- twine
- labels

## Directions

1. Pick the raspberries and check carefully that there are no worms in them.
2. Rinse them briefly in cold water.
3. Wash the currants and drain them.
4. Put the fruit in a pan, bring it to a boil with the lid on and let the fruit cook without the lid, stirring occasionally, until reduced.
5. Add the sugar and let the jam cook with it until it reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369).
6. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Frambozen- en Bessensapsaus (Raspberry and Berry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dl berry juice
- 1/2 dl raspberry juice
- 1 1/2 dl water
- approximately 70 g sugar
- 20 g sago or potato starch

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as berry juice sauce

title: Frambozen-ijs (Raspberry Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 g raspberries
- 500 g sugar
- 1/2 liter water or milk
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1/4 liter cream
- a little carmine

## Directions

1. Prepare the whole as strawberry ice cream (recipe 884).

title: Frambozenazijn (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 kg raspberries
- 2 l pickling vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take 1 kg raspberries, clean them, wash them carefully and let them stand for 24 hours, covered with the vinegar.
2. Then carefully pour the liquid, without stirring, onto 1 kg fresh raspberries, which have also been picked and washed, and let them stand again for 24 hours.
3. Repeat this procedure to get a very fragrant extract if desired once more.
4. Then measure the amount of juice and add 750 g sugar per liter of liquid.
5. Dissolve this completely in it, but make sure that the liquid does not boil.
6. Skim off the foam as carefully as possible and fill well-cleaned and boiled bottles with the raspberry vinegar.
7. Close them with boiled corks.
8. Serve the raspberry vinegar with water or soda water (2 spoons per glass).

title: Frambozencompôte (Raspberry Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ kg raspberries
- approximately 2½ dl water
- 75 to 100 g sugar
- some lemon juice
- some sago

## Directions

1. Prepare it as strawberry compote.

title: Frambozengelei (Raspberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- red raspberries
- half an ounce fish glue
- bread sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Crush red raspberries finely and strain the juice through
2. Cook half an ounce fish glue with bread sugar and some water
3. Add the raspberry juice and let it cook like berry jelly, using 1 ounce sugar for every ounce of juice

title: Frambozenjam (Raspberry Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg raspberries (cleaned)
- 2¼ kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- elastic bands
- string
- labels

## Directions

1. Pick the raspberries, check carefully that there are no worms in them
2. Wash them briefly in cold water, put them in a pan, bring them to a boil with the lid on and let the fruit cook without the lid, stirring occasionally
3. Add the sugar and let the jam cook until it reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369)
4. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983)

title: Frambozensapsaus (Raspberry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 dl raspberry juice
- 2 1/2 dl water
- approximately 60 g sugar
- 20 g sago or potato starch
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as berry juice sauce
2. Add some lemon juice off the heat

title: Frambozensaus (Raspberry Sauce) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g perfect raspberries
- 4 dl water
- 20 g sago or potato flour
- 75 g sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as strawberry sauce (recipe 758).
2. Present the sauce with maizena, semolina pudding etc.

title: Frambozensaus (Raspberry Sauce) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g raspberries
- 4 egg yolks
- 125 g bastard sugar
- 4 dl white or Rhine wine
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as strawberry sauce (recipe 759).
2. Present it with warm semolina, rice pudding etc.

title: Franse omelet (French Omelet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper and salt to taste
- Maggi's Aroma to taste
- 50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper, salt and some Maggi's Aroma.
2. Add the water and pour into a hot skillet in which the butter or margarine has melted, but especially not browned.
3. Prick continuously with a fork until the eggs begin to set across the entire surface.
4. Allow the omelet to become very light brown on the bottom and fold it in thirds.
5. Serve the omelet with salad or bread or fill it with ragout.

title: Franse omelet (French Omelet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper
- salt
- Maggi's Aroma
- 50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper, salt and some Maggi's Aroma.
2. Add the water and pour them into a hot frying pan in which the butter or margarine has been melted, but especially not browned.
3. Prick continuously with a fork until the eggs begin to set over the entire surface.
4. Let the omelet become very light brown on the bottom and fold it in three.
5. Serve the omelet with salad or bread or fill it with ragout.

title: Fricandeau (Veal Fricandeau)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal fricandeau
- Water

## Directions

1. Take a fricandeau of veal and lard it only on top
2. Set it with heat from above, at first with moderate heat while adding some water
3. Then increase the heat and pour the sauce over it when serving

title: Fricassé (Veal Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- best part of veal breast
- bouillon
- flour
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- crushed herbs
- egg yolks (optional for binding)

## Directions

1. Take the best part of a veal breast and cut it into pieces
2. Cook it with bouillon until half done
3. Roast flour and butter, add it to the meat and let it fry together
4. Add salt, pepper, nutmeg, and crushed herbs
5. Pour hot bouillon over it
6. Let everything braise until done
7. When serving, strain the jus through a sieve; if too thin, bind it with a couple of egg yolks

title: Fricassésaus (Fricassee Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 dl bouillon
- 2 1/2 dl cream
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- lemon juice
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Remove from heat and blend with the egg yolks, add lemon juice, pepper and salt to taste and serve with meat timbal among other dishes

title: Fried Rabbit (Gefruite Konijnen)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- unsalted butter
- milk
- egg yolks
- nutmeg
- salt
- sugar
- bread
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces and fry in unsalted butter
2. When half fried, chop a portion of the pieces very fine, adding milk, two egg yolks, grated nutmeg and salt
3. Pour this over the rabbit when it is sufficiently fried
4. Add some butter and sugar, each in equal amounts, stirring continuously
5. Serve with chopped bread and arrange chopped vegetables around the edge of the plate

title: Frikadel van Kreeften (Lobster Frikadels)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Can of lobster
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 egg white
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Some slices of bread soaked in milk
- Biscuit crumbs or breadcrumbs
- Butter or margarine, salad oil, beef fat or frying fat

## Directions

1. Take the meat from the lobster and chop it
2. Mix it well with the spices, the soaked bread and egg yolk
3. Make small frikadels from this, which are dipped in loosely beaten egg white thinned with water
4. Roll them in biscuit crumbs or breadcrumbs and fry them in one of the aforementioned fats until light brown

title: Fritures (Batter for Frying)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- First recipe: 1 egg, 70 g flour, 1 dl beer or sparkling water, pepper and salt
- Second recipe: 1 egg, 75 g flour, 15 g cornstarch, 3/4 to 1 dl milk, pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg with the pepper and salt, add the flour (or both types of flour) and as much liquid until the flour no longer floats.
2. Stir the lumps from this batter and add the remaining liquid.
3. Make frying fat hot in a deep iron pot.

title: Fritures (Fried Batter Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 70 g flour
- 1 dl beer or sparkling water
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Beat the egg with the pepper and salt
2. Add the flour and enough liquid until the flour no longer rises in clouds
3. Stir out any lumps from the batter and add the remaining liquid
4. Heat frying fat in a deep iron pot
5. Let the frying iron warm in the fat and dip it in the batter
6. Let the batter dry briefly and fry the fritters in the hot fat until light brown
7. Let them drain on gray paper and fill them warm with ragout
8. If necessary, preserve them completely cooled in tins
9. If they have become soft, place the fritters nested inside each other (to maintain shape) briefly in the oven

title: Fruit Pie (Vruchtentaart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- hard butter or margarine
- flour
- 1 egg
- sugar
- lemon juice
- water
- raw or stewed fruit

## Directions

1. Sift flour into a bowl, add butter or margarine and chop finely until it resembles breadcrumbs
2. Beat the egg with sugar and lemon juice and add to the mixture
3. Stir together as lightly as possible, adding water as needed to form a firm dough ball
4. Roll out the dough on a flour-dusted surface with a flour-dusted rolling pin into a round sheet, larger than the baking dish
5. Place the required fruit in the baking dish
6. Sprinkle sugar between the raw fruit and pile the fruit high in the middle of the dish, ensuring any stewed fruit is cold
7. Moisten the edge of the dish with water, lay the dough sheet over it generously, press onto the moistened edge with a fork and trim the overhanging part
8. Cut figures from the remaining dough and attach as garnish with water
9. Brush everything with egg yolk beaten with water, prick the dough in several places
10. Bake in a fairly warm oven until light brown and cooked through (3/4 hour to 1 hour)

title: Galantine (Galantine)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g veal cutlets
- 250 g pork cutlets
- 50 g smoked ox tongue
- 50 g fresh bacon
- 1 egg
- 50 g old bread without crust
- 10 g salt
- some pepper and nutmeg
- 1/2 tin of truffles
- 100 g pork caul
- 1/2 litre aspic in two colours
- notched lemon slices
- parsley
- capers

## Directions

1. Soak the pork caul for some time in lukewarm water and wash it thoroughly clean. Wash the cutlets, pass them twice through a meat grinder and chop them finely. Cut the bacon, which has been stripped of rind and washed, into cubes, do the same with the tongue and the truffles. Beat the egg with pepper, salt and nutmeg.
2. Stir through the bread soaked in water or milk and squeezed out between two spoons and the remaining ingredients, until no bread pieces are visible. Form a firm, oblong ball from it, place this on the pork caul and fold it around it on all sides.
3. Roll the meat in a doubled cloth and tie it as tightly as possible. Bring water with plenty of salt to a boil in a large pan with a small plate in the bottom, place the galantine in it and cook it for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Press it afterwards (with the cloth around it) for 24 hours between 2 flat plates with a heavy weight (weights or stones) on top. Then take it out of the cloth and cut it into thin slices.

title: Galettes (Savory Pastry Cases)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- pepper and salt to taste
- white or brown ragout

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, add the butter or margarine to it and cut it as fine as breadcrumbs.
2. Stir pepper and salt and the beaten egg through this mixture.
3. Add enough water, if necessary, so that the dough does not crumble and roll it out to 1/2 cm thickness on a floured board or table.
4. Brush tartlet molds with some butter or margarine and line them generously with a portion of the dough.
5. Prick here and there in the bottom to prevent it from puffing up and bake the galettes in a moderately warm oven light brown and done (15 to 20 minutes).
6. Remove them from the molds and fill them as hot as possible with ragout.

title: Galettes (zoete) (Sweet Galettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 30 g sugar
- 1 egg
- fresh fruits or fruits in juice

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, add the butter or margarine and cut it fine as breadcrumbs.
2. Stir the sugar and the beaten egg through this mixture.
3. Add enough water if necessary so the dough no longer dusts and roll it out to ¼ cm thickness on a flour-dusted board.
4. Grease tartlet molds with some butter and fill them quite full with a portion of the dough.
5. Prick here and there in the bottom to prevent bubbles and bake the galettes in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked.
6. Remove them from the molds and fill them after cooling with fresh fruits (e.g., strawberries with whipped cream) or fruits in water.

title: Game Sauce (Wildsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Butter
- Vinegar
- One tablespoon fried flour
- Syrup or brown sugar
- A splash of Rhine wine
- Fine ginger
- Crushed cloves
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Mix water, butter, and vinegar with one tablespoon fried flour
2. Add syrup or brown sugar, a splash of Rhine wine, fine ginger, crushed cloves, and lemon slices
3. Stir everything well and let it cook until it has proper thickness

title: Gans (Goose)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 10 large sour apples
- salt

## Directions

1. If not already done, remove the tear gland.
2. Wash the goose thoroughly inside and outside, salt it and fill it with chopped sour apples.
3. Close the rear, roast it as you would turkey (recipe 322).
4. Note: One can also fill a goose with chestnuts (see turkey) or with soaked dried prunes.

title: Garnalen (Shrimp)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g shrimp
- salt water
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- vinegar
- parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the shrimp with salt water
2. Let them drain well on a sieve
3. Mix with some salt and pepper, oil and vinegar
4. Place on the serving dish
5. If desired, make a border around it with very finely chopped or cut parsley, or sprinkle parsley over the top

title: Garnalen in aspic (Shrimp in Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g peeled shrimp
- 1 l light aspic
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- slice of tomato

## Directions

1. Let the washed shrimp drain well.
2. Prepare the dish as lobster in aspic.

title: Garnalen in Schelpen (Shrimp in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g shrimp
- 2½ dl milk or half cream, half milk
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or 18 g Duryea cornstarch
- fine parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Inspect the shrimp carefully and wash them in salted water.
2. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102 or recipe 329.
3. Mix it with the shrimp and the seasonings.
4. Fill the dampened shells with this mixture to about 3/4 full.
5. Finish them as indicated in recipe 102.
6. Note: When the shrimp have been peeled yourself, they do not need to be washed.

title: Garnalen met mayonnaise (Shrimp with Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g shrimp
- approximately 2 tablespoons mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Prepare the shrimp as indicated in the Garnalen recipe
2. Mix them with some sharp-flavored mayonnaise
3. Place on the serving dish

title: Garnalen met Mayonnaise (Shrimp with Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g shrimp
- 2 tablespoons mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Prepare the shrimp as indicated in recipe 3.
2. Mix them with some sharp-flavored mayonnaise and place them on the plate.

title: Garnalen met Roomsaus (Shrimp with Cream Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g shrimp
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 4 dl milk
- 1 dl cream
- pepper and salt
- half-moons of feuilletée or toasted bread

## Directions

1. Rinse the shrimp with salted water, warm them well in a colander over a pan with hot water.
2. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see recipe 337).
3. Mix it to taste with some pepper and salt, put warm shrimp in it and place everything on a warmed dish.
4. Garnish the edge with half-moons of feuilletée (see recipe 846) or toasted bread.

title: Garnalencroquetjes (Shrimp Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g shrimp
- 1 1/2 dl milk, cream, or half milk, half cream
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 20 g flour
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon Maggi's aroma
- 1/2 egg or one egg white for breading
- 2 sheets gelatin or 4 g powdered gelatin
- bread crumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Wash the shrimp in water with salt.
2. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102.
3. Mix it with the gelatin, the shrimp, and the spices and let the mixture, spread on a board, become almost stiff, but ensure it is still good to handle.
4. Form the croquettes in the manner as indicated for meat croquettes (see recipe 116), bread them double, and treat them further entirely as meat croquettes.

title: Garnalenpasteitjes (Shrimp Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled shrimp
- cream
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Take peeled shrimp and stew them with some cream, butter and a little flour and fill the pastries with it

title: Garnalenragout (Shrimp Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g shrimp
- 1 1/2 dl milk
- 1/2 dl cream
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- finely chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon Maggi's Aroma
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way.
2. Rinse the shrimp with salted water, carefully search for impurities, warm them by hanging them in a sieve or colander over hot water.
3. Then stir them through the sauce.
4. Mix this to taste with some pepper, salt, parsley and Maggi's Aroma and carefully add it from the heat to the beaten egg yolk.
5. Fill the pastries with this mixture.

title: Garnalensaus (Shrimp Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l fish bouillon or salted water
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- piece of mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- small carrot
- sprig of parsley
- 1 egg
- 100 g shrimp

## Directions

1. Rinse the shrimp with salted water and let them drain well
2. Let the herbs steep in the fish bouillon
3. Pour through a sieve and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
4. Warm the shrimp in the sauce
5. Blend to taste with pepper, salt and the beaten egg
6. Serve this sauce with sole fillets, turbot etc.

title: Gasconnade (Gascon-Style Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- 8 to 10 balls of garlic
- 10 anchovies in strips
- garlic
- bouillon
- butter
- flour
- large glass brown jus
- bouillon

## Directions

1. From a leg of lamb, remove the membranes as much as possible
2. Lard it with 8 to 10 balls of garlic and 10 anchovies plucked into strips
3. Roast it on the spit until done
4. Make a sauce from 20 balls of garlic cooked in bouillon until nearly done, fried in butter with flour, a large glass of brown jus and an equal amount of bouillon in which the garlic was cooked
5. Let it all cook well together and serve the leg in it

title: Gebakken aardappelen (Fried Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cold cooked potatoes without crumbs
- approximately 75 g fat or oil

## Directions

1. Slice the potatoes into not too thick slices.
2. Heat the fat in the frying pan until blue smoke rises from it.
3. Lay the slices in, fry them on both sides quickly until light brown and crispy.
4. Turn them with a knife or pancake turner.
5. Shake the potatoes around if many need to be fried at once.

title: Gebakken Bloedworst (Fried Blood Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 600 g baking blood sausage
- 50 g butter or margarine or lard
- approximately 2 sour apples

## Directions

1. Slice the sausage into slices of approximately 3/4 cm thickness, remove the skin.
2. Heat the fat in a frying pan and fry the blood sausage on both sides quickly brown.
3. If desired, fry apple slices in the same fat as indicated in the recipe for pork roll.

title: Gebakken Bokking (Fried Smoked Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 smoked herrings
- butter oil, salad oil, butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut off the head of the smoked herrings
2. Cut open the back along the backbone, split the fish open
3. Remove the backbone, innards, black membrane and swim bladder, but if possible leave the soft roe or hard roe inside
4. Clean the smoked herrings or (better) wipe them with a damp cloth
5. Dry them with a dry cloth and rub them in with pepper and salt
6. Heat the oil until blue steam rises and fry first the opened side brown, then the outside
7. Let them drain on gray paper

title: Gebakken Bot (Fried Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- flounder of 5 to 6 in the 5 ounces
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Take flounder of that size, score it, sprinkle with salt and fry it slowly with butter in the frying pan

title: Gebakken Forel (Fried Trout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg trout
- 75 g butter or margarine or 1 dl salad oil
- breadcrumbs
- 1 egg
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, leave them whole but give the fish a couple of incisions
2. Treat them further as you would sole (recipe 214)

title: Gebakken Geep (Fried Asp)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg asp
- 100 g butter or margarine or 1.5 dl salad oil
- flour or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat the fish as described in recipe 208, but divide it into pieces and do not be frightened by the green backbone

title: Gebakken Grutjes (Fried Buckwheat Groats)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover buttermilk with buckwheat groats
- lard, frying fat or salad oil

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover buttermilk with buckwheat groats into not-too-thick slices and fry them in lard, frying fat or salad oil in the frying pan on both sides until light brown.
2. Ensure the fat is not too hot, as the groats will stick and burn.
3. Serve with molasses or brown sugar.

title: Gebakken Ham (Baked Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked ham
- bread dough or Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare as for boiled ham
2. Then wrap it in a bread dough of finger thickness, or sprinkle it on all sides with Parmesan cheese, and let it brown in the hot oven

title: Gebakken kaas (Fried Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 niet te dunne plakken kaas a 50 g per stuk (4 not too thin slices of cheese, 50 g each)
- 60 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- 1 eiwit (egg white)
- paneermeel (breadcrumbs)

## Directions

1. Cut the crust off the cheese.
2. Beat the egg white loose with one spoon of water.
3. Pull the slices through the egg white, let them drain well and pull them through breadcrumbs once more.
4. Fry the slices in the butter or margarine, which has been made dark brown, quickly brown on both sides.
5. Finish the jus in the usual manner.
6. Serve fried cheese as hot as possible.
7. Serve the fried cheese with cauliflower, salsify, leek.

title: Gebakken Kalfskarbonaden (Fried Veal Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Crushed rusks
- Meat juices

## Directions

1. Pound the chops well flat, prick them with a pointed knife
2. Smear them with butter and roll them in fine salt, pepper and crushed rusks
3. Fry them quickly on the grill or in a frying pan until done
4. Pour the meat juices over them when serving

title: Gebakken Kalfskarbonaden in Papieren (Veal Chops in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Crushed rusks
- Writing paper

## Directions

1. Treat as above but roll them in writing paper smeared with butter
2. Roast them on the grill

title: Gebakken Kalfslever (Fried Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- water
- cloth
- potato flour
- grated old white bread
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the liver in clean water for a couple of hours
2. Dry it with a cloth
3. Remove the skin
4. Cut out the tendons
5. Cut into slices the thickness of two fingers
6. Roll them in potato flour and grated old white bread in equal parts
7. Fry them in butter on both sides until brown
8. Serve with salad

title: Gebakken Kalfslever (Fried Veal Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- water

## Directions

1. Place the liver in clean water for a couple of hours
2. Dry it with a cloth and remove the skin
3. Cut out the sinews
4. Cut it into slices two fingers thick

title: Gebakken Kalfslever, Runderlever of Varkenslever (Fried Calf's, Beef, or Pork Liver)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg liver
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Slice the liver into finger-thick slices, remove the blood vessels and membranes, wash it and rub it with some pepper and salt.
2. Roll it through some flour and fry it in the brown butter in 3-4 minutes until done and only lightly colored.

title: Gebakken kalfslever, runderlever of varkenslever (Fried Veal, Beef or Pork Liver)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kg liver
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt, pepper, flour

## Directions

1. Slice the liver into finger-thick slices, remove the blood vessels and membranes, rinse it and rub it with some pepper and salt
2. Roll it in some flour and fry it in the brown butter in approximately 4 minutes until cooked and only lightly coloured

title: Gebakken Kalfstong (Fried Veal Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tongue
- melted butter or fat
- hard biscuit
- sour sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue until done and remove the skin
2. Slice it
3. Dip the slices in melted butter or fat
4. Roll them in hard biscuit
5. Fry them in a frying pan or on a grill
6. Serve with sour sauce

title: Gebakken Lamsribben (Fried Lamb Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb ribs
- bacon
- onions
- spices

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the ribs
2. Lard them with fine pieces of bacon
3. Braise them well in a pan until done
4. You can also add a couple of onions with some spices, which makes them tasty
5. They are eaten with their own jus

title: Gebakken Makreel (Fried Mackerel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 mackerels
- butter oil, salad oil or butter
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare as described for gebakken bokking (recipe 209)

title: Gebakken Mosselen (Fried Mussels)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels as indicated in recipe 195
2. Remove them from the shells
3. Coat them in flour mixed with salt and pepper
4. Fry the mussels brown (for example in deep frying fat) and let them drain on porous paper

title: Gebakken Paling (Fried Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick eel
- salt
- butter
- crushed biscuit
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take thick eel, score it, cut it into pieces
2. Let sit in salt for some time and dry them
3. Spread the pieces with butter and sprinkle with crushed biscuit mixed with pepper
4. Fry them slowly in the pan until done

title: Gebakken Paling (Fried Eel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg eel
- 70 g butter or margarine or 1 dl salad oil
- flour or breadcrumbs
- 1 egg
- salt

## Directions

1. For frying eel one preferably orders thick eel
2. Clean the eel as indicated for boiled eel (recipe 190)
3. Treat the eel, which can be coated in salt and pepper, as you would fried sole, but allow 20 to 30 minutes for cooking time
4. Do not fry eel on too hot a fire

title: Gebakken Panharing (Fried Raw Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 raw herrings
- butter oil or salad oil
- flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare as described for gebakken bokking (recipe 209)

title: Gebakken pisang (Fried Banana)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 6 pisangs (bananas)
- 25 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the fruits, place them in the butter or margarine that has been made light brown in the frying pan and fry them carefully, turning occasionally, until light brown on all sides.
2. Serve them with roasted meat.

title: Gebakken Rolpens (Fried Pork Roll)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 slices pork roll
- some flour
- 50 g pork roll fat or butter
- lard, butter or margarine
- approximately 2 sour apples

## Directions

1. Slice the pork roll into slices of approximately 1 cm thickness, remove the membrane.
2. Roll the slices in flour, heat the fat in a frying pan and fry the pork roll on both sides brown.
3. Core the apples, peel them, slice them into as many slices as there are pork roll slices and fry them carefully on both sides brown in the same fat.
4. Place a slice of apple on each slice of pork roll.
5. Make the gravy in the usual manner.

title: Gebakken saucijsjes of gebakken verse worst (Fried Sausages or Fried Fresh Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g sausages or fresh sausage
- 40 g lard or margarine
- flour

## Directions

1. First manner: Rinse the sausages with lukewarm water, dry them with a clean cloth or between a piece of brown paper. Sprinkle a little flour over them and fry them in the fat or margarine over a not too hot fire to prevent the skin from bursting, brown and cooked (approximately 15 minutes). Serve the sausages with cauliflower or sauerkraut.
2. Second manner: Make the rinsed sausages or sausage cooked in as little water as possible, brought to a boil. Let this evaporate completely, remove the lid from the pan and roast the sausages or sausage on both sides in their own fat brown (approximately 10 minutes).
3. Note: If not enough fat has come out during cooking for frying, some margarine, lard or beef fat can be added

title: Gebakken Schol (Fried Plaice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large plaice for frying
- butter oil or salad oil
- flour or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, wash it well inside and outside
2. Cut it in, rub it on both sides with plenty of salt and let it lie on a colander for half an hour so the salt can penetrate
3. Heat enough oil in the frying pan so the bottom is well covered
4. Ensure blue steam rises from the fat
5. After drying, coat the fish on both sides in flour or milk
6. Fry the light side of the fish on a brisk fire until light brown, then the dark side
7. Shift the fish back and forth in the pan while frying
8. When the fish is done, let it drain well on porous paper on both sides and serve as indicated in the rules for frying fish

title: Gebakken schol (Fried Plaice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 nice frying plaice
- butter or salad oil
- flour or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, wash the fish well inside and out, score it, rub it well with salt on both sides and let it lie on a colander for half an hour so that the salt can be absorbed.
2. Make enough oil in the frying pan hot so that the bottom is well covered.
3. Ensure that blue steam rises from the fat.
4. After drying, roll the fish on both sides through flour or milk.

title: Gebakken Tong (Fried Sole)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 baking soles
- 1 dl salad oil or 75 g butter or margarine
- salt
- flour
- milk or breadcrumbs
- 1 egg white or half an egg

## Directions

1. First method: Clean the soles. If this has not been done by the fishmonger, do as follows: cut off the head, make an incision in the skin at the tail and strip it off all at once, remove the innards, cut off part of the tail and side fins, wash the fish well
2. Rub it on both sides with salt and let it lie salted on a colander for half an hour
3. Dry it, then coat it on both sides in flour or milk
4. Fry the sole in hot salad oil or browned butter on both sides until light brown and cooked through (when the fish is done, it will split along the side fins)
5. Let it drain on porous paper and serve with salad and mayonnaise
6. Second method: Clean the fish as described above. After the salt has penetrated and the fish is dried, first pass it through beaten egg or egg white mixed with water, then coat it in fine breadcrumbs
7. Fry it in the same way as above. When frying breaded fish, move it especially carefully by shaking the pan. Let the fish drain on porous paper and save the fat after straining it for another time

title: Gebakken Tongen (Fried Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongues
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Treat as fried flounder

title: Gebakken Uien (Fried Onions)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g onions (approximately 4 pieces)
- 30 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the onions, wash them, cut them into the thinnest possible slices.
2. Heat the butter or margarine until light brown in the frying pan, add the onions and fry them, turning constantly, until light brown.
3. Remove them immediately from the pan, as they will otherwise become too dark.

title: Gebakken Zalm (Fried Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- thickly cut pieces of salmon
- salt
- pepper
- crushed biscuit

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon into reasonably thick pieces
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and let sit three hours
3. Roll them in crushed biscuit and fry them slowly in a frying pan

title: Gebakken zwezerik (Fried Sweetbreads)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- 75 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the sweetbreads as indicated in recipe 293 for 20 minutes
2. After removing the membrane and fat, press them for a quarter hour between 2 bowls or plates with a heavy weight on top
3. Slice them diagonally and fry these slices in butter or margarine until brown and done
4. Finish the gravy with a little milk
5. Arrange the sweetbreads on a platter, pour the gravy over them and garnish the platter if desired with mashed potatoes and young vegetables of contrasting color arranged in sections around them
6. Note: the sweetbread slices can also be double-breaded before frying

title: Gebakken Zwezerikken (Fried Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- beaten eggs
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- crushed hard biscuit
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the sweetbreads until half done and cut them into slices
2. Roll them in beaten eggs
3. Sprinkle them with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and crushed hard biscuit
4. Fry them with butter in the pan until done

title: Gebraden appelen (Baked Apples)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 sour apples
- 100 g sugar
- 2 teaspoons fine cinnamon
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 dl water
- 50 g raisins
- some ground coconut

## Directions

1. Core the apples, peel them and place them with the hole facing up in a heat-proof dish
2. Mix the sugar with the fine cinnamon and fill the apples with this mixture and the well-washed raisins
3. Place a knob of butter or margarine on each apple
4. Pour the water into the dish and let the apples become tender in a moderately warm oven (approximately 20 to 30 minutes), but ensure they do not fall apart
5. Baste them from time to time with the liquid
6. Sprinkle with some ground coconut towards the end

title: Gebraden duiven (Roasted Doves)
body:
## Ingredients

- doves
- ground meat
- parsley
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Roast them in a copper pan, either larded or roasted with some ground meat, a little parsley, salt and water.
2. They need an hour to be ready.

title: Gebraden haan (Roasted Rooster)
body:
## Ingredients

- rooster
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- fine salt
- fresh butter
- bouillon
- flour
- ginger
- cloves
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut off the head, tail and half the wings.
2. Wash it with half water and half vinegar.
3. Sprinkle with pepper and fine salt and place on the spit.
4. Roast slowly, constantly basting with fresh butter.
5. Make a sauce from bouillon, fresh butter, flour, ginger, cloves and pepper.
6. Let the sauce boil and pour it over the rooster.
7. When serving, place some lemon slices on top and fasten the head, tail and wings back on with pins.

title: Gebraden Haas (Roasted Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- salt
- pepper
- water
- vinegar
- bacon
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. After skinning and gutting the hare, rub it inside and out with salt and pepper
2. Let it soak in water for a couple of hours and then lay it for 2 days in vinegar and salt
3. Lard it with thin strips of bacon that have been rolled in salt and pepper
4. Place it straight in a hare pan with meat forks against the hindquarters
5. Add necessary amount of butter and boiling water
6. Roast until done with fire above and below, basting from time to time with the cooking liquid
7. At the end, sprinkle some fine breadcrumbs over it

title: Gebraden Ham (Roasted Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- ground biscuit
- pepper
- water
- red wine

## Directions

1. Treat it as described above, but cook it for only one hour
2. Then remove the rind and sprinkle it with ground biscuit and pepper, some water and red wine, now let it roast in a roasting pan, with fire underneath and above, and finish roasting until done

title: Gebraden hoen (Roasted Hen)
body:
## Ingredients

- hen
- fine salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Sprinkle fine salt inside the hen and rub it on the outside.
2. Roast it with fire above and below, constantly basting with its own juices, in brown butter until done.

title: Gebraden Kalfslever à l'Italienne (Fried Calf's Liver Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- salt
- pepper
- two eggs
- a spoon of oil or butter
- a little flour
- oil or butter
- sour sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the calf liver into thin strips
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Beat two eggs with a spoon of oil or butter
4. Roll the strips in a little flour
5. Fry them in oil or butter
6. Eat them with a sour sauce, very hot

title: Gebraden Konijn (Roasted Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- young rabbit
- water
- salt
- pepper
- fine breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Put the rabbit, which should be especially young, on with some water
2. Sprinkle it with fine salt, pepper and ground breadcrumbs
3. Place it straight in a roasting pan with butter and baste constantly
4. Make sure it becomes nicely browned

title: Gebraden kuikens (Roasted Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- crushed rusks

## Directions

1. Cut the squabs in half and flatten them.
2. Brush them with butter and sprinkle with pepper, salt and crushed rusks.
3. Roast until done.
4. Serve with a lemon or shallot sauce.

title: Gebraden Lamsbout (Roasted Leg of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg
- crushed rusk
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Beat and cut the leg well
2. Sprinkle it with salt, pepper, cloves and nutmeg, along with crushed rusk
3. Place it in a pan with fire above and below
4. When half done, sprinkle it once more with rusk and add lemon slices
5. Take care to baste it well

title: Gebraden Osselapjes (Alternative) (Roasted Beef Slices - Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef slices
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Take beef slices, place them beforehand for a couple of days in vinegar in which salt, pepper and cloves have been added.
2. Braise them in that liquid for two hours and then roast them in a dish with heat above and below.

title: Gebraden Osselapjes (Roasted Beef Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef slices
- Flour
- Butter
- Onions, finely chopped
- Salt
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Pound the slices well tender, roll them in flour and roast them brown in butter.
2. Then place them in a pan with finely chopped onions, salt and cloves, and let them stew for about an hour.

title: Gebraden Ossenhaas (Roasted Beef Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tenderloin (ossenhaas)
- Morels sauce

## Directions

1. Lard a beef tenderloin and roast it over a good fire.
2. Make a sauce of morels with it, as described in the article on ragouts of morels, but somewhat thinner.

title: Gebraden Patrijzen (Roasted Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- three partridges
- livers of three partridges
- hard boiled eggs
- breadcrumb
- sliced bacon
- six to eight celery tops
- good bouillon
- butter
- potato starch
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop the livers of three partridges with hard boiled eggs and breadcrumb together
2. Fill the partridges with this mixture
3. Cook these for some minutes
4. Cover them with sliced bacon and let them braise slowly
5. Meanwhile cut the white part of six to eight celery tops into pieces one inch long
6. Let them cook for one hour in good bouillon
7. When the sauce boils well, add a piece of butter, potato starch, pepper and salt
8. Strain them and add some lemon juice
9. Serve this sauce with the birds

title: Gebraden peren (Roasted Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- cinnamon
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Take pears, peeled or unpeeled as desired
2. Set them upright and tightly in a dish
3. Sprinkle them with cinnamon
4. Let them roast in the oven with some butter and water added
5. Or roast them with charcoal fire from above and below

title: Gebraden Rib (Roasted Rib)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib roast
- Salt
- Beef fat from the breast
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Jus
- Potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Pound and salt the rib roast as needed.
2. Lard it with fat from the breast, rolled in cloves, pepper and salt, and roast the piece in an oven, on a spit or in an iron pot, continually basting with the jus.
3. If you want potatoes with it, cook them first half done, and then roast them with the rib.
4. When serving, arrange them on the dish around the meat.

title: Gebraden Runderschijf (Roasted Beef Slice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef slice
- Salt
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Handle the slice as described for the rib on the previous page, rub it well inside with salt, cloves and pepper and let it stand in vinegar for a couple of days.
2. Roast it just like the rib roast.

title: Gebraden Snoek (Fried Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pike
- salt
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the pike along the dorsal fin
2. Season it in salt
3. Roll it in flour and fry it

title: Gebraden Speenvarken (Roasted Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- one suckling pig
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- a good knob of butter
- thin slices of bacon
- an orange or lemon

## Directions

1. Rub the suckling pig well inside with pepper, salt and cloves, put a good knob of butter inside and close the belly again with skewers
2. Bend the legs as if it were lying on its knees, cover it with thin slices of bacon and roast it in a roasting pan or on a spit, basting constantly
3. When serving, remove the skewers and place an orange or lemon in the pig's mouth

title: Geconfijte kweeperen (Candied Quinces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg quinces
- 3 liters water
- 2¼ kg sugar
- 4 cloves (optional)
- 6 cm cinnamon stick (optional)
- Lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the pears carefully, cook them for several minutes in boiling water, then peel them, cut them into quarters and remove the cores.
2. Bring the water and sugar to a boil, add the fruit and if desired the spices and some of the peels and pits bound in a piece of cheesecloth.
3. Let everything simmer gently together until the pears are cooked and have become a nice red color (1 to 2 hours).
4. Place them with the liquid in a bowl and let them stand until the next day.
5. See if the liquid has become syrupy.
6. If not, cook the syrup down until it coats a cold spoon dipped into it.
7. Cook the fruit for 5 minutes more and place the mass in a well-cleaned Cologne pot.
8. Check after several days to see if the syrup is the right consistency and cook it down again if necessary.
9. Add lemon juice if desired.
10. Close the pot with damp parchment paper and label it with name and date.

title: Gedroogde Abrikozen (Dried Apricots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g dried apricots
- 60 g sugar
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the apricots several times with lukewarm water, let them soak for 24 hours with 2½ times as much water as apricots.
2. Bring them to a boil with the same water and let them cook slowly until tender (approximately 10 minutes).
3. Add the sugar and carefully thicken the liquid with a little mixed sago.

title: Gedroogde Appelen (Dried Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- preferably Kannetjesappelen variety, sour or sweet

## Directions

1. Peel the apples
2. Remove the cores
3. Dry as for pears

title: Gedroogde Appelen (Dried Apples)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g dried sour apples
- 100 g sugar
- ½ dl berry juice
- peel of ½ lemon or piece of cinnamon
- some sago

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the cores, wash the apples several times with lukewarm water, let them soak in plenty of cold water for 24 hours.
2. The next day, bring them with the same water and the flavoring to a boil and let them cook gently until tender (approximately ¾ hour).
3. Remove the flavoring, add the sugar and berry juice and carefully thicken the liquid with a little mixed sago.

title: Gedroogde Capucijners (Dried Marrowfat Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g marrowfat peas
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the marrowfat peas as brown beans. Scoop them with a skimming spoon from the pan and serve them with gravy or fried bacon.

title: Gedroogde Peren (Dried Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- preferably Rietperen variety

## Directions

1. Peel the pears
2. Cut in half
3. Dry loosely in the oven, shaking frequently

title: Gedroogde Peren Californische (Dried Californian Pears)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g Californian pears
- 75 g sugar
- some sago
- peel of ½ lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the pears several times with lukewarm water, let them soak for 24 hours in plenty of cold water.
2. Bring them to a boil with the same water and the lemon peel and let them cook gently until tender (approximately 1 hour).
3. Add the sugar, remove the lemon peel.
4. Carefully thicken the liquid with some mixed sago.

title: Gedroogde Peren Rode (Dried Red Pears)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g dried red pears
- 75 g sugar
- some berry juice
- peel of ½ lemon
- some sago

## Directions

1. Cook them as the Californian pears from the previous recipe.
2. Then add berry juice and sugar, remove the lemon peel and thicken the liquid with some mixed sago.

title: Gedroogde Pruimen (Dried Plums)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g dried plums
- 60 g sugar
- peel of ½ lemon
- some berry juice
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the plums several times with lukewarm water, let them soak for 24 hours in just enough cold water to cover them.
2. The next day, bring them with the same water and the lemon peel to a boil and let them cook gently until tender (20 to 30 minutes).
3. Add the berry juice and sugar and carefully thicken the liquid with some mixed sago.
4. If no berry juice is added, use less sugar.

title: Gedroogde Snijboonen (Dried String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried string beans
- water

## Directions

1. Soak for one night
2. Cook until tender
3. Drain off a little water
4. Stew as fresh string beans

title: Gedroogde Tuinbonen (Dried Garden Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g dried garden beans (large)
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), reckon as cooking time 1¼ to 1¾ hour and serve them with fried bacon.

title: Gefarceerde Snoek (Stuffed Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pike
- two biscuits soaked in milk
- two eggs with white
- four anchovies
- nutmeg
- salt
- butter
- one lemon without skin
- water
- two anchovies
- oysters
- crushed biscuit

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the pike and boil briefly
2. Remove the bone and mince finely
3. Mix with two biscuits soaked in milk, two eggs with white, four anchovies, nutmeg, salt, butter and one lemon without skin, all ground together
4. Shape to the form of a pike in a dish
5. Add enough water so it is well submerged
6. Stir in two anchovies
7. Let braise for one hour
8. Sprinkle crushed biscuit over the fish and add some oysters at the end

title: Gefarceerde Snoek (Stuffed Pike) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pike
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 120 g old bread without crust
- 3 eggs
- approximately 1 dl milk or cream
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice (optional)
- 75 to 100 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the pike in the usual way (see recipe 182) in plenty of boiling salted water. Remove the meat from the bones while still warm, but make sure no bones remain. Chop or grind it finely and mix it with the beaten eggs, into which the bread soaked in milk (or cream) has been stirred. Add pepper, salt and nutmeg to taste and the 150 g butter or margarine softened somewhat. Place the mixture in a buttered fish-shaped mold or shape it as much as possible like a pike in a fireproof dish. Pour lemon juice over it if desired, place 100 g butter or margarine in small lumps on top and let the stuffed pike brown lightly in a moderately warm oven (1 hour).

title: Gefarceerde Snoek (Stuffed Pike) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike with some eel, together yielding 1 kg fish meat
- 3 eggs
- 100 g old bread without crust
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs
- 100 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned fish in the usual way until done (see recipes 182 and 189). Remove the meat from the bones while still warm, chop it finely and mix it with the eggs, the soaked bread and the seasonings. Give it the shape of a pike in a fireproof dish. Sprinkle the fish with breadcrumbs, add the butter or margarine and lemon juice and let it brown in a moderately warm oven, basting it from time to time with the butter or margarine.

title: Gefarceerde Uien (Stuffed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- large onions
- ground veal or pork
- bouillon
- butter
- salt
- mace
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel the onions thickly and hollow them out
2. Fill them with balls of ground veal or pork
3. Cook them until done in bouillon with butter, salt and mace
4. Make a sauce from a portion of the cooking bouillon, binding it with a couple of egg yolks
5. Pour this sauce over the onions when serving

title: Gehakt (Ground Meat)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg ground meat
- 1 egg
- 75 g old bread without crust, or 150 g bread and no egg
- pepper
- 7 1/2 g salt
- nutmeg
- at least 100 g butter, margarine or fat
- lean gravy leftovers

## Directions

1. Beat the egg with the salt, pepper and nutmeg.
2. Soak the bread in some water or milk, press it out well, add the spices and salt and stir this all through the egg until it is evenly mixed with it.
3. Add the ground meat and stir everything with two spoons until it has become an even mass and no bread pieces are visible.
4. Form 2 (or more) firm balls from it.
5. Make butter, margarine or fat hot in a braising pan or small roasting pan.
6. Place the ground meat in it, sprinkle it optionally with some breadcrumbs and braise it brown and done (45 minutes per 600 g, with pork ground meat in 50 minutes per 600 g).
7. Finish the gravy with water or milk and with the lean gravy leftovers.

title: Gehakt in Deeg (Ground Meat in Dough for 8 Persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g ground veal
- 300 g ground pork
- pepper, salt and nutmeg to taste
- 300 g flour
- 3 eggs
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 50 g yeast
- 1 1/2 dl milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put the flour in a bowl, make a small well in the middle, add the yeast and stir from the middle gradually the lukewarm milk, the melted butter or margarine and the eggs, which have been beaten with salt.
2. Rub out the lumps from this batter and let it rise covered (1/2 hour to 3 quarters of an hour).
3. Roll it out on a floured sheet, lay the ground meat mixed with the seasonings on it and roll it in firmly.
4. Give it the shape of a loaf or place it in a bread mold brushed with butter.
5. Bake it on a baking sheet in a fairly warm oven light brown and done (about 3 quarters to 1 hour) and serve it hot with curry sauce or cold.

title: Gehakt met Citroensaus en Rijst (Ground Meat with Lemon Sauce and Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g ground calf meat
- 200 g ground pork meat
- 50 g old bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 50 g onion
- 1 egg white (optional)
- 30 g butter
- 30 g flour
- 3 dl bouillon
- 1 lemon
- 1 egg yolk (optional)
- 150 g rice

## Directions

1. Mix the ground meat with soaked bread, spices, finely chopped onion and egg white.
2. Form 4 oblong balls from this mixture and roll them in flour.
3. Make the butter light brown in a pan, add the flour and stir until the flour is light brown.
4. Add the bouillon, let the sauce cook, add the grated lemon zest and braise the meat balls in it until cooked (approximately 1/2 hour).
5. Remove the meat, strain the sauce and finish it with lemon juice and egg yolk.
6. Cook the rice in the usual manner, press it in rinsed eggcups and make a ring of it on a flat meat platter.
7. Place the meat in the middle and pour the sauce over the meat.

title: Gehakt met Harde Eieren (Ground Meat with Hard-Boiled Eggs for 8 Persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g ground veal
- 1 egg
- 50 g old bread without crust
- pepper, salt and nutmeg to taste
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 100 g butter or margarine
- egg white
- breadcrumbs
- scalloped lemon slices
- parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the egg with salt, pepper and nutmeg.
2. Soak the bread in some water or milk, squeeze it firmly between two spoons and stir it through the egg until it is evenly mixed with it.
3. Add the parsley and the ground meat and stir everything with two spoons until it becomes an even mass and no bread pieces are visible.
4. Press the ground meat into a 1 1/2 cm thick layer on a floured dish.
5. Lay the hard-boiled eggs in the middle, with the ends cut off on both sides to fit them well together, in a row one after the other, after the eggs without ends have been passed through egg white loosened with water.
6. Fold the ground meat around them, but make sure the eggs do not protrude at the sides.
7. Roll the ball through the egg white loosened with water, then through breadcrumbs and fry it in the butter or margarine, which has been made light brown in the roasting pan, brown and done (45 to 50 minutes).
8. Remove the ground meat from the roasting pan, slice it thinly with a sharp knife when it has cooled completely and arrange these slices overlapping on the dish.
9. Garnish with scalloped lemon slices, fresh sprigs of parsley and, if desired, some chopped aspic in different colors.

title: Gehakt van Vleesresten (Minced Meat Balls from Leftovers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g meat leftovers
- 125 g stale bread
- 1 egg
- 50 g onions
- Some parsley
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 50 g fat from gravy, butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Prepare the minced meat as indicated in recipe 91.
2. Form it into one large or four small balls and fry them with some remaining fat from the gravy in the frying pan on all sides until brown.
3. Finish the gravy with some water.

title: Gekonfijten Oranje-Appelen (Candied Oranges)
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- sugar
- rainwater

## Directions

1. First let the oranges soak and then cook them fairly tender, setting them with cold rainwater
2. Now melt sugar and cook it, continuously skimming; add the apples and cook them another half hour; now remove them from the syrup and put them in bottles, cook the syrup a bit thicker and pour it on hot
3. Seal the bottles well

title: Gekookt Lams- of Schapevleesch (Boiled Lamb or Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or mutton meat
- cold water
- hot water
- boiling water
- onions
- some yellow carrots
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat for this purpose well in cold water
2. Broil it with hot water once more and cover it with boiling water
3. Cook it with onions, some yellow carrots and salt, skimming carefully
4. Lamb meat requires 1 hour, but mutton requires 2 to 3 hours to become tender

title: Gekookt Ribbestuk (Boiled Rib Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib roast
- Salt
- Water
- Pears or dried fruit (optional)

## Directions

1. Place a rib roast in salt for 8 to 9 days.
2. Cook it with water until done.
3. You can cook pears or dried fruit in the cooking liquid.

title: Gekookt Rundvleesch (Boiled Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- A loin or rump cut of beef
- Brine (salt water)
- Water
- Anchovies or butter sauce

## Directions

1. Take a loin or rump cut, wash it well and place it in brine for a couple of days, basting it daily with brine.
2. Hang the meat with cold water and let it cook steadily for 3 to 4 hours depending on size, skimming well.
3. Ensure the pot remains filled with bouillon, refilling with boiling water from time to time as it evaporates.
4. Serve this meat either with an anchovy or butter sauce.
5. The bouillon is good for making soup, or one can prepare pears, apples, carrots etc. with it.

title: Gekookt Rundvleesch met Peren, Appelen (Boiled Beef with Pears and Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Pears
- Apples

## Directions

1. Prepare as described for beef with savoy cabbage above.
2. You may add the pears with or without skins, as preferred.

title: Gekookt Rundvleesch met Savoyekool (Boiled Beef with Savoy Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Savoy cabbage
- Salt
- Butter sauce
- Grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Handle the meat as described for boiled beef above.
2. When the meat is half cooked, add the cabbage with a little salt and let it cook until done.
3. When serving, transfer the cabbage to a colander, let it drain, and place it on a dish with butter sauce and some grated nutmeg over it.
4. Serve the meat separately.

title: Gekookte Rollade (Boiled Beef Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef roll (rollade)
- Pepper
- Salt
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Unroll the rollade, rub it well with pepper and salt, scatter ground cloves over it and some bay leaves, and let it stand in good wine vinegar for three days.
2. Now roll it up again and tie it closed.
3. Place it in a pot, well covered, and let it simmer slowly for 3 to 4 hours.
4. Eat it warm with anchovies, or cold with mustard and some pickled vegetables.

title: Gekookte Tongen (Cooked Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongues
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Skin and score the tongues, let them soak in water and cook them in water with salt, the same as flounder

title: Gekookte zwezerik (Cooked Sweetbreads)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- salt
- mace
- cayenne pepper
- piece of carrot
- parsley
- fleurons
- 4 dl egg sauce, lemon sauce, or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Place the sweetbreads in cold water for 1/2 hour
2. Blanch them: bring to a boil with cold water, drain when it boils, then cover again with cold water with mace, cayenne pepper, carrot piece, parsley sprig and salt
3. Let them cook until done (20 to 30 minutes)
4. Rinse them with cold water, remove the fat and membrane, and slice them
5. Braise the slices briefly in the sauce prepared in the usual way (see the recipes)
6. Arrange on a platter and garnish with fleurons (recipe 847)

title: Gelardeerde Patrijzen (Larded Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- larded partridges
- butter

## Directions

1. Roast the larded partridges brown under continuous basting with butter

title: Gele Erwten (Yellow Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g yellow peas
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), but reckon as cooking time 1½ to 1¾ hour. Serve them with parsley sauce.

title: Gele Erwtensoep (Yellow Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow peas
- rainwater
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat entirely as described for green pea soup

title: Gelei van Champagne Wijn (Champagne Wine Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful fresh orange blossoms
- two ounces fish glue
- five ounces sugar
- a bottle champagne wine

## Directions

1. Take a handful fresh orange blossoms and use only the petals, let them boil briefly in boiling water
2. Take two ounces fish glue and let it dissolve well
3. Cook it with the mixture and five ounces sugar
4. When it is almost completely stiff, add a bottle champagne wine
5. Pour into a mold

title: Gemalen rijstpudding (Ground Rice Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 90 g ground rice
- 75 g sugar
- ¼ stick vanilla, vanilla sugar or ½ lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare the pudding as semolina pudding. Like semolina pudding, ground rice pudding can also be prepared with eggs and almonds. For quantity and preparation see the recipes of semolina pudding.

title: Gemarineerde haring (Marinated Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 herrings
- 20 white peppercorns
- 3 bay leaves
- 100 g onion
- 10 cloves
- 1 Spanish pepper
- a few notched slices of lemon
- about 1 l good vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings in water with some milk for 24 hours, refreshing it if necessary.
2. Cut them open on the belly side, remove the intestines, but leave the head and skin on.
3. Wash them well on the inside and outside.

title: Gemarineerde Haring (Marinated Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 herrings
- 20 white peppercorns
- 3 bay leaves
- 100 g onion
- 10 cloves
- 1 Spanish pepper
- a few sliced lemon slices
- 1 liter good vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings for 24 hours in water with some milk, refreshing the water as needed
2. Cut them open along the belly side, remove the entrails, but leave the head and skin attached
3. Wash them thoroughly inside and out
4. Arrange them in a well-cleaned large bottle, so that the head of one herring lies against the tail of the previous one
5. Arrange the bay leaves, pieces of Spanish pepper, sliced lemon slices and onion slices between the wall of the bottle and the herrings
6. Place the remaining spices in the middle and pour vinegar over the herrings until they are covered
7. Store for at most 4 weeks, as otherwise the bones will entirely dissolve

title: Gemarineerde Kalfsbout (Marinated Veal Knuckle)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal knuckle
- Wine vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Shallots

## Directions

1. Take a medium-sized veal knuckle, which must be very pale
2. Place it in a large pan with 3 bottles of good wine vinegar, a little salt, some pepper, bay leaves, thyme and shallots
3. Let it marinate for four to five days, turning it regularly
4. Lard the knuckle and roast it on the spit for four hours, wrapped in paper to keep it pale
5. You may make a white or brown sauce to serve with it

title: Gemarineerde Lamsbout (Marinated Leg of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- anchovy or herring
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- bay leaves
- wine vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the leg and beat it gently
2. Lard it with anchovy or herring
3. Sprinkle the leg with pepper, salt and cloves
4. Place it with some bay leaves for 6 days in wine vinegar
5. After that, braise it with the marinade in a pan until done
6. If the liquid is too sharp, thin it with some water

title: Gemarineerde Reebout (Marinated Venison Haunch - 8 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg venison haunch
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- 2 bay leaves
- 10 peppercorns
- 9 cloves
- some salt
- 1/4 l water
- 1/4 l vinegar
- 100 g larding bacon
- 200 g butter or margarine
- 1/4 dl cream or yoghurt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, place it for 2 to 3 days in half water, half vinegar with the spices.
2. Turn it over from time to time in the meantime.
3. Rub the venison haunch with salt, lard it and roast it 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g in the butter or margarine.
4. Finish the gravy with cream (or yoghurt) and the liquid in which the meat was placed.

title: Gemberborstplaat (Ginger Breast Candy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g sugar
- 1 dl water
- 1 level teaspoon ginger powder
- 50 g candied ginger with syrup

## Directions

1. Stir the ginger powder through the sugar, add the water and let this sugar solution boil for 10 minutes.
2. Add the chopped ginger with syrup and let it cook for another 1 to 2 minutes.
3. Remove the breast candy from the heat, let it become thick and fluid while constantly stirring, and treat it further as described in the first breast candy recipe.

title: Gemberkoekjes (Ginger Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flour
- 400 g butter or margarine
- 400 g sugar
- 100 g fresh ginger
- 100 g almonds
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Cream the butter until fluffy, add the sugar, the sifted flour, the peeled and chopped or ground almonds, the ginger cut into pieces and the beaten egg.
2. Roll this dough out to 1/2 cm thickness on a sheet dusted with flour.
3. Cut out cookies with cookie cutters, place them on a greased baking sheet, brush the top with some beaten egg yolk mixed with a little water.
4. Bake the cookies in the upper part of a fairly warm oven until light brown and done (10 to 15 minutes), remove them from the baking sheet and let them cool.

title: Gembertaart (Ginger Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- 200 g dark brown sugar
- 200 g flour
- 200 g wet ginger
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add alternately an egg yolk and some sugar until a completely smooth, airy mass is obtained.
2. Mix through this with a few strokes the sifted flour, the ginger cut into pieces with some syrup.
3. Beat the egg whites very stiffly and mix the batter through this.
4. Bake the ginger cake in a greased cake or tart pan.
5. For this, place the pan in a moderately warm oven and let it stand for approximately 1.5 hours.
6. This heavy and sweet cake becomes done more slowly than unfilled pastry.
7. Treat the ginger cake further as described in previous recipes.

title: Gemengde Abrikozenschotel (Mixed Apricot Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 g dried apricots
- 25 g raisins
- 4 dates
- 25 g dried prunes
- 25 g ground almonds

## Directions

1. Pit the dates and prunes. Wash the fruits well and soak apricots, prunes and raisins. Put the almonds on with cold water and let them come to a boil (stew); when the skin comes off easily, pour the almonds over with cold water, remove the skins. Grind them through the almond mill. Mix the dried fruits together, if necessary with some brown sugar. Lay the halved dates on top and sprinkle the ground almonds over them.

title: Gemengde Appelschotel (Mixed Apple Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ apple
- ½ banana
- ½ orange
- 4 figs
- 1 tablespoon ground hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Cut the well-cleaned apple into thin slices. Cut the peeled banana and orange into slices. Wash the figs in hot water and cut them through. Peel the hazelnuts and grind them. Mix the fruits together and sprinkle the ground nuts over them.

title: Gemengde Perenschotel (Mixed Pear Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pear
- one slice of melon
- 4 reine claudes (plums)
- ½ peach

## Directions

1. Pit the plums, peel the melon and remove the seeds. Peel the pear and peach and cut them into pieces. Mix the fruits together.

title: Geparelde Gerst met Karnemelk (Pearled Barley with Buttermilk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pearled barley
- Buttermilk

## Directions

1. Cook as groats with buttermilk, using just over 1 can of buttermilk per ounce of barley

title: Geperst Vlees (Pressed Veal for 12 Persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 veal cutlets of 250 g each
- 500 g ground veal
- 1 egg
- 50 g old bread
- 10 g salt
- pepper and nutmeg to taste
- 1 veal foot
- 1 dl tarragon vinegar
- 12 g white gelatine or powdered gelatine
- parsley
- capers
- scalloped lemon slices

## Directions

1. Rinse the foot, place it in cold water just covering it and let it simmer gently until 2 dl of broth remains.
2. Dissolve the gelatine, which has been soaked in cold water for 10 minutes, in the broth and add the vinegar.
3. Rinse and salt the cutlets, mix the ground meat in the usual way with the egg, the soaked bread and the seasonings and layer it alternately with the meat in the designated mold.
4. Pour the broth over it, close the mold with the heavy lid that fits it and cook the mixture for 2 hours in a bain marie.
5. Let it cool, turn it out after removing the fat from it and garnish it with fresh sprigs of parsley, capers and scalloped lemon slices.

title: Gepofte Kastanjes (Roasted Chestnuts)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg chestnuts
- 40 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash the chestnuts, dry them and make a deep cross in them.
2. Brown the butter or margarine in an iron pot, add the chestnuts and roast them on a fairly hot fire, stirring occasionally, until they are cooked, that is until the shells burst open (25 to 30 minutes).
3. Serve them with butter or margarine as a winter evening treat.
4. Note: Instead of in an iron pot, the chestnuts can also be roasted in hot ashes or in the oven.

title: Geraspte Taart (Grated Apple Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 sour apples
- 4 eggs (1 with white, 3 yolks only)
- 1½ rusk
- a piece of cinnamon
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- sugar
- 2 lemons with juice and zest, grated

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.
2. Put into a tart crust and bake.

title: Gerezen Pannekoeken (Raised Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces wheat flour
- 2.5 ounces buckwheat flour
- salt
- 3 eggs
- warm milk
- yeast
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a thick batter with 2.5 ounces wheat flour, 2.5 ounces buckwheat flour, salt, 3 eggs and warm milk
2. Add yeast and let rise
3. Fry in butter in a pan

title: Gerezen Pannekoeken (Risen Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g flour or pancake flour
- approximately 3/4 liter milk mixed with hot water
- 15 g yeast
- 75 g lard, salad oil or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Add enough lukewarm liquid to the flour so it does not fly about and stir out the lumps from this batter
2. Gradually add the remaining liquid and the yeast mixed with lukewarm milk
3. Let the batter rise, covered, in a warm place for three quarters of an hour to 1 hour
4. Stir the salt through
5. Heat enough lard, salad oil or margarine in the pancake pan so the bottom is just sufficiently greased
6. Pour enough batter so the bottom is well covered and fry the pancake on a fairly brisk fire on both sides until light brown and cooked through
7. Serve with brown sugar or syrup

title: Geroerde Eieren (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- several eggs
- nutmeg
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat several eggs finely
2. Place on a stove with fire
3. Add nutmeg, salt and butter
4. Stir until it becomes crumbly and reaches a proper thickness

title: Gerolde Kalfslapjes of Looze Vinken (Rolled Veal Scallops or False Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely cut strips of kidney fat
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- crushed hard biscuit
- thin veal scallops
- lemon peel
- onions
- finely cooked parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the kidney fat strips with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and crushed hard biscuit
2. Place these in thin veal scallops along with a piece of lemon peel, onions, and finely cooked parsley
3. Roll them up and bind them securely
4. Brown butter in a pan
5. Roll the scallops in crushed hard biscuit
6. Place them in the pan and baste them continually with their own jus
7. They will be ready in about an hour

title: Gerookte Ham (Smoked Ham)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked raw ham
- cold water

## Directions

1. Weigh the smoked raw ham, brush it well clean, wash it with lukewarm water and soak it for 24 hours in cold water.
2. Place it in boiling water, just enough to cover it and cook it gently (approximately 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g).
3. Test if the ham is done by seeing if the rind comes off easily.
4. Cut it off warm from the part that will be used and keep the rest of the ham under the rind.
5. Serve the ham cold or warm sliced thin, for example with garden beans and brown butter.
6. Note: When cooking only a piece of the ham, hang the remaining part in a linen or brown paper bag in a cool place, where the ham is well protected from flies. The liquid in which ham has been cooked can be used for pea soup, it is usually fatty, more or less salty, but not aromatic.

title: Gerookte Ossentong (Smoked Beef Tongue)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked beef tongue
- lukewarm water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Wash and brush the tongue as clean as possible, then soak it for 24 hours in cold water, changing it repeatedly.
2. Wash it well again with lukewarm water and let it cook gently with plenty of boiling water (approximately 3 to 3 1/2 hours).
3. Test if it is done by seeing if the skin comes off easily.
4. Remove the skin while the tongue is warm and remove the fat and small bones from the throat part.
5. Warm the tongue again in the cooking liquid if necessary and then slice it.
6. Serve young garden beans and brown butter or bleached celery and stewed tomatoes with mashed potatoes with smoked beef tongue.

title: Gerookte Ossentong (Smoked Beef Tongue)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Cold water
- Warm water
- Young garden beans
- Brown butter or celery
- Stewed tomatoes
- Potato purée

## Directions

1. Wash and brush the tongue thoroughly clean.
2. Soak it for 24 hours in cold water, changing the water regularly.
3. Wash it again thoroughly with lukewarm water.
4. Cook it in plenty of boiling water over a gentle fire until tender (approximately 3 to 3½ hours).
5. Test if it is done by checking if the skin comes off easily.
6. Remove the skin while the tongue is still warm and remove the fat and small bones from the throat piece.
7. Warm the tongue again in the cooking liquid if necessary and slice it.
8. Serve with young garden beans and brown butter or celery and stewed tomatoes with potato purée.

title: Gerooste Elft (Roasted Shad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one shad
- butter
- flour
- oil
- capers or gherkins, or sorrel sauce

## Directions

1. After the shad is well cleaned, roll it in butter and flour
2. Roast it on a grill between buttered papers that are folded closed (oil may also be used)
3. Place it on a dish with a sauce of capers or gherkins, or sorrel sauce

title: Geroosterd brood met sardines (Toasted Bread with Sardines)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices stale casino bread
- 6 sardines

## Directions

1. Cut very thin slices of casino bread, remove the crusts and cut them in half
2. Toast them on a baking sheet light brown in the oven
3. Clean the sardines by removing the innards
4. Lay two half sardines on each slice of bread

title: Gerstewater (Barley Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pearl barley
- Water
- Wine or berry juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. For every half ounce of pearl barley, add one can of water
2. Let it cook gently for two hours and strain through a cloth
3. You can then use it with the addition of some wine or berry juice, sweetened with sugar to taste

title: Gestoofd Kalfsvlees (Braised Veal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g lean veal
- 4 dl water with salt
- small carrot
- parsley
- pieces of mace
- pinch of thyme
- 3 white peppercorns
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 3 dl bouillon
- 1 egg yolk (optional)
- 1/2 dl milk
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and cook it in salted water with herbs until tender (approximately 1/2 hour). Cut it into slanting slices and keep these warm in a little bouillon to prevent drying out.
2. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring it to a boil while stirring. Add while stirring 3 dl of the remaining strained bouillon and let the sauce cook while stirring until done (10 minutes).
3. If desired, mix it with the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the milk, and warm the meat slices in it.
4. Put everything on a platter and garnish the dish optionally with mushrooms and fleurons (see recipe 847).

title: Gestoofd Konijn - 2e recept (Stewed Rabbit - Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 80 g flour
- lemon
- pepper
- salt
- 4 dl bouillon or water with salt

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit as indicated in recipe 304.
2. Brown the pieces in the butter or margarine, which has been made light brown in a pan, evenly brown.
3. Place them in a braising pan.
4. Add the flour to the butter or margarine, stir out the lumps and slowly pour the bouillon or water with the salt while stirring, let the sauce cook through while stirring (approximately 10 minutes), add some lemon juice, pour this over the rabbit, place a couple of notched lemon slices in it and stew the meat for another 45 minutes on a low heat, stirring occasionally.

title: Gestoofd Konijn - le recept (Stewed Rabbit - First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- some salt
- piece of mace
- small carrots
- sprig of parsley
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 4 dl bouillon
- some lemon juice
- some salt

## Directions

1. Cut the legs of the rabbit into 2 pieces, divide the body into 5 or 6 pieces, wash them and cook them in salted water with herbs until done (1 hour).
2. Heat butter or margarine and flour, stirring, bring to the boil, add the bouillon in small amounts while stirring, in which the pieces were cooked, and let the sauce cook through while stirring (10 minutes).
