3. Stew the meat in it for another 20 minutes and add lemon juice to taste.

title: Gestoofde aardappelen (Stewed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- bouillon
- butter
- nutmeg or pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes half done
2. Stew them with bouillon, butter and nutmeg or pepper

title: Gestoofde Abrikozen (Stewed Apricots)
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- white wine
- bread sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut them in half and remove the pits.
2. Stew them for a quarter hour with white wine, bread sugar, and cinnamon.

title: Gestoofde Abrikozen (Stewed Apricots)
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- white wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut them in half and remove the pits
2. Stew them for a quarter hour with white wine and sugar

title: Gestoofde Asperges (Stewed Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- sorrel
- butter
- nutmeg
- egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Break off the tips of the asparagus and remove all the woody parts
2. Wash well
3. Cook with the tips
4. Let drain well
5. Stew with melted sorrel, butter and nutmeg
6. If desired, bind the liquid with egg yolks

title: Gestoofde Asperges (Stewed Asparagus)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 kg stewing asparagus
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean the asparagus as described in the previous recipe
2. Break them into pieces of approximately 6 cm length
3. Wash them and cook them in abundant boiling water with a little salt until done (1/2 hour to 3/4 hour)
4. When the asparagus is almost done, make sauce in the ordinary manner
5. Stew the drained asparagus in it (15 minutes) and grate some nutmeg over the dish

title: Gestoofde aubergines (Stewed Eggplants)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small or 2 large eggplants
- 15 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- 2 small onions
- 2.5 dl tomato sauce
- 1.5 tablespoon very finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplants.
2. Cut them into pieces of approximately 5 cm long.
3. Fry the chopped onions in the butter or margarine.
4. Add the tomato sauce (see recipe 340).
5. Stew the eggplant pieces in it for approximately 30 minutes.
6. Add the parsley at the end.

title: Gestoofde Bessen (Stewed Berries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg berries
- approximately 100 g sugar
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the berries, remove them and let them cook on gentle heat, shaking occasionally, for 10 minutes.
2. Add the sugar and bind the liquid with some mixed sago.

title: Gestoofde Biet (Stewed Beetroot)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beetroot
- onions
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Slice cooked beetroot and onions
2. Stew with a little water, vinegar, pepper and a small piece of butter
3. After stewing one hour, pour the liquid into a small pan
4. Stir in a little flour and cook a bound sauce from it
5. Pour the sauce over the dish
6. If using raw onions, place them at the bottom of the pan and stew for two hours

title: Gestoofde Bleekselderij (Braised Celery)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bunches celery
- 2.5 dl milk or celery cooking water
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the white and tender celery stalks into pieces of 6-8 cm length, peel the broad pieces and scrape the narrow pieces.
2. Wash them and place them with little boiling water and salt.
3. Cook them gently until tender (3/4 to 1 hour) and let them drain in a colander, but if desired catch the liquid.
4. Prepare the sauce in the manner as for cauliflower sauce.

title: Gestoofde bleekselderij (Stewed Pale Celery)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bunches celery
- 2.5 dl milk or celery stock
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the blanched and tender celery stalks into pieces of 6 to 8 cm length, peel the broad pieces and scrape the narrow pieces.
2. Wash them and place them in a pot with little boiling water and salt.
3. Cook them gently until tender (3/4 to 1 hour) and let them drain in a colander, optionally catching the liquid.
4. Prepare the sauce as indicated for cauliflower sauce (recipe 328), let it cook until done (10 minutes) and stew the celery in it (approximately 10 minutes).

title: Gestoofde bloemkool (Stewed Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- rainwater
- butter
- flour
- mace
- salt
- bouillon or sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned cabbage into large pieces
2. Cook it in rainwater, but especially not too much
3. Let it drain on a colander or place it in cold water to keep it white
4. Make a sauce of butter, flour and some mace, a little salt and bouillon or sweet milk
5. Stir it well together and let the cabbage stew in it

title: Gestoofde Champignons en Cantharellen (Braised Mushrooms and Chanterelles)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 500 g mushrooms or chanterelles
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- a few drops of lemon juice
- flour
- a few drops of milk

## Directions

1. Cut or break off the woody base parts of the stems. (Also brush these bits clean and use them to make some mushroom broth.)
2. Brush and scrape the mushrooms clean, cut them in half if they are large, wash and rinse them repeatedly until no sand remains.
3. Place the mushrooms on very low heat with butter or margarine, salt and a few drops of lemon juice.
4. Let them cook slowly (approximately 20 minutes).
5. Bind the liquid that has formed on the mushrooms with flour that has been mixed with milk and adjust the seasoning if needed.

title: Gestoofde duiven (Stewed Doves)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- bouillon or jus
- wine
- onions
- cloves
- lemon juice
- doves

## Directions

1. Brown butter and flour.
2. Add bouillon or jus, a glass of wine, two sliced onions, crushed cloves and lemon juice.
3. Cut the doves into pieces, place everything in a pan and let it stew for an hour.

title: Gestoofde eendvogels (Stewed Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- anchovies
- verjus
- morels
- spices
- lemon peel
- salt
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Cook the ducks half done.
2. Place them in a pan and add anchovies, a glass of verjus, some morels, spices, lemon peel, salt and bouillon.
3. Cover well and let them stew further. In this way all poultry can be stewed.

title: Gestoofde garnalen (Stewed Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimp
- salt
- parsley
- butter
- wine

## Directions

1. Cook the shrimp
2. Put them on a colander and scatter salt over them
3. Peel them
4. Chop them together with a handful of parsley
5. Stew them gently with butter in a pan
6. Add some wine and let them stew

title: Gestoofde Haas (Stewed Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- butter
- flour
- jus
- cloves
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- sugar
- bay leaves
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cut the hare into pieces and wash thoroughly
2. Brown some butter and flour, add jus, cloves, finely chopped onions, pepper, salt, sugar, some bay leaves and lemon peel
3. Place the hare in this and let it braise thoroughly

title: Gestoofde hoenders (Stewed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- water
- butter
- veal bouillon
- rusks (finely ground)
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- grated lemon zest
- flour
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens into pieces and cook them in water until tender, skimming well.
2. Take the necessary amount of butter and brown it.
3. Stir in veal bouillon, finely ground rusks, finely sliced onions, pepper, salt, nutmeg, grated lemon zest, flour and chopped parsley.
4. Place the chicken pieces in this mixture and let them stew until done.

title: Gestoofde Kabeljauw (Stewed Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces
2. Wash them clean
3. Let them cook in boiling water with sufficient salt, skimming well
4. When it has boiled over three times, it is done
5. Cook the liver separately
6. Serve with creamed butter with mustard

title: Gestoofde Kabeljauwstaart (Braised Cod Tail)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cod tail
- salt
- water
- pepper
- nutmeg
- crushed biscuit
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let the tail hang one day and night in open air or in a clear cellar
2. Place it in a deep tin dish
3. Sprinkle salt over it and add enough water so it is well covered
4. Cook until done
5. Pour off the water leaving two teacups worth
6. Split the tail open and remove the bone
7. Add pepper, nutmeg, crushed biscuit and lemon juice
8. Apply heat above and below and let it braise thus

title: Gestoofde Kabeljauwstaart (Braised Cod Tail)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cod tail
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 1 lemon
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- 1 Maggi's Bouillon cube
- water
- potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. METHOD 1: Remove the backbone from the tail by loosening it along the entire length and pulling it out, or by cutting the fish open along one side and removing the backbone. Place the scaled, washed and salted fish in a fireproof dish with some salted water in which lemon juice has been added and in which, if desired, 1 Maggi's Bouillon cube has been dissolved. Distribute the butter or margarine in pieces on top and let the fish braise in a moderately warm oven, covered with a lid, until almost cooked (approximately 30 minutes). Baste it from time to time with the liquid. When the fish is almost cooked, sprinkle some breadcrumbs over it, lay a few thin slices of lemon on top and let it brown and cook without a lid.
2. METHOD 2: First cook the tail whole until almost cooked and then braise it in a fireproof dish. The braising can be completed more quickly in this case than in the first method, but it is not advisable to place boiled potatoes with the fish, as these might not cook through.

title: Gestoofde Kabeljauwstaart (Stewed Cod Tail)
body:
## Ingredients

- cod tail
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Let the tail hang in the open air or in a clear cellar for a day and night
2. Place it in a deep tin plate
3. Sprinkle some salt over it and add enough water so that it lies well covered

title: Gestoofde Kalfscoteletten (Braised Veal Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Crushed rusks
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Treat as fried veal chops, but instead of frying them, add a lemon cut into slices and let them braise slowly until done

title: Gestoofde Kalfsnier (Stewed Veal Kidney)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 veal kidneys or 3 pork kidneys or 1 beef kidney
- 4 dl lean gravy with water or bouillon
- some flour
- bay leaf
- 2 cloves
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 100 g onion (1 large)
- 30 g flour
- 60 g butter or margarine
- parsley or English sauce and soya

## Directions

1. Soak the kidneys for several hours in cold water, remove the fat and slice them diagonally.
2. After sprinkling the slices with pepper and salt, roll them in some flour and fry them brown in the browned butter or margarine.
3. Remove them from the frying pan.
4. Add the flour and the minced onion to the fat and fry this light brown.
5. Add the warm bouillon, stirring.
6. Add the spices and let the sauce simmer briefly.
7. Stew the kidney pieces in it for half an hour.
8. Stir from time to time to prevent burning.
9. Add, if desired, at the last moment some very finely chopped parsley or English sauce and soya and remove the spices from the ragout.

title: Gestoofde Kersen (Stewed Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pluck the stems from the cherries and remove the pits.
2. Cook them with sugar, skimming well.
3. Remove the cherries from the liquid.
4. Rasp lemon peel into the liquid and cook it into a syrup.
5. Pour this syrup over the cherries when serving.

title: Gestoofde Kersen (Stewed Cherries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cherries
- approximately 100 g sugar
- 1 1/2 dl water
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the cherries, remove the stems and pit them, but make sure all the juice is caught.
2. Set them on with the water, sugar and juice and let them cook gently for 5 minutes.
3. Bind the liquid with a little mixed sago.

title: Gestoofde Komkommers (Braised Cucumbers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cucumbers
- 1/4 litre of liquid from the cucumbers or 2 dl liquid from the cucumbers and approximately 1/2 dl vinegar
- 20 g flour or 10 g cornstarch
- 20 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers thinly, remove the bitter ends if necessary and cut them lengthwise into quarters.
2. Remove the seeds with a silver spoon and divide the cucumbers into pieces of approximately 5 cm length.
3. Let them cook tender in as little boiling water as possible (15 to 20 minutes), place them in a colander to drain, but catch the liquid.
4. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as cauliflower sauce or cornstarch sauce (see recipe 328 or recipe 329), but use the cucumber liquid, preferably mixed with some vinegar.
5. Braise the cucumbers in it for another approximately 10 minutes.

title: Gestoofde Komkommers of Augurken (Stewed Cucumbers or Gherkins)
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow cucumbers
- butter
- vinegar
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and cut them into long strips
2. Stew them in a pan with some butter and vinegar
3. When serving, bind the liquid with a couple of egg yolks

title: Gestoofde kropsalade (Stewed Lettuce Heads)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce heads
- bouillon
- butter
- mace
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Rinse the heads in soft water and let them drain thoroughly on a colander
2. Put them in a pan with bouillon, butter and ground mace
3. Let them stew for a quarter hour
4. When serving, take the heads out and place them on a plate
5. Pour the remaining liquid, mixed with some egg yolks, over them

title: Gestoofde Kruisbessen (Stewed Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- a little water
- sugar
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the berries with a little water until done.
2. Let them drain and stew them with sugar.
3. Afterward, add beaten eggs.

title: Gestoofde Kruisbessen (Stewed Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- water
- sugar
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the berries with a little water until done
2. Let them drain and stew them with some sugar
3. Add beaten eggs afterward

title: Gestoofde kuikens (Stewed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- water
- cooked parsley
- pepper
- salt
- white wine
- egg yolks (5)
- nutmeg
- vinegar
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the squabs into quarters.
2. Place them in a pan with water so they are covered.
3. Stew them with a handful of cooked parsley, pepper, salt and white wine.
4. Add 5 egg yolks, nutmeg, vinegar and a lump of butter.
5. Let everything come to a boil together.

title: Gestoofde Mosselen (Braised Mussels)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter or margarine
- flour
- mussel water
- a splash of milk

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels as indicated in recipe 195.
2. Make a sauce from butter, flour, mussel water and a splash of milk according to recipe 351.
3. Braise the mussels, which have been removed from their shells, in this sauce.

title: Gestoofde Mosselen (Braised Mussels)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- flour
- mussel water
- milk

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels as indicated in recipe 195
2. Make a sauce from butter, flour, mussel water and a splash of milk according to recipe 351
3. Braise the mussels, which have been removed from their shells, in this sauce

title: Gestoofde oesters (Stewed Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- oyster liquor
- butter
- hardtack
- nutmeg
- salt
- water
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the beards from the oysters
2. Lay them in a pan with their own liquor, some butter, hardtack, nutmeg, salt and water
3. Let them stew
4. Finish with egg yolks
5. Squeeze lemon juice over them

title: Gestoofde Osselapjes (Braised Beef Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef slices
- Pepper
- Salt
- Water
- Fine onions
- Cloves
- Ground rusks

## Directions

1. Beat the slices well to tenderize and rub them in with pepper and salt
2. Lay them in an earthenware pan with some water
3. Put fire above and below and braise them for a good half hour
4. Sprinkle them with fine onions, cloves and ground rusks
5. Baste them constantly with the jus and braise them for another half hour

title: Gestoofde Ossennieren (Stewed Ox Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox kidney
- 1 hectogram butter
- salt
- pepper
- 25 decagram flour
- white wine (optional)
- 2 deciliters water
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the ox kidney lengthwise.
2. Give each piece a cut to the depth of 5 streep.
3. Let the butter melt in the sauté pan and when well melted, put the kidney in the pan and add salt and pepper.
4. Shake it every minute to make it cook evenly.
5. After 6 minutes of stewing, drain the kidney in a colander.
6. Put the kidney back in the pan and sprinkle it with flour, let it stew for 2 minutes.
7. Add a little white wine if desired and in any case 2 deciliters water with some Liebig's Meat Extract.
8. Let it cook a little longer together and add a tablespoon of chopped parsley.

title: Gestoofde Paling (Braised Eel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg eel
- 75 g butter or margarine
- lemon
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. METHOD 1: Clean the eel in the usual way (see cooked eel recipe 190), wash the pieces and rub them with pepper and salt. Place the butter or margarine in small lumps in the fireproof dish, sprinkle some breadcrumbs over it and lay a few thin slices of lemon on top. Let the eel in a moderately warm oven, first covered, then without a lid, cook and brown (25-30 minutes). If you do not have an oven, braise the eel in a flat braising pan over a gentle fire. Baste the eel repeatedly with a little of the liquid.
2. METHOD 2: First cook the cleaned eel in boiling salted water (approximately 5 minutes, depending on the thickness of the eel). Then place the pieces in the fireproof dish and treat them further as braised fish (recipe 219).

title: Gestoofde Peren (Stewed Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears, peeled
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- a splash of red wine or berry juice
- cochineal (optional, for color)

## Directions

1. Stew the peeled pears with water, sugar, cinnamon, and a splash of red wine or berry juice.
2. For a nice color, cochineal may be added.

title: Gestoofde Peren (Stewed Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- red wine or fruit juice
- cochineal

## Directions

1. Stew the peeled pears with water, sugar, cinnamon and a splash of red wine or fruit juice
2. For a nice color, one can add some cochineal

title: Gestoofde Peren (Stewed Pears)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg stewing pears
- approximately 50 g sugar
- 3/4 dl berry juice (optional)
- peel of 1/2 lemon or piece of cinnamon stick
- some sago

## Directions

1. Peel the pears thinly, cut them in half or quarters if they are large and remove the cores.
2. Leave small pears whole, but in this case peel the stems thinly.
3. Wash them and set them on with enough boiling water so they are half submerged, with the lemon peel or cinnamon stick.
4. Let them cook gently, shaking occasionally, for 3 to 4 hours.
5. If the pears do not cook red, add berry juice and sugar half an hour before they are done.
6. If the fruits do cook red, let only some sugar cook with them for the last 1/4 hour.
7. Remove the flavoring agent and bind the liquid with some mixed sago.

title: Gestoofde Pisang (Stewed Bananas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 bananas
- 2 1/2 dl water
- juice of 1 lemon
- approximately 60 g sugar
- optionally a splash of Rhine wine
- some sago

## Directions

1. Peel the bananas, cut them lengthwise in half, then cut each half into 4 pieces.
2. Bring the water with the lemon juice to a boil, place the pieces in it and let them cook until completely soft (7-10 minutes).
3. Remove them carefully from the liquid, add the wine and bind it with some mixed sago.
4. Pour it over the banana and serve this cold.

title: Gestoofde Pisang (Stewed Bananas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pisangs
- 2½ dl water
- juice of 1 lemon
- 60 g sugar
- a splash of Rhine wine (optional)
- some sago

## Directions

1. Peel the pisangs, cut them lengthwise in half, then cut each half into 4 pieces.
2. Bring the water with the lemon juice to a boil, lay the pieces in it and let them cook until completely soft (7 to 10 minutes).
3. Remove them carefully from the liquid, add the wine to the liquid and thicken it with some mixed sago.
4. Pour it over the pisang and serve this cold.

title: Gestoofde Prei (Braised Leeks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bunches leeks, or approximately 2 kg
- 2.5 dl leek cooking water
- 0.5 dl vinegar
- 30 g flour or 15-20 g Duryea corn starch
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the leeks by cutting off the outer leaves and the roots and a piece of the stalk.
2. Cut only the white and yellow parts into pieces of 4-5 cm length.
3. Rinse them several times in plenty of water and cook them in boiling water with salt (approximately 1/2 hour).
4. Let them drain in a colander, catching the liquid.
5. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328) or corn starch sauce (see recipe 329), but use the leek cooking water and vinegar instead of milk.
6. Braise the vegetables in this sauce for approximately 15 minutes, if desired add them to the egg beaten in the bowl.
7. If desired, prepare the leeks according to the second preparation method mentioned above for onions.

title: Gestoofde Pruimen (Stewed Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- water
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Remove the stones, wash the plums, and let them drain.
2. Stew them on a gentle fire with water, sugar, and a lemon peel.

title: Gestoofde Schol (Braised Plaice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plaice
- salt
- butter
- lemon
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cut the plaice into pieces and wash them well
2. Place them on a dish, sprinkle with salt and add a good piece of butter, along with some lemon and lemon peel
3. Let them braise gently until done

title: Gestoofde selderijknol (Stewed Celeriac)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 celeriac roots
- 2.5 dl milk
- 20 to 30 g flour or 15 g corn flour (Duryea)
- 20 to 30 g butter or margarine
- salt and nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the celeriac into fairly thick slices, peel them and cut them into strips.
2. Wash them and cook them until tender in plenty of boiling salted water (approximately 1/2 hour).
3. Let them drain in a colander as dry as possible and stew them for 10 minutes in the sauce made from butter, flour and milk or celeriac stock, prepared in the same way as cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328) or corn flour sauce (see recipe 329).
4. Grate some nutmeg over it in the dish or sprinkle some celery salt over it.

title: Gestoofde sla (Braised Lettuce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg braising lettuce
- 2 1/2 deciliters milk (or liquid from the lettuce)
- 25 g flour or 15 g Duryea maizena
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut away the withered leaves and a piece of the stem.
2. Cut the heads, starting from the stem, into thin strips.
3. Wash them repeatedly until completely clear water comes off.
4. Prepare the lettuce further exactly as endive (recipe 426).

title: Gestoofde Snippen (Stewed Snipes)
body:
## Ingredients

- snipes
- red wine
- giblets
- breadcrumb
- butter
- crushed cloves
- brown sugar
- wine
- capers

## Directions

1. Gut the snipes and let them stew for a quarter hour in a little red wine
2. Fry the giblets finely and mix them with crumbled bread
3. Braise them brown in butter
4. Add crushed cloves, brown sugar and wine
5. Let everything stew well with capers until done

title: Gestoofde Snoek (Braised Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pike
- salt
- water
- butter
- flour
- anchovies
- lemon peel
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the pike into pieces and salt them, not too harshly
2. Cook them in water
3. Make a sauce of butter, a little flour, some anchovies with bones removed, a lemon peel, pepper and a glass of water
4. Let these cook together, then add the pike and let everything braise well together, adding lemon juice from time to time

title: Gestoofde Snoek, Anders (Braised Pike, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pike
- wine
- water
- butter
- one spoon grated North Holland cheese
- capers
- pepper
- grated white bread
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the pike first and place it in a dish with some wine and as much water as needed
2. Add butter, one spoon grated North Holland cheese, capers, pepper, grated white bread and lemon juice
3. Let it braise well

title: Gestoofde Stokvis (Braised Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- beaten dried cod
- water
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- flour
- butter
- finely cut onions
- nutmeg
- crushed biscuit

## Directions

1. Soak the beaten dried cod one night in water
2. Cook it well and remove the bone
3. Place it in a pan with finely cut salt, pepper, mustard, flour, butter, finely cut onions, nutmeg and some crushed biscuit
4. Braise until the onions are well done

title: Gestoofde Uien (Braised Onions)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Just over 1 kg onions
- 2.5 dl onion cooking water
- 0.5 dl vinegar
- 30 g flour or 15-20 g Duryea corn starch
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the onions, wash them and cook them in plenty of boiling water with salt (3/4 to 1 hour).
2. Let them drain in a colander.
3. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as cauliflower sauce or corn starch sauce (see recipe 328 or recipe 329), but use onion cooking water and vinegar instead of milk.
4. Braise the onions in this sauce for approximately 10 minutes.
5. Alternatively: Clean the onions in the usual way, wash them and place them on a very low heat with little water, butter or margarine, vinegar, salt and pepper. Let them cook gently (approximately 1/2 hour) and thicken the liquid with some mixed flour or Duryea corn starch.

title: Gestoofde Vis (Braised Fish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked fish
- diluted fish water, salted water or fish bouillon
- lemon juice
- Maggi's Aroma (optional)
- lemon slices
- butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Use leftover cooked fish, remove the skin while the fish is still warm
2. Take the fish flesh in pieces as large as possible from the bone and lay it in a heat-proof dish
3. Add enough diluted fish water, water with salt or fish bouillon so the bottom is covered
4. Drizzle with lemon juice and if desired a little Maggi's Aroma
5. Lay a couple of sliced lemon slices and here and there a good piece of butter or margarine on top
6. Sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, clean the rim of the dish
7. Let the fish braise gently in a moderately warm oven and become light brown
8. Baste it from time to time with the liquid

title: Gestoofde Zoete Appelen (Stewed Sweet Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the apples into quarters and remove the cores
2. Stew them with some butter, sugar and cinnamon

title: Gestoomde Gort (Steamed Barley)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g Alkmaar barley
- 4 1/2 dl water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the barley until the water that runs off is completely clear.
2. Soak it for 24 hours in 2 1/2 times as much cold water as barley and some salt.
3. Set the bowl with barley in a pan with enough boiling water so that it comes just below the rim of the bowl.
4. Put the lid on the pan and let the water boil until the barley is done (approximately 2 to 2 1/2 hours), but make sure the water in the pan is refilled meanwhile.

title: Gestoomde rijst (Steamed Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 3 1/2 dl water
- wat zout

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst zéér goed uitlekken.
3. Doe hem in een kom met + 1 1/2 maal zoveel koud water als rijst en wat zout.
4. Zet de kom in een pan met zoveel kokend water, dat dit even onder de rand van de kom staat.
5. Leg het deksel op de pan en laat het water koken, tot de rijst gaar is (+ 1 uur), maar zorg, dat het water niet in de kom kan koken en intussen zo nodig telkens bijgevuld wordt.

title: Getruffeerde Duif (Truffle-Stuffed Pigeon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pigeons
- 120 g veal forcemeat
- 4 truffels or some mushrooms
- some old bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1 1/2 tablespoon Madeira (optional)
- 75 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Mix the forcemeat in the usual way, but stir in the chopped truffels, which may be soaked in Madeira if desired.
2. Carefully loosen the breast skin of the cleaned pigeons with your finger and fill the space between the breast meat and skin with the mixed forcemeat (not too stiff!).
3. Sew the skin closed again, salt the pigeons and roast them in the usual way (see recipe 318) until brown and cooked through.
4. Serve them with salad or compote.

title: Getruffeerde Fazant (Truffle-Stuffed Pheasant)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- 150 g veal forcemeat
- 20 g old bread without crust
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 truffels
- 1/4 dl Madeira (optional)
- 150 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see: truffle-stuffed turkey (recipe 324).
2. Allow 1 to 1 1/2 hours as roasting time.

title: Getruffeerde Fazant (Truffled Pheasant)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- 150 g ground veal
- 20 g old bread without crust
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 truffles
- 1/4 dl Madeira (optional)
- 150 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. For preparation see: Truffled turkey (recipe 324).
2. Allow 1¾ to 2 hours roasting time.

title: Getruffeerde Kalkoen (Truffle-Stuffed Turkey)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- 250 g veal forcemeat
- 250 g pork forcemeat
- 1 egg
- 50 g old bread without crust
- 1/2 can truffels
- 1/2 dl Madeira (optional)
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 250 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash the turkey thoroughly inside and outside.
2. Mix the forcemeat in the usual way, but stir in the truffels cut into small pieces, which may be soaked in Madeira if desired.
3. Carefully loosen the breast skin of the turkey, but make sure it does not tear anywhere.
4. Fill the space between the breast meat and skin with the forcemeat (not too stiff!).
5. Sew the skin closed again, salt the turkey and roast it as indicated in the previous recipe.

title: Getruffeerde Varkenspoten (Truffled Pork Trotters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pork trotters
- 150 g ground veal
- 50 g old bread
- salt and nutmeg
- 1/2 tin of truffles or truffle peelings
- 150 g pork caul
- frying fat
- breadcrumbs for coating
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Rinse the trotters with lukewarm water and let them cook in boiling salted water until tender (approximately 2 hours)
2. Carefully remove all bones and chop or grind the meat as finely as possible
3. Stir the ground veal in a saucepan over a small fire until it turns gray
4. Cut the truffles or truffle peelings, which may optionally have been soaked in Madeira, into small square pieces and mix them with the ground meat, the soaked bread, the meat from the trotters, the salt and nutmeg
5. Form this mixture into elongated rolls (2 by 8 cm)
6. Roll each one in a piece of pork caul that has first been soaked in lukewarm water
7. Double coat them and fry them in steaming frying fat or salad oil
8. Let them drain on gray paper
9. Place them against a center piece of toasted bread
10. Garnish the dish with fresh parsley sprigs and serve with salad

title: Gevulde beschuitbollen (Filled Rusk Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 rusk rolls or other small rolls
- approximately 8 small sour apples
- 50-60 g sugar
- 25 g currants
- fine cinnamon
- 40-50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook applesauce in the usual way, mix it with 35-40 g of the sugar and the well-washed currants.
2. Cut the rusk rolls in half, hollow them out, fill them with applesauce and press both halves firmly together.
3. Brush the rolls on the outside with melted butter or margarine.
4. Roll them through the rest of the sugar mixed with fine cinnamon, lay them on a baking sheet and let them become warm and crispy in a moderately warm oven.

title: Gevulde boterkoek (Filled Butter Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For the dough: 500 g flour, 400 g butter or margarine, 400 g sugar, 1.5 eggs
- For the filling: 125 g almonds, 125 g sugar, 1.5 eggs, finely grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Knead butter or margarine, flour, sugar and 1.5 eggs into a firm ball.
2. Divide the dough in half and press one half into a greased cake pan.
3. Grind the peeled almonds finely, mix them with sugar, the beaten egg and the grated lemon zest and pass the almond mixture through the mill once more.
4. Place the almond mixture on the dough in the pan, spread the other half of the dough over it and bake the cake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done (1 to 1.5 hours).
5. Check if it is done by inserting a knitting needle; it should come out dry.
6. Turn the cake out onto a cake rack and let it cool.

title: Gevulde Broodjes (Stuffed Bread Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 small bread rolls
- 150 g cold meat
- 1½ dl milk, broth or jus with water
- 15 g flour
- 15 g butter or margarine or 7 g Duryea cornstarch
- 15 g butter or margarine
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 40 g butter or margarine to brush

## Directions

1. Cut the top cap off the bread rolls, hollow them out and brush the inside with some melted butter or margarine.
2. Chop the meat and the washed parsley finely and stir this with the seasonings through the sauce, which is made like that for 'meat in shells' (recipe 102).
3. Fill the bread rolls with this mixture and place the hollowed-out and brushed lids on them.
4. Brush the entire outside with melted butter or margarine and let the bread rolls become warm and crispy on a baking sheet or in a roasting pan in a moderately warm oven, but make sure they do not become too brown.
5. Note: One can also use shrimp instead of meat. Preparation and quantity remain the same in that case.

title: Gevulde eieren (1e recept) (Stuffed Eggs, 1st Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 50 g butter or margarine
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs hard (8 to 10 minutes), place them in cold water, peel them, cut them crosswise in the middle and remove the yolks
2. Cut off the tips of the half whites so they can stand
3. Rub the yolks completely fine, gradually add the butter or margarine beaten to cream and as much mustard, pepper, chopped parsley and salt as needed until a well-flavored mixture is obtained
4. Fill the half whites with this so that the yolks stick up a bit and the shape of a whole egg is obtained
5. Place the eggs on the plate
6. Note: Filling is easiest by piping the mixture with a piping bag with fluted nozzle into the half whites

title: Gevulde eieren met ansjovis (3e recept) (Stuffed Eggs with Anchovies, 3rd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 8 anchovies
- pepper
- salt
- capers

## Directions

1. Treat the eggs as indicated in recipe 29
2. Rub the yolks completely fine, gradually add the butter or margarine beaten to cream and the anchovy purée, which is made by pressing 6 soaked and cleaned anchovies through a hair sieve
3. Stir in enough pepper (and salt) until a tasty mixture is obtained
4. Fill the half whites with this so that the yolks stick up a bit and the shape of a whole egg is obtained
5. Garnish them with thin strips of the remaining anchovy and capers
6. Note: See note under recipe 29

title: Gevulde eieren met Parmezaanse kaas (2e recept) (Stuffed Eggs with Parmesan Cheese, 2nd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 30 g grated cheese
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Treat the eggs as indicated in recipe 29
2. Rub the yolks completely fine, gradually add the butter or margarine beaten to cream, the grated cheese, pepper and salt to taste
3. Fill the half whites with this so that the yolks stick up a bit and the shape of a whole egg is obtained
4. Note: See note under recipe 29

title: Gevulde kalfsborst (Stuffed Veal Breast for 3 Days)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg veal breast (without bone)
- 500 g ground veal
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 75 g old bread
- 1/2 tin truffles or truffle peelings
- 200 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Prepare the ground meat in the usual manner, but stir in finely cut truffles
2. Cut the veal breast with a sharp knife along one length side between the membranes open, wash the meat, spread the ground meat between the layers and sew the meat closed
3. Rub it with salt and braise it as indicated for veal fricandeau (35-40 minutes per 500 g)
4. Finish the jus with water if necessary
5. Cut the meat with a very sharp knife into thin slices

title: Gevulde kip (Stuffed Chicken)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kip (chicken)
- 100 g kalfsgehakt (veal mince)
- 2 truffels (truffles)
- peper (pepper)
- zout (salt)
- nootmuskaat (nutmeg)
- 15 g oud brood (old bread)
- 100 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- Madeira (optionally)

## Directions

1. Meng het gehakt op de gewone wijze aan, maar roer er de gehakte truffels, die desverkiezende in wat Madeira geweekt zijn, door.
2. Maak het borstvel der gewassen kippen voorzichtig los en vul de ruimte tussen het borstbeen en het vel met het aangemengde gehakt (niet te stijf).
3. Naai het vel dan weer dicht, zout de kip en braad ze op de gewone wijze (zie recept 309) bruin en gaar.
4. Maak de jus met wat water af, giet hem over de kip, die op een schaal gelegd is, en bind om de pootjes een manchetje.

title: Gevulde Komkommers (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 small cucumbers
- 200 g ground pork meat
- 35 g old bread
- 20 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut off the bitter ends if necessary and cut them in half lengthwise.
2. Remove the seeds with a spoon and cook them in abundant boiling salted water until done (10-20 minutes).
3. In the meantime, mix the ground meat with soaked bread, pepper, salt and nutmeg.
4. Melt the butter or margarine in the pan, add the meat and let it cook, stirring occasionally, until done but not brown.
5. Fill the cucumbers with it, place them in a fireproof dish, pour bouillon or lean jus with water over them, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, place pieces of butter or margarine here and there, clean the edges and let them brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Gevulde Kool (Stuffed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- string
- veal, pork or beef mince
- butter
- nutmeg
- bouillon or jus

## Directions

1. Hollow out the cabbage and tie it with string to prevent it from falling apart
2. Cook it a couple of times
3. Place a ball of veal, pork or beef mince in the opening
4. Stew with butter, nutmeg and a little bouillon or jus
5. Cut into four pieces when serving

title: Gevulde kool (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Savoy cabbage (medium-sized)
- 200 g veal mince
- 200 g pork mince
- 100 g old bread
- pepper, salt, nutmeg to taste
- 40 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs
- Maggi's Aroma

## Directions

1. Carefully remove all leaves from the cabbage, wash them and cook them for 15 to 20 minutes in plenty of boiling salted water.
2. Let them drain in a colander.
3. Meanwhile, prepare the mince in the usual way, but make sure it is not too dry, as it becomes difficult to handle.
4. Place a washed white cloth in a colander or deep bowl, carefully arrange a layer of the largest cabbage leaves in it and spread a layer of mince over it.
5. Place another layer of leaves on top, then mince again and continue in this way until everything is used.
6. Bind the cloth tightly with string around the cabbage and cook it for 1.5 hours in plenty of boiling salted water, but make sure a plate lies at the bottom of the pan to prevent burning.
7. Remove the cabbage from the cloth, place it with the smooth side up in a fireproof small dish, pour some bouillon or jus with water over it.
8. Season to taste with Maggi's Aroma, sprinkle breadcrumbs over the cabbage, place pieces of butter or margarine on top and let the cabbage become light brown in the oven.

title: Gevulde Meloen (Filled Melon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 melon
- 4 mandarins
- 3 slices pineapple
- 2 pears
- 10 apricots
- 20 raspberries
- 20 pitted cherries
- 3 bananas
- Approximately 100 g sugar (to taste)

## Directions

1. Cut a cap off the melon at 2/3 of its height.
2. Remove the seeds and scoop out the fruit flesh from the melon with a silver spoon.
3. Divide this into chunks.
4. Let part of the fruit flesh stand for one hour, with the mandarin segments, the pineapple pieces, pieces of pear, the apricots, raspberries, cherries, banana slices and the sugar.
5. Fill the hollowed melon with the fruit mixture before serving and place the hollowed cap on top.
6. Whipped cream can be served with this.

title: Gevulde olijven (Stuffed Olives)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 olives
- 6 anchovies
- 40 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Clean the anchovies as indicated in recipe 17
2. Press them through a hair sieve
3. Mix the resulting purée with the butter or margarine beaten to cream
4. Cut the olives open far enough so that the pits are easy to remove
5. Fill them with the anchovy butter, set them, returned to their original form, in a section of the plate
6. As a variation, use some olives filled with pimento

title: Gevulde Paprika's (Stuffed Peppers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 red or green peppers
- 150 g veal mince
- 150 g beef mince
- 1 onion
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 3 dl tomato sauce or 3 dl paprika liquid
- 1/2 Maggi's Justa tablet
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- 20 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the peppers. Cut the peppers lengthwise down the middle or cut off a cap. Remove the seeds and seed membranes (these are sharp in taste). Wash the peppers.
2. Cook them for approximately 10 minutes in boiling salted water.
3. Mix the mince with the chopped onion and the seasonings. Cook it through but not brown with some butter or margarine in a frying pan. Fill the peppers with this mixture.
4. Place them in an ovenproof dish. Pour the liquid over them; either the tomato sauce (see recipe 340) or the Maggi's Justa tablet prepared with paprika liquid, first finely grated, mixed and brought to a boil, by which the sauce is thickened.
5. Sprinkle some breadcrumbs over it.
6. Place small knobs of butter or margarine here and there and let the dish in a moderately warm oven get a brown crust.
7. Serve with rice or macaroni.

title: Gevulde Paprika's (Stuffed Peppers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 red or green peppers
- 150 g ground calf meat
- 150 g ground beef meat
- 1 onion
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 3 dl tomato sauce or 3 dl pepper broth
- 1/2 Maggi's bouillon tablet
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- 20 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Remove the stem from the fruits.
2. Cut the peppers in half lengthwise or cut off a cap.
3. Remove the seeds and seed membranes (these have a sharp taste).
4. Wash the fruits.
5. Cook them for approximately 10 minutes in boiling salted water.
6. Mix the ground meat with the chopped onion and spices.
7. Cook it but do not brown it with some butter or margarine in the pan.
8. Fill the fruits with this mixture.
9. Place them in a fireproof dish.
10. Pour the liquid over them, either the tomato sauce or pepper broth.

title: Gevulde selderijknol (Stuffed Celeriac)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small celeriac roots
- 150 g veal mince
- 150 g pork mince
- 150 g old bread
- pepper, salt, nutmeg to taste
- 40 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs
- lean jus with water or bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel the roots, wash them and cook them until tender in plenty of boiling salted water (3/4 to 1 hour).
2. Cut off the top cap and hollow them out.
3. Meanwhile, prepare the mince in the usual way with the soaked bread and the spices.
4. Melt 20 g butter or margarine in the pan, add the mince and keep stirring until it is cooked (approximately 10 minutes), but make sure it does not brown.
5. Fill the celeriac roots with it, replace the lids and place them in a fireproof dish.
6. Add some lean jus with water or bouillon, sprinkle some breadcrumbs over it, place a piece of butter or margarine on each root, clean the edge of the dish and let a light brown crust form on it in a moderately warm oven.

title: Gevulde Sinaasappelen (Filled Oranges)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 5 oranges
- juice of 2 lemons, together 4 dl, optionally made up with some water
- 75 g sugar
- 15 g red gelatin, optionally 1/2 dl Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Use oranges with attractive skin for this dish, cut them in half crosswise and press them out gently without damaging the skin.
2. Mix the juice with the lemon juice, sugar and gelatin, which has been soaked in cold water for 10 minutes, and warm it until the gelatin and sugar are dissolved.
3. In the meantime, clean the inside of the orange shells, remove the white fibers, wash them inside and out and fill them with the strained liquid.
4. It is sufficient to fill 8 half shells with this gelée.
5. Let them set cold and firm.
6. Optionally garnish the edges with whipped cream mixed with some superfine sugar and place a French fruit in the middle, or cut them in half with a damp warm knife.
7. Note: The amount of sugar depends on the acidity of the fruit. Taste the juice first before adding sugar.

title: Gevulde Sinaasappelen (Stuffed Oranges)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange
- 1 banana
- 12 cherries (50 g)
- ⅛ bottle whipped cream (for 6 oranges)

## Directions

1. Halve the cleaned orange and hollow it out. Peel the banana and beat it foamy. Pit and wash the cherries. Mix the orange flesh with the banana mash and the cherries. Carefully remove the white fibers from the orange skins and clean them well. Fill them with the mixture and garnish with whipped cream and a cherry.

title: Gevulde Tomaten (Stuffed Tomatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large tomatoes
- 150 to 200 g ground calf meat
- 20 g old bread or breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 25 g onion (optional)
- parsley
- 25 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the tomatoes, wash them, dip them briefly in hot water, remove the skin, cut off the top cap and hollow them out carefully.
2. Mix the ground meat in the usual way with soaked old bread, pepper, salt and nutmeg, optionally mixed with finely chopped onion and parsley.
3. Melt 10 g butter or margarine in the pan and cook the meat in it, stirring constantly, until done (10-15 minutes), but ensure it does not brown.
4. Fill the tomatoes so the meat protrudes slightly above the rim.
5. Place them in a fireproof dish with a piece of butter or margarine on each tomato.
6. Press the fruit flesh and caps through a sieve and add this with water, lemon juice and salt to the tomatoes.
7. Let the tomatoes, sprinkled with breadcrumbs, stew in a mild oven for 15-20 minutes until done.

title: Gevulde Tomaten (Stuffed Tomatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tomatoes
- 4 asparagus
- 50 g cauliflower florets
- 4 carrots
- salad dressing from recipe 952
- some mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Wash the tomatoes, remove the crown, cut off the cap, rub the fruit pulp and cap through a horsehair sieve, mix this with the grated asparagus and carrots, which are first peeled or scraped, and the grated florets of the washed cauliflower. Add the salad dressing (with a small amount of lemon juice) through it. Fill the hollowed out tomatoes with the mixture and garnish the tomatoes with some mayonnaise (recipe 954).

title: Gevulde Uien (Stuffed Onions)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Lisbon (flat) onions
- 100 g ground calf meat
- 100 g ground pork meat
- 25 g old bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl lean jus or bouillon
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Clean the onions, wash them and cook them in abundant boiling salted water until done.
2. Mix the ground meat in the usual way, melt 10 g butter or margarine in the pan and cook the meat in it, stirring constantly, until done (15-20 minutes), but ensure it does not brown.
3. Hollow out the onions, fill them with the meat and place them in a fireproof dish.
4. Place a piece of butter or margarine on each onion, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, add bouillon or lean jus with water and let them stew gently in the oven for a quarter hour.

title: Gewelde boter (Melted Butter)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- approximately 2 dl warm water
- table salt (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy.
2. Add the lukewarm water drop by drop until a thick, light sauce is obtained.
3. Add table salt if necessary.
4. Serve with asparagus, Brussels chicory, Hamburg rib, etc.

title: Gherkin Sauce (Augurkesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Gherkins
- Bouillon
- Vinegar
- Shallots
- Fried butter
- Salt
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Peel the gherkins, remove the seeds, and cut them into pieces
2. Cook them until soft in bouillon with vinegar, shallots, fried butter, salt, and bay leaves

title: Gierst met Melk of Water (Millet with Milk or Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- Millet
- Milk or water
- Dried prunes
- Butter
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- White wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook millet with three times as much water or milk as millet
2. Cook some dried prunes with water, butter, sugar and cinnamon until half-cooked to use as a sauce alongside
3. For taste, you can also add a little white wine to the sauce

title: Gooische Pannekoeken (Gooi-region Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 full tablespoons wheat flour
- 2 tablespoons buckwheat flour
- 3 eggs (2 with whites, 1 without white)
- sweet milk
- 1 lump butter
- boiling water
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Mix 2 tablespoons wheat flour, 2 tablespoons buckwheat flour, 3 eggs with sweet milk until it reaches the consistency of sweet cream.
2. Put a lump of butter in a small bowl, pour boiling water over it, and slowly pour this into the batter.
3. Stir together and add sugar to taste.
4. To bake, put one sauce spoon of batter into the pan and let it quickly spread. The pan should be moderately warm.

title: Gort (Barley)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g Alkmaar barley
- 9 dl water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the barley until the water that runs off is completely clear.
2. Soak it for 24 hours in 5 times as much cold water as barley.
3. Bring it with the same water to a boil on a brisk fire.
4. Add some salt and let the barley cook gently without stirring until done and dry (approximately 1 1/2 to 2 hours).

title: Gort met Karnemelk (Barley with Buttermilk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter buttermilk
- 100 to 150 g Alkmaar barley
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the buttermilk with salt to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Sprinkle in the washed barley as in the previous recipe, stir the porridge back to a boil.
3. Let it cook gently on a soft fire, stirring occasionally, slowly until done (approximately 2 to 2 1/2 hours).

title: Gort met Melk (Barley with Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 100 to 150 g Alkmaar barley
- salt
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Wash the barley and soak it overnight in 1/2 liter cold water and let it drain.
2. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
3. Stirring, sprinkle in the soaked barley, continue stirring until the milk boils again.
4. Let the porridge cook gently on a soft fire, stirring occasionally, slowly until done (approximately 2 1/2 hours).

title: Gort met Rozijnen (Barley with Raisins)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g Alkmaar barley
- 100 g raisins
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the barley until the water that runs off is completely clear.
2. Soak it for 24 hours in 5 times as much cold water as barley.
3. Add the well-washed raisins with their stems removed and the salt.
4. Cook the barley as described in the barley recipe.

title: Gort of Grutten met Rozijnen (Groats or Grits with Raisins)
body:
## Ingredients

- Groats or grits
- Raisins

## Directions

1. Cook as described for groats, adding raisins equal to half the amount of groats

title: Gortbeuling (Barley Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oat barley
- 1 pound currants
- half pound raisins
- 1 ounce cinnamon
- half ounce cloves
- nutmeg
- salt
- 1 pound lard
- intestines
- lukewarm water

## Directions

1. Soak 2 cups oat barley overnight
2. Mix with 1 pound currants, half pound raisins, 1 ounce cinnamon, half ounce cloves, plenty of nutmeg, a little salt, and 1 pound lard
3. Loosely stuff into well-cleaned intestines
4. Hang them in lukewarm water and bring just to a boil

title: Goudse moppen (Gouda Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flour
- 350 g sugar
- 300 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients into a firm ball
2. Press or roll it on a greased sheet to approximately 1/2 cm thickness
3. Make diamond-shaped figures in the top with a pancake knife
4. Sprinkle the top with some sugar
5. Cut the cake into pieces approximately 5 cm long and 1/2 cm wide
6. Place the pieces on a greased baking sheet at some distance from each other
7. Bake in the middle of a moderately warm oven until lightly brown and done (20-30 minutes)
8. Remove them with a pancake knife from the sheet and let them cool

title: Goulasch (Goulash)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g meat (e.g. lamb)
- Salt
- 2 onions
- 35 g flour
- 2 teaspoons paprika
- 2 to 3 tablespoons thick tomato puree
- 50 g fat, butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into small cubes.
2. Shake it through the flour, which is mixed with salt.
3. Heat the fat in the roasting pan and fry the meat in it, shaking occasionally, until it begins to color.
4. Add the chopped onions and let them become light brown.
5. Add paprika and tomato puree to the meat and add enough water so that the meat is almost covered.
6. Let the goulash stew gently for 1 1/2 hours covered, stir the dish occasionally and add a little more water if necessary.
7. Serve goulash with mashed potatoes or dry rice.

title: Grapefruit met ananas (Grapefruit with Pineapple)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 grapefruits
- 4 slices of pineapple
- 4 candied cherries
- sugar
- Rhine wine, kirsch or sherry

## Directions

1. Cut the grapefruits across and loosen the flesh completely along the edges and membranes with a grapefruit knife
2. Remove the pits
3. Loosen the flesh with the point of the knife so that the segments can be taken out of the skin
4. Divide each slice of pineapple into 6 pieces
5. Place a chilled slice of pineapple first in a cold wide champagne glass, then the chilled grapefruit pieces sprinkled with sugar
6. If desired, pour some Rhine wine, kirsch or sherry over it and garnish with a candied cherry

title: Grauwe Erwten (Grey Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g grey peas
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), but reckon as cooking time 1½ to 2 hours. Serve them with gravy or fried bacon.

title: Griesmeelkransjes (Semolina Rings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l milk
- 135 g semolina
- 1/4 vanilla pod or 3/2 lemon zest
- 75 g sugar
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 5 egg yolks
- some candied fruits or marmalade
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Prepare semolina porridge in the usual way, but add the butter
2. Remove the flavoring, stir in the egg yolks one by one off the heat
3. Spread the dough on a greased baking sheet to 1/2 cm thickness
4. Cut out circles with a greased ring, make a round hole in the middle of each circle (e.g. with an apple corer)
5. Remove the rings from the baking sheet with a sharp, thin knife and fry them brown in hot frying fat
6. Let them drain well on porous paper
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and place a candied fruit or some marmalade on each hole

title: Griesmeelpap (Semolina Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 100 g semolina
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
2. Sprinkle in the semolina while stirring and let the porridge cook while stirring until done (4 to 10 minutes).
3. Use less semolina for thin porridge.

title: Griesmeelpudding (eenvoudige) (Simple Semolina Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 90 g semolina
- 75 g sugar
- ¼ stick vanilla or ½ lemon peel
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook semolina porridge in the usual manner, but allow the flavoring to steep in it.
2. Remove the vanilla or lemon peel, add the sugar and pour the mixture into a mold rinsed with cold water.
3. Let the pudding cool and become firm.
4. Turn it out onto a moist plate and serve it with a fruit juice sauce or stewed fruit.

title: Griesmeelpudding (met eieren en amandelen) (Semolina Pudding with Eggs and Almonds)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ l milk
- ¼ stick vanilla, vanilla sugar or peel of ½ lemon
- 65 g sugar
- 75 to 85 g semolina
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 40 g almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare the pudding as semolina pudding with eggs.
2. Stir in the peeled and chopped almonds together with the semolina and sugar.
3. If desired, replace the almonds with hazelnuts, or use the semolina with small almonds as it comes in commerce.

title: Griesmeelpudding (met eieren) (Semolina Pudding with Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ l milk
- ¼ stick vanilla, vanilla sugar or peel of ½ lemon
- 65 g sugar
- 75 to 85 g semolina
- 1 to 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook semolina porridge in the usual manner with the sugar and flavoring.
2. After removing the vanilla or lemon peel, add it vigorously stirring to the beaten egg yolks.
3. Mix the mixture as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white and pour it into a mold rinsed with cold water.
4. Turn the pudding out onto a moist plate after cooling and serve fruit juice sauce or stewed fruit with it.

title: Griesmeelschoteltje (Semolina Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 100 g semolina
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 100 g sugar
- 1/4 vanilla stick or 1/4 lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare semolina porridge as described in the previous recipe, but mix it with half of the sugar.
2. Beat the egg yolks smooth with the other half of the sugar, vigorously add the hot semolina porridge from which the vanilla or lemon peel has been removed.
3. Beat the egg white stiff and fold it in as lightly as possible.
4. Put the mixture in an oven-safe dish and let it rise and brown in a moderately warm oven.
5. Serve immediately when it comes out of the oven.
6. If desired, a layer of macaroons can be placed in the middle of the dish, approximately 150 g for this quantity.

title: Griesmeelsoep (Semolina Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon of beef or 1 liter water and 4 bouillon cubes (recipe 35)
- 30 g semolina
- 50 g veal ground meat
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Prepare the bouillon in the usual manner, and if desired let a piece of mace cook in it.
2. Bring the strained bouillon to a boil, sprinkle the semolina into it while stirring, and add the meatballs prepared in the usual manner (recipe 37). Or: cook the semolina in water with salt until done, then add the bouillon cubes.
3. Let the soup simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, and then carefully add it to the egg beaten in the soup tureen.

title: Griespodding (Semolina Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces semolina
- nearly 1 can sweet milk
- 1 ounce melted butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons bread sugar
- almond essence
- 1 grated lemon zest
- cinnamon
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook 2.5 ounces semolina with nearly 1 can sweet milk to a pap
2. stir in 1 ounce melted butter, 6 egg yolks, 4 tablespoons bread sugar, almond essence, 1 grated lemon zest and cinnamon
3. add the pap and the whites of 6 eggs beaten to snow
4. pour into the mold
5. let it cook until done

title: Griespodding Anders (Semolina Pudding, Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound semolina
- 0.25 pound bread sugar
- 2 grated lemon zests
- 1 can milk
- 4 eggs
- almond essence
- berry sauce

## Directions

1. Let 1 can milk come to a boil and stir in the semolina, bread sugar and grated lemon zests
2. let it cook with constant stirring
3. remove from heat
4. beat 4 eggs with their whites and stir through
5. some add almond essence which makes it very tasty
6. serve in small molds or bowls, cold with berry sauce

title: Griessoep (Semolina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- semolina
- strong bouillon
- mace

## Directions

1. Cook as described for the sago soup with bouillon

title: Griet (Brill)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 portions of brill (approximately 1 kg)
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)
- 1/2 onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse the portions well
2. Cook them in boiling water with salt (some vinegar and the 1/2 onion if desired) until the fin comes off easily (approximately 10 minutes)
3. Remove them with 2 skimming spoons or a fish slice from the pan and place them on the fish plate
4. Garnish with fresh sprigs of parsley
5. Serve with egg sauce, butter sauce, or caper sauce

title: Groene Boter (Green Butter)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or vegetable butter
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped herbs (parsley, chervil, celery)

## Directions

1. Stir the butter until it becomes salve-like and mix it with the finely chopped herbs.

title: Groene boter (Green Butter)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g boter of plantenboter
- 1 eetlepel fijngehakte kruiden (peterselie, kervel, selderie)

## Directions

1. Roer de boter tot deze zalfachtig geworden is en vermeng hem met de fijngehakte kruiden.

title: Groene Erwten (Green Peas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g green peas
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), but reckon as cooking time 1¼ to 1¾ hour. Serve them with parsley sauce.

title: Groene Komkommers in 't Zuur (Green Cucumbers in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- green cucumbers
- Spanish pepper
- bay leaves
- fennel
- horseradish
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the whole green cucumbers in a butter brine for 14 days
2. Let them drain well and add Spanish pepper, bay leaves, fennel and horseradish
3. Put them in an earthenware pot and seal it well with a bladder

title: Groene Pruimen te Konfijten (Candied Green Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces green plums
- three-quarters pound sugar
- pomegranate water

## Directions

1. The plums must first be heated with pomegranate water and sugar, but well skimmed, then set aside; do this about three times, until they are green
2. Then lay the plums on a colander, covered with vine leaves
3. Then cook the syrup, and when it is thick enough, pour it cold over the plums

title: Groene Pruimen te Konfijten (Green Plums Preserved in Sugar Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces of green plums
- three-quarters pound of sugar
- pomegranate water

## Directions

1. The plums must first be boiled with pomegranate water with the sugar, but well skimmed, then set aside; do this about three times, until they are green
2. Place the plums on a colander, covered with grapevine leaves; then cook the syrup and when thick enough, place it cold on the plums

title: Groenten in mosterdzuur (Mixed Pickles)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 bottles pickling vinegar
- 40 g sugar
- 20 g kitchen salt
- 10 g ground turmeric
- 10 g ginger powder
- 60 g flour
- 120 g mustard powder
- a little over 1 kg vegetables, such as: cauliflower, carrots, princess beans, snap beans, broad beans, garden peas, onions, gherkins, cucumbers

## Directions

1. Mix the turmeric, ginger and mustard powder, salt and flour with some of the cold vinegar into a thin paste.
2. Bring the remaining vinegar to a boil, pour the mixed mixture through while stirring and let the sauce simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Meanwhile, clean the vegetables, divide them into smaller pieces if necessary, wash them and cook them for 5 minutes in boiling water. Dry them in a cloth.
4. Cook them for 5 minutes in the sauce, put the mixture in well-cleaned small bottles. Make sure the vegetables are under the sauce, pour a little oil on top and close them with damp parchment paper. Provide them with a label with name and date.

title: Groentesoep (Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- soup vegetables
- water
- salt
- cooked rice
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- small meatballs made from calf's meat

## Directions

1. Take the soup vegetables and wash them carefully in plenty of cold water, chop them very finely and let them cook almost done in water with salt.
2. Add some cooked rice to them, let them cook together gently.
3. Add Liebig's Meat Extract to taste. To enhance the flavor, you can add some small meatballs made from calf's meat.

title: Groentesoep (Vegetable Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter beef bouillon (recipe 35) or 4 Maggi's bouillon cubes with 1 liter water
- 35 g rice or vermicelli
- vegetables in season (approximately 300 g), for example in summer: peas, carrots, string beans, cauliflower, etc.
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the bouillon in the usual manner and strain it. Add the washed rice or vermicelli and the finely cut and washed vegetables and let everything cook together gently until the vegetables and rice or vermicelli are tender (approximately 1/2 hour).
2. If desired, thicken the soup with a little sago and stir in finely chopped parsley away from the heat.

title: Groentesoep zonder Vleesch (Vegetable Soup without Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh sorrel
- two heads of lettuce
- chopped young chervil
- water
- salt
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Take a handful of fresh sorrel, two heads of lettuce, some chopped young chervil
2. Set this to cook with a little water and salt
3. Cook this for half an hour and beat three eggs into it when it is ready

title: Grote Pastei (Large Pastry - First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or calf kidney fat
- 100 g flour
- salt
- water (approximately 1/2 dl)
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846)
2. Roll out the dough the last time to a thickness of approximately 1/2 cm
3. Cut out 3 circles using a cutter form with a diameter of 10 to 12 cm
4. Cut lids from 2 of the circles with a diameter of 8 to 10 cm
5. Flatten one circle, lay it on the baking sheet and brush it with egg yolk beaten with a little water
6. Glue the remaining rings to the largest circle with a little water or egg white
7. Prick the bottom of the formed pastry here and there with a larding needle to prevent too much rising
8. Place a few paper wads in the pastry to support the edges as it rises upward
9. Brush only the top ring with the yolk beaten with water
10. Bake the pastry and lid in a hot oven until light brown and cooked through (15 to 20 minutes)
11. Do not look at it during the first 5 minutes, as there is a risk of it sinking
12. Remove the pastry and lid from the baking sheet, hollow out the inner uncooked part somewhat, fill it with ragout and place the lid on top

title: Grote Pastei Vol au Vent (Large Pastry Vol au Vent - Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or calf kidney fat
- 100 g flour
- salt
- water (approximately 1/2 dl)
- white paper
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846)
2. Roll it out the last time into a large, broad sheet to a thickness of 1/4 cm
3. Cut out a circle with a diameter of 18 cm (for example by laying a small plate or lid on it) and place it on a baking sheet
4. Cut long, narrow strips from thin white paper, roll them into a ball and place this on the dough
5. Cut a larger circle from the remaining dough (for example with a diameter of 24 cm)
6. Lay this over the paper and glue the edge with water to the lower circle, making sure the folds are evenly distributed
7. Roll out the leftover dough thinly, cut long, narrow strips from it, lay these crosswise over the pastry, making sure that a triangle or quadrangle is formed in the middle of the pastry, which will later be cut out to serve as the lid
8. Place a small ball of dough in the middle as a knob for the lid, glue the strips and knob with water and brush everything with egg yolk beaten with water
9. Bake the pastry in a hot oven
10. Do not look at it during the first 5 minutes, as there is a risk of it sinking
11. Bake until light brown and cooked through (approximately 20 minutes)
12. Cut out the little lid, carefully remove the paper (for example with scissors), fill it with ragout and place the lid back on

title: Haagse bluf (The Hague Bluff)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dl berry or raspberry juice
- 100 g sugar
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Put berry or raspberry juice, sugar and the unbeaten egg white together in a very deep bowl. Beat the mass for approximately 20 minutes with a well-galvanized whisk or with silver forks until it is stiff.

title: Haagse Bluf (The Hague Puff)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dl berry or raspberry juice
- 100 g sugar
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Put the berry or raspberry juice, sugar and the unbeaten egg white together in a very deep bowl.
2. Beat the mixture for about 20 minutes with a well-galvanized whisk or with silver forks until it is stiff.

title: Haas (Roasted Hare)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 250 g butter or margarine
- 150 g larding bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the hare or (preferably) wipe it with a damp cloth.
2. Cut the larding bacon into very thin strips and lard the back with one row of bacon strips, the hind quarters with two rows.
3. Rub the hare inside and outside with some salt.
4. Heat the butter or margarine thoroughly (but not brown) in a hare pan or roasting pan, place the hare in it, baste it on all sides and put it in the oven.
5. Place the lid over the hare pan, over the roasting pan place a piece of gray paper smeared with butter or margarine and baste the meat frequently.
6. Add some water to the butter when it threatens to brown.
7. After 2 to 2½ hours, depending on the weight of the hare, test whether the hare is done by seeing if the meat from the hind quarter can be easily removed with a spoon.
8. Remove it from the pan, make the butter so hot that a crust forms on the bottom of the pan, let it cool on low heat and gradually add hare blood mixed with some red wine, cream or water while stirring.
9. Let the gravy simmer briefly while stirring.
10. Remove the strings and pegs from the hare before serving and serve with apple sauce or red cabbage.
11. Note: You can also first cut the hare into pieces, place them for 24 hours in water and vinegar with a couple of bay leaves, some cloves and some white peppercorns, then brown and roast them. The gravy can optionally be finished with some of the liquid in which the hare was soaked.

title: Haché (Minced Meat Stew)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g cold meat
- 50 g flour
- 50 g fat from gravy and 1/2 l water with 2 Maggi's Bouillon cubes, 1/2 l diluted lean gravy or 1/2 Maggi's Gravy tablet
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 cloves
- Piece of Spanish pepper (optional)
- 300 g onions
- 1/2 dl vinegar
- Maggi's Aroma

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped onions very light brown in the fat, add the flour and fry, stirring constantly, the flour and onions uniformly brown.
2. Add the liquid and the spices to this, let the sauce cook for 5 minutes while stirring and add the meat cut into small cubes and the vinegar.
3. Let the haché stew slowly on low heat for at least 1 hour, but stir occasionally to prevent burning.
4. Before serving, remove the spices and add, if desired, a little sugar and English sauce and soya or Maggi's Aroma.
5. If the sauce is too thin when serving, it can be bound with some mixed maize starch, which is allowed to cook for 3 minutes while stirring.
6. The amount of water to be added to the gravy depends entirely on how salty it is.

title: Haché van Kalfsvleesch (Veal Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal, cut into small pieces
- finely chopped onions
- brown sugar
- pepper
- crushed cloves
- salt
- potato starch
- vinegar or red wine

## Directions

1. To cold meat cut into small pieces, add finely chopped onions, brown sugar, pepper, finely crushed cloves, a little salt, potato starch, and vinegar or red wine as desired
2. Braise for 1.5 hours

title: Haché van Rundvleesch (Beef Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold beef, cut into small pieces
- Onions (finely chopped)
- A spoonful of brown sugar
- Pepper
- Cloves (finely crushed)
- Fine salt
- Potato flour
- Vinegar or red wine

## Directions

1. Cut cold beef into small pieces
2. Add finely chopped onions, a spoonful of brown sugar, pepper, finely crushed cloves, fine salt, some potato flour and vinegar or red wine according to preference
3. Let it stew for three hours

title: Haché van Schapevleesch met Eieren (Minced Mutton with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from a roasted leg of mutton
- parsley
- a little vinegar
- shallots
- bouillon
- fried bread
- four or five oeufs de boeuf

## Directions

1. Take the meat from a roasted leg of mutton, chop it very fine and add parsley, a little vinegar and shallots
2. Let it cook in bouillon in a pan
3. When ready, place it on a dish, arrange some pieces of fried bread around it as a border, and decorate it with four or five oeufs de boeuf

title: Haché van Varkensvleesch (Minced Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- a sufficient quantity of pork
- jus
- onions, finely cut
- pepper
- salt
- finely ground biscuit
- a splash of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut a sufficient quantity of pork into slices, add some jus, finely cut onions, pepper, salt and finely ground biscuit, as well as a splash of vinegar, and let it braise for about half an hour

title: Haché-rijstpudding (Hashed Meat and Rice Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter haché according to recipe 89
- 200 g rice

## Directions

1. Prepare haché as indicated in recipe 89
2. Cook the rice in the usual manner
3. Line a butter-greased warm pudding mold along the bottom and sides with rice
4. Place the haché, with the herbs removed, in the middle and lay a layer of rice on top
5. Close the mold in the usual manner and cook the pudding 1 1/2 hours in a bain marie, ensuring the water is refilled to just below the rim of the mold
6. Turn the pudding out onto a warmed plate and serve warm

title: Halve Gedraaide (Dutch Cold Meat Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cold meat
- 8 cooked potatoes
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- 4 pickled onions
- 4 pickled gherkins
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes, mix them with the chopped meat, 3 chopped onions and 3 snipped or chopped gherkins.
2. Stir in enough salad oil, vinegar, pepper and salt until a cohesive, well-flavored mixture is obtained.
3. Lay the mixture on a plate, smooth it, but ensure it is slightly higher in the middle than at the edges.
4. Garnish with the remaining onions, gherkins, hard-boiled egg cut into quarters and fresh sprigs of parsley.
5. Note: Instead of oil and vinegar, one can also stir mayonnaise or salad sauce through the mixture.

title: Halve Maantjes (Half Moon Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 10 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- 1 egg
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 50 g sugar

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the known manner (see recipe 835) from flour, yeast, salt, milk, egg, melted butter or margarine and sugar and let it rise.
2. Form small half moons from the dough and let them rise for another half hour on a buttered baking sheet under a damp cloth.
3. Brush them with some beaten egg and bake them in a hot oven light yellow and done (about 15 minutes).

title: Halve maantjes or Fleurons (Half Moons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g hard butter or margarine
- 50 g flour
- some water
- some salt
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846), roll it out the last time to a thickness of 1/4 cm.
2. Cut out half moons with a cookie cutter or glass and brush with egg yolk that has been beaten with water.
3. Lay them on a baking sheet and bake them in a hot oven, placed in the lower half, light brown and cooked through (approximately 10 minutes).
4. Use them with ragout or as garnish for dishes.

title: Ham and Potato Croquettes
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g ham
- 250 g cooked potatoes
- 50 g old bread without crust
- milk or water
- pepper
- salt
- 30 g butter, margarine or frying fat
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Chop the ham finely, mix it with finely mashed potatoes and the bread, which has been soaked in water or milk and squeezed between two spoons.
2. Add pepper and salt to taste and stir everything together until well combined.
3. Form small balls from the mixture, roll them in breadcrumbs and fry them in a pan in which the butter has been made light brown, or in hot frying fat on all sides until brown.

title: Hamballetjes (Ham Balls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g ham
- 250 g cooked potatoes
- 50 g stale bread without crust
- Some milk
- Pepper
- Some salt
- 30 g butter, margarine or deep-frying fat

## Directions

1. Mince the ham finely, mix it with finely mashed potatoes and the bread, which has been soaked in some water or milk and squeezed out between two spoons.
2. Add pepper and salt to taste and stir everything together so that it is well mixed.
3. Form small balls, roll them in breadcrumbs and fry them in the frying pan, in which the butter has been made light brown, or in the hot frying fat on all sides until brown.

title: Hambroodjes (Ham Bread Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g ham
- 100 g cold veal
- 20 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 1 to 1.5 dl water with salt or stock
- 2 eggs
- 8 thin slices of old bread
- breadcrumbs
- salad oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Grind the ham and meat together.
2. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 102 and mix the meat with it.
3. Add salt and pepper if needed.
4. Cut thin slices of old bread, remove the crusts and make the pieces equal in shape.
5. Spread them with a thick layer of the mixture, so that it lies higher in the middle than at the edges.
6. Beat the eggs, soak the bread rolls in them until they are completely absorbed, drip the top repeatedly with the egg.
