7. Roll them through fine breadcrumbs and fry them brown in hot salad oil.
8. Let them drain on gray paper and place them on a small plate on which a folded cloth lies.

title: Hamburger Rib (Cured and Smoked Beef Rib)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Hamburger rib (cured and lightly smoked beef rib)
- Water
- Butter or margarine
- Garden peas

## Directions

1. Brush the rib clean in warm water.
2. Place it in plenty of boiling water and cook for half an hour per 500 g.
3. Serve with melted butter or margarine and garden peas.

title: Hangop (Curdled Milk Dessert)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 l buttermilk
- 4 large rusks
- brown sugar
- some milk or cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Place a cloth rinsed in clean water in a colander, set it in a deep bowl and pour the buttermilk into it
2. Let the watery part of the buttermilk drain off
3. From time to time scoop the thick part out of the cloth and continue until the hangop is completely drained
4. Beat the hangop smooth and stir, if the hangop is too sour, some milk or cream through it
5. Serve it with brown sugar and rusks or with cornflakes

title: Harde Wenertaart (Hard Wener Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g flour
- 200 g butter or margarine
- 150 g brown sugar
- 1 egg or 2 egg yolks
- jam
- water glaze
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Make a dough prepared according to recipe 770
2. Stir in the egg or yolks
3. Bake 3 cakes on a baking sheet or in sand cake rings (20-30 minutes)
4. Let the dough layers cool
5. Spread them with jam
6. Stack the cakes on top of each other
7. Cover the top with water glaze (see recipe 819)
8. Decorate the pastry with a border of finely cut candied fruit

title: Haring met Mayonnaise (Herring with Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salt herrings
- 3 tablespoons mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Clean the herring as described above.
2. Cut them into diagonal pieces, place them in a section of the plate and pour some mayonnaise over them.

title: Haring met tomatenpurée (Herring with Tomato Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salted herrings
- tomato purée

## Directions

1. Soak the herring for several hours in water with a little milk
2. Make an incision along the back and cut open the belly side
3. Remove the intestines, roe or milt, and the black membrane around the intestines
4. Pull off the skin and cut off the head
5. Wash the herring thoroughly clean
6. Carefully remove the backbone and small bones
7. Cut the herring into diagonal pieces
8. Place them in a section of the serving dish

title: Haring met Tomatenpurée (Herring with Tomato Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salt herrings
- tomato purée

## Directions

1. Soak the herring for several hours in water with some milk.
2. Make an incision along the back, cut open the belly side, remove the entrails, soft roe or hard roe, and the black membrane around the entrails.
3. Pull off the skin and cut off the head.
4. Wash the herring thoroughly, carefully remove the backbone and small bones, cut the herring into diagonal pieces, place them in a section of the plate and pour some tomato purée over them.

title: Haringsalade (Herring Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- onions, finely cut
- cooked beets
- hard-boiled eggs, finely made
- sour apples
- parsley
- cold potatoes, finely grated (optional)
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the herring, hold it by the tail and split it in two after opening it on the back.
2. Remove the backbone and cut it into long strips.
3. The dish can be made decorative and arranged by placing the following ingredients in compartments: finely cut onions, cooked beets, hard-boiled eggs finely made, sour apples, and parsley, and if desired, cold finely grated potatoes.
4. Serve the dish after garnishing the edge with the white and yolks of eggs and gherkins cut into slices.
5. Dress with oil and vinegar.

title: Haringsaus (Herring Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Sweet milk
- Onion
- Flour
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Water
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean a herring and soak it in sweet milk
2. Remove the bones and chop it finely with an onion
3. Fry some flour brown in butter
4. Add bouillon, water and pepper
5. Add the herring
6. Finally squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
7. Let it cook through briefly

title: Haringsla (Herring Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 2 sour apples
- 1 egg
- 1 beet
- 8 cooked potatoes
- 1 head of lettuce or 1 bunch of curly endive or 100 g field lettuce
- pickled onions and gherkins
- 3 tablespoons salad oil
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the herrings as indicated in recipe 5. Cut them into small pieces.
2. Boil the egg hard (8 minutes), peel it, separate the white from the yolk and mash both separately.
3. Peel and mince the apples, mash the potatoes.
4. Clean the lettuce, cut it finely, wash it and squeeze it as dry as possible.
5. Peel the cooked beet, cut it into slices of approximately 1/4 cm thickness and cut these into small cubes.
6. Mix most of all these ingredients together, but reserve a small part for garnish, except for the apples as they discolor quickly.
7. Stir in oil and vinegar or some mayonnaise to taste and some pepper and salt.
8. Lay this mixture on a plate, smooth it, but ensure it is slightly higher in the middle than at the edges.
9. Use the remainder as garnish and lay it on in sections or stripes, ensuring the underlying mixture is completely covered.
10. Further garnish the plate with onions and gherkins.
11. Note: The egg can also be cut into quarters or sixths and used only as garnish.

title: Havergortdrank voor Zieken (Barley Drink for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- good barley groats, washed several times
- water
- salt, to taste
- Liebig's Meat Extract, to taste

## Directions

1. Let the good barley groats, which have been washed several times, cook for at least a full hour in water.
2. Pour through a sieve and if necessary dilute with water.
3. Add salt and Liebig's Meat Extract to taste and let it all cook together well.
4. This is a strengthening and refreshing drink for the sick and convalescent.

title: Havermout met Karnemelk (Oatmeal with Buttermilk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l buttermilk
- 90 to 120 g oatmeal
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the buttermilk with salt to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Prepare the porridge further as described in the previous recipe.

title: Havermoutkoekjes (Oatmeal Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g oatmeal
- 3 dl water
- 50 g flour
- salt
- 50 g sugar
- 75 g butter or margarine
- fine cinnamon or grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Soak the oatmeal in water for 1 hour.
2. Add the flour, sugar, flavoring, and if necessary more water until a fairly thick batter is obtained.
3. Heat the butter or margarine in a frying pan.
4. Fry small round cookies from this batter until light brown and cooked through on low heat.
5. Serve them warm with brown sugar.
6. Note: one may optionally add 1-2 eggs and more flour.

title: Havermoutpap (Oatmeal Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter milk
- 100 g oatmeal
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil, stirring sprinkle in the oatmeal.
2. Let the milk come to a boil again while stirring and let the porridge cook.

title: Havermoutpap (Oatmeal Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l milk
- 100 g oatmeal
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Sprinkle the oatmeal in while stirring.
3. Let the milk come to a boil again while stirring and then cook the porridge on a gentle fire, stirring occasionally, until the oatmeal is cooked and the porridge is evenly bound (at least 20 to 25 minutes).
4. For quick-cooking oatmeal, let it cook for 5 minutes and use 1.25 l milk.

title: Hazebrood (Hare Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from a cooked hare
- veal
- bread
- calf liver
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Take the meat from a cooked hare, mix it with some veal and chop very finely
2. Make a dough with bread, add calf liver
3. Place it in a mold that is lined with bacon strips on the bottom and around the sides
4. Cook in water so the mold sits one inch above the water and water does not boil into it
5. After it has cooked somewhat, place a lid with fire on the casserole
6. When it has roasted for one hour, serve it
7. Can be served with a wine sauce like hare pepper

title: Hazelnotenschoteltje (Hazelnut Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 large rusks
- 1/2 l milk
- 3 eggs
- 80 g sugar
- 150 g unpeeled hazelnuts
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Rub the rusks fine in the warm milk.
2. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar, vigorously add the warm porridge and the peeled and grated or ground hazelnuts.
3. Beat the egg white stiff and fold it in lightly.
4. Place the mixture in a heatproof dish.
5. Smooth the top, sprinkle with a mixture of breadcrumbs, fine hazelnuts and sugar, clean the edge of the dish and let it become light brown and rise in the oven.

title: Hazenpastei (Hare Pâté)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 500 g veal
- 500 g fresh bacon
- 1/2 bottle or tin of truffles (optional)
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- some vinegar
- some lard
- 4 cloves
- 2 bay leaves
- 10 white peppercorns
- minced onion

## Directions

1. Remove all bones from the hare, cut the meat into pieces, wash it and place it for 24 hours in half water, half vinegar with the spices.
2. Dry the nice pieces from back and thigh, cut them into thin slices and lard these with very thin strips of bacon.
3. Grind the washed meat and bacon twice through the meat grinder, do the same with the remaining pieces of hare and mix this mass with pepper, salt and nutmeg to taste.
4. Choose a pâté pot that can be completely filled with this quantity.
5. Cover the bottom and side walls with thin slices of bacon, arrange first a layer of the slices, place a layer of meat mixture on top and continue until everything is used up.
6. Pour the marinade mixed with some bouillon over it until the mass is covered.
7. Close the pot with the lid and brush the edge with a paste of flour and water.
8. Place the pâté in a fairly warm oven and let it stand there for approximately 3 hours.
9. Loosen the lid, pour a layer of melted lard over it, close the pot again and seal the edge of lid and pot with foil.
10. Store the pâté in a cool place.

title: Hazensoep (Hare Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover hare
- 1 l lean hare stock with water
- a couple of chopped carrots
- 1 piece Spanish pepper
- 50 g onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 cloves
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- Cayenne pepper to taste
- red wine or English sauce and soya

## Directions

1. Let the hare cut into pieces infuse gently (1 to 1 1/2 hours) in the hare stock with the herbs and enough water added so that it is not too salty
2. Strain this and add it to the melted butter or margarine which has been brought to a boil together with the flour while stirring
3. Let the soup simmer while stirring for 10 minutes
4. Add the hare meat cut into small equal pieces and if desired some Cayenne pepper
5. Stir in the wine or English sauce and soya away from heat

title: Hazepeper (Hare Pepper Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 1 liter lean gravy with water or 1 liter broth
- a piece of Spanish pepper
- 3 bay leaves
- 6 cloves
- 50 g onion
- 120 g flour
- 120 g butter or margarine
- some English sauce and soy or some red wine
- 20 g sugar
- approximately 1/2 dl vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted hare into pieces; add enough water to the lean gravy

title: Hazepeper (Hare Pepper Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- water
- butter
- red wine
- vinegar
- brown sugar
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- onions
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the hare into pieces and wash it
2. Boil in water for half an hour, then drain off most of the water
3. Add plenty of butter, a beer glass of red wine, half a beer glass of vinegar, a spoon of brown sugar, salt, pepper, ground cloves, very finely cut onions and crushed breadcrumbs
4. Let everything braise slowly together

title: Hazepeper (Hare Pepper Stew)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- 1 l lean hare gravy with water or 1 l bouillon
- a piece of Spanish pepper
- 3 bay leaves
- 6 cloves
- 50 g onion
- 120 g flour
- 120 g butter or margarine
- some English sauce and soya or some red wine
- 20 g sugar
- 1/2 dl vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted hare into pieces; add enough water to the lean hare gravy so that it is not too salty.
2. Make a bound brown sauce in the usual way from butter or margarine, flour and lean gravy or bouillon.
3. Add the pieces of hare, the spices bound in a cheesecloth bag and the vinegar, and let the hare pepper stew on a low heat, stirring occasionally, for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Remove the spices and add the sugar, English sauce and soya or some red wine.

title: Heerekoekjes (Gentleman's Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted white bread
- eggs
- ginger
- wine
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a batter of roasted white bread, eggs, ginger, wine and sugar
2. Fry cookies in a pan with butter
3. When serving, sprinkle sugar over them

title: Heilbot (Halibut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 portions of halibut (approximately 3/4 kg)
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook according to the method for brill (recipe 174)
2. Cooking time approximately 15 minutes

title: Herb Vinegar (Kruidenazijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 bottles ordinary vinegar
- Four bunches tarragon without stems
- 4 handfuls poppies
- 4 handfuls fresh elderflowers
- 2 handfuls pimpernel
- 2 handfuls basil

## Directions

1. Add the tarragon without stems, poppies, elderflowers, pimpernel, and basil to 6 to 8 bottles of ordinary vinegar
2. Let it steep in the sun in a calabash
3. Strain through a woolen bag
4. Pour into ordinary bottles

title: Hernhuttertjes (Hernhutter Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g butter or margarine
- 80 g brown sugar
- 125 g flour
- 70 g currants
- 20 g almonds

## Directions

1. Grind the peeled almonds finely
2. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy
3. Add the sugar, sifted flour, washed currants that have been pitted, and ground almonds
4. Form small balls from this dough
5. Bake them on a greased baking sheet in the middle of a moderately warm oven until lightly brown and done (15 to 20 minutes)
6. This dough can also be used to fill the small Hernhutter molds (see recipe 770)

title: Hersens in Schelpen (Brains in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- brains of one calf
- 2 cloves
- 4 white peppercorns
- 2½ dl broth
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt and parsley
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the brains for a couple of hours in cold water, place them in plenty of boiling water with salt, cloves and peppercorns and let them cook until done (25 to 30 min.)
2. Remove the skin.
3. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102, but in this case use the strained broth.
4. Mix it with the brains cut into pieces and the chopped parsley, add salt and lemon juice to taste and fill the dampened shells with this mixture for about 3/4.
5. Finish them as described in recipe 102.

title: Hoefijzerkoekjes (Horseshoe Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g flour
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 150 g powdered sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- some scraped vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream the butter or margarine until fluffy, add the sifted sugar and the egg yolks one by one.
2. Mix this batter with the sifted flour and make small rolls on a sheet dusted with a little flour, 1/2 cm thick and 10 cm long.
3. Arrange them in the shape of a horseshoe on a baking sheet greased with butter or margarine, making sure there is some space between them.
4. Brush the cookies with beaten egg and bake them in a fairly warm oven until light brown and done.
5. Remove them from the baking sheet and let them cool.

title: Hoenderragout (Chicken Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- ground veal (5 oz per pair)
- poultry (5 oz per pair)
- sweetbreads
- butter
- lemon juice
- mace
- flour
- parsley

## Directions

1. Bring the chickens to a boil, then add for each pair 5 oz of ground veal, 5 oz of poultry and some sweetbreads.
2. Let everything cook together until done.
3. Take some of the liquid, add butter, lemon juice and mace, and bind with flour.
4. Add parsley, which should be removed before serving.
5. Let everything stew for a good quarter hour.

title: Hoenders met kastanjes (Chickens with Chestnuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- mace
- nutmeg
- chestnuts
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the chickens half done.
2. Add mace, nutmeg, and peeled chestnuts cut into quarters, some of which should be grated.
3. Also add butter and let everything stew together.

title: Hoenders à la Tartare (Chicken Tartare Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- melted butter
- two small onions
- parsley
- mushrooms
- pepper
- salt
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut young chickens in half.
2. Singe them over the fire with melted butter, two small finely chopped onions, parsley and mushrooms, and pepper and salt.
3. Cover with breadcrumbs and roast over a hot fire.
4. Serve with a bound sauce (see sauces section).

title: Hollandse Saus (Dutch Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- pepper, salt and lemon juice to taste
- 3 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the milk, pepper and salt and add 25 g of the butter or margarine
2. Allow the mixture to thicken over a bain-marie
3. Gradually stir in the rest of the butter or margarine in small quantities
4. Finish with pepper, salt and lemon juice to taste and serve with turbot, salmon, sole, etc.

title: Honingcake (Honey Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g butter or margarine
- 250 g honey
- 225 g flour
- 25 g Duryea cornstarch
- 5 eggs
- 50 g honey for garnishing
- some whipped cream

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the yolks one by one.
2. Then add in small quantities the sifted flour and cornstarch and the honey.
3. Beat the egg white stiffly and stir the mass through with a few strokes as quickly as possible.
4. Put the batter in a greased cake pan and let it slowly become light brown and done in a moderately warm oven (approximately 1 hour).
5. Turn the cake onto a cake rack and let it cool.
6. Then spread some honey over it and spray the cake with whipped cream using a piping bag with a fluted nozzle.

title: Hoofdkaas (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head or calf's cheek
- pork head
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg
- cold water
- molds or small bowls

## Directions

1. Cook a calf's head or calf's cheek and a pork head until so done that the bones fall out
2. Remove the skin, membrane and bones
3. Chop it finely with salt, pepper, cloves and nutmeg
4. Rinse the molds or small bowls with cold water
5. Fill with the prepared mixture

title: Hoofdkaas (Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head or calf's slice
- 1 pork head
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg
- vinegar
- water
- fat

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's head or slice and pork head until the bones fall out
2. Remove skins, membranes, and bones
3. Mince finely with salt, pepper, cloves, and nutmeg
4. Rinse molds or small dishes with cold water
5. Place the prepared meat in the molds and let cool
6. Place in an earthenware pot with vinegar and half water
7. Pour a layer of fat over the top

title: Hoofdkaas (Head Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head or 1 pork head (or ½ calf's head + ½ pork head)
- 2 kg beef or pork meat (or 1 kg beef + 1 kg pork meat)
- about 40 g salt
- pepper
- 1 grated nutmeg
- about 1½ tablespoon ground cloves
- about 1 dl vinegar
- some gherkins
- ½ to 2½ l broth in which the head was cooked

## Directions

1. Wash the head and the meat, cook the head for 5 hours in plenty of boiling water with salt, let the meat cook along for 3 hours.
2. Then carefully remove all the bones from the meat and chop or grind the meat finely.
3. Mix it with the broth until a not too stiff mass is obtained, add salt, the spices, the vinegar and if desired some chopped gherkins.
4. Put the mass in bowls or molds rinsed with vinegar up to 1 cm below the rim. Let it cool and place a properly fitting piece of white paper on it, with an opening cut in the middle.
5. Pour some vinegar on top and store the head cheese in a cool, dust-free place. Pour some vinegar on top whenever it evaporates.

title: Hoofdkaas van Varkenslever (Pork Liver Headcheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half Dutch pounds pork liver
- one Dutch pound bacon
- two and a half ounces unmelted lard
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- spices
- bacon or lard for layering

## Directions

1. Chop the pork liver, bacon and unmelted lard very finely with parsley, pepper, salt and spices
2. In a casserole, place a piece of lard or a slice of bacon at the bottom to prevent burning
3. Layer three fingerheights of the chopped mixture, then a slice of bacon, then three fingerheights of chopped mixture again, continuing until the pan is full
4. Cover with a slice of bacon
5. Roast in the oven for three hours, then let cool
6. Warm it when ready to eat
7. Can be served cold with aspic if desired

title: Hoofdkaas à la Minute (Marinated Meat Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g meat scraps
- 2.5 dl meat jus with water or bouillon or 2.5 dl water with one Maggi's bouillon cube
- 1 dl slightly sour vinegar
- 8 g white gelatin
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- pickled onions and gherkins
- capers
- parsley
- 2 hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Chop or grind the meat finely.
2. Soak the gelatin 10 minutes in cold water, squeeze it well, and then dissolve it in the warmed jus with water (or bouillon).
3. Stir through the meat, vinegar, salt, pepper and nutmeg.
4. Rinse a stone pudding mold with cold water, garnish it along the bottom and sides with hard-boiled egg cut into quarters, capers, pieces of gherkin and fresh sprigs of parsley.
5. Gently press the meat mixture into it and let it cool and set.
6. Turn out the hoofdkaas onto a meat platter and garnish the platter with onions, gherkins, parsley and the remaining egg.
7. Note: One can also stir some finely chopped onions and gherkins through the mixture.

title: Hop (Hops)
body:
## Ingredients

- hops
- cold water
- bouillon
- butter
- flour
- nutmeg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the hops to the length of a finger
2. Cover them with cold water and cook until done
3. Let them drain
4. Stew them for another quarter hour in bouillon with butter, flour, nutmeg and lemon juice

title: Hop Salade (Hop Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- hops
- salt
- water
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the hop to the length of a finger.
2. Cook it with salt and water until done.
3. Eat it with oil, vinegar, and pepper.

title: Hopjespudding (Hopjes Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 dl cream
- 3 1/2 dl milk
- 200 g sugar
- 17 1/2 g white gelatin or 15 g powdered gelatin
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- some strong coffee extract
- salt

## Directions

1. Slowly bring the cream with the milk and vanilla to a boil
2. Put 175 g sugar in a large pan, let it stand on a not too fierce fire until it has become caramel and begins to foam over the entire surface
3. Then pour the boiling liquid slowly but all at once from the fire into it
4. Add 25 g sugar
5. If necessary, let the undissolved caramel dissolve briefly on the fire
6. Dissolve the gelatin, which has been soaked 10-15 minutes in cold water beforehand, in the warm mass
7. Remove the vanilla, add coffee extract and salt
8. Stir from time to time until the pudding begins to become jelly-like
9. Pour the mass into a mold rinsed with cold water and let the pudding become cold and stiff
10. Turn it out onto a damp dish and serve with vanilla sauce

title: Horseradish Sauce (Mieriksaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bouillon
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Mace
- Bread sugar
- Salt
- Crushed biscuit
- Grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Cook bouillon with vinegar, butter, mace, bread sugar, salt, and crushed biscuit
2. Stir in grated horseradish
3. This sauce must be very thick

title: Hutspot (Dutch Meat and Vegetable Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped beef ribs
- water
- salt
- carrots
- onions
- potatoes, pre-cooked

## Directions

1. Cook the chopped beef ribs until done in water with some salt
2. Add carrots and some onions and let them cook until tender
3. When serving, add the pre-cooked potatoes

title: Hutspot met Klapstuk (Hotchpotch with Brisket)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg large carrots
- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- 350 g onions
- 1/2 kg brisket
- 40 g fat from gravy, beef fat or lard
- 3/4 litre water
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the brisket, place it in boiling water with salt and let it cook for 1 1/2 hours on a gentle fire.
2. Peel the carrots, wash them, shred them and add them to the meat.
3. Peel the potatoes and onions, cut them into quarters, wash them and add them after a quarter hour to the meat and carrots.
4. Add the fat if the brisket is lean and let everything cook together on a gentle fire until tender and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
5. Add more water if needed, but make sure the mash does not become too moist.
6. When everything is cooked, remove the meat from the pan and mash or stir it together with a wooden spoon or with a masher.

title: Hutspot met Witte Bonen (Hotchpotch with White Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg large carrots
- 1 kg potatoes
- 250 g white beans
- 350 g onions
- 60 g fat

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in the usual manner (recipe 493) until tender.
2. Peel the carrots, shred them, wash them and add enough water (or if available: bean water) to half cover them.
3. Let them cook for a quarter hour, then place the peeled and cut potatoes and onions on top, add the fat and the beans and let everything cook together on a gentle fire until tender and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
4. Add more water if needed, but make sure the mash does not become too moist.
5. Mash or stir everything quickly together with a wooden spoon or with a masher.

title: Huzarensalade (Hussar Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold veal or beef
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Pickled shallots
- Gherkins
- Spanish pepper
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt
- Mustard
- Soya sauce

## Directions

1. Cut cold veal or beef into cubes
2. Mix with finely made hard-boiled eggs, pickled shallots, gherkins, and Spanish pepper
3. Mix together with a sauce made from oil, vinegar, pepper, salt, mustard and soya sauce

title: Huzarensla (Hussar Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cold meat
- 2 sour apples
- 1 egg
- 8 cooked potatoes
- 1 beet
- 1 head of lettuce or 1 bunch of curly endive or 100 g field lettuce
- pickled onions and gherkins
- 3 tablespoons salad oil
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the hussar salad as in recipe 95, but use meat instead of herrings.

title: Ice Punch (IJspunsch)
body:
## Ingredients

- One can Rhine wine
- Juice of ten lemons
- Five ounces sugar
- Half a can arak

## Directions

1. Let one can Rhine wine, the juice of ten lemons, and five ounces sugar freeze in an ice bath as previously taught
2. When frozen, put it in a bottle with a wide mouth and add half a can of arak

title: IJspudding (Ice Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter vanilla ice cream
- 1/2 liter coffee ice cream
- French candied fruits

## Directions

1. Make vanilla and coffee ice cream in the known manner (see recipe 880 and recipe 883).
2. Then divide an ice mold, either with tins or with cardboard plates, into vertical parts, carefully fill these with the ice cream types, remove the partitions.
3. Press the ice cream as firmly as possible.
4. Close the mold well, for example with a strip of surgical tape, so that no salt water can enter.
5. Place it in a bucket in which finely crushed raw ice and salt have been added.
6. Wrap the bucket in one or more blankets or molton cloths.
7. Let the pudding stand like this for at least 2 hours.
8. If the raw ice has already largely melted, then it is necessary to remove the water and refill the remainder with newly crushed ice and salt.
9. After the indicated time, remove the mold from the bucket and rinse it with cold water to prevent salt water from entering.
10. Hold it just before turning out in very lukewarm water.
11. The pudding then comes loose from the mold and can be easily turned out.
12. This is done on a chilled plate.
13. Quickly garnish the pudding with halved French candied fruits and if desired whipped cream.
14. Serve it immediately and provide waffle cookies.

title: Ikan Kemirie (Fish with Kemiri Nuts)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Some pieces of fish (haddock, mackerel)
- Salt
- Pepper
- 20 kemiri nuts
- 10 boiled lomboks
- 6 garlic cloves
- 1/2 teaspoon djienten
- 1 teaspoon ketoembar
- 1 teaspoon laos
- A small piece of trassi
- Lemon juice or assem water
- 1/2 l milk with salt or coconut milk
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Pepper and salt the pieces of fish and fry them in the butter or margarine
2. Pound the spices finely and fry them
3. Add the milk with salt or coconut milk to it
4. Let this mixture come to a boil and add the fish with assem water or lemon juice
5. The sauce should look red and be thickened

title: Ingelegde Snijboonen (Pickled String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled string beans
- water
- bouillon
- pepper
- smoked or pickled sausage (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as they come from the salt in a copper kettle
2. Let them drain on a colander when cooked
3. When cold, press them out
4. Set them in water to extract, changing the water a couple of times
5. Stew them with water, bouillon and pepper
6. Alternatively, add smoked or pickled sausage, omitting the bouillon

title: Inleggen van Postelein (Pickled Purslane)
body:
## Ingredients

- purslane
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the purslane
2. Boil briefly in the water that clings to it
3. Once softened and cooled, place with its liquid in bottles or jars
4. Seal airtight and store upside-down on slats

title: Inleggen van Soepgroenten (Pickled Soup Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 basket salad
- 1.5 baskets endive
- 1 basket sorrel
- 8 small baskets purslane
- fat

## Directions

1. Cook salad and endive completely and press very dry
2. Cook purslane and sorrel in their own liquid, keeping the liquid as it helps preserve
3. When all is cooled, mix together
4. Place in bottles with a layer of fat on top
5. Seal well with corks

title: Jachtschotel (Hunter's Casserole)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g cold meat or less
- 1 kg cooked potatoes
- 100 g onion
- 300 g sour apples
- 40 g fat from gravy, margarine or butter
- 4 dl lean gravy with water or bouillon or 4 dl water with 2 Maggi's Bouillon cubes, or 1/2 Maggi's Gravy tablet
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Follow the preparation for philosopher (recipe 85) third method.
2. However, add chopped sour apples to this dish.

title: Jan in de Zak (John in the Sack)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g flour
- 150 g currants, raisins and candied citrus peel
- 1 egg
- 20 g yeast
- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter as for ketelkoek (see previous recipe).
2. Make sure that in the meantime a linen sack is rinsed with lukewarm water and sprinkled with flour on the inside.
3. Put the dough in it, tie the sack closed, but make sure there is sufficient room for the dough to rise.
4. Cook the pudding about 2 hours in a pan of boiling water, but place a small plate at the bottom to prevent sticking.
5. Make sure the water never stops boiling.
6. Test if the pudding is done by inserting a knitting needle, which must come out dry.
7. Let it dry without the sack and serve with a syrup sauce, or butter or margarine and syrup, or butter or margarine and brown sugar.

title: Jonge hanen (Young Roosters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 jonge haantjes (4 young roosters)
- minstens 150 g boter of margarine (at least 150 g butter or margarine)
- wat zout (salt)

## Directions

1. Voor de bereiding zie kip (recept 309).
2. Reken als braadtijd 20 minuten.

title: Jonge Hanen (Young Roosters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 young roosters
- at least 150 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see chicken (recipe 309).
2. Allow 20 minutes to 1/2 hour as roasting time.

title: Juliennesoep (Julienne Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- bouillon made from 500 g beef shank meat
- small carrots
- peas
- small pieces of cauliflower
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make fragrant bouillon from the meat in the usual way.
2. Strain it through a flannel or double-folded linen cloth so that all fibers are removed.
3. Clean the vegetables, divide them into small pieces or cut the carrots with a fancy knife.
4. Wash them and cook them in some boiling salted water or in some bouillon until tender.
5. Let them drain in a colander and warm them in the bouillon.
6. Just before serving, stir in the very finely chopped parsley.

title: Jus Soep (Jus Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or beef jus
- bread crumbs
- old white bread
- eggs
- nutmeg
- lime juice
- egg yolks
- toasted white bread slices

## Directions

1. Take jus, whether from veal or beef, remove the fat and let the jus melt in water
2. Add bread meatballs with some lime juice and let it cook well
3. When serving, beat in some egg yolks according to quantity
4. Make the bread meatballs from crumbled stale white bread, some jus, eggs and a little nutmeg
5. If not using bread meatballs, pour the broth over toasted slices of white bread when serving

title: Kaakjes (Little Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces wheat flour
- 8 eggs (6 with whites)
- 2 ounces currants
- 1 cup melted butter
- milk, yeast, and sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 5 ounces wheat flour and 8 eggs (6 with whites) for a long time.
2. Add 2 ounces currants, 1 cup melted butter, milk, yeast, and sugar.
3. Bake in a tart pan.

title: Kaakjes Anders (Little Cakes, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- 3 ounces melted butter
- ¼ pound sugar
- ¼ pound currants
- cold butter for kneading

## Directions

1. Knead 1 pound wheat flour, 3 ounces melted butter, ¼ pound sugar, and ¼ pound currants with cold butter.
2. Form into flat balls and bake thoroughly in a tart pan.

title: Kaapse wolken (Cape Clouds)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l milk
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 60 g sugar
- 2 eggs
- 25 g Duryea maizena
- some powdered sugar
- red sugar

## Directions

1. Bring the milk slowly to the boil with the vanilla. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, stir in some powdered sugar, form little balls with 2 spoons and cook these briefly in the boiling milk. Remove them with a skimming spoon from the milk. Add the beaten yolks to the maizena, stir the lumps out of this mass and combine it carefully, stirring well, with as much boiling milk until the egg yolks are well warmed. Then pour everything back into the pan, add the sugar and let the custard, stirring with a whisk, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes. Remove the vanilla from it, stir the custard from time to time to prevent a skin from forming. Put the custard in a flat custard dish, lay the egg white balls on it and sprinkle these with some red sugar.

title: Kaasbeschuitjes (Cheese Rusks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 small rusks
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 60 g cheese
- cayenne pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Stir the butter until creamy, add the grated cheese and pepper and salt to taste.
2. Spread this mixture in a thick layer on the rusks and let them become light brown in a baking tin in a moderately warm oven.

title: Kaascroquettes (Cheese Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g grated aged cheese
- 2 dl milk
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- pepper
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Warm the butter or margarine with the flour, stirring, until a smooth mixture, gradually add the milk and cream and let the sauce cook through, stirring (approximately 10 minutes).
2. Mix it with the grated cheese and season with pepper and salt to taste.
3. Let the mixture cool spread out, shape croquettes in the usual manner (see recipe 116), bread them double and fry them brown in hot frying fat.
4. Let them drain on gray paper and place them on a plate covered with a folded cloth.

title: Kaascustard (Cheese Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 thin slices of casino bread
- 4 thick slices of cheese
- some celery salt
- salt
- 3 eggs
- approximately 1/2 l bouillon made from 2 cubes
- aroma or spicy sauce
- some grated cheese
- approximately 50 g butter or margarine
- a few stuffed olives or a pickle

## Directions

1. Grease a long ovenproof dish (preferably rectangular and glass) with some butter.
2. Cut the old bread so that the sandwiches fill the dish without large gaps.
3. Make sandwiches from the slices of bread, filled with cheese and sprinkled with celery salt.
4. Fill the bottom of the dish with these and make a second layer if possible (possibly with more bread).
5. Make sure the bread stays at least 1.5 cm below the rim.
6. Beat the eggs, add bouillon and seasonings.
7. Pour this over the bread and let the dish stand for at least 1 hour so the liquid soaks into the bread.
8. Make sure the liquid still level with the bread afterwards.
9. Sprinkle the top with grated cheese, lay slices of olive or pickle on it and put the dish in a fairly hot oven to set the egg and brown the top.

title: Kaasdomino's (Cheese Dominoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 thin slices of sweet, dark rye bread without crust
- 100 g grated cheese
- 10 g flour
- 15 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl milk
- salt
- Cayenne pepper or ground Spanish pepper
- 1 truffle

## Directions

1. Cut thin, oblong slices of rye bread in the shape of large domino stones.
2. Make a sauce in the usual way from butter or margarine, flour and milk; stir in 75 g grated cheese over heat and season with pepper and salt to taste.
3. Spread this mixture on the rye bread slices and sprinkle the rest of the grated cheese over them.
4. Cut thin strips of truffle and use these to divide the slices into two equal halves.
5. Using round-cut pieces of truffle, make eyes on the stones.

title: Kaasrand met Champignonragout (Cheese Ring with Mushroom Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For the ring: 50 g butter or margarine
- 50 g flour
- 2 dl milk
- 150 g old Dutch or Parmesan cheese
- 2 eggs
- pepper and salt
- For the ragout: 35 g butter or margarine
- 30 g flour
- 2 dl bouillon
- 1 dl cream or milk
- 1 tin of mushrooms
- 50 g cheese
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Grease a rice ring mold thickly with butter or margarine, coat it. Make a thick sauce from butter or margarine, flour and milk and away from the heat mix it with 150 g cheese, pepper and salt to taste, the egg yolks and last the stiffly beaten egg white.
2. Fill the ring 3/4 with it, close it with a lid and let it cook 30 to 35 minutes in a bain-marie. Let it dry without a lid in front of the open oven door and turn it out onto a warm platter.
3. Prepare mushroom ragout in the usual way, stir 50 g cheese through it, fill the ring with it or pour the ragout over it.

title: Kaassandwiches (warm) (Warm Cheese Sandwiches)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dunne sneetjes casinobrood zonder korst (thin slices of casino bread without crust)
- 8 plakjes ham (ham slices)
- 16 plakjes kaas (cheese slices)
- 40 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)

## Directions

1. Cut stale bread (preferably casino bread) into equal, thin slices.
2. Cut off the crusts.
3. Place on each slice a piece of cheese that fits exactly.
4. On half of the slices place a matching slice of ham and now place the other slices, with the cheese facing down, on top.
5. Let butter or margarine turn light brown in the pan, carefully place the cheese sandwiches in it and fry them light brown on both sides.
6. Be careful when turning so that the layers do not slip apart; hold them preferably with a fork or knife.

title: Kaassaus (Cheese Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter bouillon or milk
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt
- 60 g old Gouda cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Add the grated cheese and pepper and salt to taste and serve with cauliflower, salsify, Brussels endive

title: Kaassoesjes (Cheese Puffs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dl water with salt
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 60 g flour
- 2 eggs
- Filling: 50 g butter or margarine, 150 g grated cheese, 1 egg yolk, pepper, salt

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the butter or margarine to a boil
2. Stirring, sprinkle the sifted flour in and keep stirring until the dough comes away from the pan like a ball
3. Then add the eggs one by one away from the heat
4. Using a piping bag or butter syringe, or lay small mounds of this dough on a buttered baking sheet with a teaspoon and bake the puffs light brown and cooked through in 10 to 15 minutes in a hot oven
5. For the filling, stir the butter or margarine until creamy, mix it with the egg yolk and stir in the grated cheese with some pepper and salt

title: Kaassoufflé's (Cheese Soufflés)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 5 eggs
- 100 g old Gouda cheese
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cream the butter or margarine, add the egg yolks one by one, mix them with grated cheese, pepper and salt to taste and finally as airy as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
2. Fill soufflé dishes greased with butter or margarine halfway with this mixture, let them become light brown in a moderately warm oven and rise (approximately 15 minutes).
3. Serve them immediately.

title: Kaasstokjes (Cheese Sticks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter
- 100 g flour
- 5 g salt
- water
- egg yolk
- 50 g Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846), but mix it with almost all of the grated cheese
2. Roll it out the last time to a thickness of approximately 1/2 cm, cut strips from it 10 cm long and 2 cm wide
3. Brush these with beaten egg yolk with water, sprinkle them with the reserved grated cheese
4. Bake them in a hot oven until light brown and cooked through in approximately 15 minutes

title: Kabeljauw (Cod)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 portions of cod (approximately 3/4 kg)
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse the portions well
2. Cook them in boiling water with salt and some vinegar until a fin comes off easily (15 to 20 minutes)
3. Remove them with a fish slice or a pair of skimming spoons from the water and place them on the fish plate
4. Serve with butter sauce, mustard sauce, egg sauce, melted or glazed butter or margarine

title: Kabeljauw, Gekookt (Boiled Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned cod
- coarse salt
- vinegar
- mustard sauce or melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the well cleaned cod into pieces and wash it well
2. Place it in water with a good amount of coarse salt that has been brought to a boil
3. Add a splash of vinegar and constantly skim
4. Let it cook fresh for a few minutes
5. Serve it with a mustard sauce or with melted butter
6. Cook the liver separately with the milt or roe
7. The liver can also be rolled in flour after being briefly boiled and cooled, browned and cooked in butter, and some mustard sauce with vinegar poured over it

title: Kabeljauwstaart met Oesters (Cod Tail with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- one good cod tail
- oysters

## Directions

1. Cook the tail as regular cod
2. Make a sauce with oysters and pour it over

title: Kabeljauwstaart met Oesters (Cod Tail with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- good cod tail
- oyster sauce

## Directions

1. Take a good tail
2. Cook it like ordinary cod
3. Make a sauce with oysters and pour it over

title: Kabinetpudding (Cold) (8 persons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 125 g sugar
- ½ vanilla pod or vanilla sugar
- 1 dl rum or arak
- 4 eggs
- 100 g currants
- 100 g raisins
- 50 g candied citron
- 35 g white gelatin or 30 g powdered gelatin
- angelica and candied fruit pieces

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the currants and raisins, rinse them with lukewarm water, cover with boiling water and cook for 10 minutes. Let them drain well in a sieve.
2. Chop the candied citron finely.
3. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar vigorously together, add as much of the milk mixed with the vanilla that has been brought to a boil until they are thoroughly warmed and the sugar is dissolved.
4. Return everything to the pan, let it bind in a bain-marie while stirring well, but ensure it does not boil.
5. Remove the mass from the heat and stir in the soaked gelatin, mixed dried fruit and rum or arak.
6. Let the mixture cool, stirring occasionally, add the stiffly beaten egg whites, let everything together, also stirring occasionally, become jelly-like and pour it into a pudding mold rinsed with unbeaten egg white.
7. Let the pudding cool and set firmly, turn it out onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.
8. Note: The pudding mold rinsed with egg white can be garnished with strips of angelica and candied fruit pieces.

title: Kalfgehakt (Veal Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 1.5 Dutch pound ground veal
- white bread or hard biscuit soaked in milk
- 2 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Knead the ground veal with milk-soaked bread or biscuit that has been pressed out, 2 eggs, salt, nutmeg, and pepper
2. Mix well together
3. Form one or two balls
4. Braise them with butter in a pan or dish
5. Baste well
6. To thicken the jus, add a little water
7. There must be heat above and below

title: Kalfsbiefstuk (Veal Steak)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg veal steak
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- some milk

## Directions

1. For preparation see beef steak; however use no pepper.
2. Let the butter or margarine become light brown, because the meat is light and must bake fairly long (15 minutes per 500 g).
3. Finish the gravy as indicated for beef steak.

title: Kalfsborst op de Engelsche Manier (Veal Breast in the English Manner)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Salt
- Parsley
- Sorrel
- Butter
- Flour
- Lemon peel
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the veal with some salt in water until done
2. Place the meat on a dish and pour the following sauce over it: take equal amounts of parsley and sorrel, a lump of butter rolled in flour, some finely cut lemon peel and grated nutmeg
3. Let it cook until it becomes a bound sauce
4. Veal ribs or kidney piece can be treated the same way

title: Kalfsbout Gevuld (Stuffed Veal Knuckle)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal knuckle
- Ground veal or pork
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut open the knuckle at the end and fill it with ground meat
2. Bind it closed again
3. Smear it with butter and sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. Brown the butter, add the knuckle with heat from above and below
5. Baste it continuously with its own juices and let it braise for about 2 hours

title: Kalfscarbonaden of Kalfscoteletten (Veal Cutlets)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg carbonades or cutlets (4 pieces)
- 75 g veal fat, butter or margarine
- 1 lemon
- 7 1/2 g salt

## Directions

1. See the previous recipe for preparation.
2. Note: Cutlets or loin carbonades have only small pieces of backbone attached, while rib carbonades have additionally a long bone, a rib.

title: Kalfschenkelsoep (Veal Shank Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shank with bones
- water
- salt
- rice
- vegetables
- veal meat for meatballs
- ground mace

## Directions

1. Treat as veal shank soup
2. You need not cook it the day before as you can leave the bones in
3. This soup needs only three hours of cooking
4. Add the meatballs half an hour before serving

title: Kalfscoteletten met Dikke Saus (Veal Cutlets with Thick Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick veal cutlet
- water
- bouillon
- flour
- butter
- morels (optional)

## Directions

1. Take a thick veal cutlet and be careful not to pound it too flat
2. Lard it very finely
3. Cook it in a pan with water and bouillon
4. Mix flour with bouillon and butter
5. Make a thick sauce with or without morels as desired

title: Kalfsfricadellen (Veal Fricadelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- lean veal
- bacon
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- spices
- parsley
- bay leaves
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Slice lean veal to the thickness of a finger
2. Beat 5 or 6 pieces well and lard them thickly with bacon
3. Put butter in a pan and place the fricadelles lard-side down
4. Braise them gently until cooked
5. Turn them and let them rest a bit longer
6. Remove them and rub a tablespoon of flour well through the pan
7. Add a spoon of bouillon, stirring first to prevent discoloration
8. Return the fricadelles to the pan
9. Add the necessary bouillon with spices, parsley, some bay leaves, and lemon slices
10. Braise it all well together

title: Kalfsfricandeau (Veal Fricandeau)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg veal fricandeau
- 150 g butter or margarine or veal fat, or half veal fat and half butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- (a few sheets of gelatine)

## Directions

1. Rinse the meat and salt it
2. Let the butter or margarine become very light brown or the fat become very hot (especially not too hot, as the meat must remain light in colour, which is not easy due to the long roasting)
3. Roast the meat as indicated for roastbeef (see recipe 227), but calculate roasting time as 35 minutes per 500 g (for a large fricandeau or for fricandeau that will be sliced cold: 30 minutes per 500 g)
4. If necessary, lay a butter-greased paper over the meat in the oven if it becomes too dark in colour
5. Finish the gravy with some water
6. Note: large pieces of veal that roast long enough will give a gelatinous gravy. When this is desired from smaller pieces, dissolve a few sheets of gelatine soaked in cold water or some powdered gelatine in the gravy

title: Kalfsgebraad met Ragoutsaus (Braised Veal with Ragout Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions
- flour
- butter
- water
- braising jus
- gherkins
- pimento
- salt

## Directions

1. Braise two onions and a spoon of flour in butter until golden brown
2. Add a couple of glasses of water and braising jus
3. Add gherkins cut into pieces, pimento, and salt
4. Once the gherkins are soft, place the veal roast in it
5. Let it heat through in the sauce

title: Kalfshersenen met Hollandsche Saus (Calf Brains with Dutch Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains from three calf heads
- water
- vinegar
- white or brown sauce
- eggs
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take the brains from three calf heads and clean them very well
2. Remove the thin membrane around them and the fibers within
3. Place them in boiling water with a little vinegar and let them steam, being careful to keep them whole
4. Dish them up and pour a white or brown sauce over them, made with vinegar and eggs, or with bouillon and vinegar

title: Kalfshersenen met Hollandsche Saus (Calf's Brains with Dutch Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains from three calf heads
- water
- a little vinegar
- white or brown sauce
- vinegar
- eggs
- or bouillon and vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the brains from three calf heads and clean them very well
2. Remove the thin membrane around them
3. Remove the fibers that are in them
4. Place them in boiling water with a little vinegar and let them simmer, taking care that they stay whole
5. Dish them up and pour a white or brown sauce over them, made with vinegar and eggs, or with bouillon and vinegar

title: Kalfshersenragout (Veal Brains Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal brains
- Bouillon
- Eggs
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Finely chopped parsley
- Butter
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Remove the membranes from veal brains and put them in bouillon with some beaten eggs
2. Add salt, nutmeg, finely chopped parsley, butter and lemon slices
3. Braise it with heat under and above until done

title: Kalfskarbonaden (Braised Veal Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops, cleaned of membranes and bones
- melted butter
- brown jus
- parsley
- crushed thyme
- finely chopped shallots
- Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Roll the cleaned veal chops in melted butter
2. Place them in an earthenware pan
3. Add brown jus, parsley, crushed thyme, and finely chopped shallots
4. Braise them slowly
5. When serving, add two or three glasses of Rhine wine

title: Kalfskop Gebraden (Roasted Veal Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal head
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Cold butter
- Crushed rusks
- Water

## Directions

1. After the head has been soaked and blanched, sprinkle the inside with fine salt, nutmeg and pepper
2. Put some cold butter in the brains and bind it closed
3. Smear the head with butter all around, roll it in crushed rusks
4. Place it in a roasting pan with a sufficient amount of water and roast in the oven
5. Serve with the same sauce as the boiled head

title: Kalfskop Gekookt (Boiled Veal Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal head
- Salt
- Egg sauce
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Split the head in half and let it soak overnight in pump water
2. To cook it, bind it and hang it in plenty of cold pump water with a pair of handfuls of salt
3. Let it cook for 3½ hours, watching carefully for scum
4. Serve with egg sauce and grated horseradish

title: Kalfskop in Ragoutsaus (Veal Head in Ragout Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled veal head
- Butter
- Onions
- Flour
- Cooking liquid from the head
- Raisins
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Salt
- Syrup
- Vinegar or red wine

## Directions

1. Cut a boiled veal head, the heart and if desired the lung, into small pieces
2. Fry a couple of finely chopped onions in butter and add a spoonful of flour, stirring until everything is brownish
3. Add some of the liquid in which the head was cooked
4. Add to the sauce some raisins, pepper, cloves and salt, a spoonful of syrup and some vinegar or red wine
5. Let everything cook well together in the sauce

title: Kalfskop op de Wijze van Schildpad (Veal Head Turtle Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal head, cooked and cold
- Butter
- Flour
- Onions
- Meat juices
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Lemon peel
- Cloves
- Salt
- Parsley
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. When the head is done and cold, cut it into square pieces
2. In a pan, put some butter with a couple of spoonfuls of flour, along with a good amount of finely chopped onions and some meat juices
3. Let it cook with the meat
4. Add a little pepper, a couple of bay leaves, lemon peel, some cloves and salt to taste
5. Finally add some chopped parsley and some Madeira wine
6. Let everything braise together

title: Kalfslapjes (Veal Chops)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg veal chops
- at least 75 g veal fat, butter or margarine
- 1 lemon
- 7 1/2 g salt

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD. Wash or scrape the chops and rub them in with the salt.
2. Make the fat, butter or margarine hot in a frying pan and fry the chops brown on both sides in it. (With a small number the chops can be fried directly in the stewing pan.)
3. Place them in a stewing pan, pour the finished gravy over it, add some lemon juice and a few slices of lemon without pits and let the chops stew gently for another half hour to three quarters of an hour.
4. SECOND METHOD. Wash the chops, rub them in with the salt.
5. Roll them through some beaten egg with water, then through breadcrumbs.
6. Fry them in the hot fat, butter or margarine brown and done on both sides (± 20 minutes).
7. Slide the chops carefully back and forth while frying.
8. Remove them from the fat and finish the gravy with some water.

title: Kalfsnierstuk (Veal Kidney Piece)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg veal kidney piece
- 100 g butter or margarine or veal fat, or half veal fat and half butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- (a few sheets of gelatine)

## Directions

1. For preparation see Veal Fricandeau (recipe 239)

title: Kalfsoesters (Breaded Veal Cutlets)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 veal cutlets of 50 to 75 g each
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- 10 g salt
- Lemon juice
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Wash or scrape the veal cutlets and rub them with salt.
2. Beat the egg and mix it with 2 tablespoons water or alternatively 1½ tablespoon water and some lemon juice.
3. Dip the veal cutlets in the egg mixture, making sure it covers all sides well.
4. Roll them in fine breadcrumbs and fry them in butter or margarine that has been heated until hot, carefully until light brown and cooked through (10 minutes).
5. Move them back and forth while frying, but ensure the crust does not come loose anywhere.
6. Finish the jus with a little water.

title: Kalfspoulet (Braised Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast (poulet)
- butter
- nutmeg
- mace
- jus
- salt
- lemon slices
- flour (optional for binding)

## Directions

1. Place the breast in a pan with a knob of butter, nutmeg, and mace
2. Braise until brown together
3. Add jus, a little salt, and several lemon slices
4. Braise thoroughly
5. To thicken, you may add a little flour

title: Kalfsrollade (Veal Roulade)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg veal roulade
- 100 g butter or margarine or veal fat, or half veal fat and half butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- (a few sheets of gelatine)

## Directions

1. For preparation see Veal Fricandeau (recipe 239)

title: Kalfsschijf (Veal Slice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal slice
- Crushed rusks
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Treat as veal ribs, but when half done, sprinkle with crushed rusks
2. Also place lemon slices on the slice and let it braise with them until done

title: Kalfsschijf (Veal Slice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg veal slice
- 100 g butter or margarine or veal fat, or half veal fat and half butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- (a few sheets of gelatine)

## Directions

1. For preparation see Veal Fricandeau (recipe 239)

title: Kalfsschouder (Veal Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal shoulder
- Flour
- Aromatic herbs
- Mushrooms
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Pepper sauce

## Directions

1. Let the shoulder soak in water beforehand, then roll it in flour and fry it in the pan
2. When brown, let it braise in an earthenware dish
3. When almost done, add some aromatic herbs and mushrooms
4. Finish it with some vinegar and chopped onion
5. Serve with pepper sauce

title: Kalfstong met Saus (Veal Tongue with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tongues
- water
- flour
- bouillon
- vinegar
- spices

## Directions

1. Boil the tongues for half an hour and then cook them for three hours until done
2. Make a sauce with flour, bouillon, a splash of vinegar, and a little spices
3. Remove the skin from the tongues
4. Place them on a dish and pour the sauce over them

title: Kalfsvlees Chragout (Veal Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked or roasted veal
- Finely chopped onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Cloves
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut cooked or roasted veal into small cubes
2. Fry some finely chopped onions with butter and flour until done
3. Add bouillon, salt, cloves and some lemon juice
4. Let it stew well with the meat

title: Kalfsvlees Chragout Anders (Veal Ragout, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted veal
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Flour
- Chopped onions
- Wine vinegar or lemon
- Meat drippings
- Pepper
- Mace

## Directions

1. Take roasted veal and cut it with mushrooms into small pieces
2. Roast a lump of butter, stir in as much flour as needed for the ragout
3. Add a couple finely chopped onions
4. Let them braise together until brown
5. Add a couple spoons wine vinegar or some lemon, some meat drippings, pepper and mace
6. Put the meat and mushrooms in

title: Kalfsvleeschpasteitjes (Veal Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- 2.5 ounces ground veal
- shallots
- butter
- 2.5 ounces raw ground veal
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- mace
- bouillon
- egg

## Directions

1. Line small molds with puff pastry about the thickness of a knife back
2. Cut out pieces from the pastry with a tin cutter large enough to serve as lids for the pastries
3. Take 2.5 ounces ground veal, chop some shallots through it and let it cook in a pan with a piece of butter
4. Take 2.5 ounces raw ground veal, pound it finely in a mortar
5. Stir salt, pepper, nutmeg and a little mace through it
6. Cook it over with some bouillon and fill the pastries with it when cold
7. Close the pastries with the lids, brush with diluted egg and bake on a low fire

title: Kalfszwezerikken (Veal Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 2 egg yolks
- milk
- sweetbreads
- bouillon
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix flour, 2 egg yolks, and milk; set to fire and stir until thick
2. Previously cook the sweetbreads in bouillon with a little salt until well done
3. Place them on a dish and pour the above sauce over them
4. Sprinkle grated nutmeg over it
5. Lemon juice poured over is very nice

title: Kalkoen (Turkey)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- at least 250 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 500 to 750 g chestnuts (depending on the size of the bird)

## Directions

1. Wash the turkey inside and outside.
2. Cook the scored chestnuts for 10 minutes in boiling water, remove the outer shell and brown skin and fill the turkey, which has been salted inside and outside, with them.
3. Sew the rear closed.
4. Make the butter or margarine light brown in a roasting pan or iron pot, place the turkey in it, roast it until light brown and cooked through (3 to 3 1/2 hours).
5. Add a little water to the butter or margarine from time to time when it threatens to become too brown.
6. When the turkey is roasted in the oven, cover it with a piece of paper smeared with butter or margarine.
7. Turn it in the iron pot from time to time.
8. Finish the gravy if needed with water or cream.
9. Serve with compote.

title: Kalkoenkuikens (Turkey Poults)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey poult
- 200 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey poult as described in recipe 322.
2. Allow approximately 2 1/2 hours as roasting time.

title: Kalkoensoep (Turkey Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- water
- salt
- rice
- ground mace
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Treat as chicken soup, but the turkey must be well browned before putting it in the vegetables

title: Kalte Ente (Wine Bowl)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle red Bordeaux
- 1 bottle Rhine wine or Mosel wine
- 40 g sugar
- peel of one lemon
- slice of lemon
- 1 bottle champagne or soda water

## Directions

1. Mix the red and white wine together.
2. Add the sugar, the peel of one lemon, a slice of lemon.
3. After a quarter hour, remove the peel and the slice.
4. Pour in the champagne or soda water and serve the bowl in crushed ice.

title: Kampersteur
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- salt and pepper
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs into slices
2. Place them on a plate
3. Add salt and pepper
4. Pour mustard sauce over them

title: Kampersteur (Sliced Hard-Boiled Eggs with Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- pepper
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Slice the hard-boiled eggs
2. lay them in a dish
3. sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. pour mustard sauce over them

title: Kandeel (Mulled Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle Rhine wine
- half pound sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 12 eggs
- half lood cinnamon

## Directions

1. A bottle of Rhine wine, half a pound sugar, the juice of a lemon, 12 eggs and half lood cinnamon are stirred over moderate heat until it boils

title: Kandeel (Spiced Wine)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 bottle Rhine wine
- 1 1/2 dl water
- 20 cloves
- cinnamon stick
- peel of 1/2 lemon
- 150 g brown sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Let the spices infuse for 1 hour in the water.
2. Sieve the liquid and let it cool.
3. Whip the yolks with brown sugar and salt until foamy.
4. While whisking, add wine and spice infusion and let the mass thicken au bain-marie.
5. Pour the drink into small cups.

title: Kaneelsaus (Cinnamon Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 30 g sugar
- 30 g flour
- a piece of cinnamon stick or 1 teaspoon fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix the flour (with the fine cinnamon) and the sugar with a little cold milk into a thick paste
2. Stir out the lumps and add enough milk until a thin paste is obtained
3. Bring the remaining milk (with the cinnamon stick) to a boil
4. Pour the mixed paste in while stirring and let the sauce simmer while stirring (approximately 10 minutes)
5. Present the sauce with cooked rice, barley, etc.

title: Kaneelwafelen (Cinnamon Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces best flour
- Just over 1 ounce sugar
- Equal amount butter
- 2 drams cinnamon
- 1 egg with white

## Directions

1. Make a dough
2. Bake in a waffle iron until done

title: Kappers (Capers)
body:
## Ingredients

- East Indian cherry seed (capers)
- wine vinegar
- whole peppercorns

## Directions

1. Put the East Indian cherry seed in wine vinegar and whole peppercorns in an earthenware pot or bottle
2. Seal it with a bladder

title: Kappertjeskool (Capon Cabbage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 capon cabbages
- 2.5 dl milk
- 40 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. For the preparation, see Savoy Cabbage (recipe 440).

title: Kappertjessaus (Caper Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- small carrot
- sprig of parsley
- 4 white peppercorns
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 1/2 tablespoons capers
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let the spices steep in the liquid, strain it, prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce), mix it away from the heat with the egg yolk, the capers and lemon juice to taste
2. Serve with turbot, sole, calf's tongue, eel, etc.

title: Karnemelk met Appelen (Buttermilk with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cans buttermilk
- old-baked white bread of 10 to 15 cents, cut into pieces
- some peeled good sour apples, cut into cubes
- a little aniseed
- molasses or brown sugar

## Directions

1. Take 3 cans buttermilk
2. Add old-baked white bread cut into pieces
3. Add some peeled good sour apples cut into cubes and a little aniseed
4. Let everything cook together
5. Serve with molasses or brown sugar

title: Karnemelk met Meel (Buttermilk with Flour)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cans buttermilk
- 1 cup flour
- molasses and butter

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup flour into 6 cans buttermilk
2. Cook while stirring constantly
3. For a stiffer version as midday food, use 1 cup flour per 3 cans buttermilk
4. Serve with molasses and butter

title: Karnemelkpap (Buttermilk Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liter buttermilk
- 90 to 100 g flour
- salt

## Directions

1. METHOD 1: In the pan in which the porridge will be cooked, mix the flour thickly with some cold buttermilk. Carefully stir out the lumps from this mixture, gradually add the remaining buttermilk, put the pan on the fire and stir continuously until the porridge is cooked through and done (5 to 10 minutes).
2. METHOD 2: Bring the greater part of the buttermilk to a boil, stirring occasionally. Mix the flour thickly with some cold buttermilk, stir out the lumps and add enough buttermilk until a thin pap is obtained. Pour this stirring into the boiling buttermilk and let the porridge cook while stirring (5 to 10 minutes).

title: Karnemelkpudding (Buttermilk Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter buttermilk or yogurt
- juice of 2 lemons
- grated peel of 1 lemon
- 200 g brown sugar
- 22 g leaf gelatin with a few sheets of red gelatin or 18 g powdered gelatin and a few drops of carmine

## Directions

1. Soak the leaf gelatin 10-15 minutes in cold water, dissolve it with as little boiling water as possible
2. Mix the buttermilk in a bowl with the cooled gelatin, lemon juice, grated lemon peel and sugar
3. Let everything become jelly-like, stirring occasionally
4. Pour the mass into a stone mold rinsed with cold water and let the pudding become cold and stiff
5. Turn it out onto a damp dish and serve with vanilla sauce
6. Note: If powdered gelatin is used, it need not be soaked, only dissolved, and color the pudding with carmine. Do not prepare buttermilk pudding the day before in warm weather, as there is a great chance of fermentation

title: Karper (Carp)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg carp
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the carp, rinse it
2. Cook it in boiling water with salt until a fin comes off easily (approximately 10 minutes)
3. Serve it with melted butter or margarine and very finely chopped parsley

title: Kasseler Rib (Cured and Smoked Pork Rib)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Kasseler rib (cured and lightly smoked pork rib)
- Butter or margarine
- Spanish sauce
- Cognac

## Directions

1. Method 1: Treat the rib as for Hamburger rib. Serve with melted butter or margarine.
2. Method 2: Brown the cleaned rib in butter or margarine (50 g per 500 g) and cook until done (½ hour per 500 g). Serve with Spanish sauce mixed with some cognac.

title: Kastanjepurée (Chestnut Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg chestnuts
- 1/2 dl cream or 40 g butter or margarine
- 2 dl bouillon
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts until tender, as described in recipe 504.
2. Peel them in small portions at a time and press them through a sieve.
3. Mix them with the cream, butter or margarine and enough bouillon to achieve the consistency of mashed potatoes.
4. Stir in pepper and salt to taste and serve the purée with pork cutlets, veal oysters, etc.

title: Kastanjepurée met slagroom (Chestnut Purée with Whipped Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g chestnuts
- 2½ to 3 dl milk
- ¼ stick vanilla
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 60 g sugar
- 2½ dl whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 50 g powdered sugar or bastard sugar
- French fruits

## Directions

1. Wash the chestnuts, cut them deeply, place them with just enough boiling water to cover them and let them cook gently for 10 minutes.
2. Peel them 2 or 3 at a time and place them in the milk, which has been brought to a boil with the vanilla.
3. Let them cook gently in this until done (approximately ½ hour).
4. Press them through a horsehair sieve and mix this purée with the butter and sugar.
5. Place it on a plate and let it cool.
6. Beat the cream with the sifted powdered sugar or bastard sugar until stiff and mix this with the stiffly beaten egg whites and the vanilla pulp.
7. Garnish the purée with this mixture.
8. If desired, place a French fruit here and there on top or pour some sugar burned to caramel over it.

title: Kastanjesoep (Chestnut Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- water
- salt
- veal bouillon
- roasted bread

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts in water with a little salt
2. Carefully remove them from all shells and pound them finely in a mortar
3. Rub them with some veal bouillon through a strainer
4. Add as much bouillon as needed and as desired for the consistency of the soup
5. Let it cook for a moment and add roasted bread cut into cubes

title: Kastanjesoep (Chestnut Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water with salt
- + 20 chestnuts
- 25 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the chestnuts, make a cross on them, set them with enough boiling water so they just cover and let them cook for 10 minutes
2. Peel them then as quickly as possible and let them cook further in 1 l boiling water with salt (+ 1/2 hour)
3. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve, rub the chestnuts through and mix this purée with the cooking liquid
4. Melt 20 g of the butter or margarine and add the flour, bring it to a boil while stirring, add the liquid gradually in small portions while stirring and let the soup cook while stirring until done (+ 10 min.)
5. Add the remaining butter or margarine, salt and nutmeg to taste and if desired some milk
6. N.B. Do not cook the chestnuts until done in the liquid in which they are set, as this would discolor the soup

title: Katjang Goreng (Fried Peanuts)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- peanuts
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel the peanuts
2. Put some butter in the frying pan, let it become hot and fry the nuts with constant stirring until they are light brown in color

title: Kattetongen (Cat's Tongues)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g powdered sugar
- 100 g flour
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- a pinch of salt
- 2 egg whites (+ 1/2 dl)

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the unbeaten egg white, the sifted powdered sugar, some scraped vanilla and lastly the sifted flour.
2. Put the dough in a piping bag with a smooth nozzle and pipe long strips onto a greased baking sheet, ensuring they are spaced apart.
3. Bake them in a moderately warm oven very quickly until light brown and done, remove them from the sheet with a thin, sharp knife and let them cool.

title: Kattetongen (Cat's Tongues)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g powdered sugar
- 100 g flour
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- some salt
- 2 egg whites (approximately 1/2 dl)

## Directions

1. Cream the butter or margarine until fluffy, add the unbeaten egg white, the sifted powdered sugar, some scraped vanilla and lastly the sifted flour.
2. Put the dough in a piping bag with a smooth nozzle and pipe long strips onto a greased baking sheet, making sure they are spaced some distance apart.
3. Bake them in a moderately warm oven.

title: Kaviaarbroodjes (Caviar Sandwich)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices stale casino bread
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 50 g caviar

## Directions

1. Cut very thin slices of bread, remove the crusts, divide them into 2 pieces and toast them light brown
2. Let them cool, spread them fairly thickly and lay a layer of caviar on top
3. If desired, add some very finely scraped onion

title: Keizerinnerijst (Empress Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l milk
- 50 g rice
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 75 g sugar
- 2.5 dl whipped cream
- 8 g gelatin or 6 g powdered gelatin
- some maraschino

## Directions

1. Cook rice porridge in the usual way until very soft (recipe 577), but let the vanilla cook in it.
2. Mix it, just before the rice is done, with the sugar, but make sure the rice grains are not ground fine.
3. Remove the vanilla.
4. Remove from heat and dissolve the soaked and well-squeezed gelatin in it.
5. Let the mixture cool, add the maraschino.
6. Beat the cream stiff, add the rice porridge to it and let this mixture stand, stirring occasionally, until it becomes gelatinous and the rice grains no longer sink.
7. Pour it into a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white and let the pudding become cold and stiff.

title: Kerrysaus (Curry Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 40 to 50 g butter or margarine
- 2 teaspoons curry
- 50 g onion (1 medium-sized)

## Directions

1. Fry flour, chopped onion and curry in the butter or margarine, add the liquid by small amounts, stirring constantly, and let the sauce cook, stirring, (10 minutes)
2. Pour it through a sieve into the sauce bowl and serve with cooked chicken, stewed cold meat, calf poulette, hard-boiled eggs, meat timbale, etc.

title: Kerrysoep (Curry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef and veal (in ratio 2 to 3)
- flour
- 4 large onions
- 2 to 3 Spanish peppers
- 1 to 2 tablespoons curry
- meatballs with chopped meat or hard egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the beef and veal into thin strips, roll them in flour and fry them in the pan
2. Cut 4 large onions into slices
3. Let them cook with 2 to 3 Spanish peppers, skim off the fat
4. Bind the soup with flour mixed with 1 to 2 tablespoons curry
5. Serve with meatballs with chopped meat or hard egg yolks

title: Kerrysoep (Curry Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l beef bouillon or 1 l water and 4 Maggi's bouillon cubes
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 50 g onion
- 1 teaspoon curry
- 1 egg (optional)
- 2 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- piece of Spanish pepper

## Directions

1. Make bouillon in the usual way with the spices and sieve it. Melt the butter, fry the curry, the chopped onion and the flour in it and gradually add, while stirring, the bouillon or water with dissolved bouillon cubes.
2. Let the soup cook while stirring for 10 minutes and if desired pour it through a sieve into the egg that has been beaten in the soup tureen.

title: Kerrysoep van Bruine Bonen (Curry Soup of Brown Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l brown bean liquid with remaining brown beans
- approximately 20 g flour
- approximately 20 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- 3 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 50 g onion
- 1 teaspoon curry

## Directions

1. Let the remaining beans cook fine in the bean liquid to which cloves, bay leaves, and Spanish pepper have been added
2. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve and rub the beans through until only the skins remain
3. Brown the flour and curry in the butter or margarine that has turned brown, add the chopped onion and cook everything together evenly brown
4. Add the bean liquid to this and let the soup cook for approximately 10 minutes while stirring
5. Pour it through a sieve into the soup tureen and serve with bread cut into dice and fried in butter

title: Kerrysoep van Witte Bonen (Curry Soup of White Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bean liquid with remaining beans
- approximately 15 g flour
- approximately 20 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- 50 g onion
- 1 teaspoon curry
- piece of mace
- 1 bay leaf
- some parsley

## Directions

1. Let the remaining beans cook fine in the bean liquid to which mace, bay leaf, and some parsley have been added
2. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve and rub the beans through until only the skins remain
3. Melt the butter or margarine, fry the curry, the chopped onion, and the flour in it
4. Gradually add the bean liquid and let the soup cook while stirring until done and bound (10 minutes)
5. If desired, pour it through a sieve before serving

title: Kerrysoep van witte bonen (Curry Soup with White Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bean cooking liquid with leftover beans
- 15 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 50 g onion
- 1 teaspoon curry
- piece of mace
- 1 bay leaf
- parsley

## Directions
