1. In the bean cooking liquid, to which mace, bay leaf and parsley have been added, let the leftover beans cook until fine. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve and rub the beans through until only the skins remain.
2. Melt the butter or margarine, fry the curry, the chopped onion and the flour in it, gradually add the bean liquid while stirring and let the soup cook and thicken while stirring (10 minutes).
3. If desired, pour it through a sieve, serve with small cubes of bread fried in butter.

title: Kersen (Preserved Cherries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg cherries
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries, wash them and fill the well-cleaned bottles with them up to 3 cm below the rim.
2. Pour enough sugar syrup over them so that the cherries are just submerged.
3. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clips and heat them for 20 minutes to 80° C.
4. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.
5. (For corked bottles see the rules on page 366).

title: Kersen op Brandewijn (Cherries in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cherries
- 150 g sugar
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 0.5 l brandy

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries. Wash them, let them drain. Put them in the well-cleaned bottle with the sugar, the cinnamon stick and the brandy. Seal the bottle airtight and let the contents stand for at least 3 months before using.

title: Kersen op eigen vocht (Cherries in Their Own Juice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg cherries
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries, wash them and remove the pits, but make sure all the juice is caught.
2. Put the cherries with the juice in well-cleaned bottles, scatter some sugar between them, close the bottle with ring, lid and clips and heat it for 20 minutes to 80° C.
3. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.
4. (For corked bottles see the rules on page 366).

title: Kersencompôte (Cherry Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cherries
- approximately 2 dl water
- 1 dl berry juice
- approximately 100 g sugar
- some sago

## Directions

1. Prepare it as strawberry compote, but add the berry juice to the sugar syrup after the cherries have stewed in it and thicken the sauce with mixed sago.
2. Serve the compote for example with pigeon.

title: Kersengebak (Cherry Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 and a half ounces butter
- 2 eggs
- 6 drams sugar
- Half a pound flour
- Candied or fresh cherries

## Directions

1. Make a dough
2. Roll out in round form and make an edge
3. Cover with candied or fresh cherries
4. After one hour of baking, sprinkle with sugar

title: Kersenjam (Cherry Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3¾ kg cherries (approximately 3 kg without pits)
- 2¼ kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- elastic bands
- string
- labels

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries, wash them, remove the pits
2. Put them in a pan, bring them to a boil with a lid on and let them cook without the lid, stirring occasionally, but be careful to prevent burning
3. Add the sugar and let them cook quickly until the marmalade reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369)
4. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983)

title: Kersenpannekoekjes (Cherry Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 175 g flour
- 1/2 l milk
- salt
- 80-100 g butter or margarine
- 50 cherries
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter the same way as for crepes (recipe 643).
2. Melt some butter or margarine in the frying pan, pour a couple of spoonfuls of batter onto it, immediately place some cherries divided into quarters and pitted and destemmed on it.
3. Press them into the batter and cover them with some batter.
4. Fry the pancakes on both sides until light brown and cooked through.
5. Serve with powdered sugar.
6. Note: See the note under the previous recipe.

title: Kersensoep (Cherry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh sour cherries
- water
- lemon peels
- hardtack
- 3-4 cloves
- sugar
- red wine
- salt
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take fresh sour cherries and crush them in a mortar with the pits broken
2. Put everything in a pot with water, some lemon peels, hardtack and 3-4 cloves
3. Cook it until the flesh of the cherries has merged with the mass
4. Now strain it through a sieve
5. Bring it to a boil again and serve it with some sugar and red wine
6. After first stirring through some salt and cinnamon

title: Kerstbrood or Weihnachtsstolle (Christmas Bread or Christmas Stollen)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 20 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- 80 g butter or margarine
- 60 g candied peel
- 35 g almonds
- 75 g raisins
- 35 g orange peel strips
- grated peel of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 60 g sugar

## Directions

1. Make bread dough as described for white bread (recipe 835) from flour, yeast, salt, egg and milk.
2. Let it rise.
3. Then mix in the washed and dried currants and raisins with the finely cut candied peel, the snipped almonds and orange peel strips, the melted butter or margarine, the sugar, the cinnamon, the grated lemon peel.
4. Put the dough in a greased bread pan or let it rise covered in a bowl for 1 hour 15 minutes.
5. Bake the bread in the pan as described for white bread.
6. For a true Weihnachtsstolle, flatten the dough and give it the shape of a round cake.
7. Fold it double, but make sure the bottom half is larger than the top.
8. Press the dough well together.
9. Let it rise covered on a greased baking sheet for a while.
10. Bake the stollen as indicated for white bread (see recipe 835).
11. Dust it with sifted powdered sugar.
12. Tie a red ribbon around it and stick a sprig of holly between.

title: Kerstkrans (Christmas Wreath)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- some water
- salt
- 125 g sweet almonds mixed with 4 bitter almonds
- 125 g sugar
- 1 egg
- some grated lemon zest or scraped vanilla
- candied fruits, orange strips and water glaze for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846), roll it out one last time to a thickness of 1/4 cm and width of 12 cm. Peel the soaked almonds, grind them twice with the sugar, egg and flavoring through the almond mill. Form small rolls from this almond paste on a table dusted with a little flour, approximately 2 cm thick. Place these against each other in the middle of the dough, wrap the dough around them and seal the ends with water. Place the dough with the sealed side down on the baking sheet, form it into a wreath and seal the ends together. Bake the wreath in a hot oven until light brown and cooked (approximately 20 minutes). Let cool, garnish with water glaze, candied fruits and orange strips.

title: Kersttaart (Christmas Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 200 g butter or margarine
- 250 g sugar
- 5 eggs
- 250 g sultana raisins
- 100 g candied peel
- 25 g candied orange peel
- 100 g almonds
- salt
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- grated zest of 1 lemon
- milk
- 200 g powdered sugar
- 2 egg whites
- juice of ¼ lemon
- candied peel
- red cherries
- ¼ liter whipped cream with sugar
- silver balls

## Directions

1. Cream butter or margarine until fluffy
2. Add salt, lemon zest and eggs one at a time, stirring each egg in completely
3. Alternately add sifted flour mixed with baking powder and sugar
4. Stir until batter is thick, white and fluffy
5. Add milk if necessary
6. Finally add peeled and ground almonds with chopped candied peel and orange peel, and cleaned and dried raisins
7. Butter a round cake pan, pour in batter
8. Bake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked through (2 to 2½ hours)
9. Test for doneness by inserting a knitting needle, which should come out dry
10. Turn out onto a cooling rack and let cool
11. After cooling, spread egg white glaze over the cake
12. Pipe figures with whipped cream that has been beaten stiff with sugar
13. Place silver balls here and there
14. Cut holly leaf shapes from the dark part of candied peel and make red berries from red cherries

title: Kervelsoep (Chervil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's foot per person
- cold water
- salt
- semolina or other flour
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- finely chopped chervil

## Directions

1. Take one calf's foot per person, chop it finely and put it on with cold water and salt, cooked for 2 hours, properly skimmed and strained through a sieve.
2. Then make the soup thick with semolina or other flour and stir in one teaspoon of Liebig's Meat Extract (one calculates one teaspoon of Meat Extract for 3 to 4 bowls) to give it a wholly excellent strong flavor.
3. Finally, finely chopped chervil is added and cooked through for 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Kervelsoep (Chervil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-chosen chervil
- flour
- water
- strong bouillon
- eel pieces
- white bread crumb
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop good chosen chervil with some flour finely
2. Stir it in boiling water until the chervil is done
3. Then add a bowl of strong bouillon, some pieces of eel and crumb of white bread, as well as a little salt
4. Let everything cook well done

title: Kervelsoep (Chervil Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bouillon or water with salt
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped chervil
- (1 egg)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine and bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour
2. Add the liquid gradually in small portions while stirring and let the soup cook until done (10 min.)
3. Remove from heat and mix in the fine chervil and carefully add to the egg, which has been beaten in the soup tureen, stirring vigorously

title: Kervelsoep (Chervil Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bouillon or salted water
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 2 tablespoons finely chopped chervil
- 1 egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine and bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour, add the liquid in small amounts at a time while stirring and let the soup cook until done (10 minutes).
2. Remove it from the heat and mix in the fine chervil and if desired add it to the egg that has been beaten in the soup tureen, stirring vigorously.

title: Ketelkoek (Kettle Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces groats
- 6 ounces wheat flour
- 2 ounces raisins
- 4 eggs
- salt
- a piece of yeast

## Directions

1. Mix 5 ounces groats and 6 ounces wheat flour with 2 ounces raisins, 4 eggs, salt and a piece of yeast into a thick batter
2. Put it in a mold or sack and let it rise
3. Put it in boiling water and let it cook
4. After an hour of cooking, turn the mold or sack over and let it finish cooking
5. Serve with melted butter or meat broth

title: Ketelkoek (Kettle Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g flour
- 150 g currants, raisins and candied citrus peel
- 1 egg
- 20 g yeast
- 3 dl lukewarm milk
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put the flour in a bowl, add the egg and lukewarm milk and stir out the lumps from this batter.
2. Add the well-washed currants and raisins, which have been stripped of their stems, the chopped candied citrus peel, the salt, and lastly the yeast mixed with a little lukewarm milk.
3. Let the batter rise, covered, in a lukewarm place (1/2 hour to 3/4 hour).
4. Grease the inside of a hot pudding mold with butter (also the lid) and sprinkle it with fine breadcrumbs.
5. Put the batter in it, but make sure the mold is only filled 2/3 to 3/4.
6. Close the mold firmly and cook the pudding about 2 hours in a water bath, but make sure the water is constantly filled to just below the rim of the pudding mold.
7. Check if the pudding is done by inserting a knitting needle, which must come out dry.
8. Let it dry without the lid and serve the pudding with butter or margarine and syrup or sugar, or if preferred with a syrup sauce.

title: Kievitsbonen (Pied Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g pied beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), reckon as cooking time 1 to 1½ hour. Serve them with fried bacon.

title: Kievitseieren koken (Cooked Plovers' Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- plover eggs
- water
- watercress or similar greens
- red radish

## Directions

1. Wash the eggs, place them in cold water, bring them quickly to a boil and let them cook for 2 minutes.
2. Then immediately place them on a small dish on which there is a folded cloth and some watercress or similar greens.
3. Serve them with red radish.

title: King's Punch (Koningspunsch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of twelve lemons and Chinese oranges
- One kilo sugar
- Half a can water
- Three bottles champagne wine
- One bottle arak

## Directions

1. Cook the juice of twelve lemons and Chinese oranges with one kilo sugar and half a can water until it reaches a thick syrup, well skimmed
2. When it has cooled slightly, add three bottles of champagne wine and one bottle of arak
3. Stir well

title: Kip (Chicken)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kip (chicken)
- 80 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- wat zout (salt)

## Directions

1. Was de kip met lauw water af of veeg ze met een vochtig doekje van binnen en van buiten schoon, wrijf ze in met wat zout.
2. Strooi ook binnenin wat zout en doe er een stukje boter of margarine in.
3. Maak de rest in de braadslede of een stoofpan lichtbruin, leg hierin de kip, bedruip ze aan alle kanten met het hete vet en braad ze lichtbruin en gaar (5 kwartier), sluit de pan.
4. Keer de kip, wanneer ze in een stoofpan gebraden wordt, telkens om en voeg er zo nodig wat water bij als boter of margarine dreigt bruin te worden.
5. Bedruip de kippen, wanneer ze in een braadslede gebraden worden, dikwijls en leg zo nodig een ingevet stuk wit papier op de borsten, wanneer deze te bruin worden.
6. Probeer of de kip gaar is, door met een vork tussen de gewrichten te prikken; deze moet er dan gemakkelijk doorgaan.
7. Maak de jus op de gewone wijze af met water.
8. Leg de getrancheerde kip op een schaal, giet er wat jus over, maak manchetjes aan de poten, geef er gestoofde vruchten of sla bij.

title: Kip a la Marengo (Chicken Marengo)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 grote kip of 2 kleine (1 large chicken or 2 small)
- wat zout (salt)
- 60 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- 1/2 l magere jus met water of bouillon (1/2 liter lean gravy with water or bouillon)
- stukje Spaanse peper (piece of Spanish pepper)
- 2 laurierbladen (2 bay leaves)
- mespunt thijm (pinch of thyme)
- takje peterselie (sprig of parsley)
- 50 g bloem (flour)
- 50 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- 3 eetlepels tomatenpurée (3 tablespoons tomato puree)
- 2 theelepels suiker (2 teaspoons sugar)
- 1 blikje champignons of wat fleurons (1 tin of mushrooms or some fleurons)

## Directions

1. Snijd de vleugels en poten der kip af, verdeel de romp in 4 stukken, was ze, wrijf ze in met wat zout en bak ze lichtbruin (z.g. sauteren).
2. Laat de kruiden in de magere jus, vermengd met zoveel water, dat hij niet meer te zout is, trekken.
3. Zeef het vocht en bereid er op de gewone wijze saus van met de boter of margarine en de bloem.
4. Voeg er de tomatenpurée en de suiker bij en stoof er de stukken kip gedurende 3/4 uur op een zacht vuur in.
5. Leg de kip op een schotel, giet er de saus over en garneer ze met champignons of fleurons.

title: Kip met rijstrand (Chicken with Rice Ring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 grote kip of 2 jonge kippen (1 large chicken or 2 young chickens)
- stukje foelie (piece of mace)
- worteltje (small carrot)
- peterselie (parsley)
- zout (salt)
- 250 g rijst (rice)
- desverkiezende 1 lepel kerry (optionally 1 spoon of curry)
- 1/2 l citroen- of tomatensaus (1/2 liter lemon or tomato sauce)

## Directions

1. Snijd de kippen in stukken, was en kook ze in ruim kokend water met zout en kruiden in een neteldoeks lapje gebonden gaar (1 uur a 5 kwartier).
2. Kook de rijst op de gewone wijze, vermeng hem desverkiezende, als hij half gaar is, met kerry en druk hem zo stevig mogelijk in een met koud water omgespoelde rijstrand.
3. Stort deze dadelijk op de verwarmde schotel, leg er de stukken kip in en bedek ze met een citroen- of tomatensaus, die op de gewone wijze, doch met kippenbouillon, bereid is.

title: Kip met truffels (Chicken with Truffels)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- parsley
- truffels

## Directions

1. Roast the chicken on the spit.
2. Stuff it with breadcrumbs, butter, parsley and finely chopped truffels.
3. Cover the chicken on the spit with paper smeared with butter.

title: Kippenragout (Chicken Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young chickens
- Rainwater
- Salt
- Chicken feet, hearts and gizzards
- Veal balls
- Sweetbreads
- Asparagus
- Parsley roots
- Nutmeg
- Veal bouillon
- Egg yolks
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut well-cleaned young chickens in pieces and cook them in rainwater with salt, skimming well
2. Put them with the chicken feet, hearts and gizzards, along with some veal balls and a couple sweetbreads, in a pan
3. Add asparagus, parsley roots, fine salt and nutmeg
4. Cover all with needed veal bouillon and let it cook again
5. Remove the parsley roots and braise the ragout with some egg yolks and flour

title: Kippensoep (Chicken Soup, 8 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 soup chicken
- 2 liters water with salt
- mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- bay leaf
- 2 small carrots
- parsley
- 30 g rice
- 60 g flour
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the washed and cut-up chicken in the water with salt and spices (approximately 2.5 hours).
2. Strain the bouillon and cut off the soft, white pieces of meat from the chicken to serve as garnish in the soup.
3. Add the bouillon, after having carefully skimmed off the fat, while stirring to the melted butter or margarine and which has then been brought to the boil together with the flour and from which the lumps have been rubbed out.
4. Let the soup cook while stirring until done (10 minutes).
5. Warm the pieces of meat in it, add the rice, which has been cooked until tender in some chicken bouillon or water with salt, and carefully pour the soup to the egg yolks, which have been beaten in the soup tureen.

title: Kippesoep (Chicken Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two chickens
- water
- salt
- rice
- ground mace
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop one or two chickens into pieces and cook them in water, skimming well
2. When the meat is well cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and hang it with the meat again, adding rice, mace and salt; cook until the soup is bound
3. When serving, it is good and tasty to beat a couple of egg yolks through it
4. You can also add vegetables to this soup

title: Kippesoep voor Zieken (Chicken Soup for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- one old rooster
- water
- salt
- vermicelli
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take an old rooster and treat it as described above for chicken soup, but use vermicelli instead of rice
2. When serving, stir egg yolks through it and remove the meat
3. This soup must cook one hour longer

title: Kippetjes in Zure Room (Chicken in Sour Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tender young chickens
- salt
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 1 small onion
- 1/3 l sour cream
- 1 teaspoon paprika

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned chickens into pieces.
2. Salt them and stew them in the butter or margarine with a chopped onion (approximately 30 minutes).
3. Pour in the sour cream and add the paprika.
4. Let the chickens stew in this sauce for another quarter hour.
5. Add more water if necessary.

title: Kipsalade (Chicken Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted chickens (two or three)
- oil
- vinegar
- head lettuce
- hard-cooked eggs (4)
- anchovies
- capers
- tarragon
- pimpernel

## Directions

1. Take the leftovers of two or three roasted chickens and cut them into small pieces.
2. Place them in a pan and pour oil and vinegar over them, as for salad.
3. Arrange them on a platter and around the edge place head lettuce cut into quarters.
4. On each quarter place a hard-cooked egg, also divided into quarters.
5. Fill the spaces between with anchovies, capers and chopped tarragon and pimpernel.
6. Before serving, dip the lettuce quarters in a mixture of oil and vinegar, then pour some more of this mixture over the platter.

title: Kleine cakes (Small Cakes - approximately 15 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 200 g brown sugar
- 250 g flour or 220 g flour and 20 g Duryea cornstarch
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- grated zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter as described in the previous recipe.
2. Fill buttered cake pans 3/4 with the batter.
3. Bake the cakes in a fairly warm oven until light brown and done (12 to 15 minutes).

title: Knijpkoekjes (First Recipe) (Pinched Cookies - First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flour
- 300 g sugar
- 150 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- 5 g fine cinnamon
- some water

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients well together, add some water (approximately 1/2 to 1 dl), knead it in quickly and make approximately 80 to 90 small balls from this amount.
2. Clean the heated pinching iron with some salt and a piece of grey paper.
3. Grease it the first time with some butter or margarine, place it in the middle of a ball of dough, pinch the iron shut and bake the cookies above a moderate fire or above a gas flame on both sides until light brown and done.
4. Remove them immediately from the iron and let them cool.

title: Knijpkoekjes (Pinch Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces flour
- 2 and a half ounces syrup
- Aniseed
- Butter or fat
- Sweet milk
- 3 to 3.5 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a firm batter with flour, syrup, aniseed and butter or fat
2. Thin with sweet milk and eggs
3. Bake in a small waffle iron until done

title: Knijpkoekjes (Second Recipe) (Pinched Cookies - Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flour
- 250 g butter or margarine
- 250 g bastard sugar
- 2 eggs
- 10 g fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients well together.
2. Form approximately 80 balls from this dough.
3. Clean the heated iron with salt and a piece of grey paper.
4. Grease it the first time with some butter or margarine, place it in the middle of a ball of dough, pinch the iron shut and bake the cookies above a moderate warm fire or above a gas flame on both sides until light brown and done.
5. Remove them immediately from the iron and let them cool.

title: Knoedels (Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wheat flour
- Eggs
- Water
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough from 5 ounces of wheat flour and eggs
2. Form it into balls and roll them out again
3. Let them dry a little on a board
4. Then fold them in half and cut them into strips the width and thickness of a pipe stem
5. Cook them in water with a little salt
6. Once cooked, transfer the knoedels to cold water and let them drain and dry again
7. Melt butter in a pan and place the knoedels in it
8. Beat 4 eggs and pour them over, shake it a couple of times and let them brown in the butter

title: Koffeschuimpjes (Coffee Foam)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong coffee
- Cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 ounces sugar

## Directions

1. Take strong coffee and put it in a can of cream in which 6 egg yolks and 4 ounces sugar have been beaten
2. Pour it into forms and let it become done in the oven

title: Koffie-Créme (Coffee Cream) (for 6 people)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l whipping cream
- Approximately 3 tablespoons coffee extract
- Approximately 80 g powdered sugar or brown sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the cream stiff, mix it with the sifted powdered sugar or brown sugar and the coffee extract.
2. Serve this custard with biscuits or fill sweet fritters with it.

title: Koffie-ijs (Coffee Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/2 liter milk
- 2 tablespoons Nescafé
- 240 g sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the whole as vanilla ice cream; mix the Nescafé with egg yolks and sugar.

title: Komkommer met Room (Cucumber with Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cucumber
- 3 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Treat the cucumber as described above.
2. Let the strips, sprinkled with salt, stand for some time.
3. Let them drain as dry as possible, place them on the plate and pour some cream over them.

title: Komkommers in azijn (Cucumbers in Vinegar)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cucumbers
- 1½ bottles pickling vinegar
- 2½ g turmeric
- 5 g dried ginger
- 2 Spanish peppers
- 50 g mustard seed
- Some salad oil

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers thinly.
2. Taste the ends to see if they are not bitter.
3. Cut them off if necessary.
4. Cut them lengthwise in half, scoop out the seeds with a spoon and cut the cucumbers into blocks.
5. Set them aside for a night sprinkled with salt, then let them drain as dry as possible through a colander and place them in a well-cleaned Cologne pot or jam jars.
6. Arrange the spices between them.

title: Komkommers in azijn (Pickled Cucumbers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cucumbers
- 1½ bottle pickling vinegar
- 2½ g turmeric
- 5 g dried ginger
- 2 Spanish peppers
- 50 g mustard seed
- some salad oil

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers thinly. Test the ends to see if they are not bitter. Cut them off if necessary. Cut them lengthwise in half, scoop out the seeds with a spoon and cut the cucumbers into cubes.
2. Set them aside for one night sprinkled with salt, then let them drain as dry as possible in a colander and put them in a well-cleaned Cologne pot or jam jars.
3. Arrange the spices between them, but first tie the mustard seed in a piece of cheesecloth. Pour vinegar over them so that the pieces are amply covered and pour a layer of salad oil on top.
4. Close the pots with damp parchment paper. Provide them with a label with name and date.

title: Komkommersalade met Aardappelen (Cucumber Salad with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers, peeled
- salt
- cooked potatoes, cold
- onions, finely cut
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled cucumbers into thin slices.
2. Sprinkle with salt and let them drain.
3. Lay slices of cold cooked potatoes on a dish.
4. Toss everything with finely cut onions, salt, oil, vinegar, and pepper.

title: Komkommersaus (Cucumber Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cucumbers
- water
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take 4 cucumbers, cut them into round slices, not too thin, and press them out between linen
2. Put water and butter in a pan and bring to a boil
3. When it boils well, add the cucumbers and stir constantly so it doesn't burn
4. Add some spoonfuls of bouillon
5. Remove the cucumbers and cook the sauce a bit longer
6. Add the cucumbers back and remove from heat

title: Komkommersla (Cucumber Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cucumbers
- 2 to 3 spoonfuls salad oil
- approximately 2 spoonfuls vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- celery salt

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut off the bitter points, slice or shave them into thin slices or make long, thin strips with a mandoline.
2. Mix them with oil, vinegar, salt, celery salt and pepper.
3. Sprinkle some finely cut parsley over them.

title: Komkommersla (Cucumber Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cucumber
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- finely chopped parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumber and cut off the bitter ends.
2. Using a sambal grater, make thin strips of it.
3. Quickly mix them with some pepper, salt, oil and vinegar and place them on the plate.
4. If desired, sprinkle some parsley over it.

title: Konijn (Rabbit)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 150 g butter or margarine
- some salt

## Directions

1. Roast rabbit in the same way as indicated for hare (recipe 300) (roasting time approximately 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours).

title: Konijnen- of Jagerssoep (Rabbit or Hunter's Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one white cabbage
- water
- small piece of bacon
- two carrots
- two onions
- parsley
- rabbit

## Directions

1. Cut a white cabbage into four pieces and cook it in an iron pot with water and a small piece of bacon
2. Add two carrots, two onions and a little parsley
3. Take the rabbit and cut it into five pieces
4. If the rabbit is old, add it immediately; otherwise add it when the soup is half cooked
5. Let it cook for two and a half hours
6. Wild rabbits are to be preferred over tame ones

title: Konijnensoep (Rabbit Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 rabbits
- butter
- parsley
- onions sliced into rings
- salt and pepper
- 1.5 hectograms flour
- 1 glass red wine
- 3 liters water
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- rabbit blood
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Take one or two rabbits from which you have saved the blood for thickening. After they are well cleaned, cut them into pieces and let them brown slightly in butter, parsley and sliced onions. Add some salt and pepper.
2. When the rabbit pieces are well colored, make a roux of 1.5 hectograms flour, 1 glass of red wine and 3 liters of water.
3. Let the rabbits become done, and when done, pour them through a strainer, clean each piece well, taking care that no small bone fragments remain.
4. Put all the rabbit pieces in a pot. Let the cooking liquid simmer gently on the side of the stove so you can skim it well. Keep the rabbit pieces warm.
5. After the cooking liquid has simmered gently for half an hour, add Liebig's Meat Extract to it and thicken it with the blood, after you have skimmed off the foam. Place the rabbit pieces (without heads) in the soup tureen, pour the cooking liquid over with a little Cayenne pepper.

title: Koninginnesoep (Queen's Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 kg chicken pullet
- 1 liter water
- salt
- mace
- Spanish pepper
- carrot
- sprig of parsley
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 dl cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup as described in recipe 75, but do not use rice and finish the soup with the yolks, which have been beaten with the cream.

title: Konneveesjes (Orange Flower Meringue Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg white from 1 egg
- 1 small cut orange flower
- grated loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white until completely foamy.
2. Mix with the small cut orange flower and grated loaf sugar to make a thick paste.
3. Form into small balls by hand and bake in a tart pan with little heat.
4. Good to serve with tea.

title: Kooisla (Cabbage Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 g red cabbage
- 40 g white cabbage
- ½ head of lettuce
- 5 red radishes
- salad dressing from recipe 952

## Directions

1. Remove the cabbage varieties from the outer hard leaves, wash them and grind or cut them fine. Mix them with the lettuce, which is treated in the usual way. Stir the mixture lightly through the salad dressing. Garnish it with slices of red radish.

title: Kool Bij Vleesch (Cabbage with Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- white headed cabbage
- coarse pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Cut the white headed cabbage into quarters
2. Cook until it just begins to fall apart
3. Let drain on a colander
4. While still warm, lay in clean cloths and let stand overnight
5. Rub with coarse pepper and salt
6. Pack as tightly as possible in a pot
7. Pour vinegar mixed with mustard over it, one small spoon per cabbage
8. It must be well covered
9. Rinse with vinegar before use

title: Koolraap (Rutabaga)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1 kg rutabaga
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour or 10 g cornstarch (Duryea maizena)
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the rutabaga into not too thick slices, peel them within the yellow edge and cut them into strips.
2. Wash them, place them in boiling water so that they are just covered, add salt and let them cook until tender (approximately 1 hour).
3. Place them in a colander to drain and stew them for 5 minutes in a sauce prepared in the same way as cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328) or cornstarch sauce (see recipe 329).
4. Grate some nutmeg over them in the bowl.

title: Koolsalade (Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- warm sour sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage very long and fine.
2. Pour warm sour sauce over it.

title: Koolsoep (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 savoy cabbage
- 2 hectograms lean bacon
- salt and pepper
- parsley, thyme, and bay leaf
- 1 hectogram carrots
- 1 hectogram onions
- 2 cloves
- 4 liters water
- 2.5 decagrams bread
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves of the savoy cabbage as they are usually tough, cut the cabbage into 4 pieces, and wash them in plenty of water.
2. Boil them for 10 minutes with 2 hectograms of lean bacon in boiling water.
3. Then place them for one hour in plenty of cold water. Through this treatment you will have made the cabbage easily digestible.
4. Take the cabbage and carefully press all the water out, then season each piece with some salt and pepper.
5. Make a bundle of parsley, thyme and bay leaf, 1 hectogram carrots and 1 hectogram onions and stick 2 cloves in one of the onions.
6. Place the four pieces of cabbage in a pot or cooking pan that is large enough to contain four liters of water with the bundle and the vegetables.
7. Place the 2 hectograms of lean bacon that have been cooked with the cabbage on top of the cabbage. Pour 4 liters of water over it.
8. Let this come to a boil together, skim it carefully and let it simmer gently for 3 hours.
9. Then remove the vegetables and cabbage from the liquid. Cut the four pieces of cabbage into small pieces so that they distribute well in the soup.
10. Place them in the soup tureen, in which you have placed 2.5 decagrams of bread (cut into pieces). Pour the cooking liquid over this and add Liebig's Meat Extract to taste.

title: Koolsoep (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole white cabbage
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take one whole white cabbage and slice only the leaves finely and cook them briefly
2. Then fry the cabbage in a little butter until it begins to turn brown
3. Cook it for half an hour with bouillon to make soup

title: Koolsoep (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 savoy cabbage
- 2 hectograms lean bacon
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take a savoy cabbage, remove the outer leaves as they are usually tough, cut the cabbage into 4 pieces.
2. Wash it in plenty of water.
3. Let it come to a boil for 10 minutes with 2 hectograms of lean bacon in boiling water.
4. Subsequently place it for one hour in plenty of cold water.
5. By this treatment you will make the cabbage tender.

title: Koolsoep (Cabbage Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l light veal bouillon or 1 l water and 4 Maggi bouillon cubes
- 1/4 Savoy or white cabbage
- 1/2 tablespoon curry
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Melt the butter or margarine in a pan, add the cabbage cut into thin strips and the curry and let the cabbage braise in the butter or margarine while stirring occasionally (+ 3/4 hour), but make sure it remains light in color
2. Add the flour and gradually add the bouillon or water with the bouillon cubes
3. Let the soup cook while stirring until done (10 minutes)

title: Koomkoekjes (Comb Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound butter
- One pound flour
- 1 whole egg and 2 yolks
- 1 tablespoon milk or cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Put butter in flour
2. Add whole egg and two yolks with milk or cream
3. Make a not too firm dough
4. Let stand 3 to 4 hours in the cellar
5. Roll out
6. Cut into small angular or round cookies
7. Sprinkle with sugar
8. Bake light brown

title: Koomsche Boontjes (Pickled Roman Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- roman beans
- butter brine

## Directions

1. Cook the beans
2. Let drain and cool
3. Place in a pillowcase or small linen bags in butter brine
4. Weight down so they remain submerged
5. To eat, soak beforehand and stew as fresh beans

title: Kopje Melkchocolade (Cup of Milk Chocolate)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons Blooker's cacao
- 4 teaspoons sugar
- 1 1/2 dl milk

## Directions

1. Mix the cacao with the sugar in the designated cup.
2. Stir in cold milk until a smooth paste without lumps is obtained.
3. Pour the boiling milk over it.

title: Kopje Waterchocolade (Cup of Water Chocolate)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 teaspoon cacao
- 4 1/2 teaspoon sugar
- 1 1/2 dl boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix the cacao with the sugar in the designated cup.
2. Stir in cold water or milk until a smooth paste without lumps is obtained.
3. Pour the boiling water over it.

title: Kopschuimpjes (Head Meringues)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 250 g sugar
- 1 spoon powdered sugar
- a few drops of carmine or 15 g cacao

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar (with the cacao) in the manner as described for borstplaat.
2. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, add to it, stirring briskly, the warm sugar solution and the sifted powdered sugar.
3. Let the mixture cool a little, pipe through a piping bag with fluted nozzle onto a butter-greased and flour-dusted baking sheet in tall meringues.
4. Let them in a very lukewarm oven slowly become dry and hard, but see to it that they remain as light as possible in color.

title: Koud Vlees met Aspic (Cold Meat with Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cold meat (preferably veal)
- 2 dl curry or béchamel sauce
- For the garnish: 3 dl aspic in two colours
- notched lemon slices
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin slices, place them on a platter and cover them with the sauce, which is prepared in the usual way and, being warm, tastes sharp, by which cold it is usually good in flavour.
2. Let the sauce cool down and pour a thin layer of aspic (see recipe 555) over it. Garnish the platter further with 2 colours of very finely cut aspic with fresh sprigs of parsley, capers and notched lemon slices.

title: Koude Haringsaus (Cold Herring Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Herring
- Sweet milk
- Finely chopped onions
- 3 hard boiled eggs
- Pepper
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak a herring in sweet milk
2. Pull out the bones
3. Stir it into a sauce with some finely chopped onions, 3 hard boiled eggs, pepper, oil and vinegar

title: Kreeft in aspic (en daube) (Lobster in Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large can lobster
- 1 l white aspic
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the aspic in the usual way, pour a thin layer into a stone mould rinsed with cold water or a rice ring or into small timbale moulds and let it cool.
2. Place some pieces of lobster on it, alternated with small figures of egg white, capers and parsley.
3. Stick these with some lukewarm aspic, let the mass cool again, pour aspic over it until everything is covered and let it cool.
4. Continue until the entire mould or ring is filled.
5. Turn the daube out onto a moist plate and garnish it with lettuce and mayonnaise.

title: Kreeft met sla en mayonnaise (Lobster with Lettuce and Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small lobsters
- 2 lettuce heads
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Bring plenty of water to a boil in a large pan, immerse the lobsters in it, add plenty of salt and cook the lobsters according to their size for 20-30 minutes.
2. Remove them from the water, remove the green part, the gills, stomach and intestines.
3. Extract the meat from the body and claws by cutting these open.
4. Place the lobster with the head on a pedestal of toasted bread, placed in the middle of a large platter.
5. Arrange around it some cleaned, washed and well-drained lettuce leaves, place the pieces of lobster on these and pour or spray some mayonnaise between them.
6. Present lettuce and mayonnaise separately, or make a salad from it.

title: Kreeftencocktail (Lobster Cocktail)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tin of lobster or 1 fresh lobster (cooked)
- 4 dessert spoons mayonnaise
- 4 dessert spoons tomato ketchup
- 1 spoon sherry
- a splash of cognac
- a few drops of lemon juice
- English sauce and pepper
- a few lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. For garnish, set aside 4 nice pieces of lobster separately
2. Cut the rest into pieces and divide them over 4 chilled coupes or glasses
3. Mix together mayonnaise, ketchup, sherry, cognac, lemon juice, English sauce and pepper
4. Adjust the mixture to taste and pour it over the lobster pieces
5. Garnish the glasses with a small lettuce leaf and nice pieces of lobster

title: Kreeftensalade (Lobster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked lobster (usually from tins)
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Pepper
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Tarragon (finely chopped, sometimes)
- Parsley (finely chopped)

## Directions

1. Take cooked lobster, usually from tins, cut it into pieces
2. Arrange decoratively with salad
3. Make a sauce from hard-boiled eggs, pepper, salt, oil, vinegar, sometimes finely chopped tarragon and parsley
4. Dress the salad with this sauce

title: Kreeftensoep (Lobster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7-8 lobsters
- water
- butter
- veal bouillon
- nutmeg
- salt
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Take 7-8 lobsters, cook them with a little water done and remove the shells
2. Crush the shells very finely and fry them with butter
3. When brown, rub them through a sieve and put them with as much veal bouillon as you reckon necessary for soup in an earthen pan
4. Let them cook and add the previously cooked lobsters
5. When serving sprinkle grated nutmeg and salt to taste into it and serve it on slices of toasted bread

title: Kreeftesaus (Crayfish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish butter
- Flour
- Pure calf juice
- Pepper
- Mace
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Stir a couple spoons flour through crayfish butter and let it fry brown briefly
2. Add pure calf juice, a little pepper, mace and lemon juice
3. Let it cook through

title: Kreeftesaus Anders (Crayfish Sauce, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh butter
- Half a spoon meal
- Mace
- Lemon juice
- Bouillon
- 1 ounce butter
- Cooked and peeled crayfish
- A couple egg yolks

## Directions

1. Set some fresh butter with half a spoon meal to fire briefly
2. Add mace, lemon juice and bouillon
3. When it boils, add more than 1 ounce butter and a good portion of cooked and peeled crayfish
4. Stir a couple egg yolks finely into the sauce

title: Krentenbrood (Currant Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 10 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 2.5 dl milk
- 75 g currants
- 75 g raisins
- 25 g candied peel

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for white bread.
2. Knead in the well-washed currants and raisins, which have been pitted, and the chopped candied peel, as soon as the starter has been kneaded with the remaining milk and remaining flour.
3. Bake the bread as indicated in recipe 835.

title: Krentenbroodjes (Currant Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 125 g currants
- 125 g raisins
- 50 g candied peel
- 15 g yeast
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 2.5 dl milk
- 1 egg yolk
- 7.5 g salt
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the known manner (see recipe 835) from flour, yeast, salt, milk, melted butter or margarine and egg yolk.
2. Let the dough rise.
3. Wash and dry the currants and raisins and chop the candied peel.
4. Mix them through the dough.
5. Form small balls from this.
6. Place them on a thinly greased baking sheet.
7. Let them rise for about 30 minutes and bake them in a hot oven in about 20 minutes until done and light brown.
8. Brush them with a thin paste of milk and sugar to make them shiny.
9. Later dust them with sifted powdered sugar.

title: Krentenpannekoekjes (Currant Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 175 g flour
- 1/2 l milk
- salt
- 80-100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g currants

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter as indicated for crepes (recipe 643).
2. Stir in the currants, which have been rinsed in lukewarm water and had their stems removed.
3. Fry thin pancakes with the butter or margarine, light brown and cooked through on both sides.
4. Serve with brown sugar.
5. Note: Do not make these pancakes too thin, as the currants will burn.

title: Kringelen (Rings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g flour
- 375 g sugar
- 375 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- 10 g fine cinnamon
- grated zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients together until well mixed
2. Roll thin strips from the dough approximately 10 cm long
3. Form into rings by pressing the ends together
4. Place on a greased baking sheet with some distance between them
5. Bake in the middle of a moderately warm oven until lightly brown and done (15 minutes)
6. Remove with a pancake knife from the sheet and let them cool

title: Kroepoek (Shrimp Crackers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- kroepoek
- cooking oil or frying fat
- table salt

## Directions

1. Heat cooking oil or frying fat in a frying pan
2. Dip a small quantity of kroepoek in the hot fat, where they will expand greatly
3. Turn the kroepoek if necessary and let them fry on the other side
4. Ensure they become cooked but remain light in color
5. Let the crispy kroepoeks drain on grey paper
6. They can be kept crisp for some time in well-sealed tins
7. Sprinkle with table salt for serving

title: Krop Salade (Head Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce
- hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- mustard
- bread sugar
- tarragon and pimpernel (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the outermost leaves of the heads and cut the hearts in half, then wash them.
2. If the lettuce is rather hard, beat a few drops of oil into the water.
3. When serving, lay some hard-boiled eggs on top of the salad and toss them with oil, vinegar, and a little salt.
4. A little mustard and bread sugar make it very pleasant. Tarragon and pimpernel may also be added.
5. For wilted head lettuce, soak it for a couple of hours in cold water and remove the outermost leaves and the thick stems that are hard.

title: Kropsalade (Lettuce Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce head
- salt
- water
- veal mince
- jus or butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook whole with a little salt
2. Let drain
3. In each head insert a small ball of cooked veal mince prepared in the frying pan
4. Stew with a little jus or butter and nutmeg
5. When serving, pour jus or melted butter over it

title: Kropsla (Lettuce Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large or 3 small heads of lettuce
- 2 eggs
- 3 spoonfuls salad oil
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar or herb vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- optionally a splash of Maggi's Aroma
- chopped tarragon and pimpernel
- optionally sliced leek, or chives, or onion

## Directions

1. Remove the lettuce of the outer leaves, the hard green leaves and the leaf ribs.
2. Pluck the whole leaves into pieces, wash them, let them drain as dry as possible in a colander or shake them in a salad spinner.
3. Make hard-boiled egg fine in the salad bowl, stir in the oil, vinegar, mustard, pepper and salt, optionally the Maggi's Aroma.
4. Mix the lettuce as quickly and lightly as possible with this sauce, add the finely cut green herbs (and the leek, onion or some cut chives).
5. Optionally place a hard-boiled egg cut into wedges as garnish on the salad.
6. Note: Instead of oil and vinegar one can also use mayonnaise or salad sauce.

title: Kruidenkarbonaden (Herb Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops, cleaned of membranes and bones
- Melted butter
- Brown meat juices
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Shallots
- Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Roll the cleaned chops in melted butter and place them in an earthenware pan
2. Add brown meat juices, parsley, crushed thyme and finely chopped shallots
3. Let them braise
4. When serving, add two to three glasses of Rhine wine

title: Kruidensalade (Herb Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- pimpernel
- chervil
- sorrel
- watercress
- tarragon
- tripmadam
- purslane
- sugar loaf
- cooked beet
- salt
- oil
- herb vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together.
2. Toss with salt, oil, and herb vinegar.

title: Kruisbessen (Dried Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- preferably picked green

## Directions

1. Pick as green as possible
2. Place dry in bottles
3. Cork well and seal carefully with sealing wax
4. Store on slats, inverted

title: Kruisbessen (Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. After properly picking the berries, wash them clean and let them stew done in a pan with some water
2. Press them through a colander
3. Add sugar and cinnamon to taste, as well as beaten eggs to bind

title: Kruisbessen te Konfijten (Candied Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 parts sugar
- 7 and one-half parts gooseberries with stems and crowns removed

## Directions

1. Cook three parts sugar until it becomes syrupy; then add seven and one-half parts of berries with stems and crowns removed and let everything come to a boil
2. Then remove them to strain them, and finally cook the juice to a thick syrup
3. As soon as this is cold, place the berries with this syrup in bottles

title: Kruisbessenjam (Gooseberry Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg gooseberries
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- labels

## Directions

1. Clean the gooseberries, wash them, put them in a pan with as much water as necessary to prevent burning during the first moments.
2. Let them cook down.
3. Add the sugar, bring them to a boil with the lid on and let the jam cook without the lid, stirring occasionally, until it reaches the required thickness.
4. Treat it further entirely as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Kruisbessenmoes (Gooseberry Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg gooseberries
- 100 g sugar
- some water
- some sago or 1 egg
- some chalk

## Directions

1. Clean the berries by removing the stems and sepals.
2. Wash them, let them cook slowly on gentle heat in boiling water until they become sauce (10-15 minutes).
3. Rub them through with a wooden spoon or through a horsehair sieve, mix the sauce with the chalk and sugar and bind it with a little mixed sago, which must cook briefly, or with the egg beaten in a small bowl, into which the hot sauce is stirred carefully.

title: Kwakerspodding (Quaker's Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 crushed rusk
- 3 beaten eggs
- almond essence
- 1 grated lemon zest
- cinnamon
- sugar
- nutmeg
- sufficient butter
- 1 piece yeast
- berry juice sauce
- cinnamon
- sugar
- lemon zest
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a batter of proper thickness from 4 crushed rusk, 3 beaten eggs, almond essence, 1 grated lemon zest, cinnamon, sugar, nutmeg, sufficient butter and 1 piece of yeast
2. put in a mold and let it rise
3. when risen, close the mold well
4. set it half-way in boiling water and let it cook for a good hour
5. for this pudding use a sauce of berry juice, cinnamon, sugar and lemon zest, cooked a long time and then strained, after which butter is added and it is boiled once more

title: Kweepeergelei (Quince Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds peeled quinces
- 1 pound good tart peeled apples
- 1 to 2 bowlfuls water
- three fourths pound best bread sugar per pound of liquid
- juice of 1 to 2 lemons
- cooked lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook 2 pounds peeled quinces with 1 pound good tart peeled apples and 1 to 2 bowlfuls of water into a pulp
2. Let it strain gently through a bag or coarse cloth overnight
3. For each pound of liquid, take three fourths pound best bread sugar and the juice of 1 to 2 lemons
4. Cook for three quarters of an hour
5. Have some cooked lemon zest cut into strips in the bottom of the cups or molds
6. Add the lemon zest and lemon juice at the very end of cooking, as otherwise the lemon loses its flavor

title: Kweeën te Konfijten (Candied Quinces)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- equal weight sugar as quinces
- cochineal for coloring
- water
- cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Weigh the fruits and take the same weight of sugar
2. Peel the fruits and remove the seed cores, set them just under water and with half the sugar on the fire, with the addition of a little cochineal for coloring
3. When the quinces are done, remove them from the pan and place them with the other half of the sugar in a glazed pot, adding sufficient water
4. Put the pits and seed cores in a muslin cloth and let this cook in the liquid from which you removed the quinces; if the water has cooked down too much, add more, and let it cook until all the sugar has properly melted
5. Pour this over the quinces and seal the pot well with a bladder
6. For every 25 quinces use cochineal worth ten cents and if desired also some cinnamon

title: Kweeëngelei (Quince Jelly)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg quinces
- 6 dl water
- 3/4 of the weight of the liquid from the pears after cooking in sugar
- Jam jars
- Parchment paper
- Cellophane
- Elastic bands
- String
- Labels

## Directions

1. Wash the pears carefully, cook them in boiling water for several minutes, then peel them and cut them with the cores into pieces.
2. Place them with 9 tablespoons of sugar in the liquid and let them cook until they become soft.
3. Pour everything through a clean cloth, let the liquid drain carefully, weigh or measure it (1/2 liter = 500 g) and set it with 3/4 of its weight in sugar.
4. Let the jelly cook down, stirring occasionally, until it becomes red and syrupy.
5. Prepare further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Kweeënjam (Quince Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg quinces
- 2¼ kg sugar
- Jam jars
- Parchment paper
- Cellophane
- Elastic bands
- String
- Labels

## Directions

1. Wash the pears carefully.
2. Cook them for several minutes.
3. Peel the pears, cut them into pieces, remove the cores and set the pieces with a little of the water in which the pears were pre-cooked and the sugar.
4. Bring to a boil with the lid on the pan, then remove the lid and let them cook down until the mass becomes red and syrupy.
5. Treat the fruit further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).

title: Kwetsenjam (Damson Plum Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.75 kg damsons (+ 3 kg without pits)
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- labels

## Directions

1. Prepare the jam entirely as for plum jam (recipe 988).

title: Lammetjespap (Lamb's Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 100 g flour
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Mix the flour gradually with some cold milk into a thin paste, but ensure there are no lumps in it.
2. Bring the remaining milk with salt to a boil, pour in the mixed flour while stirring and keep stirring until the porridge is done (5 to 10 minutes).
3. Serve with brown sugar or syrup.

title: Lamsbout (Lamb Leg)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg lamb leg
- 100 g beef fat or margarine
- 15 g salt
- some pepper

## Directions

1. Cut away some of the fat if the fat layer is too thick
2. Rinse the meat, rub it with some pepper and salt and roast it in the same manner as roastbeef (recipe 227)
3. Calculate roasting time as 30 minutes per 500 g
4. Finish the gravy in the usual manner

title: Lamsbout in azijn (Lamb Leg in Vinegar)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ kg lamb leg
- 100 g beef fat or margarine
- 10 cloves
- 4 bay leaves
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 1 onion
- some pepper
- 20 g salt
- 1½ dl vinegar

## Directions

1. Rinse the meat, cut away some of the fat if necessary and set it for 24 hours in half vinegar, half water with the salt and spices
2. Turn the meat several times during this period and roast it in the manner indicated for roastbeef
3. Calculate roasting time as 30 minutes per 500 g
4. Finish the gravy with some of the liquid in which the meat has been standing
5. If desired, bind the gravy with some browned flour

title: Lamsbout met Aardappelen (Leg of Lamb with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- potatoes
- butter
- jus

## Directions

1. Roast a leg of lamb as indicated previously
2. When it is almost done, add potatoes and cook them in the jus until tender
3. Alternatively, the potatoes can be browned in butter and placed around the leg on the platter

title: Lamsbout à la Daube (Leg of Lamb à la Daube)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- salt
- pepper
- crushed cloves
- wine vinegar
- butter
- three finely cut onions
- pickled cucumbers
- half glass red wine
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat the leg well and cut it
2. Rub it with salt, pepper and crushed cloves
3. Place it in an earthenware pan
4. Pour wine vinegar over it and let it stand for 4 days, turning and basting daily
5. Braise three finely cut onions in butter until slightly tender
6. Place the leg in with them
7. When it is half done, add some pickled cucumbers cut into pieces and half a glass of red wine
8. Let it finish cooking
9. Bind the sauce with a little flour and dish it over the leg

title: Lamscoteletten (Gestoofd) (Stewed Lamb Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb cutlets
- onions
- butter
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare as calf cutlets, but add some finely cut onions browned in butter, along with some wine vinegar

title: Lamscoteletten met pikante saus (Lamb Chops with Spicy Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lamb chops
- 5 g salt
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- breadcrumbs
- 1/4 liter spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Beat, wash and salt the chops, bread them and brown them in the butter or margarine until done (about 20 minutes)
2. Arrange them shingled on a platter, tie a paper frill around the point and cover them with white spicy sauce

title: Lamscoteletten met soubisesaus (Lamb Cutlets with Soubise Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lamb cutlets
- 5 g salt
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- breadcrumbs for coating
- 3 dl soubise sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the cutlets
2. Coat them in breadcrumbs (paneer)
3. Brown and cook them in butter or margarine until done (approximately 20 minutes)
4. Arrange them on a platter in overlapping rows (dakpansgewijs)
5. Tie a paper collar (manchetje) around each bone end
6. Cover with soubise sauce (see recipe 353)

title: Lamsragout (Lamb Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb
- capers
- meat jus

## Directions

1. Treat the lamb ragout the same way as veal ragout
2. Add some capers and a little meat jus

title: Lamsragout (Lamb Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Same as veal ragout
- Capers
- Meat juice

## Directions

1. Treat it the same as veal ragout, but add some capers and a little juice from the meat

title: Lamsragout (Lamb Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of cold roasted lamb
- jus
- tablespoon capers
- lemon
- flour
- lump of butter
- salt
- calf bouillon

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cold roasted lamb and cut it into pieces
2. Add some of the jus to it
3. Add a tablespoon of capers, a lemon, a lump of butter rolled in flour, salt and some calf bouillon
4. Braise everything together well

title: Lamsragout (Lamb Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of cold roasted lamb
- jus from the roast
- one tablespoon capers
- one lemon
- a knob of butter rolled in flour
- salt
- veal bouillon

## Directions

1. Take a piece of cold roasted lamb, cut it into pieces and add some of the jus
2. Add one tablespoon capers, one lemon, a knob of butter rolled in flour, salt and some veal bouillon
3. Everything must be braised together well

title: Lamssoep (Lamb Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shank
- salt
- 5 to 6 ounces rice
- shallots
- celery roots
- butter
- lime or lemon juice
- egg sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a lamb shank thoroughly for more than two hours, skimming and removing the fat
2. Add 5 to 6 ounces rice to the broth according to quantity, add salt, some shallots and some celery roots, along with a lump of butter
3. Let everything cook for one hour
4. When serving, remove the shank and eat it with egg sauce; add some lime or lemon juice to the soup

title: Lamstongetjes (Lamb Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten to twelve lamb tongues
- parsley
- thyme
- bay leaves
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take ten to twelve lamb tongues, fill them, wash them well clean and cook them for about half an hour
2. Then change the water and let them drain, dry them afterwards and cut off the stump
3. Lard the tongues, add parsley, thyme and bay leaves with bouillon and let them braise for five hours

title: Lamstongetjes à la Braise (Lamb Tongues Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten to twelve lamb tongues
- water
- parsley
- thyme
- bay leaves
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take ten to twelve lamb tongues
2. Clean them and blanch them for a little less than half an hour
3. Change the water and let them drain
4. Dry them and cut off the root
5. Lard the tongues
6. Add some parsley, thyme and bay leaves with bouillon
7. Braise them for about five hours

title: Lange vingers (Long Fingers or Soft Biscuits)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 100 g flour
- 20 g Duryea cornstarch
- 150 g sugar
- grated zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Pipe from the batter, which has been prepared in the known way (see recipe 869), onto a greased baking sheet covered with a piece of paper, using a piping bag with a smooth nozzle, elongated cookies.
2. Bake the cookies in a hot oven until done, approximately 15 minutes, but ensure that they remain light in color.
3. If necessary, moisten the paper on the underside to easily remove the cookies from the paper.

title: Latuw (Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean the lettuce well.
2. Toss it with oil, vinegar, pepper, and eggs.

title: Laurataart (Almond Tart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- 100 g bastard sugar
- 100 g almonds
- marmalade or jam

## Directions

1. Soak the almonds in warm water, peel them and grind them finely.
2. Knead them with the butter or margarine, the sugar and the flour into a firm ball and divide it into two equal parts.
3. Press one half into a greased tart pan, but make sure the layer of dough is as thin as possible. (When using a small tart pan, divide the dough into 3 parts.)
4. Bake it in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked (15 to 20 minutes).
5. Let the cake cool, turn it out and bake the second half in the pan.
6. Let it cool as well and place both parts on top of each other with some jam or marmalade in between.
7. Sprinkle the tart with sifted powdered sugar or tie a colored ribbon around it.

title: Leeuwerikken (Larks)
body:
## Ingredients

- larks
- bacon pieces
- butter

## Directions

1. Thread the larks on skewers and roast them on the spit
2. Place a piece of bacon between each little bird
3. Leave the heads on, which are pierced with a small stick
4. Baste them with butter

title: Leipziger paaschkoekjes (Leipzig Easter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound flour
- Half a pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 2 large cups milk
- 1 pint curdled milk
- 5 drams breadcrumbs
- 4 eggs
- 1 and a half measures thick cream
- 1 ounce white sugar
- 4 drams sliced sweet almonds
- Raisins and currants
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Make a tender dough with flour, butter, eggs and milk
2. Divide into flat cakes
3. Prepare a cream with curdled milk, breadcrumbs, eggs, thick cream, white sugar, sliced sweet almonds, raisins and currants
4. Spread this cream finger-thick on the dough
5. Brush with beaten egg white
6. Bake in the oven

title: Lemon Sauce (Citroensaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks or flour
- Water
- One or two lemons
- Butter

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks or flour with water
2. Squeeze in one or two lemons
3. Let it boil and stir in a piece of butter

title: Lemon Tart (Citroentaart)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two and a half ounces rice
- Juice of 6 lemons
- Peel of one lemon, cooked until soft
- Strips of citron peel
- Sugar
- Tart crust

## Directions

1. Cook two and a half ounces rice very well, without stirring
2. Add the juice of 6 lemons, the peel of one lemon cooked until soft
3. Add strips, citron peel and sugar
4. Put it in a tart crust
5. Bake for half an hour
6. Let it cool in the pan

title: Lemonade (Limonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 2 to 3 lemons
- Cold boiled water
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Pour cold boiled water over the lemon juice
2. Add sugar to taste

title: Lende en Entrecéte (Loin and Entrecôte)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg meat
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227).

title: Leverbeuling (Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- leaf fat
- hardtack or breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- mixed spice
- pork intestines

## Directions

1. Cook the liver with the leaf fat until done
2. Chop it very finely
3. Add hardtack or breadcrumbs, along with some pepper, salt and mixed spice
4. Stuff into pork intestines
5. When stuffed, put the sausages in a kettle and bring just to a boil

title: Leverbroodjes (Fish Liver Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- livers of haddock or cod
- pepper
- salt
- 4 thin slices old bread without crust

## Directions

1. Cook the liver of haddock or cod in the usual manner until done (see recipe 179), rub it finely and mix it with some pepper and salt.
2. Place this mixture on oblong square pieces of bread, which have been stripped of the crust and lightly toasted in the oven.

title: Leversoep (Liver Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l bouillon in which the liver has been cooked
- piece of onion
- 6 cloves
- 2 bay leaves
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 50 g veal mince
- pieces of liver
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Fry the flour with the butter or margarine and the finely chopped onion and spices evenly brown. Add the liver bouillon and let the soup cook for 20 minutes.
2. Then pour it through a sieve, add the meatballs made in the usual way and let them cook until done. Add a few cubes of leftover liver and serve small cubes of fried bread with the soup.

title: Leverworst (Liver Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg pork or veal liver
- 2 kg pork meat
- 1 kg neck bacon
- 40 g salt
- 50 g onions
- 1 tablespoon ground cloves
- 15 g pepper
- 2 dl meat broth
- beef casings

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned liver, cleaned meat and neck bacon for 5 hours in plenty of boiling salted water.
2. Grind everything as finely as possible, mix it with the spices, salt, broth and very finely chopped onions.
3. Clean the casings, which have been soaked in lukewarm water for some time, thoroughly and stuff the meat through a sausage horn into the casings, not too tightly.
4. Prick occasionally with a needle while stuffing and tie the sausages closed at both ends.
5. Cook them in plenty of boiling salted water until they float to the surface (approximately 15 to 20 minutes), but let them cook very gently so they do not burst.
6. Let them cool completely, lying side by side.
7. Note: If you wish to keep the sausage for a while, let it smoke very lightly.
8. The easiest way to fill the casings is to place the sausage horn on the meat grinder with the blade removed, then pass the mixture through the grinder. The casing, which is stretched over the sausage horn, fills evenly but must be supported by hand.

title: Leverworst (Liver Sausage) (approximately 4 kg)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg pork or calf liver
- 2 kg pork meat
- 1 kg neck pork fat
- 40 g salt
- 50 g onions
- 1 tablespoon ground cloves
- 15 g pepper
- 2 dl meat bouillon
- beef intestines

## Directions

1. Cook the washed liver, washed meat and neck pork fat for 5 hours in plenty of boiling salted water
2. Grind everything as finely as possible, mix it with the spices, (the salt), the bouillon and the very finely chopped onions
3. Prepare the intestines, which have been soaked in lukewarm water for some time

title: Lijsters (Thrushes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned thrushes
- egg sauce

## Directions

1. Roast the cleaned thrushes on the spit and lard them, or put them in a casserole with fire above and below
2. Pour an egg sauce over them
3. Eight to ten thrushes make a good dish

title: Limburgse Vlaai (Limburg Pie)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- 50 g sugar
- 20 g yeast
- 1 egg
- 1.5 dl lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the known manner (see recipe 835)

title: Limburgse vlaai (Limburgian Fruit Tart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- 50 g sugar
- 20 g yeast
- 1 egg
- 1.5 dl lukewarm milk
- fruit compote (e.g., apricot or cherry compote)

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the usual way (see recipe 835) with an egg, melted butter or margarine and sugar mixed in.
2. Roll out the risen dough on a flour-dusted surface to a layer approximately 1/2 cm thick, the size of the form in which the vlaai is to be baked (usually a shallow large pastry form).
3. Grease the form with butter or margarine, cut out a circle of dough larger than the form.
4. Lay the dough layer in it and ensure the raised edge of the form is also covered with dough.
5. Prick the bottom with a fork and let it rise for another half hour under a damp cloth.
6. Bake the vlaai in a hot oven until cooked through and light brown (20 to 30 minutes).
7. Remove from the form and fill with fruit compote after cooling, or fill the vlaai before baking and sprinkle sugar over the fruit.

title: Limonade (Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 lemons
- cold boiled water
- sugar

## Directions

1. With the juice of 2 to 3 lemons, pour cold boiled water and add sugar to taste

title: Linzen (Lentils)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g lentils
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as white beans and serve them in the same way, however reckon as cooking time 1 hour and 45 minutes. Give them with sour sauce or tomato sauce.

title: Linzenplakjes (Lentil Patties)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cooked lentils
- 30 g stale bread
- 50 g onion
- 1 raw egg
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- 100 g butter or margarine
- juice of 1/4 lemon
- bread crumbs
- salt
- one large apple (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in the usual manner.
2. Make them fine and mix them with the soaked and finely made bread, the very finely chopped and fried onions, the finely divided hard-boiled egg, the egg yolk, and lemon juice.
3. Make 8 patties from this mixture and coat them in bread crumbs.
4. Beat the egg white with 1 spoon of water and coat the patties with it.
5. Coat them again in bread crumbs and brown them on both sides.
6. Finish the sauce with lentil water.
7. If desired, place a slice of fried apple on each patty.

title: Linzensoep (Lentil Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g lentils
- 1 l water
- piece of winter carrot
- 25 g onion (1 small onion)
- 1 bay leaf
- some thyme
- approximately 40 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste
- lemon juice to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the lentils, put them on with the cold water, the chopped onion and carrot, the bay leaf, and some thyme
2. Let them cook gently (1 1/2 hours), but add liquid from time to time
3. Pour the soup through a horsehair sieve, rub the lentils through, and mix the lentil purée with the cooking liquid
4. Let the soup cook gently until it is evenly bound
5. Just before serving, add the butter or margarine, salt, and lemon juice to taste
6. Serve with bread cut into dice and fried in butter or margarine

title: Linzesoep (Lentil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- rainwater
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat entirely as described for green pea soup

title: Lobster Sauce (Kreeftesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster butter
- Flour
- Clear meat juice
- Pepper
- Mace
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Stir a couple of spoons flour through lobster butter
2. Let it brown briefly
3. Add pure meat juice, a little pepper, mace, and the juice of a lemon
4. Let it cook through

title: Lofsla (Chicory Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads of chicory
- ¾ head of endive
- ½ head of lettuce
- a few slices of tomato and cucumber
- 2 tablespoons oil
- a bit of lemon juice
- a small bit of soy or Maggi's Aroma or marmite

## Directions

1. Clean the vegetables. Cut the stalk part wedge-shaped from the chicory head. Shred the chicory, endive and some of the lettuce. Keep a few lettuce leaves for garnish. Mix the oil with the lemon juice, soy, Maggi's Aroma or marmite mixed with water. Stir the shredded vegetables lightly through the salad dressing with the salad spoon and fork. Lay the salad dressing around the mixed salad and garnish the whole with the tomato and cucumber slices.

title: Londonderrysoep (Londonderry Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- light bouillon
- piece of mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 teaspoon curry
- 10 g onion
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 dl cream
- white Bordeaux wine
- 1 tin of mushrooms or small pieces of calf's head

## Directions

1. Make bouillon in the usual way with the spices, strain it, let it cool and carefully skim off the fat.
2. Melt the butter or margarine and bring flour, onion and curry to the boil while stirring, rub out all lumps, gradually add the bouillon and let the soup cook while stirring until done (10 minutes).
3. Pour it through a sieve to the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream and white Bordeaux wine.
4. Stir in the liquid from the mushrooms and the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, or the pieces of calf's head.

title: Louisepudding (Louise Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g old bread without crust
- 0.5 l milk
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- jam
- grated lemon zest
- 2 egg whites (for garnish)
- 100 g sugar (for garnish)
- angelica
- candied fruits

## Directions

1. Make the bread, freed of crusts, fine in the boiling milk, so there are no lumps
2. Add the butter or margarine
3. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar and grated lemon zest
4. Add the bread with the butter or margarine to this
5. Fold in the two stiffly beaten egg whites as lightly as possible at the end
6. Grease a warm pudding mold (also the lid) with some butter or margarine, dust it with powdered sugar and add the mixture, but ensure the mold is filled only 3/4 full
7. Close the mold firmly and cook it 1.5 hours au bain-marie, but ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold
8. Let the pudding dry out a little without the lid
9. Turn it out onto a fireproof dish and quickly coat it with the jam and then with the two stiffly beaten egg whites with sugar, sprinkle some sugar over it and quickly place it in a hot oven to brown it lightly
10. Garnish the pudding afterwards with strips of angelica and the candied fruits, which should already be ready
11. Bring the pudding to the table as hot as possible and serve with warm vanilla sauce or wine sauce

title: Macaroni Dish (Macaronischoteltje)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liters milk
- 200 g macaroni
- 100 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 1 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook macaroni pudding in the usual manner (see previous recipe), but cook the vanilla or lemon zest in with it.
2. When the pudding is done, remove it and mix the pudding with half of the sugar.
3. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the other half of the sugar, carefully add the hot pudding to it and lastly mix the stiffly beaten egg white in as lightly as possible.
4. Put the mixture in a fireproof dish.
5. Finish the dish as described in recipe 602.

title: Macaroni in Schelpen (Macaroni in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g macaroni
- pepper
- salt
- 50 g ham
- 60 g old Gouda cheese
- 35 g flour
- 35 g butter or margarine or 25 g Duryea cornstarch
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 3½ dl milk
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces, wash them and cook them until done in plenty of boiling water with salt or broth (1/2 hour) and stir occasionally.
2. Drain them.
3. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102.
4. Stir in the chopped ham and grated cheese and as airy as possible the macaroni cut into pieces.
5. Add pepper and salt to taste and fill the dampened shells with this mixture for about 3/4.
6. Finish them as described in recipe 102.
7. Note: Instead of this mixture, one can also use the mixture for macaroni with ham and cheese (see recipe 126).

title: Macaroni met Ham en Kaas (Macaroni with Ham and Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound macaroni
- salt and water
- butter
- cooked ham, finely chopped
- nutmeg
- Parmesan cheese
- half pint milk
- 4 eggs
- half ounce butter
- a little fine lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook half pound macaroni with salt and water, not too soft so the pieces remain whole
2. Cut the macaroni into pieces as desired
3. In a warm earthen pan, place a little butter, then a layer of macaroni
4. Add a layer of cooked and finely chopped ham, then sprinkle with nutmeg and Parmesan cheese
5. Continue layering until the pan is filled a finger's width below the rim
6. Stir together half pint milk, 4 eggs, half ounce butter and a little fine lemon peel
7. Pour this over the macaroni
8. Bake in a hot oven for another quarter hour

title: Macaroni met ham en kaas (Macaroni with Ham and Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g macaroni
- 100 g ham
- 100 g aged cheese
- 3 dl macaroni cooking water
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 25 g flour
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces, wash it and cook it until done in plenty of boiling salted water (1/2 hour).
2. Let it drain and reserve 3 dl of cooking water for the sauce.
3. Cut the ham finely, grate the cheese and stir it carefully with two spoons through the macaroni on low heat until the cheese threads (so-called cheese stringing), but especially make sure the macaroni stays whole.
4. Make a sauce from butter or margarine, flour and macaroni cooking water and pour this over the macaroni, add salt (and pepper) to taste, put everything in a fireproof small dish, sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, lay the 10 g butter or margarine in small pieces on top, clean the rim of the dish and let it brown in the oven or serve the hot dish in a covered dish.

title: Macaroni met Poulet (Macaroni with Chicken)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g macaroni
- 200 g calf chicken
- 100 g ground calf meat
- 1/2 l boiling water
- pieces of mace
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg (optional)
- lemon juice
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine
- old bread soaked
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the chicken, place it in the boiling water with salt and mace and let it simmer gently for one quarter hour.
