2. Break the macaroni into pieces, wash it and add it to the chicken, letting it cook together on a low heat for 1/2 hour.
3. Stir from time to time to prevent sticking, but ensure the macaroni does not break.
4. Let the meat balls, made with soaked old bread, pepper, salt and nutmeg, cook along for the last 10 minutes.
5. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring to a boil while stirring.
6. Rub out all lumps and add 3 dl of the remaining cooking liquid while stirring.
7. Let the sauce cook while stirring (10 minutes), carefully add it to the beaten egg and mix with lemon juice, the macaroni, chicken and meat.
8. Place the mixture in a fireproof dish, sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
9. Place a few pieces of butter or margarine on top, clean the edges and let it brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Macaroni met poulet (Macaroni with Veal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g macaroni
- 200 g veal for stewing
- 100 g ground veal
- approximately 0.5 l boiling water
- pieces of mace
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- optionally 1 egg
- some lemon juice
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash the veal, set it on with the boiling water, salt and mace and let it cook gently for one quarter hour.
2. Add the macaroni, which has been broken into pieces and washed, and let this cook along for 1/2 hour on low heat.
3. Stir from time to time to prevent scorching, but make sure the macaroni does not break.
4. Let the meatballs, which are made with some soaked old bread, pepper

title: Macaroni met tomaten (Macaroni with Tomatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g macaroni
- 0.5 kg tomatoes
- 50 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt
- optionally 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces of 6-8 cm length, wash it and cook it until done in plenty of boiling salted water (1/2 hour).
2. Let the macaroni drain.
3. Wash the tomatoes, slice them and lay a layer in a buttered fireproof small dish.
4. Sprinkle some pepper and salt over it and place small pieces of butter or margarine here and there.
5. Lay the drained macaroni on it and along the edge the remaining tomatoes.
6. Sprinkle pepper and salt over the tomatoes, again place small pieces of butter or margarine here and there, sprinkle fine breadcrumbs over it, clean the rim of the dish and let it braise gently in a moderately warm oven for 1/2 hour, dripping occasionally with the liquid.
7. If desired, sprinkle very finely chopped parsley over it before serving.

title: Macaroni Pudding (Macaronipap)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liters milk
- 200 g macaroni
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
2. Break the macaroni into pieces, wash it on a sieve until the water runs completely clear and sprinkle it stirring into the boiling milk.
3. Keep stirring until the milk boils again and let the pudding cook on very low heat, stirring occasionally, until cooked and thickened (approximately 3/4 hour).

title: Macaroni-gelei (Macaroni Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound macaroni
- a piece of butter
- some salt
- two ounces butter
- four ounces grated white cheese
- three ounces Parmesan cheese
- a little nutmeg
- one or two spoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni with a piece of butter and some salt for three quarters of an hour
2. Check if it is tender
3. Let it drain briefly
4. Put it in a pan with two ounces butter, four ounces grated white cheese, three ounces Parmesan cheese, a little nutmeg and one or two spoonfuls cream
5. When the cream draws in threads, place it on the dish

title: Macaronicroquetjes (Macaroni Croquettes) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g macaroni
- 2 dl cream or milk
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 25 g ham
- 30 g old cheese
- 2 1/2 sheets gelatin or 4 g powdered gelatin
- bread crumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into pieces, wash it, and cook it done in plenty of boiling water with salt, or in bouillon (1 1/2 hour) and stir from time to time.
2. Let it drain dry and cut it into small pieces.
3. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 102.
4. Dissolve the soaked gelatin in it if necessary.
5. Mix the sauce with the grated cheese and the finely cut ham, carefully stir in the macaroni cut into small pieces, add pepper and salt to taste, and let the mixture, spread on a board, become almost stiff, but ensure it is still good to handle.
6. Form the croquettes in the manner as indicated for meat croquettes (see recipe 116).
7. Bread these double and treat them further as meat croquettes (recipe 116).

title: Macaronicroquetjes (Macaroni Croquettes) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g macaroni
- 2 dl tomato sauce
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- Maggi's aroma
- bread crumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Prepare the croquettes as in recipe 120.
2. Use vegetable leftovers or stewed soup vegetables as filling if desired.

title: Macaronicroquettes (Macaroni Croquettes), 2nd Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g macaroni
- 2 dl tomato sauce
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- Maggi's aroma
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Prepare the croquettes as in recipe 120.
2. If desired, use vegetable scraps or braised soup vegetables as filling.

title: Macaronipap (Macaroni Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 200 g macaroni
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
2. Break the macaroni into pieces, rinse it on a sieve until the water runs completely clear and sprinkle it into the boiling milk while stirring.
3. Keep stirring until the milk boils again and then let the porridge cook on a very gentle fire, stirring occasionally.

title: Macaronisoep (1e recept) (Macaroni Soup, First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon of beef or 1 liter water and 4 Maggi's bouillon cubes (recipe 35)
- 40 g macaroni
- 50 g veal ground meat
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Cook the broken and washed macaroni in the bouillon, which is prepared in the usual manner and strained, until tender (approximately 3/4 hour), or cook it in water in which the bouillon cubes are later dissolved.
2. Let the meatballs prepared in the usual manner (recipe 37) cook in the soup for the last 10 minutes.

title: Macaronisoep (Macaroni Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half Dutch pound macaroni
- water
- good veal bouillon

## Directions

1. Take half a Dutch pound of macaroni and cook it almost done in water
2. Then let this macaroni cook for another half hour in good veal bouillon
3. Take care to keep the tubes as whole as possible

title: Macaronisoep (Macaroni Soup) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l beef bouillon or 1 l water and 4 Maggi bouillon cubes
- 40 g macaroni
- 50 g ground veal
- 25 g Parmesan or aged Gouda cheese (optional)
- 1 egg (optional)
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Cook the broken and washed macaroni in the bouillon, which is prepared in the usual way and sieved, until tender (approximately 3/4 hour), or cook it in water and later dissolve the bouillon cubes in that water
2. Make the meatballs from the ground veal in the usual way (see recipe 37)
3. Let the meatballs cook together with the soup for the last 10 minutes
4. Stir in the grated cheese just before serving if desired, or present it separately
5. Beat the egg in the soup tureen with the cream and carefully add the soup to it while stirring well

title: Macaronisoep (Macaroni Soup) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l beef bouillon or 1 l water and 4 bouillon cubes
- 30 g macaroni
- 25 g cheese
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 dl cream
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Cook the broken and washed macaroni in boiling salted water until tender (1/2 hour to 3/4 hour) and cut into small pieces
2. Heat butter or margarine with flour, stirring, and bring to a boil
3. Add the sieved bouillon or the water in which the bouillon cubes are dissolved while stirring, and let the soup cook while stirring until done (approximately 10 minutes)
4. Add the macaroni and grated cheese
5. Beat the egg yolk with the cream in the soup tureen and carefully add the soup to it while stirring

title: Macarons (Almond Macaroons)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled soaked almonds
- sugar
- vanilla or grated lemon zest
- wafer sheets (6 sheets)
- 30 almonds for garnishing

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds. Grind them, mix them with the sugar, the flavoring and as much of the egg white until a stiff mass is obtained (as thick as purée for potato croquettes).
2. Form small balls from this, make these somewhat flat from underneath and lay them at some distance from each other on a wafer sheet, which is laid on a baking sheet.
3. For almond macaroons place a halved almond in the middle.
4. Bake the cookies in a moderately warm oven light brown (20 to 30 minutes).
5. Then break the wafer sheet away along the edge of the macaroons.

title: Macarons (± 60 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g almonds or ground coconut
- 250 g sugar
- + 4 egg whites
- some scraped vanilla or grated lemon zest
- wafer papers (6 sheets)
- 30 almonds for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds. Grind them, mix them with the sugar, the flavoring and enough egg white until a stiff mass is obtained (as thick as puree for potato croquettes).
2. Shape small balls from this, flatten them slightly on the bottom and lay them some distance apart on a wafer paper placed on a baking sheet.
3. For almond macarons place a halved almond in the middle.
4. Bake the cookies in a moderately warm oven until light brown (20-30 minutes). Break the wafer paper away along the edge of the macarons.

title: Madeirasaus (Madeira Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl lean jus with water
- 50 g onion
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- small carrot
- 40 to 50 g flour
- 40 to 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl Madeira

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 359.
2. Remove from heat and mix with Madeira.
3. Serve with beef tenderloin, beef tongue, etc.

title: Magdalenakoekjes (Magdalena Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g butter
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 125 g sugar
- 150 g flour
- 50 g snipped almonds or chopped sucade

## Directions

1. Cream the butter until fluffy, add the egg and stir it through until it has completely mixed with the butter.
2. Add the egg yolk and the sugar and stir until the sugar is completely finely ground.
3. Mix the sifted flour with it and spread this batter as thinly as possible on a baking sheet greased with butter.
4. Make the top as smooth as possible and sprinkle it with the peeled and snipped almonds or chopped sucade.
5. Bake the cake in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done, cut it into pieces of 6 by 3 cm while still warm and remove these immediately from the baking sheet.

title: Magere groentesoep (Lean Vegetable Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water with salt
- 35 g rice or vermicelli
- vegetables in season (approximately 300 g), for example in summer: peas, carrots, string beans, cauliflower, etc., in winter: celery, Brussels sprouts, salsify, etc.
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the vegetables, divide them into small pieces, and wash them.
2. Add them with the washed rice or vermicelli to the boiling water. Add salt to taste and let everything cook together until tender (1/2 hour).
3. If necessary, thicken the soup with a little sago and stir in very finely chopped parsley away from the heat.

title: Maiskolven (Corn Cobs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 young corn cobs
- salt to taste
- a little sugar
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and the fluffy threads from the cobs.
2. Then cook the cobs in plenty of boiling water with salt and a little sugar until tender (1/2 hour, then the kernels should come away easily; larger cobs must cook longer).
3. Serve them warm on a plate with fresh butter or margarine and fine salt.

title: Maitre d'hotelboter (Maitre d'Hotel Butter)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- juice of approximately 1 lemon
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons very finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy.
2. Add the lemon juice drop by drop, then the parsley and pepper.
3. Serve with veal, cooked fish, etc.

title: Maizenapap (Cornstarch Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 l milk
- 75 g cornstarch Duryea
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch gradually with some cold milk to a thin paste.
2. Bring the remaining milk with the salt to a boil, pour in the mixed cornstarch while stirring before the milk actually boils and let the porridge cook thoroughly while stirring (3 to 4 minutes).

title: Maizenapudding (Cornstarch Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 75 g maizena Duryea
- 80 g sugar
- ½ stick vanilla, vanilla sugar or peel of ½ lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare maizena porridge as described in recipe 615, but allow the flavoring to steep in it and add the sugar.
2. Remove the flavoring and pour the pudding mixture into a mold rinsed with cold water.
3. Let the pudding cool and become firm, turn it out onto a moist plate and serve a fruit juice sauce or stewed fruit with it.

title: Maizenapudding met eieren (Cornstarch Pudding with Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 50 to 60 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 70 g sugar
- 1/2 vanilla stick, vanilla sugar, or the zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the flavoring.
2. Beat the eggs and mix with the cornstarch and sugar until a thin batter without lumps.
3. Add enough of the boiling milk so that the eggs are thoroughly warmed.
4. Pour everything back into the pan and let the mixture, stirring with a whisk, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes.
5. Remove the flavoring, pour the pudding into a mold rinsed with cold water and let it become cold and firm.
6. Turn it out onto a wet plate and serve with a fruit juice sauce or stewed fruit.

title: Maizenapudding met eieren (Cornstarch Pudding with Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ l milk
- 50 to 60 g maizena Duryea
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 70 g sugar
- ½ stick vanilla, vanilla sugar or peel of ½ lemon

## Directions

1. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the flavoring.
2. Beat the eggs and mix with the maizena and sugar to a thin batter without lumps.
3. Add enough of the boiling milk so that the eggs are well heated.
4. Pour everything back into the pan.

title: Maizenasaus (Cornstarch Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l milk
- 30 g cornstarch
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch with a little of the cold milk until a thin smooth paste.
2. Bring the remaining milk to a boil.
3. Pour this paste into the milk while stirring vigorously, taking care the milk is not yet boiling, and let the sauce cook for 3 minutes while stirring.
4. Add salt and stir in the butter or margarine.
5. Serve this sauce over cauliflower or use it for stewing various vegetables.

title: Maizenaschoteltje (Cornstarch Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 l milk
- 90 g cornstarch Duryea
- 100 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/4 lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook cornstarch porridge in the usual way (see previous recipe), but let the vanilla or lemon peel cook along with it.
2. Mix it with half of the sugar.
3. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the other half of the sugar, carefully add the hot porridge and last of all the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Finish the dish as described in recipe 602.

title: Makreel (Mackerel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg mackerel
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Clean the mackerel, make a slit along the backbone
2. Cook it in plenty of boiling water or poach it in a little boiling water with some vinegar, butter or margarine and salt. In the latter case the cooking time is longer (approximately 20 to 30 minutes)
3. Serve the fish with vinegar or mustard sauce
4. Do not keep mackerel too long, as it becomes rancid quickly

title: Mandarijnen in Gelei (Mandarins in Gelée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 6 large oranges
- juice of 3 lemons
- juice of 6 small mandarins, all made up with white wine, wine and water or water and some lemonade syrup to 1 liter liquid
- sugar to taste, at least 200 grams
- 2 egg whites
- 35 g white gelatin or 30 g powdered gelatin
- 6 mandarins
- a few green French fruits

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in a little water, add this mixture to the remaining liquid.
2. Add the (washed and soaked) gelatin and the lightly beaten egg whites.
3. Prepare the liquid as indicated in recipe 555.
4. Pour a thin layer of the clarified gelée into a pudding mold rinsed with cold water.
5. Let it set firm.
6. Peel the mandarins, carefully remove all white membranes, divide them into segments and remove as many pits as possible.
7. Place some on the cooled gelée, alternating with small pieces of candied fruit, glue them with a little lukewarm gelée, let the mass cool again, pour gelée over until the segments are covered and let everything set.
8. Continue this way until the mold is filled, turn the pudding out onto a damp plate after cooling and garnish it with whipped cream.
9. Note: If the pudding does not come out of the mold easily, it is best to dip it briefly in warm water. The gelée can also be made in a flat dish, filled with pineapple for example, and instead of juice pressed from fresh fruit, use canned fruit juice.

title: Marasquinpudding (Maraschino Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 1 dl maraschino
- 50 g sugar
- 10 g gelatine
- some green candied fruits

## Directions

1. Soak the sheet gelatine 10 to 15 minutes in cold water, then dissolve it with as little boiling water as possible.
2. Add the sugar, dissolve it completely and let this mass cool somewhat.
3. Beat the cream and the egg whites stiff, add the maraschino, the fruits cut in pieces and the cooled mass.
4. Stir from time to time until the mass begins to become jelly-like, then pour it into a pudding mold brushed with unbeaten egg white and let it become cold and firm.
5. Turn the pudding out onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.

title: Marasquinsaus (Maraschino Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 dl cooking cream
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g sugar
- 6 g Duryea maizena
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 dl maraschino

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as vanilla sauce (recipe 755).
2. Add the maraschino off the fire.
3. Give the sauce with warm semolina pudding, fine bread pudding etc.

title: Marsepein (Marzipan)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g almonds
- 200 g powdered sugar
- 1 small egg white
- a few drops of lemon juice
- optionally some rose or orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds, wash and dry them and grind them in the almond mill.
2. Mix them with the sifted powdered sugar, the unbeaten egg white, the drops of lemon juice and the rose or orange blossom water.
3. Mill this mass through a mill (with the finest blade) and catch the first portion separately, because it may have a less beautiful white color.
4. Knead the coming fine, white dough thoroughly together and let it rest 24 hours in a damp cloth.
5. Then the marzipan can be processed in various ways. For example, add some orange essence through the mass, if small root vegetables are to be made.
6. To make small potatoes, roll round balls of the dough through some cinnamon and prick it here and there.
7. Color some portions for example with cacao powder or with plant colorants.
8. Roll each portion into a sheet on a table dusted with some powdered sugar and stick them together with some water.
9. Roll them together once more and cut or stamp cookies from them.
10. Garnish them with a half hazelnut, almond or walnut.

title: May Wine (Meiwijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- A bunch of blooming and washed Lady's Bedstraw
- One and a half ounces sugar
- One bottle Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Put the Lady's Bedstraw and sugar in a terrine
2. Pour a bottle of Rhine wine over it
3. Let it steep for one hour
4. Taste to see if the herbs have steeped enough
5. Serve the drink in glasses with the green leaves in it
6. You can also add slices of Chinese orange, one in each glass
7. If you want the wine to have a stronger herb flavor, let the herbs steep longer
8. The wine must be drunk cold

title: Mayonnaise (First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg or 2 egg yolks
- 3 1/2 dl salad oil
- approximately 1/2 dl vinegar
- 1 1/2 teaspoon mustard or mustard powder
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Stir the egg or egg yolks with mustard, salt and pepper for 10 minutes with a good tinned whisk.
2. Add a small splash of vinegar and then very small amounts of oil at a time until a thick sauce is obtained.
3. If the sauce becomes too thick and can no longer be stirred well, thin it carefully with some vinegar.
4. Continue until all ingredients are used and a good-tasting, white sauce is obtained, which can be served with cold meat and cold fish.
5. Store in a jar under a layer of oil.

title: Mayonnaise (Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 raw egg yolks
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 1/4 to 2 1/2 dl salad oil
- approximately 1/2 dl vinegar
- 1 1/2 teaspoon mustard or mustard powder
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Press the cooked egg yolks through a sieve.
2. Add the raw egg yolks, mustard, pepper and salt, then drop by drop alternately oil and vinegar (see the previous recipe).

title: Mayonnaise (Third Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 dl water
- 10 g cornstarch
- approximately 1/2 dl vinegar
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 dl salad oil
- approximately 1 1/2 teaspoon mustard or mustard powder
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch gradually with the water to form a thin paste without lumps.
2. Let it simmer for 3 to 4 minutes while stirring vigorously with a whisk.
3. Remove from heat and add vinegar, mustard, pepper and salt.
4. Stir occasionally while cooling to prevent a film from forming.
5. Add the egg yolks and while beating vigorously, add the oil drop by drop.

title: Meelkoekjes (Flour Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet milk or cream
- fine flour
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir fine flour into sweet milk or cream very smoothly and let it cook through briefly
2. Remove from heat and add salt
3. Mix further with flour into a stiff dough
4. Roll out, form cookies with a glass
5. Bake slowly in butter until done

title: Meelpodding (Flour Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 ounce melted butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 grated lemon zest
- cinnamon
- circa 1 ounce bread sugar
- 5 ounces fine flour

## Directions

1. Stir 0.5 ounce melted butter for a very long time
2. add 4 eggs while stirring, 1 grated lemon zest, cinnamon and circa 1 ounce bread sugar
3. add 5 ounces fine flour and stir the flour to the thickness of ordinary pudding
4. fill a mold with it
5. let it cook vigorously for 3 hours

title: Meiknolletjes (Spring Turnips)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 small spring turnips
- 2 1/2 dl milk
- 20 g flour or 10 g cornstarch
- 20 g butter or margarine
- salt
- finely cut fresh greens or nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the turnips, wash them (cut them into quarters if they are large) and cook them in water with salt until tender (1/2 hour).
2. Let them stew for 10 minutes in the sauce made from butter, flour and milk, which is prepared in the same manner as cauliflower sauce (recipe 328) or cornstarch sauce (recipe 329).
3. Just before serving, stir in some of the finely cut green tops or grate some nutmeg over the dish.

title: Melkchocolade (Milk Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 1 chocolate cake per can milk
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. For each can of milk take one chocolate cake, bring the milk to a boil, and stir in the chocolate, which you have previously made into a smooth paste with some water, and let everything cook through well
2. Add sugar and cinnamon to taste

title: Melksoep (Milk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- flour
- egg yolks
- bread sugar
- white bread

## Directions

1. Set as much milk to the fire as you deem necessary, bring it to a boil
2. Beat some flour with a few egg yolks and bread sugar through it
3. Pour it over slices of white bread when serving
4. Be especially careful not to burn it and ensure that no burning wood lies against the pot

title: Melksoep (Milk Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 l milk
- peel of 2 lemons
- 40 g flour
- 80 g sugar
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Slowly bring 1 1/4 l milk to a boil with the thinly pared peel of a lemon.
2. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, mix it with 30 g sugar, form small balls and cook these in the boiling milk briefly.
3. Remove them with a skimming spoon.
4. Mix the flour and sugar with the remaining milk gradually until a thin paste without lumps.
5. Pour this into the boiling milk while stirring and let the soup simmer while stirring for 10 minutes.
6. Then carefully pour it with the yolks, which have been beaten in the soup tureen.
7. Place the egg white balls on top and sprinkle with some grated lemon peel.

title: Meloenen in 't Zuur (Melons in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- melons that are not fully ripe
- vinegar
- white sugar
- whole cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take melons that are not fully ripe, cutting off the small round parts but especially not the skin; cut them further into slices the thickness of a finger, remove the seed, and lay the slices in a pot
2. Pour over as much vinegar as is needed for them to be well covered and let stand covered for eight days
3. Remove the melons, arrange them in a new earthenware pan and add the same vinegar; let them cook until they can be pierced with a straw
4. Layer them in an earthenware pot, placing a layer of white sugar on the bottom and repeating this between each layer of melon, adding some whole cloves and some cinnamon
5. The vinegar in which the melon cooked must be heated again and poured over boiling; seal the pot at once with a bladder

title: Mierik Saus (Horseradish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh horseradish root
- 2 full tablespoons well washed currants
- cold water
- salt
- butter
- 1 to 2 teaspoons Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Grate the horseradish root finely.
2. Set this grating on the fire with the currants in cold water.
3. Let this cook together for 1 to 1½ hours on a gentle fire, well covered, until a soft paste.
4. Add the necessary salt, a piece of butter the size of a nut and 1 to 2 teaspoons Liebig's Meat Extract, by which the sauce becomes strong and the flavor greatly enhanced.

title: Mierikswortelsaus (Horseradish Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter bouillon or water with salt
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 tablespoon grated horseradish or more

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Mix it away from the heat with the grated horseradish
3. Serve with calf's head or cooked meat

title: Milanese lamscotelettenschotel (Lamb Chops à la Milanaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 chops
- 5 g salt
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 75 g macaroni
- 40 g ham
- 40 g Parmesan cheese
- 20 g butter or margarine
- truffles and mushrooms
- 100 g rice or mashed potato for the base
- 3 dl lean jus with water in which Spanish pepper, cloves and bay leaves have been infused
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash, salt and pepper the chops, brown them in butter or margarine and cook until done (about 20 minutes)
2. Cook the macaroni in the usual manner until done, cut into small pieces and mix with ham cut into pieces, butter, truffles and mushrooms
3. Stand the chops against the base of rice or mashed potato
4. Tie a paper frill around the point, pour the sauce prepared in the usual manner over them, sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over and arrange the macaroni around them

title: Milanese ossenhaasschotel (Beef Tenderloin à la Milanaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg beef tenderloin
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 10 g salt
- pepper
- milk
- 150 g cooked rice
- 3/4 liter brown spicy sauce
- 100 g macaroni
- 30 g ham
- 30 g smoked beef tongue
- truffle
- 1/2 tin mushrooms
- 15 g butter or margarine
- 40 g grated Parmesan or aged Gouda cheese

## Directions

1. Braise the beef tenderloin in the usual manner, cut it into not too thick slices, arrange them again in the shape of the tenderloin on a base of cooked rice or mashed potato on the platter and cover the meat with brown spicy sauce
2. Arrange around it cooked macaroni cut into pieces, mixed with finely cut smoked beef tongue, finely cut ham, truffles and grated Parmesan cheese
3. Sprinkle the tenderloin with Parmesan cheese and place halved mushrooms on it
4. Serve additional thin spicy sauce separately

title: Mirabellen op Brandewijn (Mirabelle Plums in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g mirabelle plums
- 300 g sugar
- 2.5 dl water
- lemon peel
- 1 l brandy

## Directions

1. Treat the fruit as described in recipe 1027.

title: Mirliton (Mirliton)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs
- One and a half ounces fine sugar
- White of three eggs, well beaten
- Orange blossom water
- Water
- A little flour
- Salt
- Feuilleté crust

## Directions

1. Put in a bowl two eggs and one and a half ounces fine sugar, mix well together
2. Add the white of three eggs, well beaten with a whisk
3. Mix in the bowl with a little orange blossom water, water, a little flour and salt
4. Make edges of feuilleté in your molds the thickness of a guilder
5. Put your mixture in the middle
6. Bring to a mild oven

title: Mixed Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- shallots
- small onions
- gherkins
- green beans
- peppercorns
- cauliflower without stem
- melon pieces
- cucumbers
- prunes
- water
- Spanish pepper
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook all ingredients in a little water and then place in glasses, well covered with vinegar
2. All the fruits mentioned are preserved only at the time of their ripening and treated this way
3. When you have everything together, divide them well mixed in glasses, put in each a good quantity of Spanish pepper, fill the glasses further with wine vinegar, stop them with a broad cork, cover with a bladder and store in a cool place

title: Mockturtle Ragout (Mock Turtle Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head
- 2 ox palates
- salt
- water
- shallots or onions
- mace
- peppercorns
- bay leaves
- 2½ hectogram calf thymus
- 2½ hectogram sausages
- butter
- flour
- beef bouillon
- herbs
- lemon slices without pits
- fine pepper
- cayenne pepper
- mace
- pimento
- morels
- mushrooms
- capers
- 1½ to 2 tablespoons Liebig's Meat Extract
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Set the calf's head and ox palates in cold water with salt on the fire, skim well and cook them with shallots or onions, mace, peppercorns and bay leaves for 2 to 2½ hours until tender and cut them into proper pieces.
2. Take the calf thymus, cook it, cut it into small pieces and let them with the sausages brown in butter.
3. Make flour browned in butter, add some beef bouillon drawn with herbs and shallots, stir it until a good smooth and well-bound sauce.
4. Add lemon slices without pits, fine pepper, a pinch of cayenne pepper, some mace and pimento and cook the meat together with morels, mushrooms and capers, as well as 1½ to 2 tablespoons Liebig's Meat Extract for about a quarter hour and add salt to taste.
5. When serving, stir in Madeira wine.

title: Moerbeien op Brandewijn (Mulberries in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g mulberries
- 200 g sugar
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 0.5 l brandy

## Directions

1. Treat the fruit as described in recipe 1031.

title: Mokkapudding (Coffee Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l strong coffee
- 15 g gelatin or 12.5 g powdered gelatin
- 60 g sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatin 10 to 15 minutes in cold water, then dissolve it with the sugar in the coffee, which has been warmed slightly.
2. Stir from time to time until the mixture begins to become gelatinous.
3. Pour it into a mold rinsed with cold water.
4. Let the pudding become cold and stiff.
5. Turn it out onto a damp dish and serve with cream sauce.

title: Mokkapudding met Geslagen Room (Coffee Pudding with Whipped Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l whipped cream
- 1 dl coffee extract
- 100 g sugar
- 10 g white gelatin or 8 g powdered gelatin

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatin 10 to 15 minutes in cold water.
2. Dissolve it with the sugar in the coffee extract.
3. Beat the cream stiff, pour the slightly cooled coffee extract through it while stirring.
4. Let the mixture become gelatinous, stirring occasionally, and place it in a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white.
5. Let the pudding become cold and stiff and turn it out onto a damp dish.

title: Mokkataart (Mocha Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 120 g flour
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 5 eggs
- 150 g butter
- 60 g powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon Nescafé
- 3 tablespoons water
- 1 sheet gelatin

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake like the German sand cake (see recipe 863).
2. Let it cool and cut it into two or three layers.
3. For the mocha, stir the butter until creamy and add the sifted powdered sugar.

title: Mokkataart (Mocha Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 120 g flour
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 5 eggs
- 150 g butter
- 60 g powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon Nescafé
- 3 spoons water
- 1 sheet gelatin

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake as in the German sand cake (recipe 863).
2. Let it cool and cut it into two or three layers.
3. For the mocha: stir the butter until creamy, add the sifted powdered sugar and the dissolved Nescafé.
4. Dissolve the soaked gelatin with as little boiling water as possible, let it cool and add it to the mocha.
5. Spread the different layers with mocha, place them on top of each other, also spread the top and pipe the remaining mocha in figures on it.
6. Note: If it is cold, no gelatin needs to be stirred through the mocha.

title: Molsalade (Mole Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- mole
- celeriac, finely cut
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak in water for some time.
2. Toss it with finely cut celeriac.
3. Dress with oil and vinegar.

title: Molsla (Stewed Molsla)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1 kg molsla
- 2.5 dl milk
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Sort through the molsla, wash it until the water runs completely clear and place it with the adhering water.
2. Let it cook until tender (1/2 hour) and drain in a colander.
3. Add the salt and stew it for 10 minutes in the sauce prepared in the same way as cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328).

title: Molsla of paardebloemen (Corn Salad or Dandelion Greens)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn salad
- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut off the small stems from the corn salad
2. Make sure to remove the woolly balls if the corn salad is rather old
3. Cook it more or less sour, depending on how bitter you want it
4. Then stew it slowly with butter and nutmeg

title: Montagne russe (Russian Mountain)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g biscuits or sponge cakes
- 1 1/2 deciliters Madeira, rum, or maraschino
- 3 deciliters milk
- 2 eggs
- 40 g sugar
- 10 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- some raspberry jelly
- red sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the biscuits in the Madeira, rum or maraschino and stack them pyramidally as high as possible on the plate.
2. Brush each layer with some raspberry jelly.
3. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla, pour this carefully, stirring vigorously, to the cornstarch, which has been mixed with the egg yolks.
4. Put everything back in the pan and let the custard, stirring with an egg beater, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes.
5. Pour it slightly cooled over the biscuits, let it cool further.
6. Then beat the egg white stiff, place it on top and sprinkle it here and there with some red sugar.

title: Morel Sauce (Morillesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Morels
- Bouillon
- Mace
- Egg yolks
- Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the morels well
2. Let them cook in bouillon with a little mace
3. Mix five to six egg yolks and one spoon flour with a little water well together
4. Combine with the sauce

title: Morellen in 't Zuur (Sour Cherries in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sour cherries
- 3 pounds of cinnamon
- 3 pounds of cloves
- 8 pounds of pepper
- 3 pounds of cardamom
- 5 ounces of brown sugar
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the cherries, wash and place in a large bottle
2. Take 3 pounds of cinnamon, 3 pounds of cloves, 8 pounds of pepper, 3 pounds of cardamom and 5 ounces of brown sugar, all coarsely ground
3. Pour a jug of wine vinegar over all of this, place in a tinned pan and let it cook a little; it must be well skimmed
4. When the juice has turned blue, pour it over the cherries, shaking them once or twice daily for 14 days

title: Morellen op Brandewijn (Sour Cherries in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g sour cherries
- 200 g sugar
- 6 cm cinnamon stick
- 0.5 l brandy

## Directions

1. Treat the fruit as described in recipe 1027.

title: Morellen te Konfijten (Candied Sour Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces sour cherries
- three-quarters pound sugar
- berry juice
- water

## Directions

1. Cut off the stems at the top
2. Set the sugar with some berry juice and water on the fire, let it cook until the sugar has melted
3. Then add the sour cherries and let them candy gently

title: Morellen te Konfijten (Sour Cherries Preserved in Sugar Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces of sour cherries
- three-quarters pound of sugar
- berry juice

## Directions

1. Cut off the stems at the top, cook the sugar with some berry juice

title: Morello Cherries in Brandy (Morellen in brandewijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole ripe morello cherries
- Best French brandy
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Bread sugar or white candy sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the stems off three-quarters of the way
2. Add best French brandy with some cloves, cinnamon, and bread sugar or white candy sugar

title: Morillesaus (Morel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Morels
- Bouillon
- Mace
- 5 to 6 egg yolks
- 1 spoon flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Wash and sort the morels well and let them cook in bouillon with some mace
2. Stir 5 to 6 egg yolks and 1 spoon flour with some water well together
3. Mix the sauce with this mixture

title: Moscovisch Gebak (Muscovite Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g mixture of currants, raisins and candied peel
- 5 eggs
- powdered sugar
- 100 g flour
- 20 g cornstarch
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar

## Directions

1. Stir egg yolks completely smooth with sugar
2. Rinse currants and raisins, which have had stems removed, with lukewarm water and dry in a cloth
3. Chop the candied peel
4. Stir the mixture through the yolks
5. Sift the flour and cornstarch, add hard butter or margarine and cut into small, equal pieces
6. Beat egg whites as stiff as possible, then mix as lightly as possible with butter and flour, egg yolks with sugar
7. Put batter immediately into a tulband mold that is greased and dusted with powdered sugar and flour
8. Place mold in a hot oven, after 5 minutes check carefully that the cake is not browning too much, then lay a greased piece of brown paper on it
9. Lower oven temperature and bake until brown and cooked through (30-45 minutes)
10. Test for doneness by inserting a knitting needle, which should come out dry
11. Turn out onto a cooling rack, let cool and dust through a sieve with powdered sugar

title: Mosselen (Mussels)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg mussels (750 g mussel meat)
- water
- shallots
- thyme
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash the mussels well clean, check them to ensure there are no mud mussels or gaping mussels (i.e. dead specimens).
2. Set them on with the adhering water and let them cook, shaking occasionally, until all shells have opened (-- 10 minutes).
3. Serve the mussels with oil and vinegar or with mustard or egg sauce.

title: Mosselsaus (Mussel Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l salted water or fish bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 25 mussels

## Directions

1. Cook the mussels in the usual way (recipe 195) and remove them from their shells
2. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
3. Blend over gentle heat with the mussels and serve with sole

title: Mosterdsaus (Mustard Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine, or 20 g Dureya maizena plus 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 tablespoon mustard

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce or maizena sauce)
2. Mix it away from the heat with the mustard, which to prevent lumps from forming, has been mixed with some water or some sauce
3. Serve with hard-boiled eggs, stockfish, haddock, etc.

title: Mousselinesaus (1e Recept) (Mousseline Sauce - 1st Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- pepper, salt and lemon juice to taste
- 3 tablespoons milk
- 1/2 dl whipped cream
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 355
2. At the last moment stir in as lightly as possible the cream beaten stiff with the egg white, finish the sauce with pepper, salt and lemon juice to taste and serve with salmon, turbot or sole

title: Mousselinesaus (2e Recept) (Mousseline Sauce - 2nd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl Rhine wine
- 1 dl fish stock
- 1 dl whipped cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with some salt, add the slightly softened butter or margarine and gradually add the Rhine wine
2. Stir the sauce with a whisk over a bain-marie until it begins to thicken
3. Then gradually add the fish stock, continue stirring until the sauce is bound and blend it lightly off the heat with

title: Mousselinesaus (Mousseline Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl Rhine wine
- 1 dl fish stock
- 1 dl heavy cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with some salt, add the butter or margarine that has been softened somewhat and gradually add the Rhine wine.
2. Stir the sauce with a whisk au bain marie until it begins to thicken.
3. Then gradually add the fish stock, continue stirring until the sauce is bound and remove from heat.
4. Lightly combine with the whipped cream.
5. Serve with salmon, turbot or sole.

title: Muisje (Small Mouse Cut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg muis
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 25 minutes per 500 g.

title: Mustard Sauce (Mosterdsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- A lump of butter
- One teaspoon flour
- One tablespoon sugar
- Two tablespoons mustard
- Two spoons vinegar
- Three spoons water

## Directions

1. Mix butter, flour, sugar, mustard, vinegar, and water
2. Let it boil briefly while stirring constantly

title: Méringues (Kisses)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 250 g powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, stir the sifted powdered sugar and the vanilla through it with a few strokes and pipe from this dough onto a damp wooden board small balls in the shape of a half egg.
2. Let the meringues in a lukewarm oven become dry and hard on the outside, turn them over, hollow them out and let the inside dry in the oven.
3. Fill them with whipped cream.

title: Nagenaamakte Schildpadsoep (Mock Turtle Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's head
- rainwater
- salt
- onions
- butter
- half a bottle of Rhine wine
- juice of two lemons
- zest of one lemon
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Have a calf's head split in half and soak it overnight in rainwater
2. Bind it well together and cook it with some salt for three hours, skimming well
3. Remove it from the broth and cut the meat from the bones into small cubes
4. Add some finely chopped onions fried in butter
5. Stir it with a couple of spoonfuls of salt in an earthenware pan until it is brown
6. Add half a bottle of Rhine wine and the juice of two lemons
7. Grate the zest of one lemon over it, with salt, pepper and nutmeg
8. Stir everything well together and let it stew well for about half an hour

title: Napolitaine (Neapolitan Drink)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 8 oranges
- juice of 2 lemons
- peel of 1 lemon
- 4 dl berry juice
- 200 g sugar
- 14 dl water

## Directions

1. Let the lemon peel steep in the water and dissolve the sugar in it.
2. Let this liquid cool, then add all the fruit juice and pour it several times through a flannel cloth until it is as clear as possible.
3. Pour it into well-cleaned bottles, close them with a clean cork and keep it no longer than 8 days.

title: Nassi Goreng (Fried Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 g cooked rice
- 150 g ham
- 150 g meat scraps
- 3 tablespoons ground onions
- several garlic cloves
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped fried onions
- some gravy
- 1 to 2 teaspoons sambal oelek
- salt
- 3 eggs
- 3 spoons milk
- salt
- pepper
- butter, margarine or oil

## Directions

1. Fry the ground onions and the finely chopped garlic cloves light brown in butter, margarine or oil
2. Add the sambal oelek and some salt
3. Let this fry for about 5 minutes
4. Add the pieces of meat and the sliced ham
5. Let them brown (about 5 minutes)
6. Stir in the dry rice and keep stirring so the grains do not become hard (10 minutes)
7. Add some gravy if needed
8. Fry a thin omelet in one of the above fats in the known manner (see recipe 383)
9. Slice this into thin strips
10. Garnish the dish with these strips laid crosswise over each other and the separately fried onions

title: Nesselrodepudding (Nesselrode Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ l cooking cream
- 3 eggs
- 50 g sugar
- ¼ vanilla pod or vanilla sugar
- 12 g white gelatin
- 50 g sultana raisins
- 25 g currants
- 30 g candied citron
- 50 g almonds
- ½ dl maraschino
- 70 g sugar to be caramelized

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the currants and raisins, rinse them with lukewarm water, cover with boiling water and cook for 10 minutes. Let them drain well in a sieve.
2. Peel the soaked almonds, chop or grind them finely; chop the candied citron.
3. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar and the vanilla pulp, add the cream and let the mass bind in a bain-marie while stirring well.
4. Make caramel from 70 g sugar, add it to the bound mass.
5. Remove from heat and combine everything with the mixed dried fruit, the almonds, the maraschino and the gelatin, which has been soaked for 10-15 minutes in cold water.
6. Let the mixture cool somewhat, add it to the beaten egg whites and let it become jelly-like, stirring occasionally.
7. Pour it into a pudding mold rinsed with egg white.
8. Let the pudding cool, turn it out onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.

title: Nierbroodjes (Kidney Toast, 2nd Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf kidney
- 1 egg white
- pepper
- salt
- 75 g onion
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- egg
- frying fat
- 6 to 8 thin slices of casino bread

## Directions

1. Soak the kidney several hours in cold water, remove the fat and chop or grind it finely
2. Mix it with the finely chopped onion, parsley, plenty of salt and pepper and enough egg white to obtain a fairly loose mixture
3. Remove the crusts from thin slices of casino bread, divide them in half and coat them on one side with the mixture, such that it lies higher in the middle than at the edges
4. Coat only the kidney side doubly with breadcrumbs, which is done most easily by placing the bread on the tip of a broad knife, strewing breadcrumbs over it through a sieve, then pouring beaten egg or egg white mixed with water over it and again strewing breadcrumbs through the sieve
5. Heat the frying fat in an iron pot, then place it on a gentler fire and fry the toasts light brown and cooked through in it (approximately 5 minutes), ensuring the kidney side remains longest under the fat
6. Let them drip on gray paper, place them on a warm plate covered with a cloth and serve as an appetizer
7. Garnish the plate with fresh parsley or with fried parsley

title: Nierbroodjes (Kidney Toasts - First Recipe - About 10 Pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 veal kidney
- half the weight of the kidney in veal meat
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- 1 small onion
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 dl broth
- about 2 dl milk
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 10 thin slices of bread without crust

## Directions

1. Soak the veal kidney in cold water for several hours.
2. Then place it in fresh water, bring it to a boil, pour off the water and let it cook done with fresh water and salt (about 1/2 hour).
3. Chop it finely with the meat.
4. Stir the onion and parsley, egg yolks, broth, milk, butter or margarine, pepper and salt to taste through it.
5. Make this mixture quite hot on the fire.
6. Slice thin pieces of bread, remove the crusts and make the pieces equal in shape.
7. Fry them with some butter or margarine in the pan light brown and crispy.
8. Lay a thick layer of the mixture on them and serve the toasts immediately on a warm dish decorated with a few fresh sprigs of parsley.

title: Niercarbonaden (Kidney Carbonades)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kidney carbonades of 150 g each
- 100 g butter, margarine or veal fat
- 7 1/2 g salt

## Directions

1. Treat the carbonades like veal cutlets (recipe 261), but be careful that the kidney does not come loose.
2. Stew them in the gravy made with a little water for another 1/2 hour gently.

title: Nierpastei (Kidney Pastry - serves approximately 8)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g calf kidney fat
- 200 g flour
- water and salt
- 500 g beef steak
- 2 braised calf kidneys
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846)
2. Roll it out the last time into a large thin sheet that can amply cover the inside of a round fireproof dish, with the excess dough cut off and re-rolled to serve as the lid of the pastry
3. Bake the beef steak, cut it into pieces, prepare the braised calf kidney in the usual manner (see recipe 266), stir both types of meat together and let them cool
4. Lay them on the dough in the dish, moisten the edge of the dish, lay the remaining dough somewhat generously over it and glue it to the edge of the little dish
5. Make figures in it with a fork, roll out the last remaining dough thinly, cut figures or strips from it and glue these to the lid with a little water
6. Brush everything with egg yolk beaten with a little water
7. Prick the dough here and there and let the pastry bake in a fairly hot oven until light brown and cooked through (20 to 25 minutes)

title: Niersoep (Kidney Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l lean stock with water or 1 l water and 4-5 Maggi's bouillon cubes
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 cloves
- 1 piece Spanish pepper
- 1 onion
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 calf kidney
- 3/4 tin of mushrooms (optional)
- English sauce and soya to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the kidney, soak it for several hours in cold water and let it cook gently in boiling water (approximately 1/2 hour)
2. Remove it and cut into small pieces
3. Let the herbs and chopped onion infuse in the lean stock with water or bouillon
4. Strain this liquid and add it gradually while stirring to the melted butter or margarine, which has been brought to a boil together with the flour and rid of lumps
5. Let the soup simmer while stirring for 10 minutes
6. Warm the kidney pieces in it
7. Before serving, add English sauce and soya to taste and if desired some mushrooms

title: Nieuwe aardappelen koken (Boiled New Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the potatoes in cold water for some time, then brush them clean and scrape them with a knife, as much as possible under water, until the skin is removed.
2. Wash them, set them to cook with little boiling water and some salt (10 g per litre of water).
3. Let them cook until they can be easily pierced with a fork, though not too easily (approximately 20 minutes).
4. Drain them as dry as possible, let them dry on the fire without a lid, shaking occasionally to prevent scorching.
5. Present them with melted or unmelted butter or margarine, or if desired shake some finely chopped parsley and butter or margarine through them before serving.

title: Nieuwe aardappelen koken (Cooking New Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the potatoes in cold water for some time, then brush them off
2. Scrape them with a knife, as much as possible under water, until the skin is off
3. Wash them, set them on with little boiling water and some salt (10 g per 1 water)
4. Let them cook until they can be pierced with a fork easily, but not too easily (approximately 20 minutes)
5. Drain them as dry as possible, let them dry on the fire without a lid, shaking occasionally to prevent burning

title: Normandische Tongschotel (Sole en Demi-deuil)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small cooking soles
- 18 mussels
- 200 g shrimp
- 1 tin of mushrooms
- 1/2 tin of truffles
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 3 dl fish stock
- mace
- parsley
- thyme
- 1 dl cream
- some Rhine wine (or milk)
- 2 egg yolks
- some salt (some lemon juice)

## Directions

1. Make fillets of the soles in the usual way or order them filleted, wash them and cook them in abundant boiling water with salt until done.
2. Draw from the bones with + 4 dl water, salt and herbs in + 1/2 hour some fish stock.
3. Lay the fillets crosswise on a warm platter and keep it warm on the steam.
4. Prepare the sauce in the usual way.
5. Use the sifted fish stock for this.
6. Add the cream (and the Rhine wine) to it and heat it well.
7. Carefully blend the sauce with the beaten egg yolks, diluted with some cream, add some lemon juice if desired and pour the sauce over the fillets.
8. Garnish the platter further with the shrimp washed in salt water, the very finely chopped truffels, the mushrooms cut in half and the mussels, which have been cooked in the usual way (recipe 195) and removed from the shell.

title: Noten op Brandewijn (Nuts in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- nuts
- sugar
- brandy

## Directions

1. Peel the nuts. Put them with some sugar in a well-cleaned bottle. Pour brandy over them so that the nuts are well covered. Seal the bottle airtight. Let the nuts stand for at least 3 months before using.

title: Nougat (Almond Brittle)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g sugar
- 200 g almonds
- 10 g butter or margarine (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds, dry them, snip them.
2. Put the sugar (if desired with the butter or margarine) in an iron pot or frying pan.
3. Stir it over a not too strong fire, until it has become evenly brown, and then mix it with the almonds.
4. Pour the mixture out onto a greased sheet, press it out with a lemon or damp spoon, cut it quickly into diamonds and take them off the sheet.

title: Nougat (Noogaat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Five ounces peeled sweet almonds
- Four ounces fine white sugar
- Oil
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Peel the sweet almonds, remove the skin again, wash them and let them dry
2. Cut them into pieces (in five)
3. Dry them in an oven until they become somewhat yellow
4. Put four ounces fine white sugar in a pan and let it melt on the stove, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon
5. When the sugar is melted, add the warm almonds
6. Take the pan from the fire and stir the almonds constantly
7. Take a mold which you grease with oil
8. Put your almonds in it and work them with a lemon that you hold in your hand against the mold, so it takes the shape you desire
9. Lay them as thin as possible
10. Slide out of the mold and set as cold as possible
11. You can make all kinds of shapes

title: Nougat-ijs (Nougat Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter vanilla ice cream
- 250 g crushed nougat

## Directions

1. Prepare vanilla ice cream in the usual manner, stir through the finely crushed nougat and let the mixture freeze.

title: Nougatmandjes (Nougat Baskets)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g sugar
- 200 g almonds
- 10 g butter or margarine (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare nougat, as indicated in recipe 802.
2. Roll it out on a greased, warmed sheet with a greased rolling pin to a thickness of about 1/4 cm, cut circles from it with a metal ring and press these as quickly as possible into small tart molds.
3. Warm the remaining nougat again, roll this equally thin and cut long, narrow strips from it, which are bent over and warm, by way of handles, attached to the baskets.
4. Fill the baskets with ice cream or whipped cream, mixed with powdered sugar.
5. N.B. If the nougat becomes too hard during rolling, which happens very quickly, it must be put back in the pan and warmed again. To prevent it from hardening, one preferably works at a warm place.

title: Oblie (Waffle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces wheat flour
- 2.5 ounces sugar
- 3 loods finely ground cinnamon
- 4 eggs
- 2 ounces melted butter
- A wine glass of brandy
- A little aniseed
- Warm sweet milk

## Directions

1. Beat together with warm sweet milk the wheat flour, sugar, cinnamon, eggs, melted butter, brandy, and aniseed
2. Put this batter in a waffle iron and bake it
3. When coming out of the iron, immediately roll them around a stick and when cold, take it out

title: Oesterpasteitjes (Oyster Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- potato
- oysters
- white wine
- bouillon
- egg yolk
- flour
- lemon juice
- nutmeg
- butter

## Directions

1. Line small molds with puff pastry
2. Put a potato in each and cover them
3. Let this bake done
4. Remove the potato from them and fill them with oysters stewed as follows:
5. Take equal parts white wine and bouillon, stir this with egg yolks and flour until bound
6. Add raw oysters to it with some lemon juice, nutmeg and butter and let it boil up briefly

title: Oestersaus (Oyster Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l salted water or fish bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 20 oysters
- lemon juice to taste
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Heat the oysters thoroughly in as little boiling water as possible
2. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
3. Carefully blend with the beaten egg yolk, the oysters and as much of the oyster liquid as needed until the sauce reaches the desired thickness
4. Add lemon juice, pepper and salt to taste and serve the sauce with sole or turbot

title: Oestersaus over kippen (Oyster Sauce for Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dozen oysters
- water
- butter
- flour
- parsley, chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Take 3 dozen oysters and boil them briefly
2. Remove the oysters as soon as the water boils
3. Let them drain
4. Put them in a pan with water, butter, flour and the water in which the oysters were cooked
5. Add 1.5 ounces butter
6. If desired, add chopped parsley

title: Oeufs au caramel (Caramel Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 to 5 dl milk
- 1 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 80 g brown sugar
- approximately 200 g sugar for coating the cups

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar and the vanilla pulp. Add the cold milk (for small eggs 4 dl, for large eggs 5 dl). Make caramel from the remaining sugar and let it run in 4 cups or molds so that the bottom and sides are covered with a thin layer. Then strain the egg mixture into it and place the cups in a pan with water that is kept near the boil until the egg is set (approximately 3/4 to 1 hour); a knife must then come out dry. Turn out the puddings and serve them warm or cold.

title: Oil Sauce (Oliesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three lemons
- Finely chopped parsley
- Onions
- Tarragon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water
- Good vinegar
- Oil

## Directions

1. Peel the lemons and remove as much of the white pith as possible
2. Cut them into slices of equal thickness
3. Mix with finely chopped parsley, onions, tarragon, salt, pepper, and a little water
4. Add good vinegar and oil
5. This sauce is very good with fried fish

title: Oliebollen (Dutch Fried Dough Balls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg flour
- 30 g yeast
- 1/2 liter warm milk
- 35 g butter or margarine
- 35 g sugar
- 75 g currants, raisins and candied citrus peel
- 15 g snippets
- some lemon juice and grated lemon zest
- 1 sour apple
- 1 egg
- 10 g salt
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Add to the flour enough warm milk so it no longer clouds.
2. Stir out the lumps from this batter.
3. Add the remaining milk, salt, well-washed currants and raisins stripped of their stems, the shredded candied citrus peel, sour apples, the softened butter or margarine, snippets, and optionally some lemon juice and grated lemon zest.
4. Mix the yeast with some warm milk or sugar, add it to the batter and let this rise covered on a lukewarm place for 3 to 4 hours.
5. In the meantime, heat the frying fat in an iron pot.
6. Add the batter in small portions with the help of two spoons and fry the balls light brown and cooked through (approximately 5 to 7 minutes).
7. Test if they are cooked by inserting a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
8. Let the balls drain thoroughly on gray paper, then dust with powdered sugar.

title: Oliekoeken (Oil Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces flour
- 1 and a half ounces currants
- 1 ounce raisins
- 2 drams snippets and candied peel
- 3 to 4 sour apples, cut in pieces
- Some peeled almonds
- 8 eggs
- Yeast
- One third lard and two thirds rapeseed oil

## Directions

1. Make a moderately thick batter with warm sweet milk, flour, currants, raisins, snippets, candied peel, apples, almonds, eggs and yeast
2. Pre-fry the lard and rapeseed oil with a slice of rye bread
3. Fry the cakes in this oil

title: Om eene Ham tot Roken in te Zouten (To Salt a Ham for Smoking)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- salt
- saltpeter
- optional: coarsely ground cloves, pepper, Jamaica pepper, bay leaves

## Directions

1. Meat must lie for at least 24 hours and be completely cooled before cutting
2. Rub each piece individually with salt and saltpeter carefully, calculating one pound salt and one and a half loot saltpeter for 16 pounds meat
3. Lay the pieces close together in a sturdy oak barrel with the bottom strewn with mixed salt
4. Sprinkle salt and saltpeter between each layer and arrange so salt comes on top and the specified amount is distributed evenly
5. For a more piquant taste, mix coarsely ground cloves, pepper and Jamaica pepper with the salt to rub in, and strew some bay leaves between each layer
6. When the barrel is well filled, seal it properly and turn it daily a few times on its bottom
7. Large pieces such as hams must lie four weeks before they are well penetrated with brine; smaller pieces such as tongues are ready in three weeks
8. For the ham, make an incision underneath at the large bone and put salt there so no spoilage occurs at the bone
9. When the barrel is opened and you want part of the meat to remain, place a wooden lid with a stone on top, turn the meat daily and baste it with its brine

title: Omelet (Sweet Omelet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 20 to 25 g sugar
- 25 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar.
2. Melt the butter or margarine in the skillet, pour in the mixture and prick with a fork until the omelet begins to release from the entire surface of the pan.
3. Allow it to brown lightly on the bottom, optionally place some jam or marmalade on it, fold it in thirds and sprinkle with some sifted powdered sugar.

title: Omelet met garnalenragoit (Omelet with Shrimp Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper and salt to taste
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 100 g shrimp
- 1 dl milk or cream, or half milk, half cream
- pepper and salt
- 10 g flour
- 10 g butter or margarine
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare an omelet in the usual way (see recipe 383) and fold it in thirds after inserting the shrimp ragout, which has been prepared in the usual way (see recipe 163).
2. Place on a warmed dish and serve with bread.

title: Omelet met kaas (Cheese Omelet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper and salt to taste
- 50 g Parmesan or Gouda cheese
- 25 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt, add the grated cheese and water and make an omelet as described in recipe 383.
2. Place on a warmed dish and serve with bread.

title: Omelet met nierragoit (Omelet with Kidney Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper and salt to taste
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 1 calf's kidney
- 2 dl bouillon or lean jus with water
- 20 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- onion (optional)
- Worcestershire sauce and soy sauce to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the omelet as described in recipe 383 and fold it in thirds after inserting the kidney ragout, which has been prepared as 'braised calf's kidney' (see recipe 266) and to which Worcestershire sauce and soy sauce have been added off the heat.
2. Place on a warmed dish and serve with bread.
3. Serve the remaining ragout on the side or place the ragout inside the omelet as described in the previous recipe.

title: Omelet met tuinkruiden (Omelet with Fine Herbs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons water
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley, tarragon, pimpernel, chervil, chives
- 30 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt, add most of the finely chopped herbs and the water and cook as described in recipe 383.
2. Place on a warmed plate and sprinkle the remaining herbs over it.

title: Omelette in een Vorm (Souffle Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- three full tablespoons white sugar
- four bitter cookies, finely made
- one spoon potato flour
- a little salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Take six eggs and separate the yolks only
2. Add to the yolks three full tablespoons white sugar and four bitter cookies finely made
3. Add one spoon potato flour and a little salt
4. Mix everything together
5. Prepare a mold and grease it with butter
6. Beat the egg whites, and when they become stiff, mix them with the yolks
7. Place in the mold, but do not fill it completely
8. Place in a not too hot oven
9. When cooked, turn it out onto a plate
10. It should be good in color and tremble

title: Ommelette (Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons wheat flour
- 4 eggs
- 4 teacups sweet milk
- A little sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Mix together the wheat flour, eggs, sweet milk, and sugar
2. Pour the batter into a pan with butter
3. Prick it with a fork until it is set
4. Add butter as desired

title: Ommelette with Apple (Omelet with Apple)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Wheat flour
- Eggs
- Sweet milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take thick slices of apples and let them cook gently in the pan
2. Add the aforementioned batter to the apples
3. When done, sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over it

title: Ongespouwen Wafelen (Unsprouted Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- 14 lood butter
- ½ pound sugar
- 5½ lood cinnamon
- 2 eggs
- wine and sugar

## Directions

1. Mix wheat flour, butter, sugar, cinnamon, and eggs with wine and sugar until thick enough.

title: Ongespouwen Wafelen Anders (Unsprouted Waffles, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound wheat flour
- ½ cup buckwheat flour
- 6 eggs
- 1 quart sweet milk
- ½ quart water
- ½ pound melted butter
- 1 tablespoon thick yeast with little sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the milk and add cold water so it reaches the right temperature.
2. Beat the eggs first, then add some of the milk and water.
3. Add the flour, then more milk and water, and beat well.
4. Add ½ pound melted butter and 1 tablespoon thick yeast with little sugar.
5. Let rise quickly by the fire, then set the pot aside until baking.
6. Before baking, stir in a glass of cold water.

title: Ontbeende kip (Boned Chicken)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kip (chicken)
- wat zout (salt)
- 300 g kalfsgehakt (veal mince)
- 1/2 ei (1/2 egg)
- 20 g oud brood zonder korst (old bread without crust)
- peper (pepper)
- zout (salt)
- nootmuskaat (nutmeg)
- 100 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)

## Directions

1. Snijd het vel van de kip midden over de rug los, maak met een scherp mes het vlees van de beenderen los, z6 dat het karkas en de bovenstukken der poten verwijderd kunnen worden.
2. Zout de kip van binnen en vul ze niet te stijf met het gehakt, dat op de gewone wijze aangemengd is.
3. Naai het vel weer dicht, zet de kip in haar oorspronkelijke vorm op en braad ze op de gewone wijze (zie recept 309) lichtbruin en gaar.
4. Maak de jus met wat water af.

title: Oranjemarmelade (Orange Marmalade)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 oranges or blood oranges
- 3 lemons
- 1.75 liters water
- sugar in the amount that the fruit weighs after cooking
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- rubber bands
- cellophane
- labels

## Directions

1. Brush the fruit clean, wash them and put them on with the cold water.
2. Let them cook until they are so soft that one can easily prick them with a silver fork or wooden spoon handle.
3. Take the fruit out of the liquid, weigh it and add its weight in sugar to the remaining liquid.
4. Cut the fruit in quarters, carefully remove from the flesh the pits and fibers and put the flesh in the sugar solution.
5. Cut off the white part of the peel and cut the peels into strips as thin and as long as possible.
6. Put these too in the sugar solution and now let the marmalade cook down, stirring occasionally, until it reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369).
7. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).
8. Note: In this marmalade the same weight of sugar as fruit is used, because otherwise the marmalade would be too bitter.

title: Osseborst (Beef Brisket)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef brisket
- Salt
- Water
- Flour or ground rusks
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Rub the brisket well with salt and cook it in water with a little salt until done
2. When cooked, cut it into pieces and place it in a pan with some of the cooking liquid
3. Bind the liquid with flour or ground rusks
4. Add a lump of butter, some fine salt, nutmeg, pepper and a bit of lemon peel
5. Braise it for half an hour with fire above and below

title: Ossefricadel (Beef Fricadel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dutch pound of beef slices
- 2½ ounces kidney fat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Water

## Directions

1. Lay the beef slices on a board and sprinkle with salt, pepper and cloves
2. Divide the fat so that you can roll a piece of it in each slice
3. Roll up the slices and tie them with string
4. Lay them closely in an earthenware pan, add some water
5. Braise them until they are brown

title: Ossenhaas (Beef Tenderloin for 3 Days)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg beef tenderloin
- 150 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- 10 g salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Braise the beef tenderloin, as it is a fine meat, in butter or margarine, but not in fat
2. Allow 15 minutes braising time per 500 g, but for the whole tenderloin no more than 60 minutes, as the length increases but not the thickness
3. Finish the jus with milk in the manner indicated for beef steak

title: Ossenhaas met Groenten (Beef Tenderloin with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tenderloin
- butter
- small carrots
- peas
- cauliflower
- asparagus cut into small pieces
- salt
- flour
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Take the beef tenderloin, lard it and braise it in butter.
2. Meanwhile cook small carrots, peas, cauliflower and asparagus cut into small pieces (all well washed) in salted water, almost tender.
3. Make flour and butter brown on the fire, stir it with Meat Extract until a smooth sauce.
4. Add the various vegetables to it and let them cook a little longer.
5. Just before serving add the jus from the beef tenderloin, place the beef tenderloin on a warm plate, put the vegetables around it and pour the sauce over.

title: Ossenhaas met verschillende groenten (Beef Tenderloin with Various Vegetables)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg beef tenderloin
- 100 g butter or margarine
- salt
- pepper
- milk
- 1 small cauliflower
- 1/2 tin of peas
- 1 tin of asparagus tips
- 500 g carrots
- 400 g green beans
- 1 kg potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 150 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Braise the beef tenderloin in the usual manner
2. Cut it into not too thick slices and arrange them in the shape of the tenderloin on the platter
3. Place warm, stewed vegetables of contrasting colors (such as carrots, peas, asparagus tips, cauliflower, green beans, etc.) in compartments around it
4. If desired, separate the compartments with mashed potato piped from a pastry bag
5. Pour jus finished with milk over the meat and serve the remaining jus in sauce boats

title: Osseschenkelsoep (Beef Shank Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef shank with bones
- water
- salt
- rice
- vegetables of choice
- ground mace
- beef meat for meatballs

## Directions

1. Have the meat cut from an ox shank and finely chopped
2. Crush the bones well
3. Cook the bones the day before with water and salt for 3 hours, skimming well
4. Strain the broth through a sieve
5. When cool, remove the fat
6. Put the broth on to cook and add rice and as many varieties of vegetables as desired
7. Make meatballs from the chopped meat with a piece of mace inside
8. Add the meatballs when the vegetables are cooking and let it cook for another two hours

title: Ossestaartsoep (Ox Tail Soup) (6 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox tail
- 1 1/2 l water
- 70 g butter or margarine
- 1 large carrot
- 3 onions
- bunch of celery greens
- 10 white peppercorns
- 3 bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- 60 g flour (for binding)
- 60 g butter or margarine (for binding)
- English sauce and soya to taste
- Madeira (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the tail, cut or chop it into pieces and brown them evenly in the butter or margarine with the chopped carrot, chopped onions and chopped celery
2. Put everything in a pan, add the herbs and water and let it cook gently for 2 hours
3. Fill up the bouillon from time to time to prevent it from reducing too much
4. Strain the bouillon, let it cool, skim off the fat and add it while stirring to the melted butter or margarine which has been brought to a boil together with the flour and rid of lumps
5. Let the soup simmer while stirring until cooked (approximately 10 minutes)
6. Add away from heat English sauce and soya or some Madeira and if desired a few fine pieces of meat from the tail

title: Ossetong (Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Pepper
- Salt
- Ground rusks

## Directions

1. When the tongue is cooked, remove the skin and cut it into slices
2. Roll them in pepper, salt and ground rusks
3. Fry them brown in a frying pan

title: Ossetong met Augurkjes (Beef Tongue with Gherkins)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Cloves
- Bouillon
- Flour
- Water
- Butter
- Gherkins

## Directions

1. Take a beef tongue, cook it for half an hour and skin it, then let it cool
2. Lard the tongue and place it in a pan with some crushed cloves and some bouillon
3. Let it simmer gently for 4 to 5 hours
4. Lay the tongue on a dish and split it open from the underside so that it lies on the dish like a heart
5. Make a sauce from bouillon, flour, crushed cloves, water and butter
6. When nearly done, add finely cut gherkins and pour this sauce over the tongue when serving
7. You can also leave the tongue whole according to preference

title: Ossetong met Champignons (Ox Tongue with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ox tongue
- Mushrooms
- Flour
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Handle the tongue as on the previous page; do not lard it
2. Make the sauce as with the gherkins, but use mushrooms instead
3. The mushrooms must be cooked in the sauce from the beginning

title: Ossetong met een Saus à la Matelote (Beef Tongue with Matelote Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tongue
- Red or white wine (one bottle)
- Flour
- Sugar
- Bouillon
- Shallots

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue as described previously, but do not lard it
2. Make a sauce from a bottle of red or white wine, with some flour, sugar, bouillon and shallots

title: Ossetong met Saus à la Matelote (Ox Tongue with Matelote Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ox tongue
- Red or white wine
- Flour
- Sugar
- Bouillon
- Shallots

## Directions

1. Handle the tongue as described previously, but do not lard it
2. Make a sauce from a bottle of red or white wine, with a little flour, some sugar, bouillon and shallots

title: Ossevleesch Soep (Beef Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dutch pound beef, cut into pieces
- salt
- 1.5 ounces rice
- two bunches celery
- a handful of sorrel
- a handful of chervil
- one head of endive
- some pieces of cauliflower
- one head of lettuce
- a handful of purslane
- a little ground mace

## Directions

1. Cut 1 Dutch pound of beef into pieces
2. Cook it with some salt, skimming well
3. Add 1.5 ounces rice, two bunches celery, a handful of sorrel, a handful of chervil, one head of endive, some pieces of cauliflower, one head of lettuce, a handful of purslane, and a little ground mace

title: Oublies (Wafer Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g butter or margarine
- 125 g powdered sugar
- 3 egg whites
- 75 g flour

## Directions

1. Cream the butter or margarine until fluffy, add the sifted powdered sugar and stir it completely smooth.
2. Beat the egg white until stiff as possible and mix it with a few strokes with butter or margarine and powdered sugar.
3. Then add, as quickly as possible, the sifted flour.
4. Place a piece of soft white cardboard on a greased baking sheet, 9 cm wide and 11 cm long, with a piece cut out from the middle 6 cm wide and 8 cm long.
5. Spread a layer of the batter as thin as possible in this opening.
6. Place the cardboard next to the formed cookie, spread dough in it again and continue until the baking sheet is full.
