7. Bake the oublies in a fairly warm oven as quickly as possible until light brown and done.
8. Roll them with a knife and remove them directly from the sheet.
9. Let the sheet cool before new cookies are spread on it.

title: Oude aardappelen koken (Cooking Old Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Select potatoes of equal size, peel them as thinly as possible, remove all eyes with the point of a knife and place them immediately in cold water
2. Wash them several times, check again that all eyes are removed and set them on with little boiling water and some salt
3. Shake the potatoes occasionally
4. Let them cook until they can be easily pierced with a fork, but not too easily (20 to 25 minutes)
5. Drain them as dry as possible; if they are not approximately dry-cooked, let them dry further on the fire without a lid, shaking occasionally to make them floury

title: Oyster Sauce (for Chicken, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three dozen oysters
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Water in which the oysters were cooked
- One and a half ounces butter
- Chopped parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Bring the oysters to a boil and remove them as soon as the water boils
2. Let them drain
3. Put them in a pan with water, butter, and flour mixed together, along with the water in which the oysters were cooked
4. Add one and a half ounces of butter
5. Optionally add chopped parsley

title: Paardebloemwortelen (Dandelion Roots)
body:
## Ingredients

- dandelion roots
- sorrel
- salt
- butter
- nutmeg
- bouillon (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook after removing the buds
2. Stew with a little sorrel, salt, butter and nutmeg
3. For taste, can also add a little bouillon

title: Paasbrood (Easter Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 20 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 2 dl lukewarm milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 150 g currants
- 150 g raisins
- 30 g candied peel
- grated peel of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Make a supple dough from flour, yeast, salt, egg, milk, melted butter or margarine and grated lemon peel.
2. Let the dough rise covered on a lukewarm spot for 1 hour.
3. Then mix in the cleaned and dried currants and raisins with the finely cut candied peel.
4. Let the dough rise covered on a lukewarm spot for 1 hour in a butter-greased bread pan in which a butter-greased piece of butter paper has been placed.
5. Handle the bread further as indicated for white bread (recipe 835).

title: Paling (Eel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 kg eel
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the eel (by cutting off the head, loosening the skin at the neck and pulling it off, removing the innards and cutting the eel into pieces).
2. Wash the fish well, pierce the thick pieces with a larding needle, cut off the fins.
3. Wash the eel and cook it in boiling water with salt, until it is done (6 to 8 minutes).
4. Remove the eel from the pan with a skimming spoon and serve it with egg sauce, capers or butter sauce.
5. Note: Use very thick eel for cooking eel.

title: Paling in Gelei (Eel in Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg thick eel
- 3/4 liter vinegar
- 2.5 deciliters water
- 35 g white gelatin or 30 g powdered gelatin
- 4 lemon slices
- a piece of mace
- a piece of Spanish pepper
- 4 bay leaves
- 15 white peppercorns
- 15 g salt
- parchment paper
- twine
- labels

## Directions

1. Cut the eel into large pieces, clean them in the usual way, wash them and cook them in the vinegar, which has been brought to a boil with the water, salt, bay leaves, mace and peppercorns, until done (6 to 8 minutes)
2. Remove them from the liquid and place them in well-cleaned jars or bottles, arrange the sliced lemon slices, Spanish pepper and a bay leaf between the glass and the eel
3. Dissolve the soaked gelatin in the cooking liquid, strain the liquid through a dense cloth rinsed in hot water, check if there is enough salt and pour it, after it has cooled slightly, over the eel
4. Make sure the eel is well covered with the aspic
5. Close the jars with damp parchment paper, store the eel for at least 3 weeks before use and no longer than about 6 weeks

title: Paling, Gestoofd (Braised Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium eel
- salt
- pepper
- crushed biscuit
- lemon
- water

## Directions

1. Take medium eel, cut it into pieces the length of a finger
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and braise them with crushed biscuit, lemon cut into slices and water

title: Palingbroodjes (Eel Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- eggs
- salt
- eel pieces, baked

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough of butter, flour, eggs and salt
2. Wrap it around baked pieces of eel
3. Bake until they have a nice color

title: Palingsoep (Eel Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel pieces
- cloves
- bay leaves
- bouillon
- parsley
- cold butter
- flour
- hardtack
- eggs
- egg yolks
- veal bouillon

## Directions

1. Cook the eel pieces with some cloves and bay leaves well done
2. Remove some pieces from the bone and chop them finely with water and parsley
3. Now cook bouillon in which you place a lump of cold butter and stir in a couple of tablespoons of flour until a thick sauce
4. Mix in the finely chopped eel and let it cool
5. Make small pieces with a spoon, roll them in fine hardtack and form balls
6. Roll them through beaten eggs and then again through crushed hardtask
7. Finally fry them done in butter
8. Now take the necessary veal bouillon, let it cook through with mace
9. Add the eel to it and finish it with egg yolks
10. Pour the soup over the balls, previously placed in the tureen

title: Palingsoep (Eel Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l salted water
- 300 g thin eel
- piece of Spanish pepper
- mace
- 35 g sorrel
- 35 g chervil
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg (optional)
- Maggi's Aroma if desired

## Directions

1. Clean the eel, cut it into pieces of approximately 3 cm, wash it and cook it until done (approximately 5 minutes) in the boiling water with the salt, Spanish pepper and mace.
2. Remove the eel with a skimming spoon from the pan, remove the fish bones and cook the well-washed and very finely chopped vegetables in the liquid from which the spices have been removed until done (15 minutes).
3. Melt the butter or margarine and bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour, carefully rub out all lumps and gradually add the liquid in small amounts.
4. Let the soup cook while stirring (approximately 10 minutes), warm the eel pieces in it and if desired carefully pour the soup into the egg that has been beaten in the soup tureen, stirring vigorously. Finish the soup with a few drops of Maggi's Aroma.

title: Pannekoeken (Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- 5 ounces flour
- 4 eggs
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Make a thin batter with milk of 5 ounces flour, 4 eggs and salt
2. Fry in butter into thin cakes

title: Panvis (Stockfish and Rice Pan)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cooked stockfish (= 100 g unsoaked, raw stockfish)
- 400 g cooked rice or barley
- 1 kg potatoes
- 100 g onion
- 50 g butter or margarine
- stockfish water or leftover mustard sauce
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the skins and bones from the leftover stockfish (the warmer it is done, the easier it goes).
2. Mash the stockfish, mix it with the rice, the finely mashed potatoes, the onions, which have been minced and fried light brown in butter or margarine, and some stockfish water (or leftover mustard sauce) until the mixture has reached the required thickness.
3. Add pepper, salt and nutmeg to taste.
4. Put everything in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water, sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place a few small pieces of butter on it and let the dish brown in the oven.

title: Paprika Schnitzel (Veal Cutlets with Paprika)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beautiful, lean, flat veal cutlets of at least 100 g each
- Salt
- 1 egg
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 1 to 2 red or green bell peppers

## Directions

1. Fry the cutlets as indicated in the Wiener Schnitzel recipe.
2. Remove the stems from the bell peppers.
3. Cut the fruits lengthwise in half.
4. Remove the seeds and seed membranes.
5. Wash the halved fruits and cook them in a little salted water until tender (10 to 20 minutes).
6. Cut each half into thin strips.
7. Fry these briefly in the hot butter at the end.
8. Garnish the cutlets with them, or make paprika sauce and pour it over the schnitzels when serving.

title: Paprika-kip (Paprika Chicken)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 malse kippetjes (2 tender young chickens)
- zout (salt)
- 100 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- 1 uitje (1 small onion)
- 1/3 l zure room (1/3 liter sour cream)
- 1 theelepeltje paprika (1 teaspoon paprika)

## Directions

1. Snijd de gewassen kippetjes in stukken.
2. Zout ze en stoof ze in de boter of margarine met een gesnipperd uitje (30 minuten).
3. Giet er de zure room bij en voeg de paprika toe.
4. Laat de kippetjes in deze saus nog een kwartier nastoven.
5. Voeg, zo nodig, nog wat water toe.

title: Paprikasla (Pepper Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large pimentos or peppers (preferably 2 green and 1 red)
- salt
- 1½ tablespoons vinegar
- 2 tablespoons salad oil or some salad sauce or mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Rinse the pimentos, halve them, remove the seeds and seed membranes. Cut the pimentos into narrow strips.
2. Cook these strips for about 5 minutes in some water with salt and a very small dash of vinegar. Let the pimento drain and treat them further as described in previous salad recipes.

title: Parelgort met Karnemelk (Pearl Barley Porridge with Buttermilk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l buttermilk
- 150 g pearl barley
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the buttermilk with salt to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Sprinkle in the rinsed and soaked pearl barley while stirring (see previous recipe) and prepare the porridge further, as described in the previous recipe.

title: Parelgort met Melk (Pearl Barley Porridge with Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l milk
- 150 g pearl barley
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Rinse the pearl barley and soak it overnight in cold water.
2. Let it drain well.
3. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
4. Sprinkle in the soaked pearl barley while stirring, let the milk come to a boil again while stirring and let the porridge become done on a very gentle fire, stirring occasionally (approximately 2 hours).

title: Parsley Sauce (Pieterseliésaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Fried flour and butter
- Fresh butter
- Finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Add fried flour and butter to bouillon or water with salt
2. Set it on the heat
3. While it comes to a boil, stir in a lump of fresh butter and finely chopped parsley

title: Parsley Sauce (Pieterseliésaus) - Alternative
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped parsley
- Water
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Set chopped parsley to cook with a little water
2. When done, stir in a piece of butter and some flour
3. Let it boil briefly

title: Partridge Pie (Patrijzenpastei)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four partridges, cleaned
- Liver from partridges or other birds
- Bacon
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Pastry crust of approximately three pounds

## Directions

1. Cut the legs and wings off the cleaned partridges
2. Make a farce (forcemeat) from liver, bacon, parsley, salt and pepper, mixing everything well together
3. Fill the partridges with this farce
4. Take a pastry crust of approximately three pounds
5. Make an opening in the lid
6. Bake in the oven for approximately three hours
7. Allow to cool
8. Close the hole in the lid

title: Pasteitjes (Small Pastry Cases)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or calf kidney fat
- 100 g flour
- salt
- water (approximately 1/2 dl)

## Directions

1. Prepare feuilletée (puff pastry) in the usual manner (see recipe 846)
2. Roll it out the last time to a thickness of approximately 3/4 cm and cut 12 circles with a ring or wine glass
3. From half the circles, cut lids with a smaller ring or smaller glass, which are slightly flattened and brushed with beaten egg yolk mixed with water
4. Seal the remaining rings with water or egg white onto the circles, brush only the top with egg yolk beaten with water and place the pastries and lids on a baking sheet
5. Bake them in a hot oven light brown and cooked through (15 to 18 minutes), do not look in the oven during the first 5 minutes as there is a risk of sinking
6. Remove them from the sheet, hollow out the undercooked inside part slightly, fill them with some ragout and place the lids on top

title: Pastry Crust for Pies (Korst om pastei in te doen)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two measures flour
- Half ounce salt
- Four ounces butter
- Six egg yolks
- One glass water

## Directions

1. Spread flour on the work surface, leaving a small hole in the middle
2. Place in the hole: half ounce salt, four ounces butter, six egg yolks, and one glass water
3. Mix the salt, butter and eggs together
4. Knead with the flour
5. If too dry, add a little more water, but carefully so it does not become too soft
6. Use for chicken pie or pigeon pie, or for pan fish
7. For tarts, this crust should be somewhat softer

title: Patrijs (Partridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 small or 2 large partridges
- at least 100 to 150 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 4 thin strips of larding bacon (optional)

## Directions

1. Treat the partridges as you would pigeon (see recipe 318).
2. If desired, place a piece of larding bacon over the breast while roasting or lard the partridge with thin strips of larding bacon.
3. Serve with stewed fruit or salad.
4. Note: Partridge can also be served with chestnut puree.

title: Patrijs met Zuurkool (Partridge with Sauerkraut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 partridges
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 1 kg sauerkraut
- 2 pairs knockwurst
- 500 g fairly fat bacon
- some flour
- salt
- some potato puree

## Directions

1. Roast the partridges in the usual way (see recipe 320).
2. Cook the sauerkraut in the usual way (see recipe 507) until cooked and dry, but let the lukewarm water-rinsed bacon cook with it for half an hour.
3. Cook the knockwurst like sausages (see recipe 245), but allow 10 minutes for this.
4. Cut them lengthwise in half.
5. Cut the bacon into slices and the partridges into 4 pieces.
6. Stir a couple of spoonfuls of flour through the cooked sauerkraut until the sauerkraut is bound and the flour is cooked (approximately 10 minutes).
7. Line the sides of a smooth pudding mold (e.g. warm pudding mold) with the knockwurst and bacon slices.
8. Lay a layer of sauerkraut in it, place a piece of partridge on it and continue in this way until the mold is filled, but make sure the last layer consists of sauerkraut.
9. Turn out the pudding and pipe some potato puree around it with the butter syringe.

title: Patrijzen met Witte Garnalensaus (Partridges with White Shrimp Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three partridges
- lemon slices without skin or seeds
- thin slices of bacon
- paper
- cream and shrimp sauce

## Directions

1. Take three partridges and clean them well
2. Fill them with some slices of lemon without skin or seeds
3. Lard them (wrap them with thin slices of bacon)
4. Wrap them in paper
5. Roast them three quarters on the spit
6. Serve them with a sauce of cream and shrimp

title: Patrijzenpastei (Partridge Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 partridges
- liver
- bacon
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- pastry crust

## Directions

1. Take 3 or 4 partridges that have been well cleaned
2. Cut off the legs and wings
3. Make a farce of liver, both from partridges and from other birds, with some bacon, parsley, salt and pepper, all well mixed together
4. Fill the partridges with it and take a crust (about 3 pounds)
5. Make an opening in the lid and let them bake about 3 hours in an oven
6. When done, let them cool and close the hole in the lid

title: Patrijzensoep (Partridge Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- water
- salt
- rice
- ground mace
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Treat as chicken soup

title: Peaches or Apricots in Brandy (Perziken of abrikozen in brandewijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches or apricots
- Cloves
- Best cinnamon
- Pieces of bread sugar
- French brandy

## Directions

1. Remove the stems
2. Put them in bottles
3. Add cloves and best cinnamon, along with pieces of bread sugar
4. Pour French brandy over them and seal the bottles as airtight as possible
5. Use only unblemished fruit

title: Pekelvlees (Pickled Meat)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg meat
- 250 g salt
- 10 g saltpeter
- 50 g brown sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the meat with the mixture of salt, saltpeter and sugar and pack it firmly in a small tub or in a large bowl.
2. Turn the meat over regularly and store it for at least 3 weeks before using it.

title: Pepper Sauce (Pepersaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted bread
- Bouillon
- Vinegar
- Wine
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Soak roasted bread in bouillon
2. Add vinegar, wine, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, and ginger
3. Let it all boil together briefly

title: Peren (Preserved Pears)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 pears (fine table pears)
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, leave them whole with the stem on, or cut them in half or in quarters and remove the cores.
2. Arrange them neatly in well-cleaned bottles up to 3 cm below the rim.
3. Pour enough sugar syrup over them so that the pears are well submerged.
4. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clips.
5. Heat them for 30-45 minutes to 80-90° C.
6. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.
7. (For corked bottles see the rules on page 366).

title: Peren met caramel en gebrande amandeltjes (Pears with Caramel and Roasted Almonds)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 beautiful small and perfect conference pears
- 1/4 l water
- 100 g sugar
- the juice of 1 lemon and a piece of lemon zest
- approximately 50 g shredded roasted almonds
- 100 g sugar for caramel

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, leave the stems on
2. Stew them gently until tender in water with sugar, lemon zest and lemon juice
3. Let them drain and place them in a deep dish
4. Make caramel from the 100 g sugar (see caramel custard recipe 680) and pour it over the pears when golden brown
5. Sprinkle immediately with the shredded almonds
6. Let the pear liquid with the caramel remaining in the pan come to a boil once, strain the liquid and serve it as a sauce with the pears

title: Perziken (Preserved Peaches)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 36 peaches
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches, cut them in half and remove the pits.
2. Arrange them neatly in well-cleaned bottles up to 3 cm below the rim.
3. Pour enough sugar syrup over them so that the peaches are well submerged.
4. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clips and heat them for 20 minutes to 70-80° C.
5. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.
6. (For corked bottles see the rules on page 366).

title: Perzikencompote (Peach Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 peaches
- 100 g sugar
- 2 dl water
- 3/4 dl Rhine wine
- sago (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare this compote as the apricot compote.

title: Perzikenjam (Peach Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.75 kg peaches (+ 3 kg without pits)
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- twine
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- labels

## Directions

1. Prepare this jam entirely as apricot jam (recipe 990).

title: Perzikensaus (Peach Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 peaches
- 4 egg yolks
- 100 to 125 g white bastard sugar
- 4 dl white or Rhine wine
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce like apricot sauce (see previous recipe).
2. Present it with warm almond cake, cabinet pudding etc.

title: Peterseliesaus (Parsley Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce).
2. Wash the parsley, pluck off the leaves and chop or cut them as finely as possible.
3. Mix them with the sauce off the heat.
4. Serve with

title: Peterseliesaus (Parsley Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or bouillon
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Rinse the parsley, pluck off the leaves and chop or cut them as finely as possible
3. Mix them into the sauce away from the heat
4. Serve with perch, salmon, salted fish, etc.
5. Note: Instead of butter or margarine and flour, the liquid can also be thickened with 25 g mixed sago

title: Peulen (Pea Pods)
body:
## Ingredients

- pea pods
- chopped parsley
- salt
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stew with chopped parsley, a little salt, butter and sugar, continuously shaking
2. They require two hours to become tender
3. Some cook them first

title: Peulen (Pods)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pods
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 15 g sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the pods on both sides twice
2. Wash them and set them on with enough boiling water to just cover the bottom of the pan, and salt
3. Let them cook until done and dry, stirring occasionally (10 to 20 minutes)
4. Add a little water if necessary during cooking to prevent burning
5. Shake them with the butter and add the sugar if they are not sweet enough

title: Piepkuikens (Chicks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 piepkuikens (2 chicks)
- minstens 100 g boter of margarine (at least 100 g butter or margarine)
- wat zout (salt)

## Directions

1. Voor de bereiding zie kip (recept 309).
2. Reken als braadtijd 1/2 uur a 3 kwartier.

title: Piepkuikens (Spring Chickens)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 spring chickens
- at least 100 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see chicken (recipe 309).
2. Allow 1/2 hour to 3/4 hour as roasting time.

title: Piquante Saus (Piquant Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters beef bouillon
- 15 decigrammes shallots
- 30 decagrammes butter
- 30 decagrammes flour
- 1 tablespoon washed and chopped parsley
- 1 tablespoon chopped gherkins
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel the shallots, wash them and chop them as well as the parsley.
2. Put them in a pan and add the butter and vinegar.
3. Set on the fire and stir with a spoon until the vinegar is completely reduced.
4. Add the flour and let it cook together for 2 minutes.
5. Add the 4 deciliters beef bouillon with some Liebig's Meat Extract, add a little pepper and let it cook gently for 15 minutes.
6. Finally add salt to taste.

title: Piroggen (Fried Pastries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g flour
- 1/2 dl water with salt
- 1 egg
- 25 g butter or margarine
- preserves or meat or fish ragout for filling
- egg white
- frying fat or salad oil

## Directions

1. Beat the egg, add the water with salt, the sifted flour and the melted butter or margarine
2. Stir this dough over the fire until it comes away from the pan as a ball
3. Let it cool, roll it out on a flour-sprinkled board with a flour-dusted rolling pin to 1/2 cm thickness
4. Cut square pieces from it, with the edges brushed with some egg white or water
5. Place some preserves or meat or fish ragout in the middle, fold the pieces in half, seal the edges firmly together and fry them in frying fat or salad oil with light steam coming off until light brown and cooked through
6. Let them drip on gray paper, serve the savory piroggen as an appetizer, the sweet ones cold, sprinkled with powdered sugar

title: Plum-cake
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 200 g butter or margarine
- 200 g flour
- 200 g sugar
- 200 g currants and raisins
- some lemon juice or rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter as described in the previous recipe.
2. Mix the batter, which should look thick and airy, with the currants and raisins, which have been stripped of their stems, washed and dried, and with the lemon juice or rum.
3. Put it in a greased loaf pan, which is lined on the inside along the bottom and sides with white paper (for example, grease-proof paper).
4. Bake the cake in this in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done (1.5 hours).
5. Test if it is done by inserting a knitting needle, which should then come out dry.
6. Turn it out and let it cool.

title: Plumpodding (Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- raisins
- candied citrus peel
- cinnamon snippets
- rum
- flour
- milk
- eggs
- finely chopped suet
- yeast
- butter
- meat stock
- rum sauce

## Directions

1. The day before, soak the necessary currants, raisins, candied citrus peel and cinnamon snippets in rum
2. make a batter of flour, milk and eggs
3. stir in finely chopped suet and the aforementioned currants, raisins, etc.
4. add a small piece of yeast
5. put the batter in a bag greased with butter
6. bind it loosely to allow the batter to rise
7. cook it 3 hours in meat stock
8. serve with rum sauce

title: Plumpudding (Plum Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g old bread without crust
- 125 g raisins
- 125 g currants
- 125 g sultana raisins
- 125 g sugar
- 150 g candied citrus peel
- 250 g calf suet
- 4 eggs
- grated zest of 1/2 lemon
- 3/4 grated nutmeg
- 25 g cinnamon
- 15 g almonds
- 1/2 dl rum

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the currants and raisins, wash them with lukewarm water.
2. Chop the candied citrus peel, chop the peeled almonds finely.
3. Crumble the bread, chop the washed suet, from which the membranes have been removed, finely.
4. Stir the egg yolks as smoothly as possible with the sugar, mix them with all ingredients, the last as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
5. Fill a warm pudding mold coated with butter and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs 3/4 full with the mixture.
6. Close it firmly and cook the pudding at least 8 hours in a bain-marie. Make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim of the mold.
7. Let the pudding dry without the lid, pour rum over it and bring it in flaming, or stick a sprig of holly with berries in it.
8. Serve plumpudding in small portions and serve rum sauce with it.

title: Poffertjes (Puffed Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- approximately 3 dl lukewarm milk
- 10 g yeast
- 100 g currants, raisins and mixed peel (optional)
- 125 g butter, margarine or 100 g lard
- salt
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with enough lukewarm milk so it does not fly about and stir out the lumps from this batter
2. Add the remaining lukewarm milk, the beaten egg and the mixed yeast, and optionally the well-washed currants and raisins with stems removed and the chopped mixed peel
3. Let the batter rise, covered, in a warm place for three quarters of an hour to 1 hour
4. Add the salt and quickly bake puffed pancakes with the lard, butter or margarine in the puffed pancake pan
5. Sprinkle them warm with ground sugar and place a knob of butter or margarine in the middle of each plate of puffed pancakes
6. Note: In original fair puffed pancakes, no currants etc. are added

title: Polonaise (Layered Almond Meringue)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 300 g sugar
- 50 g snippered almonds
- 2 1/2 dl whipped cream with powdered sugar and vanilla pulp

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg whites as stiff as possible, mix them with half of the sifted sugar and pipe from this through a piping bag with a smooth nozzle onto a greased and flour-dusted baking sheet, on which with a lid or bowl 4 circles are marked, 3 rings.
2. Pipe the 4th circle, starting in the middle, completely full, to serve as the base of the polonaise.
3. Let the egg white in a very lukewarm oven dry (1 1/2 to 2 hours), but see to it that it remains white in color.
4. Then beat the remaining egg white stiff, mix it with the remaining sugar and pipe a ring of it on the edge of the base.
5. Lay a hard ring on it, pipe another soft ring on it and continue thus until the last dried ring is laid on it.
6. Now spread the outside and inside of the polonaise as smoothly as possible with the remaining egg white, sprinkle the outside with snippered, light brown toasted almonds and let the polonaise in a very lukewarm oven become dry and crisp (1 1/2 to 2 hours), but see to it that it remains as light as possible in color.
7. Fill it with whipped cream, mixed with powdered sugar and vanilla pulp or with ice.

title: Portuguese Tart (Portugese Taart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- 100 g sugar
- 50 g almonds
- 1 teaspoon fine cinnamon
- 1 grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Grind the peeled almonds with 50 g of the sugar twice through an almond grinder.
2. Then knead them with the remaining ingredients into a firm ball, press it into a greased tart pan and bake the tart in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked through (½ hour to 45 minutes).
3. Test if it is cooked by pricking it with a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
4. Turn out the tart after it has cooled slightly and garnish it if desired with glaze and candied fruits.

title: Postelein (Purslane)
body:
## Ingredients

- purslane
- salt
- nutmeg
- crushed rusk
- sorrel (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash well and set it on with the water that remains in it
2. When done, add salt, nutmeg and finely crushed rusk
3. You can optionally add sorrel

title: Postelein (Purslane)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg purslane
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 1 egg or 10 g maizena (optional)

## Directions

1. Carefully sort through the purslane, cut off the roots if necessary, and wash until the water that comes off is completely clear.
2. Cut it, place it with as little water as possible and let it cook until tender, stirring occasionally (10 to 15 minutes).
3. Let it drain as dry as possible in a colander, braise it for 5 minutes with butter or margarine and salt.
4. If necessary, bind the purslane with the mixed maizena, which must cook for 3 minutes, or with the egg, which is beaten in the dish and to which the hot purslane is carefully stirred.

title: Postelein in gekurkte flessen (Purslane in Corked Bottles)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 kg purslane
- salad oil
- cork

## Directions

1. Sort through the purslane, wash it carefully until the water runs completely clear and let it cook with the adhering water until done (20 to 30 minutes).
2. Make sure that in the meantime bottles with wide necks are cleaned and boiled for 20 minutes.
3. Let the vegetable drain.
4. Fill the bottles with the purslane, press it in as firmly as possible.
5. Pour on enough cooking liquid so that the purslane is just covered.
6. Pour some salad oil on top.
7. Close the bottle, after cleaning the rim, with a scrubbed and boiled cork, cut it level with the rim, let it cool briefly and immerse the top of the bottle twice in melted sealing wax.
8. Attach a label with name and date.
9. Note: If desired, some melted butter can be poured on and a teaspoon of salt placed on top. The cooked purslane can also be placed in preserving jars and sterilized in them for half an hour.

title: Postelein, Anders (Purslane, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- purslane
- bouillon
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After washing, cut the purslane small
2. Set it dry so that it melts and cooks in its own liquid
3. When done, pour off the liquid and add bouillon in its place, along with butter, salt and nutmeg
4. When serving, beat a couple of egg yolks through it

title: Posteleinsoep (Purslane Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- purslane leaves
- nutmeg
- bouillon
- two eggs
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Take a soup bowl of purslane leaves, wash them carefully
2. Add them with nutmeg to boiling bouillon in which two eggs and a tablespoon of potato starch have been stirred together beforehand
3. Let the whole cook a little more and the soup is ready

title: Posteleinsoep (Purslane Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l purslane liquid with some purslane leaves in it
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- (1 egg)
- diced fried bread

## Directions

1. Fry the flour in the butter or margarine and add the purslane liquid gradually in small portions
2. Let the soup cook while stirring for 10 minutes and then carefully add to the egg, which has been beaten in the soup tureen
3. Serve with diced fried bread
4. N.B. If you want to make this soup from fresh purslane, you need 150 g purslane, which you cook until done in 1 l boiling water, after which you prepare soup from it as indicated above

title: Poularde with Mussel Sauce (Poularde met Mosselsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde
- mussels
- parsley
- small onions
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper
- salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the poularde
2. Remove the beard from the mussels and wash them often
3. Put them in a pan and bring to a good fire, but do not let them boil
4. Then place them dry in another pan with parsley, small onions and some butter, a little bouillon, pepper and salt
5. Then mix 3 eggs with the mussels and let them become very hot, but not boil
6. Bring the poularde hot to the plate and cover it with the sauce

title: Poularde with Oysters (Poularde met Oesters)
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde
- oysters
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper
- salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Made in the same way as poularde with mussel sauce, except that with the oysters you do not add parsley or small onions

title: Poulet met rijstrand (Veal Stew with Rice Ring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g rice
- salt
- 300 g veal for braising
- optionally 100 g ground veal
- 4 dl boiling water
- salt
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- optionally 1 egg
- mace
- thyme
- piece of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the veal and let it cook gently in the boiling water with salt (approximately 1 hour) or braise it until done.
2. Let the herbs cook along tied in a cloth.
3. If using ground veal, mix it with some soaked old bread, pepper, salt and nutmeg, shape into small balls and cook these separately in a little boiling water so the stock does not become too fatty.
4. Take the meat and herbs out of the stock and gradually add this, stirring vigorously, to the butter and flour that have been brought to a boil together and from which all lumps have been carefully rubbed out.
5. Let the sauce cook through, stirring, for 10 minutes and then warm the veal (and the meatballs) in it.
6. Cook the rice in the usual manner and press it as firmly as possible into a ring mold buttered or rinsed with water.
7. Turn the rice out as quickly as possible onto a heated plate and place the braised veal in the middle.
8. Note: If desired, curry can be cooked with the rice. In this case only mace is cooked with the veal. One can also add a little lemon juice to the sauce and finish it with an egg or egg yolk.

title: Pouletsoep (1e recept) (Veal Chicken Soup, First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g veal chicken
- 1 liter boiling water
- 50 g veal ground meat
- 35 g vermicelli
- 1 Maggi's bouillon cube
- piece of mace
- salt

## Directions

1. Place the washed chicken in the boiling water with salt and mace and let it cook gently for 1½ hours.
2. Cut the meat into neat pieces and remove the mace.
3. Add the washed and broken vermicelli and let it cook for a half hour.
4. Add the meatballs prepared in the usual manner (recipe 37) ten minutes before serving and dissolve the bouillon cube in the soup.
5. Beat the egg in the soup tureen and carefully add the soup to it while stirring vigorously.

title: Pouletsoep (2e recept) (Veal Chicken Soup, Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g veal chicken
- 1 liter boiling water
- 50 g veal ground meat
- 30 g vermicelli
- piece of mace
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the method for veal chicken soup, first recipe. Because more meat is used in this soup, presumably no bouillon cube needs to be added.

title: Prei (Leeks)
body:
## Ingredients

- leeks
- water
- salt
- butter
- vinegar
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces and cook with water and a little salt
2. Stew briefly with butter, a little vinegar, pepper and flour

title: Preisoep (Leek Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- leeks
- bread
- butter
- bouillon
- white bread

## Directions

1. Cut leeks into small pieces and fry them brown in a pan with pieces of bread and butter
2. Then cook them for half an hour in bouillon with a few slices of white bread

title: Preisoep (Leek Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water
- 1 small bunch leeks
- salt
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 30 g macaroni
- + 7 1/2 g sago
- marmite

## Directions

1. Clean the leeks, cut them into small pieces, wash them
2. Set them with the liquid and salt
3. Add the macaroni broken into small pieces and washed, and let the soup cook for 1/2 hour
4. At the end add the sago mixed with cold water and the butter or margarine
5. Let the soup cook for another minute
6. Add the marmite, which has been mixed with some water

title: Prinsessebonen (Slabonen) uit het zout met aardappelen en rookworst (Princess Beans from Salt with Potatoes and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg princess beans
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the stamppot entirely as cut beans from salt with potatoes and smoked sausage.

title: Prinsessebonen (Sugar Pods)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sugar pods
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Trim the beans on both sides twice, wash them, break them and set them on with so little water that they are cooked approximately dry by the end of the cooking time.
2. Add salt and let them cook tender (1/2 hour to 3/4 hour, for old beans sometimes longer).
3. Let them drain on a colander if necessary and braise them shortly with the butter or margarine.
4. Grate some nutmeg over the plate.

title: Prinsessebonen in het zout (Princess Beans in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 kg beans
- 1½ or 4 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Remove the ends of the beans twice on each side, wash them, break them.
2. Boil them in plenty of boiling water for 2 minutes, remove them with a skimming spoon from the pan, let them cool completely spread out.
3. Finish them as described in the previous recipe.

title: Prinsesseboonen (Pickled Princess Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- princess beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove ends and break beans
2. Handle as string beans but use less salt

title: Prinsesseboonen (Princess Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- princess beans
- boiling rainwater
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Break through the beans and set them with boiling rainwater on the heat
2. Let them drain when cooked
3. Stew them with pepper, salt and butter

title: Prophetenkoekkjes (Prophet Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound flour
- Just over 2 ounces butter
- 1 ounce sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks and milk together
2. Add butter to flour
3. Knead all together well
4. Make fifteen small heaps
5. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
6. Bake in the oven

title: Pruimen (Preserved Plums)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 kg plums
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Wash the plums with cold water and arrange them in well-cleaned bottles up to 3 cm below the rim.
2. Pour enough sugar syrup over them so that the plums are just submerged.
3. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clips and heat them for 30 minutes to 80-90° C.
4. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.
5. (For corked bottles see the rules above).

title: Pruimenjam (Plum Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.75 kg plums (+ 3 kg without pits)
- 2.25 kg sugar
- jam jars
- parchment paper
- cellophane
- rubber bands
- twine
- labels

## Directions

1. Wash the plums and remove the pits, put them with as much water as necessary to prevent burning during the first moments in a pan.
2. Let them cook down to a thick pulp.
3. Add the sugar.
4. Bring them to a boil again with the lid on and let the fruit cook without the lid, stirring occasionally, until the jam reaches the required thickness (see the rules on page 369).
5. Treat it further as strawberry jam (recipe 983).
6. Note: For this jam, yellow, blue, or green plums can be used.

title: Pruimenmoes (Plum Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg plums
- 100 g sugar
- some water
- some sago

## Directions

1. Wash the plums, remove the pits, but make sure all the juice is caught.
2. Set them on with some water and let them cook gently (about 10 minutes).
3. Sieve the sauce, add the sugar and bind it with a little mixed sago.

title: Pruimensoep (Plum Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums, cut small and pitted
- water
- sugar
- lemon peels, cut small
- 1 whole Ceylon cinnamon stick
- Malaga raisins
- red wine
- brown toasted flour
- white bread

## Directions

1. Cook finely cut plums, without pits, with water, sugar, finely cut lemon peels, a whole Ceylon cinnamon stick and some Malaga raisins
2. Then add some red wine and some brown toasted flour
3. Let it cook through once more and serve it over small pieces of white bread

title: Punch Syrup (Siroop van punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds best rum
- Two and a half ounces lemon juice
- Two and a half ounces bread sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together until the sugar is well dissolved
2. Pour into bottles and seal well with wax

title: Punchsaus (Punch Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl water and 1 dl punch, or 1 dl rum, 1 dl cognac and 3 dl water
- zest of half a lemon
- 150 g bastard sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 6 g maizena

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the very thinly pared lemon zest to a boil.
2. Mix the maizena gradually with the beaten egg yolks, add the sugar and enough of the boiling water until the egg yolks are thoroughly warmed.
3. Pour everything back into the pan and let the sauce, stirring vigorously, boil for 3-4 minutes.
4. Add the punch or cognac and rum off the fire, and present the sauce with warm semolina pudding, bread pudding etc.

title: Péches melba (Peaches Melba)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla ice cream
- 1 fresh, peeled and pitted peach half
- raspberry purée made from raspberries rubbed through a hair sieve and seasoned with sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Fill an ice bowl 3/4 full with vanilla ice cream.
2. Place a fresh, peeled and pitted peach half in it, and pour some raspberry purée over it, obtained by rubbing carefully selected and washed raspberries through a hair sieve and seasoning with sugar.
3. Garnish the whole with whipped cream.

title: Raapstelen (Turnip Greens)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 bunches young or 4 bunches old turnip greens
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the small roots and hard ends of the leaves, then cut the turnip greens into small pieces
2. Wash them carefully until the water that comes off is completely clear
3. Set them on with as little water as possible and let them cook until done (1/2 to 3/4 hour)
4. Drain them in a colander
5. Stew them for 5 minutes with the butter or margarine and salt, or with a sauce made from 30 g flour and 30 g butter or margarine, 2.5 dl milk, prepared in the known manner according to recipe 328
6. Grate some nutmeg over the dish

title: Rabarbercompôte (Rhubarb Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g red rhubarb stalks
- 2 dl water
- 150 g sugar
- some sago
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the rhubarb into equal pieces; cook them gently (approximately 8 minutes) in the water that has been brought to a boil with the lemon juice and sugar, but make sure the pieces remain whole and do not boil to pieces.
2. Scoop each piece that is done with a flat spoon into a compote dish.
3. Thicken the liquid with a little mixed sago and pour it over the pieces.

title: Rabarberjam (Rhubarb Jam)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg rhubarb
- 1 kg sugar
- 1 double packet of Opekta
- jam jars and accessories

## Directions

1. Wash the rhubarb and cut it into pieces.
2. Put the pieces with very little water on the heat and bring the rhubarb to a boil.
3. Sprinkle in the Opekta while stirring well and let the pulp boil vigorously for 1 minute.
4. Add the sugar and stir until it is dissolved.
5. Let the mass boil vigorously for 7 minutes and fill the very well cleaned jam jars as described in the previous recipes.
6. Note: Rhubarb contains little or no natural pectin. Therefore it is recommended to prepare this jam with the help of Opekta.

title: Rabarbermoes (Rhubarb Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 to 3/4 kg rhubarb
- 100 g sugar
- some mixed cornstarch (optional)
- 1 egg (optional)
- a pinch of chalk (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut young rhubarb stalks unpeeled, old stalks peeled, into chunks, wash them.
2. Set them on with a little water and let the rhubarb cook gently until soft (about 5 minutes), stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
3. Rub it through with a wooden spoon, mix the sauce with the sugar.
4. Bind it if necessary with some mixed cornstarch, which must cook for 3 minutes, or pour it into the egg beaten in a bowl.
5. When using chalk, stir in the chalk first, then the sugar into the sauce.

title: Rabarbervla (Rhubarb Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 stalks rhubarb
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 100 to 125 g sugar
- 8 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. (Peel the rhubarb), wash it, cut it into pieces. Let these cook quickly until done, stirring or shaking occasionally, and mix them with the sugar. Stir the maizena smooth with the egg yolks, add enough rhubarb so that the yolks are well warmed, pour them back into the pan and let the mass cook through for 3 to 4 minutes with vigorous stirring. Mix this after cooling with the stiffly beaten egg white and serve with biscuits.

title: Rabbit in Marinade (Konijnen en Marinade)
body:
## Ingredients

- three young rabbits
- vinegar
- parsley
- thyme
- bay leaves
- chopped onions
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- fried eggs
- white mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbits properly
2. Let them stand for several days in a pan with vinegar, some parsley, thyme, bay leaves, chopped onions, salt and pepper
3. Before they are finished, press the meat out between a linen cloth
4. Roll it in flour and fry it with butter
5. Arrange the pieces neatly once they are fried and lay fried eggs around them
6. Serve with a white mustard sauce

title: Rabbit Sausage (Konijneworst)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild rabbit
- rabbit liver
- rabbit bones
- bouillon
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- unsalted lard
- pepper
- cream
- fresh clean intestines

## Directions

1. Cook a wild rabbit and chop the meat very fine
2. Add the liver, well cleaned and cooked
3. Crush the bones first and cook bouillon from them
4. Mix the bouillon with breadcrumbs to make a dough
5. Add some butter, unsalted lard cut into cubes, pepper and some cream
6. Stuff into fresh clean intestines and cook like other sausage

title: Radijsjes (Radishes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- small radishes
- fine salt

## Directions

1. Use small, beautiful radishes
2. Cut off the roots and most of the leaves, but leave a few small leaves in the middle
3. Wash them thoroughly until all sand is removed
4. Let them drain in a colander
5. Place them on the serving dish
6. Serve with fine salt on the side

title: Ragout met Morilles en Champignons (Ragout with Morels and Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried morels
- Butter
- Lemon juice
- Flour
- Salt
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from dried morels and cut them in half or thirds if very large
2. Wash them very clean with lukewarm water and let them settle to remove sand
3. Cook them, let them drain
4. Put them in a pan with some butter and lemon juice
5. Add some flour, salt and bouillon
6. Make a ragout from these
7. For a reasonable serving, one ounce is needed

title: Ragout van Hanekammen en Pooten (Ragout of Cock's Combs and Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauce for morel ragout
- Cock's combs
- Finely chopped pork
- Chicken and cock feet, well cooked

## Directions

1. Make the same sauce as for the ragout of morels
2. Fill the bottom part of the cock's combs with very finely chopped pork
3. Add well-cooked chicken and cock feet

title: Ragout van Kalfstong (Veal Tongue Ragout)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal tongue
- 1/2 l bouillon in which mace and Spanish pepper have been infused
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 dl cream or milk
- 1/4 dl white Bordeaux wine
- 75 g ground veal
- 1 small tin of mushrooms
- a couple of drops of lemon juice if desired
- 1 to 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue until done in the way as described in recipe 267, but let the spices cook along.
2. After it has been stripped of its skin, cut it into cubes.
3. Cook the meat balls separately in some boiling water or tongue bouillon until done (10 minutes).
4. Prepare the sauce in the usual way, warm in it the pieces of tongue, the mushrooms and meat balls and add these from the heat to the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream and wine.
5. Stir some lemon juice through and fill pastries, fried items etc. with it.

title: Ragout van stokvisch (Stockfish Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- stockfish
- salt
- onions
- veal bouillon
- butter
- flour
- cream
- Cayenne pepper
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into slices
2. Sprinkle with salt and let stand for twenty-four hours
3. Brown the slices with a couple of onions
4. Set them with veal bouillon
5. Add flour-rolled butter and four spoons of cream, as well as salt and Cayenne pepper
6. Let simmer slowly
7. Serve with rice

title: Raspberry Vinegar (Frambozenazijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds raspberries
- One bottle wine vinegar
- Three-quarters pound sugar per pound of liquid

## Directions

1. Let four pounds raspberries steep in one bottle of wine vinegar for 24 hours
2. Press them out
3. Add three-quarters pound sugar per pound of liquid
4. Let it boil together until it reaches the right thickness
5. Pour into small bottles, cork them, cover with a bladder, and store in a cool place

title: Rauw gebakken aardappelen (French Fries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg potatoes
- salt
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes as thinly as possible, carefully remove the eyes and cut them into long, thin strips or make different shapes with a frying knife.
2. Wash and dry with a clean cloth as many pieces as can be fried at once and lay them in warm frying fat.
3. Let them cook (approximately 10 minutes), remove them with a skimming spoon and then fry them brown in hot frying fat.
4. Lay them on brown paper to drain, sprinkle them with table salt and serve them with steak, veal cutlet, etc.

title: Rauw gebakken aardappelen in schijfjes (Raw Fried Potatoes in Slices)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg potatoes
- approximately 75 g fat or oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes in the usual way, wash them and slice them into not too thin slices.
2. Dry them in a cloth.
3. Lay the slices in the frying pan in which the fat has been warmed, sprinkle some fine salt over them and fry them on moderately warm heat until light brown and cooked (approximately 15 minutes).
4. Turn them with a knife or pancake turner from time to time.

title: Ravigottesaus (Ravigote Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- 3 1/2 dl salad oil
- approximately 1/2 dl vinegar
- 1 1/2 teaspoon mustard or mustard powder
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- pimpernel
- shallot

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce like mayonnaise as described in recipe 367.
2. Finally stir in the washed and very finely chopped herbs.

title: Ravigottesaus (Ravigotte Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- 3½ dl salad oil
- ½ dl vinegar
- 1½ teaspoon mustard or mustard powder
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- pimpernel
- shallot

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as mayonnaise, as described in recipe 367.
2. Stir in the last the washed and very finely chopped herbs.
3. Serve with chateaubriands, beef tenderloin, cold veal.
4. Store it, if necessary, in the same manner as mayonnaise.

title: Reebout (Roasted Venison Haunch)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg venison haunch
- 50 g larding bacon
- 150 g butter or margarine
- some salt
- 1/2 dl sour cream (can also be replaced by yoghurt)

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, lard it and rub it with some salt.
2. Make the butter or margarine light brown in the roasting pan, turn the venison haunch through it and roast it 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g.
3. Baste it from time to time with the hot fat, add some water if necessary.
4. Pour the cream over it a quarter of an hour before it is done.
5. Finish the gravy with water.
6. Serve with chestnut purée and berry jelly, apple compote or lingonberry compote.

title: Reerug (Roasted Venison Loin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 4 kg venison loin
- approximately 300 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, salt it and roast it brown and done in the butter or margarine, 30 to 35 minutes per 500 g.
2. Finish the gravy in the usual way.
3. Before serving, loosen the meat on both sides of the spine and cut the thin turned-back part from the thick part.
4. Turn the loin over and remove the narrow muscle pieces from the inside.
5. Cut the thin parts into pieces and place them under the bone so that it lies well and firmly.
6. Place the two thick pieces, which have been cut into thick slices, back in their original form on the bone.
7. Pour a little gravy over the meat.

title: Reeënragout (Venison Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted venison haunch
- butter
- finely chopped shallots
- nutmeg
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted venison haunch into pieces
2. Brown the pieces in butter
3. Add finely chopped shallots, nutmeg, salt and lemon juice
4. Let it stew

title: Reeënragout (Venison Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted venison
- Butter
- Finely chopped shallots
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut pieces from a roasted venison
2. Fry them in butter
3. Add some finely chopped shallots, nutmeg, salt and lemon juice
4. Let it stew

title: Reine Claudes (Preserved Greengage Plums)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 kg reine claudes
- Sugar syrup (500 g sugar per 1 liter water)

## Directions

1. Treat them entirely as plums (see recipe 972), but heat the bottles for 20 minutes to 70-80° C.
2. Let the bottles cool with the clips on, remove the clips and label the bottles with name and date.

title: Reine Claudes op Brandewijn (Greengage Plums in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g greengage plums
- 300 g sugar
- 2.5 dl water
- lemon peel
- 1 l brandy

## Directions

1. Treat the fruit as described in recipe 1027.

title: Remouladesaus (Remoulade Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- 3½ dl salad oil
- ½ dl vinegar
- 2 teaspoons mustard or mustard powder
- 5 gherkins
- 1 shallot
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as mayonnaise (recipe 367).
2. Stir in last the very finely chopped gherkins and the shallot.
3. Serve the sauce with cold meat.

title: Rempah (Meat Balls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g ground veal
- 150 g grated coconut
- 2 kemiri nuts
- 2 tablespoons brown Javanese sugar
- salt
- 1 teaspoon sereh powder
- 60 g soaked bread
- 1 egg
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Mix the ground veal with the grated coconut
2. Fry it without fat until light brown
3. Pound it fine in the mortar with the spices, salt and sugar
4. Mix it with the beaten egg and the soaked bread
5. Add milk if necessary
6. Form into balls
7. Fry these in the hot frying fat
8. Let them drain on grey paper

title: Rexena-Appelvla (Apple Custard Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 6 level tablespoons Rexena
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 2 large baking apples

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut them into small pieces.
2. Place them in a bowl and sprinkle 2 tablespoons of sugar over them.
3. Mix the Rexena and remaining sugar with a portion of the cold milk.
4. Bring the rest of the milk to a boil and, while stirring, add the prepared Rexena mixture.
5. Let the custard cream boil briefly and then pour it over the apple pieces.
6. Let the custard cream stand for at least 4 hours to set.

title: Rhine Wine Sauce (Rijn-wijnsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wine
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put wine, sugar, and cinnamon to taste in a small pan
2. Add lemon peel and let them cook together
3. Bind this sauce with egg yolks

title: Ribstuk (Rib Steak)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg rib steak
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g if you want the rib steak well done, and 20 minutes when it need not be completely done.

title: Rietperen-pannekoeken (Pear Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dutch pound wheat flour
- Warm sweet milk
- 4 eggs
- A little fine salt
- Dry yeast
- Sugar
- Warm milk
- Peeled pear slices
- Butter

## Directions

1. Heat the sweet milk and mix gently with the wheat flour, eggs, and fine salt
2. Beat it well together and make it into a pancake batter, fairly thick, with warm milk
3. Mix dry yeast with a little sugar and warm milk, and stir it through the batter
4. Let it rise properly
5. Cut peeled pears into slices and remove the cores with an apple corer
6. Put butter in the pan and bake the slices until tender, and lay them on a plate
7. Put batter in the pan and arrange pear slices in it as close together as possible
8. Bake the pancakes, sparing no butter

title: Rijnwijngelei (Rhine Wine Gelée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter Rhine wine
- 1/4 liter water
- juice of 2 lemons
- 10 cm cinnamon stick
- 8 cloves
- lemon peel
- 35 g white gelatin
- 150 g sugar
- 2 to 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Treat the gelée as described in recipe 555 or make it in a flat dish as shown in the illustration.
2. This wine gelée can be used as is, it can also be filled with fruit (see recipe 565) or serve as a decorative top layer on other cold puddings.

title: Rijnwijnsoep (Rhine Wine Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle water
- 1 bottle Rhine wine
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons flour
- slices of lemon without pits
- bread sugar
- mace
- egg white
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 bottle water, 1 bottle Rhine wine, 6 egg yolks and 2 tablespoons flour
2. Add some slices of lemon without pits and sweeten the liquid with a sufficient amount of bread sugar
3. Set it on a strong fire, stirring until it comes to a boil
4. Now quickly pour it into a soup tureen in which you have previously put some mace
5. Beat some egg white into a stiff foam and lay it in flakes on the soup with a spoon
6. Sprinkle it thickly with bread sugar and cinnamon and close the tureen well, whereby the egg white becomes cooked by the rising steam of the soup

title: Rijnwijnvla (Rhine Wine Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 2 lemons
- 2 dl Rhine wine (or white currant wine)
- 4 eggs
- 75 g sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs with 2 egg yolks and the sugar until it is completely finely ground.
2. Add the lemon juice and the wine and let the custard bind au bain-marie while beating vigorously, but ensure that it does not boil, as the risk of curdling is then great.
3. Let the custard cool and garnish with the stiffly beaten egg white, through which some sugar has been stirred.
4. Serve with biscuits.

title: Rijnwijnvlade (Rhine Wine Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 loot potato starch
- some of the wine to be added later
- 8 egg yolks
- stick cinnamon
- 2 ounces sugar
- skins and juice of a lemon
- wine

## Directions

1. Stir the potato starch with a bit of the wine that will be added later
2. Add 8 egg yolks, stick cinnamon, 2 ounces sugar, the lemon skins and juice and the wine
3. Stir over moderate fire until it begins to boil
4. Carefully add the stiffly beaten whites of the 8 eggs
5. Place on a cool spot to cool, stirring continuously
6. Remove the lemon zest and stick cinnamon
7. Pour it over
8. Serve decorated with biscuits

title: Rijst (Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice
2. Cook it with water, using 3 cups of water per cup of rice and a little salt
3. It will be cooked in half an hour

title: Rijst (Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 1/2 l water
- wat zout

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst zéér goed uitlekken.
3. Zet hem dan op met 2 maal zoveel koud water als rijst en wat zout.
4. Breng hem op een flink vuur vlug aan de kook en laat hem daarna op een zacht vuur zonder roeren gaar en droog koken (30 minuten).
5. Neem het deksel van de pan, zodra de rijst gaar is en laat hem even opdrogen.

title: Rijst in kranten (Rice Wrapped in Newspaper)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- rijst (zoveel als nodig is om 1/4 deel van de pan mee te vullen)
- water (op 1 kopje rijst 1 1/2 kopje water)
- zout

## Directions

1. Was zoveel rijst als nodig is om 1/4 deel van de pan mee te vullen.
2. Voeg er het water bij (op 1 kopje rijst 1 1/2 kopje water).
3. Doe de rijst met water en zout in de pan, sluit deze en kook de rijst gedurende 5 minuten.
4. Leg intussen 9 4 10 kranten kruisgewijs over elkaar en leg in het midden een prop.
5. Zet de pan midden op de prop en vouw er de kranten één voor één vlug omheen.
6. Laat de rijst minstens 1 uur in de kranten staan.

title: Rijst in rijstkoker (Rice in Rice Cooker)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 3 1/2 a 4 dl water
- zout

## Directions

1. Doe de gewassen rijst met de bepaalde hoeveelheid water en wat zout in de rijstkoker.
2. Zet deze in een pan met wat kokend water.
3. Sluit de pan af en laat de rijstkoker 1/4 uur staan in het kokende water.
4. Neem het deksel van de pan, als de rijst gaar is en laat hem even opdrogen.

title: Rijst met Appelen (Rice with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Good sour apples
- Brown sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take 5 ounces of rice and 3 cups of water per cup of rice
2. Cut good sour apples into small pieces and add them
3. Cook together until it reaches the proper thickness
4. Stir in brown sugar, cinnamon and a lump of butter
5. Serve

title: Rijst met appelen (Rice with Apples)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 1/2-3/4 kg zure appelen
- 100 g suiker
- 1/2 citroenschil
- 3/4 l water
- wat zout

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst goed uitlekken.
3. Zet hem op met het water, de citroenschil en het zout.
4. Breng hem op een flink vuur vlug aan de kook en voeg er dan de appelen bij, die geschild, in vierde partjes gesneden, van de klokhuizen ontdaan en gewassen zijn.
5. Laat alles samen op een zacht vuur gaar worden, neem er de citroenschil uit en roer er met een houten lepel vlug de suiker door, zonder de korrels kapot te roeren.
6. Men kan deze massa ook in een vuurvast schoteltje doen, er wat paneermeel over strooien, er een paar klontjes boter of margarine op leggen en daarna in de oven bruin laten worden.

title: Rijst met Karnemelk (Rice with Buttermilk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liter buttermilk
- 175 g rice or more
- salt
- 1/4 liter water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice until the water that runs off is completely clear.
2. Set it on with 1/4 liter water and salt and bring to a boil.
3. Gradually add the buttermilk and let the porridge cook until done and thickened (approximately 1 1/2 hours).
4. Serve this porridge with syrup.

title: Rijst met Karnemelk of Zoetemelk (Rice with Buttermilk or Sweet Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Buttermilk or sweet milk

## Directions

1. For every 5 ounces of rice, take 3 cans of buttermilk or sweet milk
2. Cook it, stirring well

title: Rijst met Kerry (Curried Rice Casserole)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g meat leftovers
- 500 g cooked rice
- 40 g fat from gravy, margarine or butter
- 75 g onion
- 1 full teaspoon curry
- Some lean gravy with water or some bouillon, or 1/2 Maggi's Gravy tablet
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Mince or grind the meat finely, fry the chopped onion and the curry in the fat light brown.
2. Stir this through the cooked cold rice and the minced meat, moisten everything with the bouillon or the gravy with water.
3. Add pepper, salt and nutmeg as needed.
4. Place the mixture in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, place a few pieces of butter or margarine on it and let a brown crust form in the oven.
5. Alternatively, layer the mixture layer by layer with tomato slices in the dish, making sure the top layer is tomato.

title: Rijst met Krenten (Rice with Currants)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Currants
- Brown sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare as rice with apples, using currants instead of apples

title: Rijst met krenten (Rice with Currants)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 100 g krenten
- 6 a 7 dl water
- wat zout

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst goed uitlekken.
3. Ontdoe de krenten van de steeltjes, was ze in lauw water, ververs dit zo dikwijls, tot het water volkomen helder is.
4. Zet de rijst en de krenten op met 2 1/2 x zoveel koud water als rijst en wat zout.
5. Kook hem gaar als in recept 569 beschreven is.

title: Rijst met pruimen (Rice with Prunes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 200 g gedroogde pruimen
- 3/4 l water
- wat zout

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst goed uitlekken.
3. Was de pruimen in lauw water, ververs dit zo dikwijls, dat het water volkomen helder is.
4. Zet de rijst en de pruimen op met 3 x zoveel koud water als rijst en wat zout.
5. Kook hem gaar als in recept 569 beschreven is.

title: Rijst met Rabarber (Rice with Rhubarb)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rice
- 1/2 kg rhubarb
- 6 dl water
- 150 g sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice until the water that runs off is completely clear.
2. Let the rice drain well.
3. Set it on with the water and some salt and bring it to a boil on a brisk fire.
4. Add the peeled rhubarb, cut into chunks and washed.
5. Let everything cook gently until done.
6. Stir the sugar through quickly with a wooden spoon, without breaking the grains.

title: Rijst met rabarber (Rice with Rhubarb)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g rijst
- 1/2 kg rabarber
- 6 dl water
- + 150 g suiker

## Directions

1. Was de rijst tot het water, dat er afloopt, volkomen helder is.
2. Laat de rijst goed uitlekken.
3. Zet hem op met het water en wat zout en breng hem op een flink vuur vlug aan de kook.
4. Voeg er dan de (geschilde), aan blokjes gesneden en gewassen rabarber bij en laat alles op een zacht vuur gaar koken.

title: Rijst met Witten Wijn (Rice with White Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Currants
- White wine
- Good sour apples
- Ground cinnamon
- Butter
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. For every half Dutch pound of rice, take half that amount in currants and 1 bottle of white wine, along with some good sour apples
2. Let it cook slowly
3. If it becomes too thick, add a little water
4. When cooked, stir in some ground cinnamon, butter and sugar

title: Rijstebrij (Rice Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liter milk
- 150 g rice (for thin porridge) or 225 g (for medium) or 300 g (for thick)
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil on a brisk fire.
2. Stirring, sprinkle in the well-washed rice.
3. Continue stirring until the milk boils again.
4. Let the rice porridge cook on a very gentle fire without stirring until done and thickened (approximately 1 1/2 hours).
5. Stir the rice porridge only when it is done and taken from the fire.

title: Rijstepap (Rice Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet milk
- Best rice

## Directions

1. In 4 cans of sweet milk, add 2½ ounces of best rice
2. Let it cook slowly, watching carefully to prevent burning

title: Rijstetaart (Rice Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 100 g rice
- grated zest of half a lemon
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 75 g sugar
- 3 eggs
- salt
- 40 g almonds (optional)
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook rice pudding in the usual way, letting the lemon zest cook through with it.
2. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the egg yolks and sugar, then the rice pudding and the ground or chopped almonds.
3. Mix everything as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg whites and put it in a greased tart pan dusted with fine breadcrumbs.
4. Bake the cake in a moderately warm oven until light brown (3/4 to 1 hour).
5. Turn it out after cooling and dust it with powdered sugar.

title: Rijstkoekjes (Rice Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces rice
- 2 cans milk
- 7 to 8 rusks
- Ground cinnamon
- 6 finely beaten eggs
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook rice porridge from 5 ounces rice and 2 cans milk
2. Take the thickest part, add 7 to 8 rusks, ground cinnamon, 6 finely beaten eggs and a little sugar
3. Beat everything together
4. Bake small cakes of it in the pancake pan

title: Rijstkoekjes (Rice Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g cooked rice
- 80 g flour
- 80 g sugar
- 2 eggs
- 80 g butter or margarine or 3/4 dl salad oil
- scraped vanilla or grated lemon zest or fine cinnamon
- some milk

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar, add the flour, the flavoring and the cooked rice.
2. Then fold in as lightly as possible the stiffly beaten egg white and if the batter is still too thick, add some milk.
3. Fry small, round cookies from this batter in the frying pan with butter, margarine or salad oil on a gentle fire until light brown and cooked through.
4. Serve sprinkled with some crushed sugar or powdered sugar.

title: Rijstperen (Rice Pears)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 to 150 g rice flour
- 1/2 l milk
- 1/4 vanilla pod
- 2 eggs
- some marmalade
- currants
- candied orange peel
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Cook the porridge in the same way as semolina porridge
2. Remove the vanilla and mix the dough off the heat with the egg yolks
3. Shape into pears, placing some marmalade in the middle of each
4. Coat the pears double (use the egg whites for this), stick a piece of candied orange peel at the point and a currant (or raisin) at the other end
5. Fry the pears brown in hot frying fat
6. Let them drain well on porous paper, sprinkle with powdered sugar or serve with a sauce of marmalade mixed with a little hot water

title: Rijstpodding (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces rice
- sweet milk
- 6 eggs
- 2.5 ounces melted butter
- juice of a couple lemons
- grated lemon zest
- candied citrus peel
- cinnamon
- bread sugar
- cream or butter sauce

## Directions

1. Cook 5 ounces rice in sweet milk until tender and dry
2. make a batter with 6 eggs, 2.5 ounces melted butter, lemon juice, grated lemon zest, candied citrus peel, cinnamon and bread sugar
3. put in a mold as for bread pudding
4. let it cook vigorously for 1 hour
5. serve with cream or butter sauce

title: Rijstpodding Anders (Rice Pudding, Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces rice
- crushed rusk
- sweet milk
- 5 hard-boiled eggs
- currants
- salt
- nutmeg
- cinnamon snippets
- grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook 5 ounces rice with crushed rusk and sweet milk to a proper thickness
2. beat it with 5 hard-boiled and finely chopped eggs, currants, salt, nutmeg, cinnamon snippets and grated lemon zest
3. put in a mold
4. let it cook in water until done

title: Rijstpudding met Abrikozen (Rice Pudding with Apricots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 150 g rice
- some salt
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 125 g sugar
- 16 g white gelatin or 14 g powdered gelatin
- 2 egg whites
- 2.5 dl whipped cream
- 1 small can apricots or 200 g apricots cooked in the usual way

## Directions

1. Cook rice porridge in the usual way with the vanilla (recipe 577).
2. Mix it, just before the rice is done, with the sugar, but make sure the rice grains are not ground fine.
3. Soak the leaf gelatin 10 to 15 minutes in cold water, then dissolve it in the rice porridge removed from heat.
4. Let the mixture cool.
5. Remove the vanilla.
6. Beat the cream and egg whites stiff.
7. Add the rice porridge to it and let this mixture stand, stirring occasionally, until it becomes gelatinous and the rice grains no longer sink.
8. Drain the apricots, layer them alternately with the rice porridge in a mold rinsed with unbeaten egg white, but make sure the first and last layer consists of rice porridge.
9. Let the pudding become cold and stiff, turn it out onto a damp dish and serve with apricot sauce.
10. Use the apricot liquid in the sauce.

title: Rijstpuddinkjes met vruchtensapsaus (Rice Puddings with Fruit Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 150 g rice
- 75 g sugar
- salt
- 1/2 vanilla stick, vanilla sugar, or 1/2 lemon zest
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Cook thick rice porridge in the usual way (see recipe 577), but let the vanilla or lemon zest cook in it.
2. Mix some of the sugar into the rice porridge after the flavoring has been removed.
3. Stir the egg yolk smooth with the rest of the sugar and add a little of the mixture to warm the yolk beforehand.
4. Mix everything together and add the stiffly beaten egg white at the end.
5. Place the mixture in small molds rinsed with unbeaten egg white or water and let the mixture cool.
6. Turn out the puddings in the known way, garnish them with a fruit and serve with fruit juice sauce, for example apricot sauce or raspberry sauce.

title: Rijstrand met abrikozen (Rice Mold with Apricots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 200 g rice
- 100 g sugar
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon peel
- 200 g apricots
- 50 g sugar
- sago

## Directions

1. Cook thick rice porridge in the usual way (see recipe 577), but let the vanilla or lemon peel cook along with it.
2. When the rice is almost cooked, add the sugar, and before serving remove the vanilla or lemon peel.
3. Press the rice porridge as firmly as possible into a rice mold, which must be buttered when the mixture is to be turned out while warm, but is rinsed with water when turned out cold.
4. Add the stewed apricots, which are prepared in the usual way (see recipe 541).

title: Rijstrand met pruimen (Rice Mold with Plums)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 200 g rice
- 100 g sugar
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon peel
- 300 g plums
- 50 g sugar
- sago

## Directions

1. For the preparation see rice mold with apricots (see previous recipe).

title: Rijstsoep (1e recept) (Rice Soup, First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon of beef or 1 liter water and 4 bouillon cubes (recipe 35)
- 55 g rice
- 50 g veal ground meat
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Prepare the bouillon in the usual manner, and if desired let a piece of mace cook in it. Strain the bouillon and cook the washed rice in it until tender (approximately 1/2 hour). Or: cook the rice in water with salt and a piece of mace until done, then add the bouillon cubes.
2. Let the meatballs prepared in the usual manner (recipe 37) cook in the soup for the last 10 minutes.
3. If desired, beat the egg in the soup tureen and carefully add the soup to it while stirring vigorously.
4. Instead of rice, one can also use Siamese sago or pearl sago and allow 3/4 hour cooking time.

title: Rijstsoep (2e recept) (Rice Soup, Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon of beef or 1 liter water and 4 bouillon cubes (recipe 35)
- 30 g rice
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 dl cream
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Cook the washed rice in the usual manner until tender.
2. Bring butter or margarine and flour to a boil while stirring, add the strained bouillon or the water in which the bouillon cubes have been dissolved while stirring, and let the soup simmer while stirring (approximately 10 minutes).
3. Add the cooked rice and carefully add the soup to the egg yolk beaten with the cream in the soup tureen.

title: Rijstsoep (Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- hot water
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Let as much rice as there are persons rinse in hot water
2. Pour boiling bouillon over it and let it cook slowly for an hour
3. Stir the soup occasionally with a wooden spoon

title: Rijstsoep (Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 35 decigrams rice
- Water
- 1.2 liters bone bouillon
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Wash 35 decigrams of rice three times in cold water.
2. Cook the rice in 1 liter water and stir carefully with a spoon.
3. Refresh it and drain it.
4. Put the rice subsequently in 1.2 liters of boiling bone bouillon, stir it with a spoon to mix the rice well with the bouillon, and add some Liebig's Meat Extract.
5. Let the soup simmer gently for 25 minutes to give the rice time to swell, with the lid three-quarters closed.
6. Skim it and put it in the tureen.

title: Rijstvlokkenpap (Rice Flakes Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 100 g rice flakes
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil with salt, sprinkle in the rice flakes while stirring and let the porridge cook while stirring until done (approximately 10 minutes).

title: Rissoles (12 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- some water
- salt
- ragout or jam for filling

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846). Roll it out one last time into a large, thin sheet and cut out circles with a diameter of 8-10 cm. Place some jam in the middle, fold them in half, seal the edges with a little water, brush with egg yolk beaten with a little water. Place them on a baking sheet and bake in a hot oven placed in the middle, light brown and cooked (15-20 minutes). Remove from baking sheet and let cool.
