2. Alternatively: Make circles of dough as described above. Place some ragout in the middle, fold in half, seal the edges with water. Fry the rissoles in frying fat with blue vapor rising from it, until light brown and cooked (approximately 5 minutes). Let them drip briefly on gray paper, place them on a warmed dish with a finger cloth on top.

title: Roastbeef (Roast Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- Salt
- Butter
- Water

## Directions

1. Salt a piece of beef well and let it stand for 2 to 3 days
2. Brown it in an earthenware pan with some butter on the fire, turning it constantly so it does not burn
3. The fire must be fierce, and if you see the meat becomes too dry, add water and let it braise quickly
4. Test with a knife to see if it is done and serve it with its own jus poured over

title: Roastbeef (Roast Beef)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg roastbeef
- 150 g beef fat or 200 g butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Weigh the meat, wash it and rub it on all sides with salt (and some pepper).
2. Heat the fat in a roasting pan (or cut fresh fat, after washing it, into small pieces and roast these first). As soon as blue vapor comes from the fat, turn the meat on all sides through it, place the lid on the pan, reduce the heat and roast the meat on top of the stove, turning it occasionally, 15 minutes per 500 g.
3. Pour some water by the fat as soon as the color becomes too dark and the meat is evenly brown on all sides.
4. Remove the meat when it has roasted for the specified time. Test by pricking it to see if it is done inside.
5. Make the fat hot again until blue vapor comes off and a crust forms on the bottom of the pan. Pour in enough water so that the crust can dissolve in it and remove the jus immediately from the heat.
6. Alternatively: Make the fat so hot in a roasting pan that blue vapor comes off. Roll the weighed, washed and salted meat in it and roast it in a hot oven 10 to 15 minutes per 500 g. Baste it every 7 minutes with the fat during roasting. Pour water by the fat as soon as the meat is brown and the color would become too dark. Finish the jus as indicated above.

title: Roasted Capon (Gebraden Kapoen)
body:
## Ingredients

- well-larded capon
- one ounce butter
- salt
- three large cups water

## Directions

1. Take a well-larded capon and roast it with one ounce butter, salt and three large cups water
2. Take care to baste the capon from time to time with the jus
3. The jus served with it can be improved by loosening the browned bits on the pan with some bouillon and cooking them together

title: Roasted Goose English Style (Gebraden Gans à l'Anglaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat goose
- chopped onions
- goose liver
- butter
- bread crumbs
- jus or bouillon sauce

## Directions

1. Take a young fat goose
2. To see if the goose is young, try to break the point of the beak; if this is easy, you can be sure it is young
3. Clean and gut the goose
4. Fill it with chopped onions and its liver, fried in butter, and some bread crumbs made into dough
5. Sew it closed from behind and roast it on the spit
6. You can make a sauce from jus or bouillon to go with it

title: Roasted Goose with Chestnuts (Gebraden Gans met Kastanjes)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat goose
- chestnuts

## Directions

1. Fill it with chestnuts

title: Roasted Goose with Sour Apples (Gebraden Gans met Zure Appelen)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat goose
- sour apples
- spices
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as above
2. But fill the goose with sour apples, spices, cinnamon and a little sugar

title: Roasted Rabbit (Gebraden Konijn)
body:
## Ingredients

- young rabbit
- water
- fine salt
- pepper
- ground hardtack
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the rabbit in a roasting pan with water
2. Sprinkle with fine salt, pepper, and ground hardtack
3. Lay it flat in a roasting pan with butter and baste it continuously
4. Ensure it browns well
5. Serve with a lemon or shallot sauce

title: Roasted Turkey (Gebraden Kalkoen)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- fine salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey well
2. Remove the breastbone
3. Sprinkle the inside with fine salt, nutmeg and pepper, and the outside only with salt
4. Brown some butter in the pan, lay the turkey in it
5. Let it roast until done with heat under and above
6. Take care to baste it well continuously

title: Roasted Turkey with Chestnuts (Gebraden Kalkoen met Kastanjes)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- turkey organs
- veal
- bacon
- five ounces chestnuts
- salt

## Directions

1. Have the poulterer remove the bones and return them open
2. The stomach, heart, and red liver, all cooked, are finely chopped together with some veal and bacon to serve as stuffing
3. Remove five ounces of chestnuts from their shells and skin, and roast them in hot ashes
4. Lay the turkey on its breast
5. Sprinkle the inside with some salt and put the chestnuts and stuffing mixed under them
6. Sew the turkey closed on the back and roast it with continuous basting

title: Roasted Venison or Roe Haunch (Hert- te- of reebout)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely sliced bacon
- salt
- pepper
- haunch
- water
- brown ragout

## Directions

1. Roll finely sliced bacon in salt and pepper
2. Lard the haunch with it
3. Place it in a deep dish with some water on top
4. Apply good heat under and above
5. Roast it until done
6. Serve with brown ragout
7. Tail pieces of deer or roe are prepared in the same way

title: Robertsaus (Robert Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl lean jus with water
- 50 g onion
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 2 teaspoons sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 359.
2. Remove from heat and mix with mustard, which has been mixed with water or some sauce to prevent lumps, and sugar.
3. Serve with pork cutlets.

title: Rode kool (Red Cabbage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium red cabbage
- 3 dl boiling water
- 5 g salt
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 0.5 dl vinegar or 2 sour apples
- 30 g sugar
- 6 cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbage, cut it in half, remove the stem and cut it with a cabbage knife or ordinary knife into long thin strips.
2. Place it in the boiling water, salt, cloves, vinegar or snipped sour apples.
3. Let the cabbage cook slowly on a gentle heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Once the cabbage has cooked dry, add the butter, margarine or beef fat and the sugar and stew the cabbage with these for 10 minutes more.

title: Rode Wijnsoep (Red Wine Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter red wine (Bordeaux)
- 1/2 liter water
- approximately 100 g sugar
- 35 g Siamese tapioca or tapioca
- peel of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the lemon peel to the boil, add the Siamese tapioca or tapioca while stirring and let it cook over a gentle heat until the grains become completely transparent (1/2 to 3/4 hour).
2. Then remove the lemon peel, add the wine and sugar and serve the soup as soon as it has become hot again.

title: Rode Wijnsoep (Red Wine Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l red wine (Bordeaux)
- 1/2 l water
- 100 g sugar
- 35 g Siamsago or tapioca
- peel of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the lemon peel to a boil
2. Add the Siamsago or tapioca while stirring and let it simmer on low heat until the grains become completely transparent (1/2 to 3/4 hour)
3. Remove the lemon peel, add the wine and sugar
4. Serve the soup as soon as it becomes hot again

title: Roereieren (Scrambled Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper, salt and Maggi's Aroma to taste
- finely chopped green herbs such as parsley, tarragon, pimpernel, chives, chervil (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with pepper, salt, Maggi's Aroma and butter in the small pan, add (optionally the chopped herbs and) the milk and stir over gentle heat until they begin to set and a porridge-like mass forms.
2. Serve with salad or with bread or as garnish for some dishes.

title: Roereieren met ham (Scrambled Eggs with Ham)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 100 g ham
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt
2. Add the finely chopped ham and milk
3. Prepare as described in recipe 395
4. Serve with bread, lettuce or spinach

title: Roereieren met kaas (Scrambled Eggs with Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 60 g Parmesan or Gouda cheese
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt, add the grated cheese and milk.
2. Prepare further as indicated in recipe 395.
3. Serve at coffee time.

title: Roereieren met ossentong (Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Beef Tongue)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 50 g smoked beef tongue
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt
2. Add the finely chopped beef tongue and milk
3. Prepare as described in recipe 395
4. Serve with bread or lettuce

title: Roereieren met tomatenpurée (Scrambled Eggs with Tomato Puree)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 3 tablespoons tomato puree
- 30 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the pepper and salt
2. Add the tomato puree and milk
3. Prepare as described in recipe 395
4. Serve with lettuce or spinach

title: Rog (Skate)
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the skate into long strips
2. Blanch them briefly in water
3. Cook them with salt but they must cook a long time

title: Rolled Cake (Opgerolde Koek)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 50 g sugar
- 40 g flour
- 15 g cornstarch
- jam or marmalade

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar until it is completely finely ground.
2. Add the sifted flour.
3. Beat the egg white stiff and mix it as lightly as possible with the remaining dough.
4. Spread the dough onto a piece of buttered parchment paper, which is placed on a greased baking sheet and projects a little above all sides.
5. Let the cake bake in a fairly warm oven until light brown and cooked through (at least 10, at most 15 minutes).
6. Test if it is cooked by seeing if it easily comes away from the paper.
7. Meanwhile place a piece of grease-free paper on the table. Sprinkle some sugar on it and turn the cake with the brown side onto it.
8. Pull off the paper on top and then spread the light side as quickly as possible with some thin jam or marmalade, which if necessary has been warmed slightly.
9. Roll the cake as quickly as possible, cut the sides straight or diagonally and after cooling sprinkle the cake with powdered sugar or garnish it if desired with glaze and candied fruits or candied strips.

title: Rolled Chocolate Cake (Opgerolde Chocoladekoek)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 50 g sugar
- 35 g flour
- 15 g cornstarch
- 20 g chocolate powder
- 30 g melted butter or margarine
- ½ l chocolate custard
- chocolate glaze and candied fruits or sugared violets

## Directions

1. Bake the cake as described in recipe 827, after stirring the melted butter or margarine into the dough at the end.
2. Fill and garnish the rolled chocolate cake.
3. If you want to fill the cake with a cream or custard filling that cannot be spread on the warm baked goods, then roll the cake unfilled and let it cool somewhat, hanging in a cloth.
4. Roll the cake out again, spread the filling between and roll the baked good up again.

title: Rolmops (Rolled Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small herrings
- 12 pickled gherkins
- 12 pickled onions
- 2 Spanish peppers
- 4 bay leaves
- 10 cloves
- 6 g mustard seed
- 3 onions
- 4 sliced lemon slices
- 6 deciliters good vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak small herrings for 24 hours in water with some milk, refreshing as needed
2. Cut off the heads and tails, remove the skin, cut open along the belly and remove the entrails
3. Remove the bones and small bones
4. Split the opened herrings lengthwise down the back in two, wash them and roll each one up with a gherkin or onion inside
5. Fasten them with a sharply pointed stick and arrange them in a well-cleaned bottle
6. Cook the vinegar with the cloves, bay leaves, one chopped onion, the Spanish pepper and the mustard seed bound in a piece of cheesecloth for a moment
7. Arrange lemon slices, onion slices and Spanish pepper alternately between the fish and the glass
8. Pour the lukewarm vinegar over and add enough vinegar so the fish is completely covered
9. Close the bottle with damp parchment paper and let it stand for at least 6 days
10. Do not store longer than 3 weeks

title: Rolpens (Stuffed Tripe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg beef, for example pieces
- 400 g bacon
- 50 g salt
- 4 nutmegs
- 70 g ground cloves
- 20 g pepper
- tripe

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, cut it into small pieces or grind it (preferably not too fine). Cut the washed bacon into cubes, mix the meat with the salt, the bacon and the spices and let it stand overnight.
2. Rinse the tripe well on the inside and outside in lukewarm water, sew it into bags with coarse thread and fill these not too tightly with the meat.
3. Sew the tripe closed, prick it here and there to prevent bursting and cook the stuffed tripe for 2 hours in plenty of boiling water with salt and a few cloves.
4. Let it cool in a colander and store it in a Cologne pot under ¼ of the liquid in which the stuffed tripe was cooked and ¾ vinegar. Pour a little fat on top and put the rest in a Cologne pot and keep it to fry the stuffed tripe in later.

title: Rommolkantjös (Rum Molasses Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7.5 ounces flour
- Butter and milk
- 5 eggs
- 4 spoons sugar
- 2 grated lemon zests
- 2 ounces almond paste
- A piece of yeast

## Directions

1. Make a batter with butter and milk, flour, eggs, sugar, lemon zests and almond paste
2. Add yeast and let rise
3. Put in molds
4. Bake in a tart pan

title: Rommolkantjös (Rummol Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7.5 ounces flour
- butter and milk
- 5 eggs
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 2 grated lemon peels
- 2 ounces almond paste
- a piece of yeast

## Directions

1. Beat together flour, butter and milk
2. Add 5 eggs, 4 tablespoons sugar, 2 grated lemon peels and 2 ounces almond paste
3. Add a piece of yeast and let it rise
4. Place in molds and bake in a tart pan

title: Roode kool (Red Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- water
- vinegar
- syrup
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- sour apples (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop it very finely
2. Add a beer glass of water, the same amount of vinegar, a spoon of syrup, a little pepper, nutmeg, and salt to taste
3. Let this stew slowly for 4 hours
4. You can also add sour apples, but then use less vinegar

title: Roode-wijnsaus (Red Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cloves
- Cinnamon
- 2 ounces sugar
- 1 pint red Bordeaux wine
- 2 teaspoons potato flour
- Water

## Directions

1. Put three cloves, cinnamon and 2 ounces sugar in 1 pint red Bordeaux wine
2. Let it cook slowly but well
3. When it boils well, add two teaspoons potato flour stirred in cold water
4. Strain the sauce through

title: Roodekoolsalade (Red Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- cooked beet in slices
- onions, finely cut
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- warm sour sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as endive salad (see above).

title: Roomhorens (Cream Horns)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g hard butter or margarine
- 75 g flour
- some water
- salt
- 2.5 dl custard cream (recipe 816) for filling

## Directions

1. Make puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846). Roll it out very thin one last time and cut long strips from it with a width of 2 cm, make a narrow edge damp with water. Starting at the point, wrap the strips around the greased cream horn molds. Brush the top of the cream horns with egg yolk beaten with a little water, place them on a baking sheet and bake them in a hot oven until light brown and cooked (12-15 minutes). Let cool, remove the molds and fill them with custard cream prepared in the usual way.

title: Roompap (Cream Pap)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cream
- Sugar
- Eggs
- Rose water

## Directions

1. Take 10 measures of cream, add sugar and 6 eggs, of which 3 with the white
2. Cook together in a new pan
3. Once cooked, add a splash of rose water
4. It is eaten cold

title: Roomsaus (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 ounces butter
- flour and cornstarch
- parsley, chopped
- fine onions
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 1 glass cream or milk

## Directions

1. Mix 1.5 ounces butter with flour and cornstarch, a little chopped parsley, fine onions, a little salt, pepper and nutmeg
2. Mix this together with 1 glass of cream or milk
3. Cook it with continuous stirring
4. If the sauce is too thick, add more milk or cream
5. This sauce is very good with stewed turbot, cod, salt fish and potatoes

title: Roomsaus (Cream Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l cooking cream or 1/4 l cream and 1/4 l milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 g Duryea maizena
- 50 g sugar
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as vanilla sauce (recipe 139).
2. Present the sauce with mocha pudding (recipe 711).

title: Roomsaus (Cream Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 2 1/2 deciliter milk
- 1 or 2 1/2 deciliter cream, together 1/2 liter
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Mix it with pepper and salt to taste and serve with warm macaroni pudding or cooked fish
3. If desired, replace the cream with condensed milk

title: Roomsehe boonen (Cream Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- rainwater
- sweet milk
- savory herb
- parsley
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in rainwater with sweet milk, skimming carefully and quickly
2. Cook uncovered
3. Meanwhile chop savory and parsley together
4. Let this cook well with sweet milk, butter and a little salt
5. Then add the beans and let them stew uncovered for a quarter hour

title: Roomstruif or Vierstruif (Cream Puff or Four-Layer Puff)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 spoonfuls flour (approximately 45 g)
- 4 spoonfuls sugar (approximately 60 g)
- 4 spoonfuls butter or margarine (approximately 70 g)
- 1/4 l milk
- pulp from 1 stick vanilla

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar and vanilla pulp.
2. Add the sifted flour, the melted butter or margarine, and the milk gradually.
3. Ensure the batter becomes completely smooth.
4. Beat the egg white very stiffly and fold it into the batter while beating.
5. Let a very small amount of butter or margarine melt in the frying pan and pour 1/4 of the batter into it.
6. Let the cake cook and brown golden on one side on very low heat.
7. Bake the remaining dough the same way and lay the first three cakes with the fried side on the unbaked (creamy) side.
8. Turn the last cake around so the brown side is on top.
9. Sprinkle the cream puff with powdered sugar and serve immediately.

title: Roomtaart (Cream Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 150 g sugar
- 80 g flour
- 20 g cornstarch
- 2.5 dl milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 25 g flour
- 25 to 30 g sugar
- 1.5 vanilla pod

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar until it is completely finely ground.
2. Add the sifted flour and cornstarch and mix this mass as quickly as possible with the very stiffly beaten egg white.
3. Put the batter in a greased cake pan, dusted with powdered sugar and flour, and bake the cake in a fairly warm oven until light brown and done (approximately 30 to 35 minutes).
4. Cover if necessary with a greased piece of paper.
5. Check if it is done by pricking it with a knitting needle, which should then come out dry.
6. Turn the cake onto a cake rack, let it cool and cut it into three or four layers.
7. Spread these with the cream custard (recipe 816), which is prepared in the usual way.
8. Place the layers on top of each other and garnish the cake with icing and French fruits.

title: Roomvla or Banketbakkersroom (Pastry Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 dl milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 25 g flour
- 25 to 50 g sugar
- 1/4 vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar and flour until a smooth mass without lumps.
2. Slowly bring the milk with the vanilla to the boil, add it to the yolks, stirring well, pour everything back into the pan and let the custard cook, stirring well with a whisk (about 10 minutes).
3. Stir occasionally while cooling to prevent a skin from forming.
4. Note: The custard can also be mixed with stiffly beaten egg white, but then add a couple of sheets of dissolved gelatin to prevent it from collapsing over time (especially recommended for tompouces).

title: Roomvlade (Cream Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a can sweet cream
- whites of 14 to 15 eggs
- a splash of orange water
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Heat half a can of sweet cream over fire
2. Beat the egg whites until foamy
3. Add them with a splash of orange water and sugar to taste
4. Stir until thick
5. Pour quickly over

title: Rose Pudding
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 3 lemons
- 2.5 dl water
- 100 g sugar
- 16 g white and red gelatin or 14 g powdered gelatin
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- optionally 100 g candied fruit pieces

## Directions

1. Soak the leaf gelatin 10 to 15 minutes in cold water.
2. Bring the water to a boil, dissolve the sugar in it.
3. Remove from heat, dissolve the well-squeezed gelatin in it and add the sifted lemon juice.
4. Let the mixture cool somewhat and add it slowly while stirring to the stiffly beaten cream and stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. If desired, mix the pudding with pieces of candied fruit.
6. Let it stand, stirring occasionally, until it becomes gelatinous and the gelatin no longer sinks.
7. Pour it into a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white and let it become cold and stiff.
8. Turn the pudding out onto a damp dish and serve it without sauce.

title: Rozebottelstruif (Rose Hip Fritter)
body:
## Ingredients

- rose hips
- cold water
- grated stale white bread
- a glass wine
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Place rose hips in some cold water
2. Let them cook into a pulp
3. Add grated stale white bread, a glass wine, butter, cinnamon and sugar to taste
4. Stir well together
5. Bake it like any other fritter

title: Rozendaalsche Koekjes (Rozendaal Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces wheat flour
- 1 cup groat flour
- 4 eggs
- Half water and half milk
- A little yeast
- 5 ounces currants

## Directions

1. Beat together the wheat flour, groat flour, and 4 eggs with half water and half milk thinly
2. Add a little yeast at the end
3. Add 5 ounces currants through it
4. They should not be baked larger than a guilder

title: Rozijnensaus (Raisin Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l beef tongue bouillon
- 40 to 50 g flour
- 40 to 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl white wine
- 20 g sugar
- 50 g raisins
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Taste the bouillon to check if it is not too salty; if necessary add water until a good taste is obtained.
2. Prepare the sauce in the usual manner (see cauliflower sauce).
3. Wash the raisins after removing the stems with lukewarm water.
4. Soak them briefly in boiling water, let them drain in a sieve and stir them with the wine and sugar through the sauce.
5. Add lemon juice to taste, let the sauce heat through while stirring, but do not boil anymore.
6. Serve with cooked beef tongue.

title: Rum Sauce (Rumsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- One spoon flour
- Three spoons bread sugar
- Three egg yolks
- Half a bottle rum

## Directions

1. Stir one spoon flour, three spoons bread sugar, and three egg yolks well together
2. Pour in half a bottle of rum
3. Let it cook while stirring constantly

title: Rumgelei (Rum Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- six looten fish glue
- four ounces sugar
- juice of two lemons
- five small glasses best rum
- a glass clear water

## Directions

1. Take six looten fish glue and four ounces sugar
2. Add the juice of two lemons and five small glasses of best rum
3. Mix together with a glass of clear water
4. Strain through a fine silk sieve
5. Pour into the mold
6. Let it cool

title: Rumpudding (Rum Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 eggs
- peel of 1 lemon
- 1 dl rum
- 100 g sugar
- 8 g white gelatine

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar and the grated lemon peel until the sugar is completely dissolved.
2. Let this mass be bound while stirring au bain-marie.
3. Then add, away from the fire, the rum and the soaked sheet gelatine dissolved in boiling water.
4. Beat the cream stiff and stir the remaining mass through it.
5. Let the pudding become jelly-like, stirring from time to time, pour it into a mold brushed with unbeaten egg white and let it become cold and firm.
6. Turn it out onto a damp plate and serve without sauce.

title: Rumsaus (Rum Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 60 g bastard sugar
- 1 dl rum
- 1 dl water
- juice of 1/2 lemon or orange
- zest of 1/2 lemon or orange

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as wine sauce (recipe 765).
2. Serve it with warm bread pudding, almond cake pudding etc.

title: Runderbiefstuk (Beef Steak)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg beef steak
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- some pepper
- some milk and water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat off or scrape it with a knife.
2. Rub it in on both sides with the salt (and pepper).
3. Make 1/2 of the butter or margarine brown in the frying pan, which has been heated well beforehand.
4. Place the steak in it, sear it closed and fry the steak, continuously sliding back and forth and turning over, but especially not pricking, evenly brown on both sides.
5. Allow 8 to 10 minutes per 500 g for cooking time.
6. Remove the meat from the pan, add the remaining butter or margarine and let it brown so that a crust forms at the bottom of the pan.
7. Remove the pan from the heat and let the fat cool a bit.
8. Add some milk and let it brown on the fire.
9. Finish the gravy with some water.

title: Rundergehakt (Ground Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Kidney fat
- White bread without crust
- Sweet milk
- An egg
- Nutmeg
- Fine salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Mince the beef finely with some kidney fat
2. Knead in white bread without crust that has been soaked in sweet milk and squeezed dry, along with an egg
3. Add nutmeg and fine salt
4. Make one or more balls and braise them brown in butter with fire above and below

title: Runderhaas in Gelei (Beef Fillet in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 calf feet
- ½ ounce fish glue
- A beer glass of Rhine wine
- Half a beer glass of vinegar
- Juice of a couple of lemons
- A spoonful of brown sugar
- Liquid from the calf feet
- Egg whites from 8 eggs
- Bay leaves
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Take 6 calf feet and let them cook for four hours
2. Meanwhile melt ½ ounce fish glue in a small pot
3. Let the liquid from the calf feet cool, remove the fat and return to the fire in a pot
4. Add a beer glass of Rhine wine, half a beer glass of vinegar, juice of a couple of lemons, a spoonful of brown sugar and some liquid from the calf feet
5. Let it stand on the fire until it is brown
6. When the liquid is lukewarm, whisk it with slightly beaten egg whites from 8 eggs
7. Let it boil until it is clear and then strain it
8. Place the mold in the sun so the aspic cools quickly, pouring in a little of the strained liquid
9. When cooled, lay some bay leaves and 5 to 6 lemon slices on it, pouring a little liquid over
10. Now place the beef on top and fill the mold again with liquid

title: Runderkarbonaden (Beef Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chops (not too thick)
- Butter
- Lemon juice or shallot sauce

## Directions

1. Take chops that are not too thick, beat them well
2. Braise them with sufficient butter in an earthenware pan
3. When cooked, add lemon juice to the butter or eat the chops with a shallot sauce

title: Runderlappen bij. Riblappen (Beef Stew, e.g. Rib Pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 to 1 kg beef pieces
- 140 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- some pepper
- 20 g salt
- a couple of bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 1 dl vinegar or 4 tomatoes (+ 400 g)

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD. Wash the pieces, rub them in with the salt and some pepper.
2. Make the fat, butter or margarine hot in the frying pan and fry the pieces brown on both sides. (With a small number the pieces can be fried directly in the stewing pan.)
3. Place them in a stewing pan and pour over the gravy, which has been finished with some water.
4. Add the vinegar or the tomatoes sliced, washed, and the spices and let the beef pieces stew gently for at least 1 1/2 hours, turning them from time to time.
5. Strain the gravy and bind it if desired with some fine breadcrumbs or browned flour.
6. SECOND METHOD. Wash the pieces, rub them in with pepper and salt and place them for 24 hours in half water, half vinegar with the spices.
7. Turn them several times during that time and fry them as indicated above.
8. Use the liquid in which the pieces have been standing to finish the gravy.

title: Runderrollade (Beef Roulade)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg beef roulade
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g.

title: Runderrolletjes (Beef Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- beef slices
- 50 g fresh bacon
- 4 gherkins
- salt
- at least 75 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the beef slices, cut the washed bacon into 4 equally large slices, place these on the beef slices, put a gherkin in the middle and roll everything together
2. Tie the beef rolls with thin string or thread and brown them in the butter or margarine on all sides
3. Finish the jus with water and stew the meat in it for about 1 hour
4. Remove the strings or threads before serving

title: Runderrolletjes (Beef Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beautiful, lean, oblong-shaped beef cutlets of 75 g each
- 50 g fresh bacon
- 4 gherkins
- Salt
- At least 75 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the cutlets, cut the washed bacon into 4 equal slices, place these on the cutlets, place a gherkin in the middle and roll everything together.
2. Tie the beef rolls closed with a thin string or thread and fry them in butter or margarine on all sides until brown.
3. Finish the jus with water and braise the meat in it for approximately 1 hour more.
4. Remove the strings or threads before serving.

title: Runderschijf (Beef Slice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg beef slice
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 25 to 30 minutes per 500 g.

title: Rundvleesch aan het Spit (Beef on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- A reasonably fat piece of beef
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Salt a reasonably fat piece of beef well and place it in vinegar
2. When you want to use it, wash it first
3. Stud it with some cloves and put it on the spit, turning it constantly
4. Place a pan under the meat to catch the dripping jus
5. Baste the meat with the jus from time to time

title: Rundvleesch met Rijst-Macaroni (Beef with Rice-Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Rice-macaroni
- Beef bouillon
- Pepper
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cook rice-macaroni with the bouillon from the beef until thick.
2. When serving, add pepper, butter and parmesan cheese.

title: Rundvleesch met Uien (Beef with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Onions, sliced
- Apples
- Vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take sliced onions, but only one quarter as much as apples, and prepare it as described for beef with savoy cabbage, using vinegar or lemon juice.

title: Russische Salade (Russian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover meat
- Herring
- Gherkins
- Potatoes
- Apples
- Cooked mushrooms
- Capers
- Shallots or cooked onions
- Potato flour
- Salad oil
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sugar
- Mustard
- Vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut leftover meat, herring, gherkins, potatoes, apples, and cooked mushrooms into cubes
2. Add capers, shallots or cooked onions and mix everything well
3. For the sauce: mix two spoonfuls of potato flour with two spoonfuls of salad oil over the fire, stirring well
4. Once cooled, stir in two egg yolks, a little salt, pepper, sugar, two spoonfuls of mustard and more salad oil
5. Thin it with vinegar and a little water, but ensure it is not too thin
6. Pour the sauce over the salad and let it soak through well

title: Sago Dish (Sagoschoteltje)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 liters milk
- 100 g pearl sago
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 100 g sugar
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook sago pudding in the usual manner (see recipe 607), but cook the vanilla or lemon zest in with it.
2. Stir in half of the sugar when the pudding is done.
3. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the remaining sugar, carefully add the hot pudding from which the vanilla or lemon zest has been removed to it, mix it as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Finish the dish as described in recipe 602.

title: Sago Melk (Sago Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- sago flour
- cold milk
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Bring the milk to a boil, now mix some sago flour with cold milk into a paste, stir that into the milk and let it cook through
2. Add sugar and cinnamon as desired

title: Sago Pudding (Sagopap)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 liters milk
- 100 g pearl sago
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the washed sago for several hours in cold water.
2. Bring the milk and salt to a boil.
3. Sprinkle the soaked pearl sago in stirring, keep stirring until the milk boils again and let the pudding cook on low heat, stirring occasionally, until done (approximately 1 1/2 hours).

title: Sagopodding (Sago Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce sago
- 1 cinnamon stick
- sweet milk
- 9-10 crushed rusk
- 5 eggs
- butter
- bread sugar
- 1 grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook 1 ounce sago with 1 cinnamon stick in sweet milk until it has a proper thickness
2. coat this with 9-10 finely crushed rusk, 5 eggs, the necessary butter, bread sugar and 1 grated lemon zest
3. pour into the mold
4. let it cook until done

title: Sagosoep met Bouillon (Sago Soup with Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- strong bouillon
- white sago
- mace
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. In strong bouillon let white sago with some mace cook done
2. Be especially careful to stir constantly to prevent burning
3. When serving, beat egg yolks through it

title: Sagosoep zonder Bouillon (Sago Soup without Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago flour
- water
- red wine
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook sago flour with water, stirring constantly until it is thick
2. Take it off the heat and mix red wine, sugar and cinnamon through it

title: Sajoer Loddéh (Indonesian Vegetable Curry)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 sprouts
- 4 small carrots
- 5 princess beans
- 5 pea pods
- piece of cauliflower
- piece of white Savoy cabbage
- 3 onions
- 6 garlic cloves
- piece of trassi
- 7 kemiri nuts
- 6 dried chilies (without seeds)
- 2 teaspoons laos
- 3 teaspoons sereh powder
- 2 teaspoons coriander
- 2 teaspoons cumin
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 citrus leaves
- 100 g peeled cooked shrimp
- 1½ liters coconut milk
- 50 g beef fillet
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Pound the spices, except for the aromatic leaves, together with the ground onions in the mortar with the pestle until fine
2. Fry them in butter or margarine
3. Then add the finely cut vegetables and fry together
4. In a well-sealed pan, add the coconut milk or milk with salt, add the fried mixture with the meat pieces and the aromatic leaves
5. Let the sajoer steep and cook until done
6. Add the shrimp at the end

title: Sajoer van Kool (Cabbage Sajoer)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g finely cut Savoy cabbage
- 250 g onion (5 small)
- 50 g salad oil
- salt
- piece of chili pepper
- ¾ liter milk

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into the finest possible strips
2. Finely chop the cleaned and washed onions
3. Put the salad oil in a frying pan and brown the onions lightly in it
4. Add a portion of the cabbage and fry it, stirring constantly, until light brown
5. Continue until all the cabbage is used
6. Then add a piece of chili pepper and some milk
7. Let the sajoer stew on a low heat until the milk evaporates
8. Add milk again and let the cabbage stew until it is cooked through, but make sure it remains very moist
9. Serve with rice and ground meat

title: Salade van Brusselsche Kool (Brussels Sprouts Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- cooked potatoes
- celeriac pieces
- onions, finely cut
- salt
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the sprouts almost done and let them cool.
2. Cut cooked potatoes into slices.
3. Add some pieces of celeriac and finely cut onions.
4. Toss with salt, oil, and vinegar.
5. Usually served with head cheese.

title: Salade van Prinsesse- of Snijboontjes (Princess or String Beans Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- oil
- vinegar or sour sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the beans.
2. Eat them cold with oil and vinegar or sour sauce.

title: Sally Lunns
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 7.5 g salt
- 10 g yeast
- 1 egg
- 50 g butter or margarine
- some sugar
- 2 dl lukewarm milk

## Directions

1. Make bread dough in the known manner and let it rise (see recipe 835).
2. Put the dough in high cake molds that have been greased with butter or margarine or make round slices of about 3 cm thickness from it and place these on a greased baking sheet.
3. Let the batter rise for another 1/4 hour under a damp cloth.
4. Then bake them in a hot oven in 20 to 30 minutes light brown and done.
5. Cut them in half while warm, spread them, place the halves on top of each other and serve them warm.

title: Sambal (Pickled Cucumber Relish)
body:
## Ingredients

- green cucumbers
- salt
- whole white pepper
- peeled shallots
- boiling vinegar
- alum

## Directions

1. Peel the green cucumbers and remove the skin; peel them further down to the seed, roll them up and cut them finely
2. Lay them overnight in a bowl with plenty of salt and let them drain well
3. Place the cucumbers in an earthenware pot with some whole white pepper and peeled shallots, and pour boiling vinegar over them, in which a piece of alum must be dissolved
4. The cucumbers must be well covered and the pot must be sealed with a bladder

title: Sambal Ati (Chicken Liver Sambal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken livers and gizzards
- 4 lomboks
- 5 cloves white onions
- 4 tablespoons ground onions
- 1/2 teaspoon laos
- 1 tablespoon sereh powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon assem water
- 2 daon djoeroek poeroet
- 2 dl coconut milk
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Clean the livers and gizzards well, cut them into pieces and cook them
2. Pound the spices and the onions — except the leaves — finely and fry them in the butter or margarine
3. Add the pieces of liver and gizzard with some salt, the assem water, sugar and the leaves
4. Cook this with the coconut milk until the fat comes out

title: Sambal Goddek (Mixed Vegetable Sambal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage, beans, peas, peteh beans
- 100 g peeled cooked shrimp
- 2 tablespoons ground onions
- 2 tablespoons finely sliced fried onions
- 5 pieces removed, roasted kemiri nuts
- 4 finely sliced garlic cloves
- 2 tablespoons finely sliced roasted lomboks
- 2 teaspoons ketoembar
- 2 citrus leaves
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 teaspoon salt
- Three lengthwise sliced green lomboks
- 1 dl finely sliced green gooseberries
- Water
- 5 to 7 1/2 dl milk with salt or coconut milk

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned and finely sliced vegetables with the green lomboks and the gooseberries in some water with salt until cooked
2. Drain the water and add the coconut milk or milk with some salt
3. Pound the spices — except the fried onions, the leaves and the roasted lomboks — together finely
4. Add this to the mixture and cook everything through, stirring
5. Add the lomboks, the leaves and the fried onions, and make a thick mixture from this

title: Sambal Goreng Oedang (Shrimp Sambal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g peeled cooked shrimp
- 2 tablespoons finely ground onions
- 5 garlic cloves
- 1 teaspoon laos
- 2 teaspoons sereh powder
- 2 whole lomboks with seeds
- A bit of roasted trassi
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 citrus leaves
- 2 peteh beans
- Coconut milk or milk with some salt
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Pound the spices, without the aromatic leaves, finely with the ground onions
2. Fry them in the butter or margarine
3. Add the aromatic leaves, the coconut milk or milk with some salt and make a thickened sauce from this
4. Add the cooked and peeled shrimp and let them stew in it

title: Sambal Goreng Tomaten (Tomato Sambal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 not overly ripe tomatoes
- 4 slices garlic
- 3 green and 3 red finely chopped lomboks
- 1/2 teaspoon laos
- 1 spoon trassi water
- 1 dl coconut milk
- 100 g peeled cooked shrimp
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Slice the cleaned tomatoes into slices
2. Pound the spices with the ground onions
3. Fry them
4. Add the tomatoes with trassi water
5. Cook this briefly and add the coconut milk with the shrimp
6. Heat this together until the fat comes out

title: Sambal Petis (Petis Sambal)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons ground onions
- 3 garlic cloves
- 1 teaspoon laos
- 2 teaspoons trassi mixed with water
- 1 finely sliced red lombok without seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 teaspoon goela djawa
- 2 teaspoons tamarind water
- 3 tablespoons petis (petis ikan or petis oedang)
- 2 daon djoeroek poeroet
- Some salt
- 1 dl coconut milk
- 1 dl warm water
- Butter, salad oil, beef fat or frying fat

## Directions

1. Pound the spices, except the leaves and the lombok and fry the mixture yellow in one of the aforementioned fats
2. Add the warm water; let the mixture boil
3. Stir in the tablespoons of petis and the trassi water
4. Add the leaves, the lombok, the tamarind water, some salt and the goela djawa
5. Let the mixture come to a boil again and finally add the coconut milk
6. Cook this together until the oil comes out

title: Sand Cake (Zandtaart)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound butter
- One pound sugar
- One pound flour
- Twelve egg yolks
- Rose water or orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Make the butter soft and mix everything very well together, especially knead well
2. Add rose water or orange blossom water
3. Whip the egg whites well together and mix thoroughly
4. Put into a buttered mold
5. Bake in the ashes with fire above
6. When the cake is cold and done, it should crumble like sand in the mouth

title: Sand Cake, Another Way (Zandtaart - Anders)
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter measure flour
- One pound salt
- Two ounces sugar
- Seven and one half ounces butter
- Twelve eggs
- Milk

## Directions

1. Make a hole in the flour
2. Place in the hole: one pound salt, two ounces sugar, seven and one half ounces butter, and twelve eggs
3. Knead well together, at least three times
4. If too stiff, add a little milk
5. Let stand for half an hour
6. Add half a pound butter
7. Roll four times on the work surface
8. Form in a thick mold
9. Lift the edges slightly with ridges
10. Make some figures on top
11. Place on a tin sheet
12. Bake in the oven for one and a half hours

title: Sand Cake, Yet Another Way (Zandtaart - Nog anders)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound flour or one cup
- Two eggs, egg white whipped separately to foam
- Half pound melted butter
- Half pound currants
- Half pound sugar

## Directions

1. Mix two eggs with the flour before putting it in the tart pan, along with half pound melted butter, half pound currants, and half pound sugar
2. Knead everything well together
3. Give little heat from below, much heat from above
4. Bake for just over half an hour
5. Grease the pan well with butter and sprinkle with biscuit

title: Sauce with Madeira Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- One spoonful flour
- Very finely cut shavings
- Madeira wine
- A little butter
- Water or bouillon
- Butter
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with either a spoonful of Madeira wine, a little butter and water, or with bouillon
2. Let it cook for a quarter hour
3. Add more butter and sugar
4. Stir well and use with pudding

title: Saucijzebroodjes (Sausage Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sausages
- Flour
- Eggs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the sausages until done and then remove the casing
2. Make a dough from flour, eggs, and a little salt
3. Roll it out, place each sausage separately in it
4. Let them brown in the oven

title: Saucijzebroodjes (Sausage Rolls - approximately 8 pieces)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- For the dough: 125 g hard butter or calf kidney fat
- 125 g flour
- salt
- water (slightly more than 1/2 dl)
- For the filling: 75 g ground veal
- 75 g ground pork
- 25 g old bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846)
2. Grind the meat once more through the meat grinder, mix it in the usual manner with the soaked bread and a generous amount of pepper, salt and nutmeg and make small rolls from it
3. Roll out the dough the last time into a fairly thin sheet, cut pieces from it with a width of approximately 10 cm and length of approximately 16 cm
4. Lay a meat roll on it, fold the dough around it and glue it from below and on the side with a little water
5. Lay the rolls with the sealed side down on a baking sheet, make shallow notches in the top with the blunt side of a knife and brush them with egg yolk beaten with a little water or with a little milk
6. Bake the rolls in a fairly warm oven until light brown and cooked through (approximately 20 minutes)

title: Saucys de Boixlogne (Boiling Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- half lean beef
- half lean pork
- bacon
- salt
- coarsely ground pepper

## Directions

1. Mince the beef, pork, and bacon very finely together with salt and coarse pepper
2. Stuff this meat into wide casings
3. Leave in salt for two days
4. Smoke for 10 days

title: Saucys de Boulogne (Boulogne Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- lean beef
- lean pork
- bacon
- salt
- coarse ground pepper
- wide intestines

## Directions

1. Take equal parts lean beef and lean pork with bacon
2. Chop very finely together
3. Add salt and coarse ground pepper
4. Stuff this meat into wide intestines
5. Lay them in salt for two days
6. Smoke them for 10 days

title: Saus om bij koud vleesch te eten (Sauce for Cold Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- good mustard
- oil
- sugar
- parsley, chopped
- shallots, chopped
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix good mustard with oil and sugar together
2. Add a little chopped parsley, chopped shallots and vinegar to taste

title: Saus à la Tartare (Tartare Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoon parsley
- 1 shallot
- anchovy without the spine
- mixed pickles
- lemon juice
- water
- 6 spoons salad oil
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Chop a spoon of parsley, one shallot, anchovy without the spine and mixed pickles, each separately, very finely
2. Pour the juice of a lemon over it, as well as a spoon of water
3. Add 6 spoons of salad oil and a little pepper
4. Mix everything well together
5. Just before serving, add a spoon of mustard

title: Savarin met vruchten (Savarin with Fruits)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 200 g butter
- 4 eggs
- 25 g sugar
- 25 g yeast
- approximately 1 dl lukewarm milk
- fruits

## Directions

1. Make a fairly slack starter dough from approximately 100 g flour, yeast and some lukewarm milk. Let it rise in a warm place for approximately 20 minutes. Make a dough from the rest of the flour, the eggs, the sugar and possibly the rest of the milk, add the butter in small pieces at a time and knead the dough as for bread dough; then work the risen starter dough into it. Place the dough in a ring mold brushed with butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs. Let it rise for 1 hour and then bake the savarin for approximately 3/4 hour in a hot oven. Meanwhile prepare the fruits. Catch the fruit juice and add some liqueur to it or dissolve extra sugar in it. Turn out the savarin and prick holes in it with a larding needle. Soak the ring with the sweet and aromatic fruit juice. Let the ring cool, fill it with the fruits and garnish the savarin with fruits and with cream. If the savarin is served hot (and with warm compote) then the cream garnish should be omitted, but if for example a small pitcher of cream is served separately.

title: Savooische Koekjes (Savoy Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 eggs
- 9 egg yolks
- 5 ounces sugar
- 10 drams pounded almonds
- candied citrus peel (sukade)
- snippets
- 4 ounces sifted flour

## Directions

1. Stir for one hour 9 eggs and 9 egg yolks with 5 ounces sugar
2. Add 10 drams pounded almonds, candied citrus peel and snippets
3. Mix in 4 ounces sifted flour
4. Stir well together
5. Put in round molds greased with butter
6. Bake in the oven until done

title: Savoye-kool (Savoy Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoy cabbage
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into quarters and let it cook almost done
2. Drain well and chop finely
3. Add nutmeg, a little pepper, salt and milk
4. Let it stew for half an hour

title: Savoye-kool (Savoy Cabbage, Another Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoy cabbage
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the outer green leaves from the cabbage
2. Wash the cabbage and let it drain thoroughly
3. In a pan, make a sauce of butter rolled in flour, some bouillon, salt and nutmeg
4. Place the cabbage in it and let everything stew together

title: Savoye-kool, Anders (Savoy Cabbage, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- Savoy cabbage
- salt
- milk
- potato flour or flour
- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage with salt until done
2. Let it drain
3. When serving, pour over a sauce made of milk, potato flour or flour, a piece of butter and some nutmeg

title: Savoyekool of witte kool (Savoy Cabbage or White Cabbage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium Savoy cabbage
- 2.5 dl milk
- 50 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves of the cabbage, cut it into coarse pieces, remove the stem, wash it and cook it in salted water until tender (45 minutes).
2. Let it drain in a colander as dry as possible, chop it as finely as possible and stew it for 10 minutes with the milk and the butter or margarine.
3. Sprinkle in some flour if the mass is too moist, but make sure it cooks completely.
4. Grate some nutmeg over it in the bowl.

title: Schape-Rosboeuf aan 't Spit (Sheep Roast Beef on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rear part of a sheep
- wooden pins
- paper
- jus

## Directions

1. Take the rear part of a sheep
2. Break the two leg bones and beat it strongly
3. Secure it with wooden pins and give it some shape
4. Cover it with paper
5. Baste it well
6. Serve it with the jus poured over

title: Schapepootjes met een Witte Saus (Sheep Feet with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep feet
- bouillon
- two egg yolks
- a little pepper
- butter
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Take some sheep feet
2. Remove the bones up to the bend
3. Blanch them
4. Then cook them thoroughly
5. Make a sauce from bouillon, two egg yolks, a little pepper, some butter, salt and flour
6. Serve over the feet

title: Schapevleesch als Wildbraad (Mutton as Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton or lean piece of mutton
- vinegar
- ragout

## Directions

1. Soak a leg of mutton or lean piece of mutton in vinegar for 3 to 4 days
2. Roast it in a pan or on the spit
3. Serve with a ragout as described in lams-ragout

title: Schelvis (Haddock)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 haddock (approximately 1 kg)
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, make a slit along the backbone, rinse it thoroughly
2. Cook it in boiling water with salt until a fin comes off easily (approximately 10 minutes)
3. Remove it with the fish slice or a pair of skimming spoons from the pan and place it on the fish plate
4. Serve with butter sauce, egg sauce, mustard sauce, glazed or melted butter

title: Schielijk (Quick Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces wheat flour
- 0.5 quart milk
- 2.5 ounces butter
- 2 eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon
- snippets (candied citrus peel)
- sukade (candied fruit)
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook 2.5 ounces wheat flour and 0.5 quart milk until very thick
2. Stir in 2.5 ounces butter, 2 eggs, sugar and cinnamon
3. Add snippets, sukade and lemon peel
4. Bake in a cookie pan greased with butter

title: Schildpadsoep (Turtle Soup) (10-12 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's foot
- 250 g beef shank meat
- 3 large onions
- 1/2 large carrot
- 70 g butter or margarine
- 4 cloves
- 6 bay leaves
- thyme and marjoram to taste
- 20 bruised white peppercorns
- Cayenne pepper (optional)
- 90 g flour
- 90 g butter or margarine
- English sauce and soya or Madeira to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the foot and let it cook gently with 1 l water for 1 hour
2. Cut off the fine pieces of meat to serve later as garnish in the soup
3. Brown the diced shank meat, chopped onions and carrot in 70 g butter or margarine, taking care that this mixture does not burn
4. Add a little water if necessary
5. Put them in a pan, add the calf's foot and 1 l water and let everything infuse together for 3 hours
6. Strain the bouillon, add 1 l fresh water and let this broth infuse gently for another 3 hours
7. Strain it, let it cool and skim the fat from the 3 broths
8. Combine them and let them simmer gently with the herbs for a quarter hour
9. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring to a boil while stirring, rub out all lumps and gradually add the strained bouillon
10. Let the soup simmer while stirring until cooked (10 minutes)
11. Warm the meat pieces from the foot in it and add English sauce and soya or Madeira away from heat

title: Schilletjes op Brandewijn (Citrus Peel in Brandy)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- orange or mandarin peel
- lemon peel
- sugar
- brandy

## Directions

1. Peel the well-cleaned oranges or mandarins and lemons (for 6 oranges or 8 mandarins take 1 lemon) as thinly as possible, so that no white remains on the peel. Put the peel with the sugar in the well-cleaned bottle. Pour brandy over it so that the peel is well covered. Seal the bottle airtight and let the contents stand for at least 3 months before using.

title: Schol (Plaice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cooking plaice
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, make a slit along the backbone, rinse it inside and outside
2. Cook it in boiling water with salt (and a little vinegar if desired) with the light side facing up until a fin comes off easily (5 to 10 minutes)
3. Remove it with 2 skimming spoons or the fish slice from the pan and place it light side up on a fish plate
4. Serve with egg sauce, parsley sauce, or melted butter

title: Schoonmaken van gezouten haring (Cleaning Salted Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- salted herring
- water
- milk

## Directions

1. Soak the herring for some hours in water with some milk.
2. Make an incision along the back.
3. Cut open the belly side, remove the intestines, roe or milt and the black membrane that surrounds the intestines.
4. Pull off the skin.
5. Cut off the head.
6. Wash the herring thoroughly clean and cut it into diagonal pieces.
7. Arrange these in the form of the fish on the platter.

title: Schoonmaken van haring (Cleaning Herring)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- fresh parsley or red geranium flower

## Directions

1. Make an incision along the back of the herring, cut open the belly side, carefully remove the intestines, pull off the skin.
2. Cut the fish down the middle to the head, remove the bone and place the herring in its original form on a small plate.
3. Insert a sprig of fresh parsley or a red geranium flower in the mouth.

title: Schorseneeren (Salsify)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- rainwater
- a splash of vinegar
- milk
- butter
- biscuit
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook in rainwater with a splash of vinegar for two hours
2. Drain
3. Stew with a little milk, butter, biscuit and nutmeg

title: Schorseneren (Salsify)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 kg salsify
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour or 10 g cornstarch (Duryea maizena)
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the salsify with lukewarm water with a splash of vinegar.
2. Scrape them in small pieces at a time and immediately place these in cold, boiled water or water with a splash of milk or vinegar to keep the color white.
3. Wash them and cook them until tender in abundant boiling water with milk and salt (30 minutes).
4. Let them drain in a colander and stew them for 5 minutes in the sauce of butter or margarine, flour and milk prepared in the same way as cauliflower sauce (see recipe 328) or cornstarch sauce (see recipe 329).
5. Grate some nutmeg over them in the bowl.
6. Alternatively, clean the salsify by cooking them unpeeled for a few minutes, pouring cold water over them and rubbing off the black skin.

title: Schuimige Aardbeienpudding (Fluffy Strawberry Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g strawberries
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 100 to 125 g brown sugar
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 17 1/2 g gelatine with 1/2 sheet red gelatine, or 12 1/2 g powdered gelatine

## Directions

1. Pick the strawberries, wash them, select the finest ones to place whole in the pudding later and set them aside sprinkled with some sugar for a time.
2. Press the remaining strawberries through a horsehair sieve.
3. Soak the sheet gelatine 10 to 15 minutes in cold water.
4. Dissolve it with the sugar in a little boiling water (see instructions page 267, 5°).
5. Let this solution cool.
6. Stir it into the strawberry purée.
7. Add the sieved lemon juice.
8. Fold the mixture through the stiffly beaten cream and the stiffly beaten egg whites.
9. Mix with the reserved strawberries.
10. Stir the mass from time to time.
11. When the mixture becomes jelly-like, pour it into a pudding mold brushed with unbeaten egg white.
12. Let the pudding become cold and firm, turn it out onto a damp plate and serve without sauce, but garnish the pudding if desired with Rhine wine jelly and some strawberries.

title: Schuimige Aardbeienpudding (Fluffy Strawberry Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g strawberries
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 100 to 125 g brown sugar
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 17.5 g gelatin with 1/2 leaf red, or 12.5 g powdered gelatin

## Directions

1. Pick the strawberries, wash them, select the prettiest ones to put whole in the pudding later and set them aside dusted with some sugar.
2. Rub the rest through a horsehair sieve.
3. Soak the leaf gelatin 10 to 15 minutes in cold water.
4. Dissolve it with the sugar in a little boiling water.
5. Let this solution cool.
6. Stir it through the strawberry puree.
7. Add the sifted lemon juice.
8. Stir the mixture through the stiffly beaten cream and stiffly beaten egg whites.
9. Mix it with the reserved strawberries.
10. Stir the mass occasionally.

title: Schuimige Frambozenpudding (Fluffy Raspberry Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g raspberries
- juice of 1 lemon
- 100 to 125 g brown sugar
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 17 1/2 g gelatine with 1 sheet red gelatine (9 sheets), or 12 1/2 g powdered gelatine

## Directions

1. Prepare the pudding as the fluffy strawberry pudding from the previous recipe.

title: Schuimige Vanillepudding (Fluffy Vanilla Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl milk
- 2 eggs
- 100 g sugar
- 2 1/2 dl whipped cream
- 1 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 14 g gelatine or 12 g powdered gelatine

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks vigorously with a spoonful of sugar.
2. Rinse the pudding mold with unbeaten egg white and set it upside down to let the excess egg white run out and keep the mold from drying out.
3. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the split vanilla stick and the remaining sugar, pour it, stirring vigorously, into the beaten yolks, put everything back in the pan and let the mixture, beating with a whisk, be bound over the fire (but especially do not boil).
4. Dissolve the sheet gelatine, after soaking 10 to 15 minutes in cold water, in the hot liquid, which has been taken from the fire.
5. Remove the vanilla.
6. Let the mass cool briefly.
7. Beat the cream and the egg whites stiff, pour in the pudding mixture while stirring and let it stand, stirring from time to time, until it begins to become jelly-like.
8. Then pour it into the pudding mold and let the pudding become cold and firm.

title: Schuimkop (Meringue Topping)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 200 g sugar
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, quickly mix it with vanilla and approximately 175 g sugar and immediately spread it over a dish.
2. Sprinkle the remaining sugar, which has been finely crushed or ground in a mortar, over it through a sieve and let the dish become dry and crisp in a very lukewarm oven (approximately 1 hour), but ensure it stays as light in color as possible.
3. Preferably leave the oven door open.

title: Schuimomelet (Soufflé Omelet)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 35 g sugar
- 35 g butter or margarine
- grated lemon zest or vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the omelet as described in recipe 389, but plan to carry out all operations as quickly as possible one after another, since the omelet, prepared without flour, is much more likely to collapse than the one to which flour has been added.

title: Schuimomelet met meel (Soufflé Omelet with Flour)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 20 g flour
- 20 g sugar
- 25 g butter or margarine
- grated lemon zest or vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar and flavoring until a thick, white and airy mixture is obtained.
2. Mix with the sifted flour.
3. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible.
4. Add the remaining mass and fold in with a few quick strokes.
5. Ensure that in the meantime the butter or margarine has melted in the skillet, but especially not browned.
6. Pour the mixture in immediately, slide the pan back and forth on gentle heat during cooking and allow the omelet to become light brown on the bottom.
7. Place the pan in the oven until the omelet has dried out and is cooked (3 to 4 minutes) or cover the pan with a lid.
8. Slide it half onto a warmed dish and fold the other half over.
9. Optionally sprinkle with sifted powdered sugar and serve the omelet immediately.

title: Schuimpjes (Meringue Sticks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 240 g sifted sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, quickly mix it with the sifted sugar and some vanilla.
2. Put it in a piping bag with a smooth nozzle and pipe oblong strips of 8 cm length at some distance from each other onto a greased and flour-dusted baking sheet.
3. Sprinkle a little sugar over it and let the meringues in an open very lukewarm oven slowly dry and harden (1 1/2 to 2 hours), but see to it that they remain light in color.

title: Schuimsaus (Foam Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 3 dl fruit juice (berry juice or berry and raspberry juice or lemon juice with water)
- 80 g bastard sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar until they become frothy (10 minutes).
2. Then add the fruit juice drop by drop, continuing to beat.
3. Place the sauce on a gentle fire or in a bain-marie and let it be bound with vigorous beating, but do not boil, as otherwise there is a great risk of curdling.
4. Present the sauce with warm bread pudding, semolina pudding etc.

title: Schulpen (Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces wheat flour
- 3 eggs
- butter
- bread sugar

## Directions

1. Grease molds with butter
2. Make a not-too-thick batter of 2.5 ounces wheat flour, 3 eggs, butter and bread sugar
3. Pour into molds
4. Bake in a tart pan until done

title: Scones
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g self-rising flour
- 50 g sultana raisins
- 50 g sugar
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- 1.5 dl lukewarm milk
- 7.5 g salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a bowl; put the butter or margarine in it and cut the butter or margarine fine into the flour.
2. Add the sugar, the washed and then dried raisins, the beaten egg, the lukewarm milk, the salt, until a firm dough is obtained.
3. Roll this out on a flour-dusted table to a sheet of about 1 cm thickness.
4. Divide this into fairly large circles for large scones, into small circles for smaller ones.
5. Cut a cross in the large sheets with scissors.
6. Place the sheets of dough on a greased baking sheet.
7. Brush them with milk, sprinkle some sifted powdered sugar over them and bake them in a hot oven until done (about 15 minutes).
8. Cut them in half while warm and spread them with butter.
9. Place the halves on top of each other and serve them warm.

title: Selderij Salade (Celery Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- sour sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Split the celery or cut it as preferred.
2. Toss it with salt, oil, and vinegar.
3. It can also be eaten with sour sauce.

title: Selderijknol met Mayonnaise (Celeriac with Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 celeriac
- 3 tablespoons mayonnaise
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the celeriac into very thin slices, peel them and cut them into long, thin strips.
2. Wash them and set them aside for some time sprinkled with salt and sprinkled with some vinegar.
3. Let them drain afterwards, mix them with some mayonnaise and place them on the plate.
4. Sprinkle some very finely chopped parsley over them.

title: Selderijsla (Celery Salad) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large celery root
- 8 cooked potatoes
- 1 small tin of tomato purée
- 2 eggs
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the celery in the way as indicated for 'braised celery' (recipe 452), but ensure it does not become too soft. Let it drain in a colander and mix it with the potatoes, which are cut into slices.
2. Cook the eggs hard, peel them and chop the white and yolk separately into fine pieces.
3. Mix half of the tomato purée with some oil, vinegar, pepper and salt and stir this sauce as lightly as possible through the celery and potatoes.
4. Garnish the salad with the remaining tomato purée, egg white and yolk and some finely chopped parsley.

title: Selderijsla (Celery Salad) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 celery roots
- 3 tablespoons salad oil
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- optionally a dash of Maggi's Aroma

## Directions

1. Cook the celery until done in the way as indicated for 'braised celery' (recipe 452). Let it drain in a colander and prepare the salad as described in recipe 479.

title: Sesaté Koewah (Indonesian Pork Satay)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g pork cut into cubes
- 3 tablespoons ground onions
- 3 garlic cloves
- 1/2 teaspoon coriander
- 1/2 teaspoon cumin
- 1 teaspoon laos
- 1 Spanish pepper
- 1 teaspoon trassi
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon assem water
- 2 teaspoons fine turmeric
- 1 cup milk or thick coconut milk
- 1 daon djeroek poeroet
- Butter, salad oil, beef fat or frying fat

## Directions

1. Pound the spices, without daon djeroek poeroet, with the trassi and the finely ground onions; mix them with the meat
2. Fry it in one of the aforementioned fats
3. Mix it with assem water and thick coconut milk or milk with some salt
4. Add daon djeroek poeroet and let the meat stew until cooked
5. Ensure that the sauce is thickened
6. Serve the meat with the jus in a dish

title: Sesaté Koewah (Meat Skewers)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g pork cut into cubes
- 3 tablespoons ground onions
- 3 garlic cloves
- ½ teaspoon coriander
- ½ teaspoon cumin
- 1 teaspoon laos
- 1 chili pepper
- 1 teaspoon trassi
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon brown sugar
- 1 spoon assem water
- 2 teaspoons fine turmeric
- 1 cup milk or thick coconut milk
- 1 citrus leaf
- butter, salad oil, beef fat or frying fat

## Directions

1. Pound the spices, except for the citrus leaf, with the trassi and finely ground onions
2. Mix them with the meat
3. Fry in

title: Shallot or Onion Sauce (Chalotte- of Uiesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shallots or onions
- Wine or vinegar
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Chop the shallots or onions finely
2. Add wine or vinegar, pepper, nutmeg, and cinnamon
3. Let it cook together until thick

title: Sinaasappel- en bananenvla (Orange and Banana Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oranges
- 4 bananas
- 60 g sugar
- 1/2 l vanilla custard

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, carefully remove the white pith, cut them crosswise into slices and remove the pits. Peel the bananas, cut these also into little slices and layer the fruits with the sugar in a custard dish. Prepare vanilla custard in the usual way (recipe 657). Remove the vanilla from it, let the custard cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming, and pour it over the fruits.

title: Sinaasappel-ijs (Orange Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 5 oranges
- juice of 2 lemons
- mixed with as much water until 1/2 liter liquid is obtained
- 150 g sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 1.5 dl cream
- a little carmine
- grated peel of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix the orange juice with the lemon juice, the grated lemon peel and as much water until 1/2 liter liquid is obtained.
2. Dissolve the sugar in it and stir this liquid after cooling to the egg yolks, which have been beaten with the cream.
3. Add as much carmine as needed to obtain a nice red color and let the mixture freeze in the usual manner.

title: Sinaasappelcompôte (Orange Compote)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- juice of 1 orange and 1 lemon
- juice of 2 mandarins
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel 6 oranges, carefully remove all white fibers.
2. Cut them crosswise into slices and remove the pits.
3. Lay them layer by layer sprinkled with sugar in a compote dish, pour the juice of the remaining orange and mandarins over it and let the compote stand covered until all sugar is dissolved.
4. Moisten it from time to time with the liquid.

title: Sinaasappelcrème (Orange Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l milk
- 4 oranges (+ 2 dl juice)
- 2 eggs
- 75 g sugar
- 25 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks smooth with the sugar and the maizena. Add the grated peel of one orange and the milk and bring the custard, stirring well, to the boil. Let it cook through, stirring, for 3 to 4 minutes, mix it with orange juice and the stiffly beaten egg white or pipe this on as garnish after cooling. Serve with biscuits.

title: Sinaasappellimonade (Orange Lemonade) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg sugar
- 1/2 l water
- 50 g citric acid
- 30 g orange essence

## Directions

1. For preparation, see lemon lemonade (recipe 905).

title: Sinaasappellimonade (Orange Lemonade) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sugar
- 15 g citric acid
- 6 dl water
- juice of 6 oranges
- grated peel of 2 oranges
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 2 mandarins (optional)

## Directions

1. Brush 2 oranges clean thoroughly, grate off the peel and add this with the sugar to the water.
2. Let this mixture boil for 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and dissolve the citric acid in it and let the liquid cool down.
4. Then add the pressed and sieved fruit juice.
5. Pour the lemonade into boiled bottles, close them with boiled corks and provide them with a label with name and date.
6. Do not use artificially colored fruit for these recipes.

title: Sinaasappellimonade (Orange Lemonade), Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sugar
- 15 g citric acid
- 6 dl water
- juice of 6 oranges
- grated peel of 2 oranges
- juice of 1 lemon
- optionally: juice of 2 mandarins

## Directions

1. Brush 2 oranges clean, grate off the peel and add this with the sugar to the water.
2. Let this mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
3. Dissolve the citric acid in it off the heat and let the liquid cool.
4. Then add the squeezed and sieved fruit juice.
5. Pour the lemonade into boiled bottles and close them with boiled corks.

title: Sinaasappelpudding (Orange Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 150 g brown sugar
- juice of 4 oranges and 1 lemon (together 2 dl liquid, otherwise make up with water)
- 10 g gelatin with 1 leaf red

## Directions

1. Prepare the pudding entirely as lemon pudding in the previous recipe.
2. Fill the hollowed-out orange peels with it as shown in the illustration.

title: Sinaasappelsaus (Orange Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 4 oranges and 1 lemon, made up with water to 1/2 liter
- 20 g sago
- approximately 75 g sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the peel of 1 well-washed orange on an enamelled or glass grater
2. Bring the water with the sugar and the orange peel to a boil
3. Pour in the sago mixed with cold water and let the sauce simmer while stirring briefly
4. Add the sieved fruit juice
5. Present it with semolina or cornstarch pudding

title: Sinaasappelschoteltje (Orange Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oranges
- 100 g sugar
- ½ l milk
- 15 g Duryea cornstarch
- 2 eggs
- 30 g sugar
- ¼ vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, carefully remove all white skin and slice them across.
2. Place these, layer by layer sprinkled with the sugar, in a fireproof dish.
3. Prepare vanilla custard in the usual way, but use only the egg yolks in it.
4. Pour this over the fruit.
5. Beat the egg white as stiff as possible, mix it with a few spoonfuls of sifted powdered sugar and spread or pipe it over the dish.
6. Let it dry in a fairly warm oven, but ensure that it remains as light in color as possible (½ hour to 3 quarters of an hour).
7. Serve the dish warm or cold.

title: Sinaasappelvla (Orange Custard) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 3 oranges (1½ to 2 dl)
- 3 eggs
- Grated peel of 1 orange
- Juice of ½ lemon
- 7½ g Duryea cornstarch
- 50 to 60 g sugar

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar for a long time until they become thick, white and airy.
2. Add the grated orange peel, the cornstarch and the fruit juice, which has been strained, and let the custard cook au bain-marie while stirring vigorously for 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Remove from heat and mix with the stiffly beaten egg white and serve immediately after cooling with biscuits.

title: Sinaasappelvla (Orange Custard) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 4 oranges and 1 lemon (approximately 2½ dl)
- 8 g white gelatin or powdered gelatin
- Approximately 100 to 125 g sugar
- ¼ l whipping cream

## Directions

1. Mix the strained fruit juice with the sugar and the gelatin, which has been soaked in cold water for 10 to 15 minutes.
2. Heat until the sugar and gelatin are dissolved and let it cool.
3. Beat the cream stiff, stir the mixture through it and serve the custard after cooling with biscuits.

title: Sint Jans Uien in 't Zuur (St. John's Onions in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- St. John's onions
- wine vinegar
- mace
- white pepper
- salt
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. After carefully removing the outer skin, lay them in wine vinegar
2. Remove them and place in a wide bottle, scattering between them a layer of mace, white pepper, salt and bay leaves
3. Boil the vinegar in which they lay and pour it over them boiling, so that they are well covered

title: Siroop van Punch (Punch Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds best rum
- 2 and one-half ounces lemon juice
- 2 and one-half ounces powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Two pounds best rum, two and one-half ounces lemon juice and two and one-half ounces powdered sugar stir together until the sugar is well dissolved, and then put it in bottles, which you seal well

title: Sla in het zout (Lettuce in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 kg lettuce
- 1 or 3 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Treat the lettuce in the same manner as endive in salt (recipe 1019).

title: Sla uit het zout met aardappelen (Lettuce from Salt with Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg lettuce
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the stamppot entirely as endive from salt with potatoes (recipe 521).

title: Sla uit het zout met witte bonen en aardappelen (Lettuce from Salt with White Beans and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg lettuce
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 250 g white beans
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare the stamppot entirely as endive from salt with white beans and potatoes (recipe 522).

title: Sla van Brussels Lof (Belgian Endive Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g Belgian endive
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat the washed and very finely chopped endive as described in recipe 9 for tomato salad.

title: Slakropjes in een vuurvast schoteltje (Lettuce Heads in a Heat-Proof Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg braising lettuce
- 1 1/2 deciliters bouillon or lean gravy with water
- 25 g butter or margarine
- salt
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut away the withered leaves and a small piece of the stem.
2. Wash the heads under running water until all sand is removed and cook them in salted water until tender (approximately 1/2 hour).
3. Prepare them further as endive in recipe 427.

title: Slakropjes in het zout (Lettuce Heads in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 kg lettuce
- 1 or 3 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Treat the lettuce heads in the same manner as endive heads in salt (recipe 1020).

title: Slakropjes met gehakt (Lettuce Heads with Ground Meat)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 small lettuce heads
- 150 g ground veal
- 150 g ground pork
- 100 g old bread
- pepper, salt, nutmeg
