- 1 1/2 deciliters bouillon or lean gravy with water
- 30 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut away the withered leaves and a small piece of the stem.
2. Wash the heads under running water until all sand is removed and cook them in salted water until tender (approximately 1/2 hour).
3. Meanwhile, mix the ground meat with the soaked bread and the spices.
4. Form 8 small balls and one large ball from it and brown these in butter or margarine and cook through, or let them cook for half an hour together with the lettuce.
5. Place the heads in a heat-proof dish, fill them with the meat balls and place the large ball in the center.
6. Pour the finished gravy from the meat or (if it was cooked) some bouillon or lean gravy with water over it.
7. Sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place a few pieces of butter or margarine on top, clean the edge of the dish, and let it brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Slemp (Spiced Milk Drink)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 l milk
- 3/4 g saffron
- 7 g tea
- 8 cloves
- 8 cm cinnamon stick
- piece of mace
- 80 g sugar
- 20 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Tie the spices in a cheesecloth, put them in the milk and bring slowly to a boil.
2. Let them steep as long as needed until enough flavor comes into the milk (approximately 1/2 hour).
3. Squeeze the cloth gently between two spoons from time to time, add the sugar to the milk and let it simmer for another 3-4 minutes with the mixed maizena.
4. Remove the spices and pour the slemp into the warm milk kettle.

title: Slemp (Spiced Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet milk
- tea infusion
- saffron
- cinnamon
- cloves
- sugar
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pour a decoction of good tea, saffron, cinnamon and cloves into sweet milk, let this cook together well, add sugar to taste; to bind stir a couple of egg yolks through it
2. If one uses slemp syrup, count a good spoonful of syrup for each can of milk

title: Slier-asperges (Asparagus Bundles)
body:
## Ingredients

- bunches of asparagus
- water
- salt
- nutmeg
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the bottom ends of whole bunches of asparagus
2. Cook them in water with salt until done
3. Let them drain
4. When serving, sprinkle grated nutmeg over the heads and serve with melted butter

title: Small Pastries à la Reine (Pasteitjes à la reine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Feuilleté crust
- Oyster or chicken farce, or meat farce, warmed

## Directions

1. Make an elegant small mold from feuilleté with the hand, three fingers high
2. Make a small lid and place it inside
3. Bake in the oven
4. Fill the baked pastries with oyster or chicken farce or meat farce that has been warmed beforehand
5. Place the lid on top

title: Smeerbollen (Grease Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- Same as oliekoeken
- Half lard and half butter

## Directions

1. Make batter as for oliekoeken
2. Fry in half lard and half butter

title: Smeerbollen (Smeared Fried Dough Balls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g flour
- 30 g yeast
- approximately 4.5 dl warm milk
- 150 g currants, raisins and candied citrus peel
- 15 g snippets
- salt
- some lemon juice and grated lemon zest
- 3 sour apples
- lard

## Directions

1. For the preparation see oliebollen (previous recipe).
2. Fry the balls, however, in hot lard until brown and cooked through, let them drain thoroughly on gray paper and dust them with powdered sugar.

title: Smoddervisch van Baars, Bot of Paling (Smoddervisch of Bass, Flounder or Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds fish (bass, flounder or eel, one or two kinds or all three mixed)
- 4 large handfuls cooked onions
- nutmeg
- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook 3 pounds fish nearly done in a tinned kettle
2. Pour off the water cleanly without removing the fish
3. Sprinkle over the fish 4 large handfuls of cooked onions cut into small pieces
4. Add grated nutmeg and a little parsley finely chopped, and a quantity of butter
5. Hang the kettle with all this over the fire and shake until the butter is melted
6. Pour into the dish without being concerned about the order it lies in, as the fish may well break

title: Smoddervisch van baars, bot of paling (Smothered Fish of Perch, Flounder or Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds fish
- 4 large handfuls cooked onions, cut into small pieces
- grated nutmeg
- parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- peeled shrimp (as noted in the NB)

## Directions

1. Cook 3 pounds fish until nearly done in a tinned kettle
2. Pour off the water cleanly without removing the fish
3. Scatter 4 large handfuls of cooked onions cut into small pieces over the fish
4. Add grated nutmeg, a little finely chopped parsley, and a quantity of butter
5. Hang the kettle with all of this over the fire and shake until the butter is melted
6. Pour into the serving dish without worrying about the arrangement, as the fish may break
7. Add peeled shrimp when serving the fish

title: Sneedjes Brood voor Versterking van Zieken (Bread Slices for the Strengthening of the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- small or thin slice of bread
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- fine salt

## Directions

1. Spread a small or thin slice of bread with Liebig's Meat Extract and sprinkle with fine salt.
2. This is a pleasant and light food that proves to be of excellent service for both suffering and convalescent patients.

title: Sneeuwballen (Snowballs) (Makes ~40)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl water
- some salt
- 250 g flour
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g currants
- 7 large or 8 small eggs
- powdered sugar
- lard oil or frying fat for frying

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the butter or margarine and some salt to a boil.
2. Stir in the sifted flour and keep stirring until the dough releases from the pan as a ball.
3. Remove immediately from heat and stir in the whole eggs one by one.
4. Finally mix the dough with a few quick strokes with the well-washed and dried currants.
5. In the meantime, heat frying fat in a deep iron pot.
6. Place it on a lower heat, let it cool a bit and add small balls of dough.
7. Let the snowballs rise and bake light brown and cooked (7 minutes), remove them with a frying spoon from the fat, let them drain well on porous paper and sprinkle them with powdered sugar after cooling.

title: Sneeuwballon (Snow Ball)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound flour
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 12 eggs (6 with whites, 6 without whites)
- unsalted butter

## Directions

1. Cook a cup of sweet milk, then sprinkle in ½ pound flour.
2. Stir until done, so that it no longer sticks to the knife.
3. Add 12 eggs, half with whites and half without.
4. Beat until smooth and fry in unsalted butter.

title: Sneeuwpudding (Snow Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 1 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- 90 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 80 g sugar
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook stiff cornstarch porridge in the usual way, but let the vanilla cook in it and add the sugar, mix the porridge off the heat with the very stiffly beaten egg white.
2. Pour the mixture into a mold rinsed with water, let it cool.
3. Turn the pudding out onto a wet plate and serve with pitted cherries or fruit juice sauce.

title: Sneeuwpudding (Snow Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter milk
- 1 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- 90 g Duryea cornstarch
- 80 g sugar
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook stiff cornstarch paste in the usual manner, but let the vanilla steep in it and add the sugar
2. Mix the paste off the fire with the very stiffly beaten egg white
3. Pour the mass into a mold rinsed with water, let it cool
4. Turn the pudding out onto a damp dish and serve with pitted cherries or fruit sauce
5. Note: Prepare this pudding only a few hours before use, as the egg white will separate with long standing

title: Snijbonen (Cut Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cut beans
- 30 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 10 g sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Trim the beans on both sides twice, wash them and cut them with a bean cutter, ordinary knife or bean cutting mill into long, thin strips.
2. Set them on with enough boiling water to just cover the bottom of the pan and add salt.
3. Let them cook tender and dry, shaking occasionally (1/2 hour to 3/4 hour).
4. Add some water if necessary during cooking to prevent burning.
5. Shake them with the butter or margarine, add sugar if the beans are not sweet enough.

title: Snijbonen in Flessen (String Beans in Bottles)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 kg string beans
- salt (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the string beans on both sides 2 times, wash them, cut them with a string bean knife or grinder into thin strips.
2. Cook them for several minutes in boiling water.
3. Let them drain.
4. Pack them firmly into the well-cleaned bottles with the designated masher up to 4 cm below the rim.
5. Pour enough of the cooking liquid until the beans are just covered.
6. Add salt if desired.
7. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize them for 1½ hours.
8. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.

title: Snijbonen in het zout (Green Beans in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 kg green beans
- 1½ or 4 kg salt
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Remove the ends of the green beans twice on each side, wash them, dry them with a clean cloth.
2. Cut them with a green bean knife or grind them through a green bean mill.
3. Blanch them for 5 minutes in boiling water and let them cool spread out.
4. Press them firmly layer by layer with the salt in a well-cleaned Cologne pot.
5. Make sure the last layer is salt.
6. Cover them with a clean cloth, place a board and a stone on top.
7. Store them as indicated in the general instructions.
8. If using 4 kg salt, seal the pot with a board or damp parchment paper when in use.

title: Snijbonen uit het zout met aardappelen en rookworst (Cut Beans from Salt with Potatoes and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cut beans
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the cut beans as indicated for salted vegetables.
2. Let them drain in a colander.
3. Peel the potatoes, cut them into quarters, wash them and place them in the bottom of the pan.
4. Add enough boiling water so they are about half submerged.
5. Add the cut beans, salt if needed, and the washed smoked sausage, and let everything cook together on low heat until done and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
6. Add more water if needed, but ensure the stamppot does not become too moist.
7. Remove the sausage and quickly mash everything together with a wooden spoon or masher.

title: Snijbonen uit het zout met witte bonen en rookworst (Cut Beans from Salt with White Beans and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cut beans
- 400 g large white beans
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the cut beans as indicated for salted vegetables.
2. Cook the beans in the usual way (recipe 493).
3. If desired, add the washed smoked sausage half an hour before serving, or prepare it separately in boiling water.
4. Mix the cut beans and white beans together and serve the sausage on the side.

title: Snijbonen uit het zout met witte bonen, aardappelen en rookworst (Cut Beans from Salt with White Beans, Potatoes and Smoked Sausage)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg cut beans
- 1½ kg potatoes
- 250 g white beans
- 400 g smoked sausage
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the cut beans as indicated for salted vegetables and let them drain in a colander.
2. Cook the soaked white beans in the usual way (recipe 493).
3. Peel the potatoes, cut them into quarters, wash them and place them in the bottom of the pan.
4. Add enough boiling water or bean broth so they are about half submerged.
5. Add the cut beans, white beans and washed smoked sausage, and let everything cook together on low heat until done and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
6. Add more water if needed, but ensure the stamppot does not become too moist.
7. Remove the sausage and quickly mash everything together with a wooden spoon or masher.

title: Snijboonen (Pickled String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Brush beans clean and mince by hand or with a mill
2. Line pot or barrel with a pillowcase
3. Place minced beans inside with plenty of salt
4. Place a cloth and fitted board with heavy weight on top
5. Every 14 days, clean the cloth and stone

title: Snijboonen (String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- a little water
- a small piece of butter
- a little salt
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Set the beans with a little water on the heat
2. Add a small piece of butter, a little salt, and a little sugar
3. Shake continuously
4. They require two hours to be ready

title: Snijboonen (String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- water
- butter
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Set them on with a little water
2. Add a piece of butter, some salt and a little sugar
3. They require two hours to be ready
4. Must be shaken constantly

title: Snippen met den Drek (Snipes with the Giblets)
body:
## Ingredients

- snipes
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash them clean but do not gut them
2. Rub them with salt and pepper
3. Rolling them in butter, let them braise well brown in a pan with fire above and below

title: Snoek (Pike)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pike
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the pike, rinse it
2. Cook it in boiling water with salt until the fin comes off (approximately 10 minutes)
3. Remove it with the fish slice or 2 skimming spoons from the liquid
4. Serve with egg sauce or melted butter

title: Snoekbaars (Pike-perch)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pike-perch
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the pike-perch, which has much fewer bones than pike, as described for pike
2. Pike-perch is often supplied filleted

title: Snoeksoep (Pike Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- grated bread
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- eggs
- hardtask
- butter
- veal bouillon
- mace

## Directions

1. Cook the pike done and let it cool a little
2. Remove the bones but keep some pieces of the back whole
3. Make a stiff dough from grated bread, nutmeg, pepper, salt and eggs
4. Chop the remaining fish finely through it and make balls
5. First roll them in fine hardtask, then in beaten egg yolks and then again in the hardtask
6. Fry these balls in butter and place them on a sieve to drain
7. For the soup take a sufficient amount of veal bouillon
8. Let it cook through well with some mace
9. Serve it in a tureen in which you have previously placed the balls

title: Soep van gepelde garst en aardappelen (Soup of Hulled Barley and Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium hulled barley
- water
- piece of beef fat
- bone bouillon
- celery
- leek
- potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Let medium hulled barley cook briefly with little water and a piece of beef fat, and gradually pour in bone bouillon. After one hour of cooking, add celery and leek, let everything cook well through (for 3 hours) and stir it now and then.
2. In the last hour, cook an equivalent quantity of potatoes in water and salt until well done and add them to the soup with a small amount of Liebig's Meat Extract and some pepper.

title: Soep van Runderbeenderen (Beef Bone Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef bones
- water
- salt
- rice
- vegetables
- ground mace

## Directions

1. Have the bones well crushed
2. Cook them thoroughly the day before use
3. Strain the broth through a sieve
4. When cool, remove the fat
5. Proceed as with the beef shank soup above
6. The meatballs can be removed if desired

title: Soep van Rundvleesch met groenten (Beef Soup with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef piece in proportion to number of people
- pump water
- salt
- celery
- beet
- finely ground mace

## Directions

1. Hang a piece of beef with pump water and salt to cook in proportion to the number of people
2. Let it cook for two hours, paying good attention to skimming
3. Add celery, beet and finely ground mace
4. Let it cook another hour
5. Remove the meat when serving, which is served separately

title: Soep van Rundvleesch met groenten (Beef Soup with Vegetables) - Alternative Method
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Dutch pound beef
- salt
- half ounce rice
- two bunches celery
- handful sorrel
- handful chervil
- bunch endive
- some pieces cauliflower
- head of lettuce
- handful purslane
- finely ground mace

## Directions

1. Cut 1 Dutch pound beef into pieces
2. Cook it with salt, paying good attention to skimming
3. Add half ounce rice, two bunches celery, a handful of sorrel, a handful of chervil, a bunch of endive, some pieces of cauliflower, a head of lettuce, a handful of purslane and a little finely ground mace

title: Soep van Rundvleesch met groenten (Beef Soup with Vegetables) - Third Method
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from an ox shank
- beef bones
- water
- salt
- rice

## Directions

1. Have the meat cut from an ox shank and finely chop it
2. Crush the bones well
3. Cook them the day before for 3 hours with water and salt
4. Strain the liquid through a sieve
5. When ready to use, remove the fat
6. Set the obtained liquid to cook
7. Add rice and vegetables as desired

title: Soep voor een zieke (Soup for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- finely crushed hardtack
- water
- a few slices of lemon
- salt
- sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- meat broth (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook finely crushed hardtack with water until done
2. Add a few slices of lemon, a little salt and some sugar
3. When serving, stir an egg yolk through it
4. If the soup should be strengthening and the sick person may eat meat, use meat broth instead of water

title: Soep à Maigre in den Zomer (Summer Meatless Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetables of the season
- five cent roll
- butter
- nutmeg
- mace
- salt
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. For this soup take all the vegetables the season provides
2. Cook them with a five cent roll, cut into pieces and roasted
3. Add a lump of butter with some nutmeg, mace and salt
4. When serving, you can stir through a few egg yolks to thicken it

title: Soezen (Choux Pastry Puffs) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 dl milk or water
- a little salt
- 2 large eggs
- some coarse sugar

## Directions

1. Bring the liquid with the butter or margarine and a little salt to the boil and let it come up once.
2. Stirring, add all the sifted flour at once and keep stirring until the dough comes away from the pan as a ball.
3. Remove it immediately.
4. Stir in the eggs one by one away from the heat and pipe this mass in oblong balls onto a buttered baking sheet.
5. Sprinkle them with some coarse sugar and bake the puffs in a fairly hot oven until light brown and cooked (about 15 minutes).
6. After cooling, fill them with cream custard or pastry cream (see recipe 816) or jam.
7. Note: The puffs can also be brushed with dark brown water glaze (see recipe 819).

title: Soezen (Choux Pastry Puffs) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 85 g butter or margarine
- 85 g flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 1/2 dl water
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Bring the water with the butter or margarine and a little salt to the boil.
2. Stirring, add all the sifted flour at once and keep stirring until the dough comes away from the pan as a ball.
3. Remove the pan immediately and stir in the whole eggs one by one.
4. Fill sand-tart molds greased with butter three-quarters full with this dough and bake the puffs in a moderately warm oven until light brown and cooked (20 to 30 minutes).
5. After cooling and filling, sprinkle them with powdered sugar.

title: Soezen (Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces butter
- 0.5 quart water
- 5 eggs
- 12.5 drams flour

## Directions

1. Cook 2.5 ounces butter with 0.5 quart water thoroughly while stirring constantly and vigorously
2. Let it cool somewhat
3. Beat 5 eggs until they foam well
4. Mix in 12.5 drams flour
5. Add the cooked butter and beat smooth
6. Fill molds halfway and place in a tart pan with heat only from below, uncovered, and let bake until done

title: Sorrel Sauce (Zuringsaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped sorrel
- Butter
- Crumbled white bread

## Directions

1. Add sufficient butter and crumbled white bread to chopped sorrel
2. Let it cook

title: Soubisesaus (Soubise Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l cream
- 100 g onion (1 medium-sized)
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 25 g flour
- 6 mushrooms
- 3 truffles
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Braise the shredded onion in 10 g butter or margarine until cooked (15 to 20 minutes), but ensure it remains light in color
2. Press this mixture through a horsehair sieve and blend with a sauce made from 25 g flour, 25 g butter or margarine and cream
3. Stir in the remaining butter or margarine and some pepper and salt
4. Mix in the chopped mushrooms and truffles and serve the sauce with sweetbreads

title: Sour Egg Sauce (Zure Eiersaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two parts water
- One part butter
- One spoonful wine vinegar
- Grated nutmeg
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Cook two parts water and one part butter, stirring constantly
2. Add a spoonful of wine vinegar and some grated nutmeg
3. Beat in some eggs

title: Spaansche Brij (Spanish Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet milk
- Rice flour
- Egg yolks
- Sugar
- Rose water

## Directions

1. In 4 cans of sweet milk, add 6 ounces of rice flour
2. Stir in 16 to 20 egg yolks with a little sugar and rose water
3. Cook until it is properly thickened
4. You can also reduce the quantities proportionally depending on the number of people

title: Spaansche pasteitjes (Spanish Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- breast of chicken
- bacon
- calf sweetbread
- shallots
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare as with veal pastries
2. Cook the breast of a chicken, a piece of bacon and a calf sweetbread well done
3. Chop it now with some shallots, salt and nutmeg as finely as possible and fill the pastries with it

title: Spaansche Saus (Spanish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef bouillon
- 1 bouquet of parsley, thyme and bay leaves
- 2 carrots
- 1 decigram salt
- 3 milligrams coarsely ground pepper
- 5 decigrams sugar
- Liebig's Meat Extract
- 4 hectogrammes clarified butter
- 4 hectogrammes flour

## Directions

1. Let the beef bouillon come to a boil, skim it, and add the bouquet of herbs, carrots, salt, pepper and sugar.
2. Let this cook together and add the necessary Liebig's Meat Extract.
3. Strain through cloth.
4. Make a roux from the clarified butter and flour, let it take on a brown color while stirring constantly on the fire.
5. When the roux is brown enough, moisten it with the broth described above.
6. Stir on the fire with a wooden spoon and as soon as it boils, place the pan on the corner of the stove and let it simmer gently for 2 hours, keeping the lid three-quarters closed.
7. Skim it and remove the fat twice during the process.
8. Strain through cloth and keep for use.

title: Spaanse Rijst (Spanish Rice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 225 g rice
- salt
- 120 g sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 stick vanilla or the peel of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cook rice porridge in the usual manner (see rice porridge recipe), but let the vanilla or lemon peel cook with it.
2. In a fireproof dish, stir the egg yolks smooth with half the sugar.
3. Carefully add the hot rice porridge, from which the vanilla or lemon peel has been removed and in which the remaining sugar is dissolved.
4. Stir the stiffly beaten egg white through as lightly as possible.
5. Clean the rim of the dish and let it rise and brown in a moderately warm oven.
6. Serve as soon as it comes from the oven.

title: Spaanse saus (Spanish Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl lean jus with water
- 50 g onion
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 cloves
- small carrot
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- splash of Worcestershire sauce
- 2 teaspoons sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 359.
2. Remove from heat and mix with sugar, Worcestershire sauce and soy sauce to taste.
3. Serve with beef tenderloin, lamb cutlets, etc.

title: Spaghetti met Tomatenpurée (Spaghetti with Tomato Paste)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g spaghetti
- salt
- 1/2 tin tomato paste
- 85 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Break the spaghetti into small pieces, wash it and cook it in boiling salted water for 20 minutes until tender.
2. Place the spaghetti in a colander and rinse it with cold water.
3. Warm the spaghetti with butter or margarine, tomato paste and pepper and let the mixture stew for another 10 minutes.
4. Optionally place it in a fireproof dish, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, place a few pieces of butter or margarine on top and let it brown in the oven.

title: Speculaas (Speculaas Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g flour
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 2.5 g ground cloves
- 1/2 teaspoon fine cinnamon, or 5 g speculaas spices
- some nutmeg
- one spoon milk
- 75 g dark brown sugar
- a pinch baking powder
- some rice flour

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients, with the sugar dissolved in the milk, into a ball and press it firmly into a speculaas mold dusted with rice flour.
2. Cut away any excess dough with a sharp, thin knife, carefully lift the cookies from the mold and place them on a baking sheet dusted with flour, spaced some distance apart.
3. Bake them in the middle of a fairly hot oven until light brown and done (approximately 15 minutes), remove them from the sheet and let them cool.

title: Speculaas (Spice Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g flour
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 2.5 g ground cloves
- 1/2 teaspoon fine cinnamon or 5 g speculaas spices
- some nutmeg
- one spoon milk
- 75 g dark brown sugar
- one pinch baking powder
- some rice flour

## Directions

1. Knead all ingredients, with the sugar dissolved in the milk, into a ball
2. Press it firmly into a speculaas mold sprinkled with rice flour

title: Speenvarken in het Zuur (Suckling Pig in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- one suckling pig, cut into 4 pieces and head split
- buttermilk
- water
- lemon slices
- bay leaves
- whole pepper
- nutmeg
- cooked vinegar that has cooled

## Directions

1. After cutting the suckling pig into 4 pieces and splitting the head, soak it in water for 2 days, changing the water each day
2. Cook it in half buttermilk and half water, skimming well
3. When cooled, place the pieces in an earthenware pot and place between each layer some lemon slices, some bay leaves, some whole pepper and some nutmeg
4. Pour over everything one bottle of cooked vinegar that has cooled, and enough buttermilk so that everything is submerged
5. When using the meat, take some bay leaves and lemon slices with it and let it drain well

title: Spekkoek (Layered Spice Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 170 g butter or margarine
- 170 g sugar
- 85 g flour
- 5 eggs
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons fine cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon crushed cloves
- finely crushed cardamom
- butter or margarine for greasing

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the sugar and egg yolks and stir until the sugar is well ground.
2. Add the spices and sifted flour.
3. Beat the egg white as stiffly as possible, fold in the remaining mixture.
4. Grease a cake pan with butter or margarine, spread a layer of batter as thinly as possible and bake in a moderately warm oven until light brown.
5. Brush with melted butter or margarine, spread a second layer and bake until light brown as well.
6. Continue until all the batter is baked, turn the cake out onto a cake rack and let it cool.
7. Dust with powdered sugar and cut into very thin slices.
8. Note: While baking, keep the remaining batter as cool as possible so the egg white does not collapse. If making a large cake, the spices can be stirred through one half of the dough while the other half remains white, making the separation between layers more visible. This cake can be stored for a long time in a tin.

title: Spekpannekoeken (Bacon Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g flour
- 3/4 liter lukewarm milk (optionally with water)
- 15 g yeast
- 100 g fatty smoked bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter in the manner indicated for risen pancakes (see previous recipe)
2. Rinse the bacon with lukewarm water or scrape it, cut away the rind as thinly as possible and cut the bacon into thin slices
3. Fry 3 to 4 slices of bacon in the pancake pan until the bottom of the pan is sufficiently greased with fat
4. Pour enough batter so a fairly thick pancake is formed and the pieces of bacon cannot fall out
5. Fry the pancake on both sides until light brown and cooked through
6. Serve the bacon pancakes with syrup or brown sugar
7. When much needs to be baked, it is better to first fry out all the bacon and keep it warm in a steel pan or enamel bowl, using as much bacon and bacon fat as needed for each pancake

title: Speksaus (Bacon Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter bean water
- 150 g smoked bacon
- 100 g onion (2 medium-sized)
- 3 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 25 g sago or potato flour

## Directions

1. Rinse the bacon with lukewarm water or scrape it with a knife
2. Cut it into small cubes and fry these lightly brown in the frying pan
3. Remove them from the fat and fry the chopped onions lightly brown in it
4. Add the bacon and onions to the bean water in which the spices have steeped and which has been strained
5. Thicken this with the sago or potato flour mixed with cold water
6. Serve the sauce with brown beans or capers

title: Spiced Tart (Kruidtaart)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g butter or margarine
- 125 g flour
- 125 g currants
- 125 g raisins
- 125 g dark brown sugar
- 2 teaspoons fine cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon grated nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon crushed cloves
- 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the eggs one by one and stir them in until they are completely mixed with the butter or margarine.
2. Then add the sugar and stir it as finely as possible.
3. Add the sifted flour, some salt, the spices and lastly the washed and dried currants and raisins.
4. Put the dough in a greased tart pan and bake the tart in a fairly warm oven until light brown and cooked through (approximately 1 hour).
5. Test if it is cooked by pricking it with a knitting needle, which should come out dry.
6. Turn the tart out onto a cake rack, let it cool and store it in a well-closed tin.

title: Spiegeleieren (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 eggs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Break 3 or 4 eggs onto a flat earthen plate
2. Sprinkle lightly with salt and pepper
3. Place on a not too strong fire
4. As soon as the eggs set or coagulate, it is ready
5. Loosen with a knife to transfer them

title: Spiegeleieren (Fried Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 30 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Warm the butter or margarine, but not brown, in the frying pan.
2. Carefully crack the eggshells on the rim of the pan, let the eggs slide into the butter, but make sure the yolk remains whole and in the middle.
3. Fry them on a gentle heat until the white has set, place them on a heated plate and serve them with pepper and salt.
4. Note: You can also place the eggs in a flat heat-resistant plate in a moderately warm oven until the white has set.

title: Spiegeleieren met gebakken tomaat (Fried Eggs with Fried Tomato)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 12 slices tomato (2 tomatoes)
- 35 g butter or margarine
- parsley

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs as indicated in recipe 375 and place them on a heated plate.
2. In the browned butter or margarine, quickly fry the tomato slices on both sides until light brown.
3. Arrange the fried tomato around the fried eggs.
4. Place a sprig of parsley here and there and present the fried eggs with pepper and salt.

title: Spiegeleieren met ham (Fried Eggs with Ham)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 slices ham (approximately 80 g)
- 4 eggs
- 20 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Fry the slices of ham on both sides briefly in the frying pan.
2. (If the ham is not fatty enough, the butter or margarine should be melted in the pan first.)
3. Break an egg carefully over each slice, fry them in the manner described in recipe 375, place them on a heated plate and serve them with pepper and salt.
4. Note: See note under recipe 375.

title: Spiegeleieren met kaas (Fried Eggs with Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 slices of old cheese
- 35 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Fry the slices of cheese briefly in the butter or margarine, break the eggs over it, fry them as described in recipe 375.
2. Place them on a heated plate and serve them with pepper and salt.

title: Spiegeleieren met spek (Fried Eggs with Bacon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 60 g smoked bacon

## Directions

1. Fry the bacon, cut into thin slices, on both sides in the frying pan until the bottom has enough fat.
2. Then carefully break the eggs over it, fry them as described in recipe 375.
3. Serve them as fried eggs with ham (recipe 376).
4. Note: See note under recipe 375.

title: Spiegeleieren met uien en kaas (Fried Eggs with Onions and Cheese)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 50 g old Gruyère, Parmesan or Gouda cheese
- 50 g onion (1 medium-sized onion)

## Directions

1. Make the butter or margarine light brown in a shallow heat-resistant plate, add the finely chopped onion and fry it light brown.
2. Sprinkle half of the grated cheese over it, carefully break the eggs on it, but make sure the yolks remain whole.
3. Sprinkle the other half of the cheese over it and let the plate stand in a moderately warm oven until the white has set.

title: Spiegeleieren op brood (Fried Eggs on Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 thin slices of old bread without crust
- 60 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Fry in the frying pan, in which the butter or margarine has been made light brown, the slices of bread, which have been trimmed of crust and cut evenly, on both sides until light brown.
2. Break an egg carefully on the warm side of each slice, fry them as described in recipe 375.
3. Place them on a heated plate and present them with pepper and salt.

title: Spiering (Fried Smelt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 smelt
- 1.5 dl butter oil or salad oil
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the smelt by cutting off the head and removing the innards
2. Check carefully that there are no smelt with worms
3. Wash them and coat them in flour mixed with salt
4. Lay the smelt with heads toward the outside and tails toward the middle like a cake in oil that is hot enough to produce blue steam
5. Fry the cake, shaking it occasionally, until light brown and cooked through
6. Turn the cake over using the help of a lid
7. Let the smelt drain on porous paper

title: Spinazie (jonge) (Young Spinach)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg spinach
- salt
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 8 slices of bread
- 20 g butter or margarine
- breadcrumbs (optional)

## Directions

1. Sort through the spinach, carefully removing weeds and seeds.
2. Place it in plenty of cold water for some time to let the sand settle.
3. Carefully remove it and wash it as many times as necessary until completely clear water comes off.
4. Let it cook until tender with as little water as possible, stirring occasionally (approximately 15 minutes).
5. Let it drain in a colander and chop it as finely as possible.
6. Braise it with butter or margarine and salt.
7. If much liquid still comes out during braising, add some breadcrumbs.
8. Place it in a dish and garnish it with pieces of hard-boiled egg and slices of bread fried light brown in butter or margarine.

title: Spinazie (Spinach)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 kg spinach
- salt

## Directions

1. Sort through the spinach, wash it until the water runs completely clear and let it cook with the adhering water (without lid) until done (10 to 15 minutes).
2. Let it drain well in a colander.
3. Chop it as finely as possible and fill well-cleaned bottles with it up to 2 cm below the rim.
4. Add salt if desired.
5. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize for 1½ hours.
6. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.

title: Spinazie (Spinach)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- butter or margarine
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs
- bread

## Directions

1. Strip the spinach, carefully remove the seeds, place them for some time in abundant cold water to let the sand settle.
2. Carefully scoop them out and wash them as many times as necessary until the water runs completely clear.
3. Let them cook until tender, stirring occasionally (approximately 20 minutes) with as little water as possible.
4. Let them drain in a colander, chop them as finely as possible.
5. Stew them for approximately 5 minutes with butter or margarine and salt.
6. Place them in a bowl and garnish them with pieces of hard-boiled egg and strips of bread fried light brown in butter or margarine.

title: Spinazie (winter) (Winter Spinach)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg spinach
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 8 slices of bread
- 20 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Strip the spinach, carefully remove the seeds, place it in plenty of cold water for some time to let the sand settle.
2. Carefully remove it and wash it as many times as necessary until completely clear water comes off.
3. Let it cook until tender with as little water as possible, stirring occasionally (approximately 20 minutes).
4. Let it drain in a colander and chop it as finely as possible.
5. Braise it for approximately 5 minutes with butter or margarine and salt.
6. Place it in a dish and garnish it with pieces of hard-boiled egg and slices of bread fried light brown in butter or margarine.

title: Spinazie met Roomsaus en Gevulde Eieren (Spinach with Cream Sauce and Stuffed Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- stewed spinach
- cream sauce
- stuffed eggs
- potato purée

## Directions

1. Make a flavorful stewed spinach and place it in the bottom of an ovenproof dish (preferably glass).
2. Pour over it a layer (at least 1 cm thick) of cream sauce.
3. Make this dish in the oven or on the stove when it is to be used.
4. Prepare stuffed eggs beforehand, 2 halves per person.
5. When serving, place the stuffed eggs in the cream sauce and serve potato purée (which was heated in the oven at the same time) with this dish.

title: Spinaziesla (Spinach Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g spinach
- 4 carrots
- 1 onion
- 1 tomato
- ½ tablespoon parsley
- 2 tablespoons salad oil
- a bit of lemon juice
- 1 tomato

## Directions

1. Clean the vegetables. Chop the spinach, grate the carrots and the cleaned onion. Cut the tomato into slices. For the salad dressing, dip the tomato in hot water, peel it, press it out. Mix the oil and lemon juice through it. Stir the vegetable mixture lightly through the sauce. Garnish the dish with a single tomato slice and sprinkle with the chopped parsley.

title: Spinaziesoep (Spinach Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g spinach
- 1 l water or bouillon
- 35 g butter or margarine
- 35 g flour
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Wash the spinach well and let it cook until done with little water. Rub it through a sieve and dilute it with water and salt or bouillon.
2. Melt butter or margarine, bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour, add the liquid in small amounts at a time while stirring.
3. Let the soup cook approximately 10 minutes and finish it with some milk.

title: Sprits (Spritz Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- 250 g butter or margarine
- 125 g brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the egg, sugar, salt, grated lemon zest, fine cinnamon and gradually the sifted flour with the baking powder.
2. Pipe this mixture with a nozzle (one straight and one fluted side) onto a greased baking sheet in long strips in the shape of an S.
3. Bake the sprits in a hot oven until light brown and done (+ 15 minutes). Remove them immediately from the baking sheet and divide them into pieces.

title: Spritsen (Spritz Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 drams butter
- Water
- 6 drams wheat flour
- 3 eggs with white
- 3 eggs without white
- Boiling butter for frying
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook butter in water
2. Stir in flour well until done
3. When slightly cooled, add 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks
4. Stir until it is a dough
5. Press through a pastry bag into boiling butter that has been well skimmed
6. Fry well until brown
7. When serving warm, sprinkle with sugar

title: Spruitjes (Brussels Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- butter
- bouillon
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- chestnuts (optional)

## Directions

1. Treat in all respects as boerenkool, but do not chop finely
2. Some add peeled chestnuts

title: Spruitjes (Brussels Sprouts)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1 kg Brussels sprouts
- salt
- 35 g butter or margarine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean the Brussels sprouts by cutting off the withered leaves and a small piece of the stem.
2. Place them for some time in abundant cold water to let the sand settle, then carefully scoop them out and wash them as many times as necessary until the water runs completely clear.
3. Let them cook until tender in abundant boiling water with salt (without a lid) (20-25 minutes).
4. Let them drain in a colander as dry as possible and stew them with the butter or margarine for 5 minutes.
5. Grate some nutmeg over them in the bowl.

title: Spruitjes met kastanjes (Brussels Sprouts with Chestnuts)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Brussels sprouts
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 500 g chestnuts
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the Brussels sprouts in the manner as described in the previous recipe.
2. Make a deep cross on the chestnuts on the rounded side, wash them and let them cook until tender in enough boiling water to just cover them (30 minutes to 45 minutes).
3. Drain them, let them dry briefly, peel them and shake them with the butter or margarine through the Brussels sprouts.
4. Stew everything together for 5-7 minutes more and grate some nutmeg over them in the bowl.

title: Staartstuk (Tail Piece)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg tail piece
- 150 g beef fat, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). The roasting time is however 25 minutes per 500 g.

title: Stamppot van Zuurkool, Aardappelen en Spek (Mashed Sauerkraut, Potatoes and Bacon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sauerkraut
- 1 1/4 kg potatoes
- 400 g salted bacon
- salt as needed

## Directions

1. Scrape the bacon or wash it with warm water.
2. Cook it with approximately 1/2 litre of water for 1/2 hour.
3. Wash the sauerkraut and lay it on the bacon.
4. Let the cabbage cook for 1/2 hour and then add the peeled and washed potatoes.
5. Make sure the potatoes are at the bottom of the pan, covered by the sauerkraut.
6. Let everything cook until tender.
7. Remove the bacon from the pan, mash or stir the cabbage and potatoes together and season to taste.
8. Serve the dish with the bacon sliced separately.

title: Stekelvarken (Hedgehog Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l milk
- 20 g Duryea maizena
- 50 g sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 stick vanilla or vanilla sugar
- 75 g almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the maizena with the egg yolks and most of the sugar. Add enough of the cold milk to obtain a fairly thin paste. Bring the remaining milk with the vanilla slowly to the boil, pour in enough of the maizena so that the yolks are well warmed. Return everything to the pan and let the custard, stirring well with an egg beater, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes. Let it cool, stirring occasionally, and pour it into a custard dish. Remove the vanilla. Soak the almonds for some time in boiling water, peel them and cut them lengthwise in half. Beat the egg white stiff, stir in some sugar and garnish the custard with it. Insert the almonds into it.

title: Stekelvarken (Hedgehog)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter melk
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 50 g suiker
- 4 eieren
- 1/2 stokje vanille or vanillesuiker
- 75 g amandelen

## Directions

1. Mix the maizena with the egg yolks and most of the suiker.
2. Add enough cold milk to obtain a fairly thin paste.
3. Bring the remaining milk with vanilla slowly to a boil, pour enough of it to the maizena so that the yolks are well warmed.
4. Return everything to the pan and let the custard cook, stirring vigorously with an egg whisk, for 3-4 minutes.
5. Let it cool, stirring occasionally, and pour it into a custard dish.
6. Remove the vanilla.
7. Soak the almonds in boiling water for some time, peel them and slice them.

title: Stewed Rabbit (Gestoofde Konijnen)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and prepared rabbit
- water
- finely chopped shallots
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Let the cleaned and prepared rabbit soak in water
2. Cut it into pieces
3. Let them cook gently in a pan with water, finely chopped shallots, some butter, salt, pepper and nutmeg
4. When serving, drizzle lime juice over it

title: Stokvis (Stockfish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 400 g stockfish
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the fish for at least 24 hours in cold water, clean it, wash it, make not too stiff rolls of it and tie them shut with string.
2. Cook the stockfish in abundant boiling water with salt until done (+ 1 hour), but ensure that the water remains at a boil.
3. Remove the strings and serve the fish with mustard sauce, rice, potatoes and fried onions.
4. Note: One can serve anchovy sauce, small red fish and fried shrimp crackers with it (see recipe 933).

title: Stokvischpudding (Dried Cod Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure milk
- 10 eggs
- 4 ounces melted butter
- 3 ounces grated bread
- cooked dried cod, cleaned of bones and skins
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat 10 eggs and stir them with 4 ounces melted butter together
2. Add 1 measure milk
3. Add 3 ounces grated bread and cooked dried cod cleaned of bones and skins
4. Add salt and nutmeg
5. Stir everything well and put it in a mold
6. Cook it for half an hour
7. Serve with an anchovy sauce

title: Strevels (Strevels)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 5 tablespoons wheat flour
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- cold water
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 5 eggs, 5 tablespoons wheat flour and 1 tablespoon sugar with cold water into a thin batter
2. Pour through a funnel into boiling butter
3. Let it brown in the butter

title: Stroopballetjes (Syrup Balls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ l syrup
- 9 spoons sugar
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and butter in the heated syrup and cook everything together until it reaches the thickness of a breast candy syrup.
2. Pour the mass onto a greased stone plate.
3. As soon as it begins to stiffen, form balls by rolling the mass into a roll, pulling out a thick ribbon and cutting it into small pieces.

title: Stroopmoppen (Syrup Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g brown sugar
- 100 g syrup
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 2 tablespoons milk
- 1 teaspoon ginger powder
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 250 g flour

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, mix it with the syrup, the sugar, the milk, the ginger powder and lastly the sifted flour, into which the baking powder has been stirred.
2. Spread the dough thinly on a baking sheet greased with butter, bake it in a moderately warm oven until light brown and done (approximately 15 minutes), cut it into square pieces while still warm (in front of the open oven door).
3. Remove these immediately from the sheet and let them cool.

title: Stroopsaus (Treacle Sauce) - First Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dl milk
- 150 g treacle
- 30 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with a little cold milk into a thick paste
2. Stir out the lumps and add enough milk until a thin paste is obtained
3. Bring the remaining milk to a boil
4. Pour the mixed paste in while stirring and let the sauce simmer while stirring (approximately 10 minutes)
5. Remove from heat and mix in the butter or margarine and the treacle
6. Serve with, among other things, kettle cake

title: Stroopsaus (Treacle Sauce) - Second Recipe
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g treacle
- 100 g butter or margarine (possibly more)

## Directions

1. Stir the butter or margarine and the treacle together in a pan over a gentle heat until well combined
2. Take care that the butter or margarine does not melt, which would make the sauce too thin

title: Sultane-saus (Sultana Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can bouillon
- 1 glass white wine
- 2 slices lemon without peel
- 2 cloves
- a little shallots
- 1 bay leaf
- parsley
- finely chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Mix bouillon with a glass of white wine, two slices of lemon without peel, two cloves, a little shallots, a bay leaf and parsley
2. Let it cook for an hour
3. Strain it through a sieve
4. Add a little cooked and finely chopped parsley, salt, pepper and a chopped egg yolk

title: Surinaamse Pudding (Warm Rusk Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large rusks
- 50 g flour
- 50 g raisins
- 50 g currants
- 2 large eggs
- 50 g sugar
- 35 g almonds
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- grated zest of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 dl milk

## Directions

1. Pound the rusks finely, wash the currants and the raisins, which have been removed from their stems, chop the peeled almonds.
2. Stir the butter or margarine until creamy, add the egg yolks and sugar and stir until completely finely ground.
3. Add the salt, the sifted flour, the baking powder, the currants and raisins, the almonds, the grated lemon zest, the rusks and the milk and mix this mixture as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Fill a warm pudding mold, coated with butter or margarine and sprinkled with breadcrumbs, 3/4 full and cook the pudding 2 hours in a bain-marie, but make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim.
5. Let the pudding dry slightly and serve it with foam sauce.

title: Tapioca met bessensap (Tapioca with Berry Juice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 dl berry juice
- 7 dl water
- 175 g tapioca
- 175 g sugar
- piece of cinnamon stick or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Rinse the tapioca and soak it for 3 hours in the water mixed with berry juice.
2. Bring it to a boil with the flavoring and let it cook on a gentle fire, stirring occasionally, until cooked (approximately 1 1/2 hours).
3. Remove the flavoring and serve the sugar separately or stir it through while the dish is still warm if it is to be served cold (approximately 175 g for this quantity).

title: Tapiocapap (Tapioca Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 l milk
- 90 to 120 g tapioca
- salt

## Directions

1. Rinse the tapioca and soak it for 3 hours in the milk with the salt.
2. Bring the porridge to a boil while stirring and then let it cook on a gentle fire, stirring occasionally, until cooked and thickened (approximately 1 1/2 hours).

title: Tapiocaschoteltje (Tapioca Dish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 l milk
- 120 g tapioca
- 100 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook tapioca porridge in the usual way (see previous recipe), but let the vanilla or lemon peel cook along with it.
2. Remove these when the porridge is cooked, stir half of the sugar through it and carefully add the porridge to the egg yolks, which have been stirred smooth with the remaining sugar.
3. Last of all fold in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible.
4. Finish the dish as described in recipe 602.

title: Tarbot (Turbot)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 portions of turbot or 1 whole turbot of approximately 1 kg
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the method for brill (recipe 174)
2. When cooking turbot whole, prepare as for plaice (recipe 173)
3. Cook for approximately 15 to 20 minutes

title: Tartarensaus (Tartar Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- 3½ dl salad oil
- 1½ dl vinegar
- 2 teaspoons mustard or mustard powder
- some onion juice or a very finely chopped shallot
- some Cayenne pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the sauce in the same manner as mayonnaise (recipe 367).
2. Stir in last the onion juice or the very finely chopped shallot and the Cayenne pepper.
3. Serve with cold meat or cold fish.

title: Telor Besegnek (Eggs Besegnek)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled halved eggs
- salt
- 2 tablespoons finely ground onions
- 1 1/2 spoon laos
- 1 1/2 teaspoon djienten
- 3 teaspoons ketoembar
- approximately 1 1/2 dl milk with salt or coconut milk
- butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Fry the onions, add the spices
2. Stir the mass well together
3. Add some milk with salt or coconut milk and let the sauce become bound
4. Pour it over the halved, hard-boiled eggs, which have been sprinkled with some salt

title: Telor Kerry Kling (Curried Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 hard-boiled, peeled, half-sliced eggs
- 2 tablespoons finely ground onions
- 4 sliced garlic cloves
- 2 1/2 teaspoons curry powder
- 1/2 teaspoon ground cloves
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 4 tablespoons separately fried onions
- 2 tablespoons Duryea cornstarch
- 1 1/2 dl milk with salt or coconut milk
- Bouillon
- Butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Pound the spices finely with the ground onions
2. Fry them in the butter or margarine; add the bouillon
3. Mix the cornstarch with some coconut milk or milk with salt and stir this through the bouillon
4. Let the mixture come to a boil for 3 minutes
5. Pour the sauce over the warm sliced eggs
6. Garnish the dish with the separately fried onions

title: Thee Zetten (Brewing Tea)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measuring spoon tea per person
- fresh boiling water
- sugar
- milk

## Directions

1. Rinse the teapot with hot water.
2. Add 1 measuring spoon of tea per person into the teapot.
3. Pour fresh boiling water over the tea, using only as much water as needed for the number of cups (average content of 4 cups of tea equals 1/2 liter water; 4 measuring spoons of tea equals 10 grams).
4. Let steep 3-5 minutes under the tea cozy.
5. Do not use a tea light.
6. Stir the tea briefly before pouring.
7. Add sugar and milk to taste.

title: Theekoekjes (Tea Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces flour
- 2.5 ounces sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1.5 ounces butter
- A little cinnamon
- A little grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Make a dough from the flour, sugar, eggs, butter, cinnamon, and grated lemon peel
2. Roll it out flat and use a glass to form it
3. Roll over it once more with a roller lightly and make little squares on it
4. Lay the cakes in an open tart pan with good fire underneath
5. As soon as they are done on one side, turn them over

title: Timbal Parisien (Parisian Timbale)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 65 g brown sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 5 g white gelatin
- 1/2 tin pineapple
- 8 g white gelatin or 7 g powdered gelatin
- 60 g brown sugar
- 1 dl maraschino or kirsch
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- some carmine
- 2 1/2 dl milk
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 vanilla stick
- 50 g sugar
- 2 1/2 dl cream

## Directions

1. Prepare a lemon bavarois for the pudding lining in the manner of lemon pudding (recipe 709).
2. Let this slide along the bottom and sides of a timbale mold rinsed with unbeaten egg white when it has almost stiffened, until it is completely covered.
3. Make the filling in the manner of pineapple pudding (recipe 723), but add some maraschino or kirsch and some carmine.
4. Put this mixture into the lemon bavarois when it has become fairly stiff.
5. Let the pudding cool, turn it out, and serve with sauce prepared in the manner of vanilla sauce, but which, as it is bound with egg yolks only, must not boil.

title: Timbal Pompadour (Pompadour Timbale)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l raspberry juice
- 1/4 l berry juice
- 100 g sugar
- 20 g gelatin
- carmine as needed
- 1/4 l milk
- 1/2 vanilla stick
- 150 g sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 20 g gelatin or 18 g powdered gelatin
- 1 1/2 dl maraschino
- 150 g candied fruits
- 1/4 l whipped cream
- 2 egg whites
- 1/4 l raspberry juice with some berry juice
- 60 g sugar
- 12 1/2 g sago
- maraschino or kirsch as preferred

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar and the soaked and well-squeezed gelatin in the berry and raspberry juice (do not let the mixture boil).
2. Add carmine as needed and let this mixture cool.
3. Make a lining along the bottom and sides of a timbale mold rinsed with cold water (in summer, for this purpose rotate the mold in ice), or use a so-called double mold.
4. For the filling, boil the milk with the vanilla and sugar, bind it with the egg yolks.
5. Remove the liquid from the heat and add the soaked and well-squeezed gelatin.
6. Let this mixture cool, stirring occasionally, add it to the whipped cream and stiffly beaten egg whites, to which the candied fruits have been added, and pour it into the mold when it has become gelatinous.
7. Turn out the pudding after cooling and serve with sauce prepared in the usual manner (see recipe 748) and to which maraschino or kirsch may be added as desired.

title: Timpen (Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces flour
- 3 beaten eggs
- A little grated lemon peel
- Ground cinnamon
- Pieces of sukade
- 1.5 ounces butter
- Equal weight water

## Directions

1. Stir the flour, beaten eggs, grated lemon peel, cinnamon, and sukade pieces into the butter and water, previously brought to a boil, until a dough
2. Form figures as desired and let them bake on a buttered plate

title: Tipsy-Cake
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 sponge cakes (kolombijnen)
- 75 g sugar
- 1/2 liter milk
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 15 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 1/2 deciliter cognac
- some marmalade or jam

## Directions

1. Slice the sponge cakes in half, spread them with some marmalade or jam and place them in a fireproof dish.
2. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla and sugar, pour this, stirring vigorously, to the cornstarch, which has been mixed with the egg yolks, put everything back in the pan and let the custard, stirring with an egg beater, cook through for 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Pour it over the sponge cakes after stirring the cognac in off the heat.
4. Beat the egg white stiff, stir some sugar through it and spread it over the custard.
5. Place the dish in a moderately warm oven and let the egg white dry, but make sure it remains as light in color as possible.

title: Tomaten Salade (Tomato Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- 1 onion
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- spices
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the tomatoes (if the skin does not come off easily, place them for a moment in boiling water), cut the tomatoes into thin slices.
2. Make the onion very fine, add oil, vinegar, salt and spices to it.
3. Pour this mixture over the tomatoes and let them marinate for 10 minutes.
4. Serve them in a salad bowl that is lined with a few nice yellow lettuce leaves. Sprinkle the tomatoes with some finely chopped parsley.

title: Tomaten Vliegenzwammen (Tomato Mushrooms)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 small tomatoes
- pepper
- salt
- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- lettuce
- thick mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut the washed tomatoes in half and carefully hollow them out.
2. Sprinkle them with some pepper and salt.
3. Peel the eggs and cut off a small cap on both sides so they can stand firmly.
4. Fill them if desired (recipe 29).
5. Place a tomato half on each egg and cover them here and there with thick mayonnaise (recipe 368) or very small pieces of the remaining egg white.
6. Garnish the plate with lettuce leaves and mayonnaise.

title: Tomatenbroodjes (Tomato Open Sandwiches)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 slices of casino bread (without crust)
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 to 2 tomatoes
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- mayonnaise with parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Toast the slices lightly brown on both sides.
2. Cut the washed tomato with a very sharp knife or tomato knife into thin slices.
3. Divide the egg using an egg slicer, a damp knife, or thread into slices.
4. Top each slice of bread with a tomato slice, sprinkle with a little fine salt, lay a slice of egg on top, sprinkle again with a little fine salt and cover with mayonnaise mixed with parsley.
5. Place the open sandwiches on a plate with a folded napkin and place a fresh sprig of curly parsley here and there.
6. Serve these open sandwiches as a starter or at coffee time.

title: Tomatengelei (Tomato Jelly) (8 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water
- 1 kg tomatoes
- 8 white peppercorns
- together 1.5 l liquid
- 1 small onion
- 1.5 bay leaf
- salt
- 60 g white gelatine
- 2 to 3 egg whites
- strips of hard-boiled egg white
- capers

## Directions

1. Make strained tomato juice in the known way.
2. Clear this liquid as described in the aspic recipe.
3. Place some strips of hard-boiled egg white and some capers on the bottom of timbale moulds rinsed with cold water and fill the moulds with the jelly.
4. Turn the jelly out onto a moist plate.
5. Serve it with some lettuce and mayonnaise or ravigote sauce.

title: Tomatenpurée (Tomato Purée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg tomatoes
- Salt
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Small preserving bottles or other bottles that are sealed with cork and wax

## Directions

1. Clean the tomatoes, wash them, put them on with some water, the herbs and some salt and let them cook for 20 minutes.
2. Press them through a horsehair sieve.
3. Check if the resulting purée has reached the required thickness, otherwise cook it for some more time in a pan without a lid.
4. Fill the well-cleaned bottles with it.
5. Close these with ring, lid and clip or with a boiled cork and heat the bottles for half an hour to 80° C.
6. Let the preserving bottles cool with the clips on, press the cork firmly in the other bottles and dip the neck of the corked bottles twice in melted wax after cooling.
7. Label the bottles with name and date and store them in the dark.

title: Tomatensaus (Tomato Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 deciliter bouillon or water with salt
- 500 g tomatoes or 4 tablespoons tomato purée
- 1 bay leaf
- 4 white peppercorns
- 50 g onion
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- optionally the juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Let the washed and bruised or sliced tomatoes cook in the water or bouillon with the spices (approximately 10 minutes)
2. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve, press the tomatoes through it and add it, stirring, to the butter or margarine, which has been melted and then brought to a boil with the flour and from which all lumps have been rubbed out
3. Let the sauce cook, stirring, (10 minutes), optionally add the lemon juice and serve the sauce with fried sweetbreads, cooked chicken or hard-boiled eggs
4. Note: If tomato purée is used, use 4 deciliter bouillon or water with salt in which the spices have steeped and stir the tomato purée through when the sauce is cooked

title: Tomatensla (Tomato Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large tomatoes
- 2 spoonfuls oil
- 1 1/2 to 2 spoonfuls herb vinegar
- salt
- 1 shallot
- pepper
- Maggi's Aroma
- optionally finely cut parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the tomatoes of their skin (if the skin does not come off easily, place them for a moment in boiling water), cut the tomatoes into thin slices.
2. Make the onion very fine, add oil, vinegar, salt and seasonings.
3. Pour this mixture over the tomatoes and let them marinate for 10 minutes.
4. Serve them in a salad bowl that is lined with a few nice yellow lettuce leaves.
5. Sprinkle the tomatoes with some finely cut parsley.

title: Tomatensla (Tomato Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tomatoes
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the crown from the tomatoes, wash them and cut them into the thinnest possible slices with a tomato knife.
2. Sprinkle some pepper and salt over them, pour some oil and vinegar on them and let this soak in for some time.
3. Then carefully place them on the plate without the liquid and sprinkle some very finely chopped parsley over them.

title: Tomatensoep (Tomato Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water with salt or bouillon
- 8 tomatoes or 1/2 kg small soup tomatoes
- 8 white peppercorns
- 75 g onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- (some lemon juice)

## Directions

1. Clean the tomatoes, wash them, crush them and let them cook finely in the bouillon or boiling water with the salt, peppercorns, bay leaf and chopped onion (10-15 min.)
2. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve, rub the tomatoes through as much as possible
3. Add gradually while stirring vigorously to the melted butter or margarine mixed with the flour and brought to a boil, from which the lumps have been rubbed out
4. Let the soup cook while stirring until done (+ 10 min.), add some lemon juice if necessary

title: Tompouzen (Custard Slice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or margarine
- 100 g flour
- some water
- salt
- 2.5 dl custard cream (recipe 816) for filling

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846). Roll it out one last time into an oblong, thin sheet and divide it into two equal pieces. Place them on a baking sheet, make small cuts here and there to prevent them from rising too much. Bake them in a hot oven until light brown and cooked (10-15 minutes). Remove from sheet, let cool. Brush one layer with custard cream, pour the other with boiling or egg white glaze with rum flavor and place it on the custard cream. Cut the cake into equal pieces, approximately 12 cm long and 5 cm wide.

title: Tong (Sole)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large or 4 small cooking soles
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the soles well, wash them and cook them in the boiling water with the salt, until a fin comes loose easily (+. 8 minutes).
2. Remove them with a fish slice or a couple of skimming spoons from the water and serve them with parsley sauce, butter sauce, shrimp, oyster, mussel, mousseline or béarnaise sauce.
3. Note: For cleaning sole see recipe 214. Nose sole and shard sole are cooked in the same way.

title: Tong met Italiaanse Saus (Sole à l'Italienne)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large cooking soles
- 1/4 l Italian sauce, consisting of: 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- mushrooms
- 1/2 l fish stock
- parsley and onion
- 2 egg yolks
- 60 g old Gouda cheese

## Directions

1. Make fillets of the soles, as indicated in recipe 198.
2. After rolling them, cook them in boiling water with salt, but let coarsely chopped onion and a couple of sprigs of parsley cook along with them.
3. Remove the fillets with a skimming spoon from the liquid and place them in a fireproof small dish.
4. Cover them with the sauce, which has been made in the usual way and which has been blended with the egg yolks and the mushrooms cut into small pieces.
5. Sprinkle grated cheese over it and let the small dish become light brown in a moderately warm oven.
6. Note: See recipe 199 for preparing fish stock.

title: Tong met witte wijnsaus (Sole with White Wine Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cooking soles
- 4 dl white wine
- 30 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Fillet the soles in the usual way, wash them, rub them with some salt, tie them and place them in an ovenproof dish in which half of the butter or margarine has been melted.
2. Add the wine and let the fish stew covered in a moderately warm oven for approximately 20 minutes.
3. Place them on a warmed platter.
4. Bring the remaining butter or margarine to a boil with the flour, rub out the lumps and gradually add the liquid in which the fillets stewed.
5. Let the sauce cook while stirring for 10 minutes, blend it with very finely chopped parsley and pepper and salt to taste.
6. Pour the sauce over the fish.

title: Tong met zalm en mousselinesaus (Sole à l'Impériale)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cooking soles
- 200 g fresh salmon
- salt
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 200 g butter or margarine for the sauce
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- 3 tablespoons milk
- 1/2 dl whipped cream
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare fillets of the soles in the usual way, wash them and rub them with some salt.
2. Remove the skin from the salmon, cut it into 12 strips and wash these.
3. Roll a piece of salmon in each fillet, but ensure that it protrudes equally on both sides.
4. Bind the rolls with a thread and stew them over a gentle fire in the butter or margarine until done (approximately 15-20 minutes), but ensure the butter does not turn brown.
5. Remove the threads, place the fish on a warmed platter and cover them with the mousseline sauce, which is prepared in the usual way (see recipe 356 or 357).
6. Garnish the platter with fleurons.

title: Tournedos (Small Beef Steaks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 8 small beef steaks from the tip of the loin at 50 or 75 g per piece (somewhat elongated in shape)
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 5 g salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare tournedos as chateaubriands, but allow 3-4 minutes cooking time

title: Truffelsaus (Truffle Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Butter
- Calf juice
- Red wine
- Peeled truffels cooked in Madeira wine
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry some flour brown in butter
2. Add calf juice, red wine, and peeled truffles cooked in Madeira wine
3. Add the juice of a lemon
4. Let it boil once, stirring constantly

title: Tuinbonen (Broad Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 kg broad beans
- summer savory
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Shell the broad beans, wash them and cook them in plenty of boiling water for 5 minutes.
2. Then fill well-cleaned bottles with them up to 4 cm below the rim.
3. Pour on enough cooked cold water so that the beans are just covered.
4. Add salt and summer savory if desired.
5. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize for 1½ to 2 hours.
6. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.

title: Tuinbonen (Garden Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg garden beans
- salt
- 2.5 dl milk
- 25 g flour
- 25 to 30 g butter or margarine
- bean herb
- parsley

## Directions

1. Shell the beans and wash them
2. Set them on with abundant boiling water with a splash of milk (to keep the color white) and salt
3. Let them cook quickly until done (approximately 1/2 hour) and drain them in a colander
4. Prepare the sauce in the manner indicated for cauliflower sauce and let the beans stew in it (approximately 10 minutes)
5. Wash the bean herb and parsley, cut them fine and mix them with the beans just before serving

title: Tuinbonen in het zout (Garden Beans in Salt)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 kg garden beans
- 500 g salt
- bottles with wide necks
- corks
- sealing wax

## Directions

1. Pod the garden beans, wash them and layer them with the salt in bottles with wide necks that have been boiled out for at least twenty minutes.
2. Pour cold boiled water over them until the beans are just covered.
3. Let the bottles stand for a few days to ferment.
4. Then seal them with clean, boiled corks and dip these twice in sealing wax.

title: Tuinbonen met Spek (Garden Beans with Bacon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg garden beans
- salt
- 200 g bacon
- parsley
- bean herb

## Directions

1. Cook the beans until done as indicated in the previous recipe
2. Mix them with the finely chopped bean herb and parsley
3. Stir the cooked or fried bacon cut into cubes or slices through them

title: Tuinkers Salade (Garden Cress Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- garden cress
- egg yolks from hard-boiled eggs
- oil
- sugar
- mustard
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the cress fine.
2. Add some yolks from hard-boiled eggs.
3. Toss with oil, sugar, mustard, salt, and lemon juice.

title: Tulband (Bread Dough Ring Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g flour
- salt
- 2 to 2½ dl milk
- 15 g yeast
- 50 g sugar
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- 50 g currants
- 75 g raisins
- 75 g candied peel
- 15 g candied orange peel snippets
- grated zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Place flour in a warmed bowl, make a well in the center, add yeast, half the milk warmed to lukewarm and half a teaspoon sugar
2. Stir with half the flour until a firm dough forms and the other half of the flour remains as a ring around it
3. Let rise for 15-20 minutes in a lukewarm place, covered
4. Add egg yolks, melted butter or margarine, remaining lukewarm milk, salt and sugar, stir in remaining flour and beat out lumps in the usual manner
5. Add currants and raisins (washed in lukewarm water and stems removed), chopped candied peel and snippets
6. Dry currants and raisins in a cloth first
7. Place dough in a greased tulband mold dusted with fine breadcrumbs, let rise covered in a lukewarm place (approximately 1 hour)
8. Bake in a fairly warm oven until light brown and cooked through (½ hour to ¾ hour)
9. Test for doneness by inserting a knitting needle, which should come out dry
10. Turn out onto a cooling rack, let cool and dust with powdered sugar

title: Tulband (Turban Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 and a half ounces flour
- 1 and a half ounces butter
- 5 egg yolks
- Half an ounce sugar
- Zest of one lemon, grated
- Milk
- Yeast
- Egg whites, well beaten

## Directions

1. Make a batter with flour, butter, egg yolks, sugar, lemon zest and milk
2. Add yeast and beaten egg whites
3. Bake in mold until done

title: Turennes (Spiced Almond Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g almonds
- 185 g sugar
- 5 g fine cinnamon
- grated zest of 1/4 lemon
- 1/4 grated nutmeg
- 2 1/2 g crushed cloves
- 3 egg whites
- 4 3 wafer sheets

## Directions

1. Peel the soaked almonds, grind or chop them as finely as possible, mix them with the spices and lastly as airy as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
2. Lay on square pieces of wafer sheet, which are laid on a baking sheet, each time a teaspoon of this dough, bake the cookies in a moderately warm oven light brown (20 to 30 minutes).
3. Then break the wafer sheet away along the edge of the turennes.

title: Turfjes met Bessensapsaus (Bread Slices with Berry Juice Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 slices of old bread
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 3½ dl berry juice
- 3 dl water
- 75 g sugar
- 10 g sago
- piece of cinnamon stick or lemon peel

## Directions

1. Remove the crusts from the bread slices and cut them evenly
2. Fry the bread slices on both sides quickly in the browned butter or margarine until brown
3. Prepare the berry juice sauce in the usual manner without sago (see recipe 748)
4. Soak the fried bread slices in the sauce until they become soft
5. Stack them pyramidally on a plate
6. Pour the remaining sauce, bound with sago, over them
7. Serve this dish warm or cold, or stack the crisp fried bread slices on top after sprinkling them with sugar and cinnamon and serve the berry juice sauce separately
8. Alternatively, instead of frying, the bread slices can be toasted on both sides so the dish contains less fat

title: Turkey à la Godard (Kalkoen à la Godard)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat turkey
- bouillon
- butter
- white sauce
- shrimp

## Directions

1. Take a fat turkey, clean it well
2. Cook it in bouillon and butter, as a fricandeau
3. When done, serve it pouring a white sauce with shrimp over it

title: Uiensaus (Onion Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter water with salt or fish water
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 50 g onion (1 medium-sized)
- optionally 1/2 deciliter vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the onion, rinse it, chop it and cook it in the butter or margarine (15 minutes), but make sure it stays light in color
2. Add the flour, stir out the lumps, mix in the liquid by small portions at a time, stirring continuously, and let the sauce cook, stirring, (10 minutes)
3. Serve with stockfish or white beans, in which latter case bean water is used and vinegar is added
4. Note: If vinegar is used, take 5 g more flour. A brown onion sauce is obtained by browning the flour and onions

title: Uiensoep (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- butter
- bouillon
- roasted bread
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Cut the onions in half so that they fall apart in half rings, lengthwise
2. Bake them in butter until they have an orange color
3. Add bouillon and let them cook for another half hour
4. Serve the soup with roasted bread
5. If you prefer the soup thick, add some potato starch

title: Uitgebeende varkensrib (Boneless Pork Rib)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg boneless pork rib
- 100 g lard, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see Pork Fricandeau (recipe 236)

title: Uitjes en Chalotten (Small Onions and Shallots in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- small onions
- shallots
- boiling water
- hot vinegar
- salt
- cold wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash in boiling water, drain, peel and cook in hot vinegar with salt, not too hard
2. When cold, place in small glasses and pour over with cold wine vinegar that has been boiled and skimmed beforehand, so that they are completely covered
3. Tie the bottles well

title: Uitjes in azijn (Pickled Onions)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg Utrecht or St. John's onions
- 2 bottles pickling vinegar
- 20 white peppercorns
- 1 Spanish pepper
- some bay leaves
- some mustard seed

## Directions

1. Set the onions aside sprinkled with salt for several hours, no longer than 12 hours, to prevent them from becoming soft.
2. Then peel them with a fruit knife or stainless steel potato knife, place them immediately in cold water, wash them and let them stand with salt sprinkled on them for 24 hours.
3. Let them drain as dry as possible in a colander and put them in well-cleaned bottles (or in a Cologne pot).
