4. Arrange the spices neatly between the glass and the onions and pour vinegar over them so that the onions are amply covered. Pour some salad oil on top.
5. Close the bottles or pots with damp parchment paper and paste a label with name and date on them.

title: Vanilla Sauce (Vanillesaus)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a vanilla stick
- Milk
- One pint cream
- Twelve loods sugar
- Four eggs
- Cold milk
- One tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Cut the vanilla stick into small pieces and slowly cook them out in milk
2. Meanwhile, quickly boil one pint of cream
3. Pour the vanilla milk into the cream, along with twelve loods of sugar and four eggs beaten in a little cold milk with one tablespoon of flour mixed in
4. Everything must cook well so that neither the flour nor the eggs are recognizable
5. Strain the sauce and it is ready

title: Vanille-ijs (Vanilla Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1/2 liter cream
- 1/2 liter milk
- 200 to 240 g sugar
- 1 vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Let the vanilla slowly steep in the milk.
2. Stir the eggs with the sugar until thick, white and fluffy.
3. Add the milk mixed with the cream to the egg mixture and warm the whole until thick enough to coat the wooden spoon with which it is stirred with a thin layer.
4. Let the mass cool down, stirring occasionally.
5. Remove the vanilla.
6. Fill the ice mold with this mixture and let it freeze as previously indicated.

title: Vanille-ijs (Vanilla Ice Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 0.5 l cream
- 0.5 l milk
- 200 to 240 g sugar
- 1 vanilla pod

## Directions

1. Let the vanilla pod infuse slowly in the milk.
2. Stir the eggs with the sugar for a long time until they are thick, white and fluffy.
3. Add the milk, which has been mixed with the cream, to the egg mixture and heat the whole until it is thick enough to coat the wooden spoon.

title: Vanille-room (Vanilla Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece vanilla
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 ounces sugar
- 1 can cream

## Directions

1. Cut the vanilla into small pieces
2. combine the eggs, cream and sugar together
3. blend them gently while cooking on the fire
4. when you think it is thick enough, let it cool

title: Vanillemelk (Vanilla Milk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 l milk
- 1 1/2 vanilla stick
- 80 g sugar
- 20 g Duryea maizena

## Directions

1. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla and let it steep gently for a moment.
2. Remove the vanilla, add the sugar and the mixed maizena and let the milk simmer for 3-4 minutes while stirring.

title: Vanillesaus (Vanilla Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter milk
- 1/2 vanilla stick or vanilla sugar
- 5 g Duryea cornstarch
- 2 eggs
- 30 g sugar

## Directions

1. Bring the milk slowly to a boil with the vanilla
2. Beat the eggs with the sugar, pour them slowly onto the cornstarch, always rubbing out the lumps
3. Add to this paste, stirring briskly, as much of the boiling milk until the eggs are warmed through
4. Then pour everything back into the pan and let the sauce simmer on a gentle heat, stirring with a whisk, for 3-4 minutes
5. Remove the vanilla and stir occasionally while cooling to prevent a skin from forming
6. Serve the sauce cold with chocolate pudding, lemon rice etc., warm with warm bread pudding, ginger pudding etc.

title: Vanillevla (Vanilla Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l milk
- 50 g sugar
- 1/2 vanilla pod
- 30 g cornstarch (Duryea brand)
- 1 to 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar and stir the cornstarch through
2. Bring the milk with the vanilla slowly to a boil
3. Add warm milk, stirring well, to the mixed cornstarch until the eggs are warmed
4. Then pour everything back into the pan and let the custard simmer on a not too fierce fire, stirring vigorously with a whisk, for 3 to 4 minutes
5. Remove the vanilla pod
6. Stir occasionally while cooling to prevent a skin from forming

title: Vanillevla met Aardbeien (Vanilla Custard with Strawberries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or vanillesuiker
- 250 tot 400 g aardbeien
- + 40 g suiker

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and calyces from the strawberries, wash them and let them drain as dry as possible in a sieve.
2. Set aside several strawberries, sprinkle them with some suiker.
3. Place the other fruits at the bottom of the custard dish and sprinkle them with suiker (more or less depending on the sweetness of the strawberries).
4. Prepare the vanilla custard as described in recipe 657.
5. Remove the vanilla, let it cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
6. Pour the custard over the strawberries.
7. Garnish the custard with the reserved fruits.

title: Vanillevla met Appelmoes (Vanilla Custard with Apple Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or vanillesuiker
- 500 g appelen
- + 50 g witte suiker
- 1/2 dl water

## Directions

1. Make appelmoes in the known manner as described in recipe 531.
2. Let this cool and place the sauce in a custard dish.
3. Pour the cooled custard, prepared as in recipe 657, over it.

title: Vanillevla met Biscuits (Vanilla Custard with Biscuits)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g boudoirs (+ 8 pieces)
- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or 1 zakje vanillesuiker
- wat jam of marmelade

## Directions

1. Prepare the custard as in the previous recipe.

title: Vanillevla met Bitterkoekjes (Vanilla Custard with Almond Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 bitterkoekjes (+ 100 g)
- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or 1 zakje vanillesuiker
- wat jam of marmelade

## Directions

1. Stir maizena, suiker, ei and some milk until a smooth paste.
2. Bring the milk with vanilla slowly to a boil.
3. When cooled slightly, soak the bitterkoekjes in it, ensuring they do not crumble.
4. Remove them with a skimming spoon, place them in a custard dish and coat them with some jam or marmelade.
5. Refill the milk to 1/2 liter.
6. Add hot milk, stirring vigorously, to the mixed maizena until the egg is warmed.
7. Pour everything back into the pan and let the custard cook on a not too high heat, stirring vigorously with a whisk, for 3-4 minutes.
8. Let it cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
9. Remove the vanilla and pour the custard over the bitterkoekjes.

title: Vanillevla met Frambozen (Vanilla Custard with Raspberries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or vanillesuiker
- 250 g frambozen
- 40 g suiker
- een paar droppels citroensap

## Directions

1. Sort through the raspberries, wash them and let them drain as dry as possible in a sieve.
2. Set aside several raspberries, sprinkle the others with suiker.
3. Place them at the bottom of the custard dish and sprinkle them with citroensap.
4. Prepare the custard as described in recipe 657.
5. Remove the vanilla, let it cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
6. Pour the custard over the raspberries.
7. Garnish the custard with the reserved fruits.

title: Vanillevla met Gedroogde Abrikozen (Vanilla Custard with Dried Apricots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or 1 zakje vanillesuiker
- 100 g gedroogde abrikozen
- 50 g suiker
- wat sago

## Directions

1. Cook the apricots according to recipe 541.
2. Place them at the bottom of a custard dish and let them cool.
3. Prepare the vanilla custard as described in recipe 657.
4. Remove the vanilla, let it cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
5. Pour the custard over the apricots.

title: Vanillevla met Sinaasappelen en Bananen (Vanilla Custard with Oranges and Bananas)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 liter melk
- 40 g suiker
- 20 g maizena Duryea
- 1 ei
- 1/2 stokje vanille or vanillesuiker
- 2 sinaasappelen
- 1 banaan
- + 60 g suiker

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, carefully remove all white membranes, slice them crosswise, carefully remove all seeds and layer them with the suiker and the peeled, sliced bananas in the custard dish.
2. Drizzle them from time to time with the juice.
3. Prepare the custard as described in recipe 657.
4. Remove the vanilla, let it cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin from forming.
5. Pour the custard over the fruits.

title: Varkens Rook- of Pekelworst (Smoked or Pickled Pork Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- For every 5 pounds of meat: 1 ounce salt, half ounce pepper and cloves

## Directions

1. Mince the meat not too finely with salt, pepper, and cloves
2. Stuff into medium casings
3. Use the specified proportions of seasonings per 5 pounds of meat

title: Varkensbiefstuk (Pork Steak)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pork steak
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 7 1/2 g salt
- some milk

## Directions

1. For preparation see veal steak (recipe 254).
2. Allow 15 to 20 minutes per 500 g for cooking time.
3. Finish the gravy as indicated for beef steak.

title: Varkenscarbonaden (Pork Chops)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pork chops (4 pieces)
- 100 g lard, butter or margarine
- some pepper
- 7 1/2 g salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see the previous recipe.

title: Varkenscoteletten met Robertsaus (Pork Chops with Robert Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pork chops
- 7.5 g salt
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 3 dl Robert sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the chops, dry them, rub them with salt and brown them in the butter or margarine which has been made light brown in the pan, cook until done (about 20-25 minutes)
2. Attach a paper frill around the bone of the chops, stand the chops against a base of toasted bread, mashed potato or rice and pour the Robert sauce over them prepared in the usual manner

title: Varkenscotletten met Augurkensaus (Pork Cutlets with Gherkin Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- nice cutlets
- gherkin sauce

## Directions

1. Take nice cutlets and flatten them, roast them on the grill and arrange them neatly on a plate with a sauce with gherkins on top

title: Varkensfricandeau (Pork Fricandeau)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pork fricandeau
- 75 g lard, butter or margarine
- 15 g salt

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef (recipe 227). Reckon 30 minutes per 500 g as roasting time.

title: Varkenshaasje (Pork Loin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pork loins (approximately 250 g each)
- 75 g butter or margarine
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef
2. However, calculate roasting time in total as 25 minutes, or cook them as a beefsteak

title: Varkenshaasje (Pork Tenderloin)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pork tenderloins (approximately 250 g per piece)
- 75 g butter or margarine
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. For preparation see roastbeef. However, reckon approximately 25 minutes total as roasting time or bake them as beefsteak.

title: Varkenslapjes (Pork Chops)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pork chops
- 75 g lard, butter or margarine
- some pepper
- 10 g salt

## Directions

1. Wash or scrape the chops, rub them in with pepper and salt and fry them in the hot fat, butter or margarine brown on both sides.
2. Finish the gravy with some water and stew the chops in it for another twenty minutes to half an hour.
3. Check whether the gravy has not become too fatty after stewing and finish it again if that is the case.

title: Varkensnier met Uien (Pork Kidney with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork kidney
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onions, finely chopped
- red or white wine
- flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take a pork kidney and place it in a pan with some butter, salt and pepper, parsley and a little finely chopped onions
2. Keep turning the kidney over, then remove it and make a sauce over it with red or white wine with some flour and bouillon

title: Varkensnieren (Pork Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- butter
- shallots, finely chopped
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys into slices and fry them in butter with finely chopped shallots and parsley, pepper and salt
2. By constantly shaking prevent it from burning
3. Now stir in some flour as needed and let it just boil; then quickly remove it from the fire

title: Varkenspooten (Pork Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork feet
- garlic
- parsley
- leeks
- celery
- water
- beaten eggs
- ground biscuit
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Split the feet lengthwise, roll each one individually in a cloth, which you then tie shut with twine
2. Place them in a pan with some garlic, a little parsley, leeks and celery; cover with cold water, let them cook for 5 hours, and let them almost completely cool
3. Remove the feet from the cloths and roll them in beaten eggs, ground biscuit and fried parsley
4. Roast them brown on the grill or in the frying pan

title: Varkensrib (Pork Rib)
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice pork rib
- coarse salt
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Take a nice pork rib and roast it entirely on a spit
2. Bring it to the table with coarse salt sprinkled on it and make a white mustard sauce, placing part of it on the dish

title: Varkensschijf (Pork Slice)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg pork slice
- 3 dl boiling water
- 15 g salt
- 50 g lard, butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Wash the meat, rub it in with the salt (and pepper), roll it through the boiling water and let it simmer gently until all the water has evaporated.
2. See then whether enough fat has cooked out of the meat so that it can continue to roast in its own fat. If this is not the case, add lard, beef fat, butter or margarine and roast the meat in it.
3. Reckon 30 minutes per 500 g as roasting time. Finish the jus in the usual way (see recipe 227).

title: Varkenstong (Pork Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- onions, finely cut
- vinegar
- red wine
- salt
- pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue until the skin can be easily removed, cut it into slices, and place them in a pan with finely cut onions, a splash of vinegar and red wine, salt, pepper and sugar to taste, and let it braise well for about half an hour

title: Veldsalade (Lamb's Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb's lettuce
- cooked beet, finely cut
- onions
- oil
- vinegar or warm sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the lettuce well.
2. Eat it with cooked beet cut finely, onions, oil and vinegar or warm sauce.

title: Veldsla (Corn Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g corn salad
- 1 egg
- 2 spoonfuls salad oil
- 2 spoonfuls vinegar or herb vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- optionally a splash of Maggi's Aroma
- sliced leek, onion, or chives

## Directions

1. Pick through the corn salad, wash it, let it drain as dry as possible in a colander or shake it in a salad spinner.
2. Prepare it as described in recipe 479.

title: Vermicelli met Melk (Vermicelli with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Vermicelli
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take 2 cans of milk and bring to a boil
2. Slowly add 3 ounces of vermicelli broken into pieces and sugar
3. Stir until the vermicelli is cooked

title: Vermicelli Pudding with Egg
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli pudding (see recipe 603)
- vanilla stick or lemon zest
- sugar
- egg yolks
- egg white

## Directions

1. Cook vermicelli pudding in the usual manner (see recipe 603), but cook the vanilla or lemon zest in with it.
2. When the pudding is done, mix it with half of the sugar and remove the flavoring.
3. Stir the egg yolks with the remaining sugar, carefully add the hot vermicelli pudding to it and lastly mix it as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Finish the dish as indicated in recipe 602.

title: Vermicellipap (Vermicelli Porridge)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 200 g vermicelli
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Bring the milk with salt to a boil.
2. Sprinkle in the well-rinsed and broken vermicelli while stirring, let the milk come to a boil again while stirring and then let the porridge become done and bound on a gentle fire, stirring occasionally (approximately 3 quarters of an hour).
3. To prevent the milk from curdling when cooking vermicelli, macaroni, spaghetti, or similar pastas, cook these items for some minutes in ample water first.
4. Let them drain in a colander and rinse them with some cold water.
5. Then put them in the boiling milk and stir them to prevent sticking to the bottom.
6. In this case, use 1/4 l less milk.

title: Vermicellipodding (Vermicelli Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- milk
- eggs
- currants
- raisins
- candied citrus peel
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the vermicelli in butter and milk until nearly dry
2. treat it further as bread pudding

title: Vermicellischoteltje (Vermicelli Custard)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.25 l milk
- 200 g vermicelli
- 100 g sugar
- 1 to 2 eggs
- 1/2 vanilla stick or 1/2 lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook vermicelli porridge in the usual way (see previous recipe), but let the vanilla or lemon peel cook in it.
2. When the porridge is done, mix it with half of the sugar and remove the flavoring.
3. Beat the egg yolks with the remaining sugar, carefully add the hot vermicelli porridge and lastly mix it as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Finish the dish as indicated in the semolina custard recipe.

title: Vermicellisoep (Vermicelli Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- strong veal or beef bouillon
- salt
- nutmeg
- mace
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take vermicelli according to the number of persons
2. Cook it for half an hour in strong veal or beef bouillon
3. Add salt, nutmeg and mace to taste
4. When serving, stir a few egg yolks through it

title: Vermicellisoep (Vermicelli Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon of beef or veal or 1 liter water and 4 bouillon cubes (recipe 35)
- 35 g vermicelli
- 50 g veal ground meat
- piece of mace

## Directions

1. Prepare the bouillon in the usual manner, and if desired let a piece of mace cook in it. Strain the bouillon and let the washed and broken vermicelli cook gently in it until tender (10 to 20 minutes). Or: cook the vermicelli in water until done, then add the cubes.
2. Let the meatballs prepared in the usual manner (recipe 37) cook in the soup for the last ten minutes.
3. If desired, beat the egg in the soup tureen and carefully add the soup to it while stirring vigorously.

title: Vermicellisop (Vermicelli Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams vermicelli
- 1 liter water
- 4 decigrams salt
- 1 liter bone bouillon
- Liebig's Meat Extract

## Directions

1. Cook 60 grams vermicelli in 1 liter water in which you have added 4 decigrams salt.
2. Refresh it and pour it through a colander.
3. Put the vermicelli in 1 liter boiling bone bouillon and add some Liebig's Meat Extract.
4. Stir gently with a spoon to prevent clumping.
5. Let it simmer gently for five minutes, keeping the lid of the pot three-quarters closed.
6. Remove the foam.
7. Taste whether the soup is salty enough and put it in the tureen.
8. Usually 1 liter bouillon is calculated for four persons.

title: Versche Gebraden Ham (Fresh Roasted Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- a fresh ham
- fine salt
- shallots
- pepper
- red wine
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. From a fresh ham, cut off the rind and rub it well with fine salt
2. Roll shallots in salt and pepper, lard it with them and let it stand for a few days
3. Roast it in a pan with fire underneath and above, adding a glass of red wine, half a glass of vinegar and half a glass of water
4. It should be particularly nicely browned

title: Versterkende Bouillon (Strengthening Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tenderloin
- calf's foot
- chicken
- water

## Directions

1. Take a beef tenderloin, a calf's foot and a chicken
2. Let this cook for 4 to 5 hours with twice as much water as the weight of the meat
3. Skim carefully
4. Strain through a sieve

title: Versterkende Bouillon (Strengthening Broth) - Alternative Method
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tenderloin
- calf's foot
- chicken
- water

## Directions

1. Take a beef tenderloin, a calf's foot and a chicken
2. Clean them well
3. Place them in a large milk can without salt or water
4. Bind the can closed with a bladder
5. Let the can cook for 5 hours in a large pot of water, ensuring the water stays at a continuous boil
6. Remove the bladder from the can
7. Pour the broth into molds
8. This is an extremely strengthening broth for the sick

title: Vinken (Finches)
body:
## Ingredients

- finches
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Place them on a plate with some water, salt and butter
2. Let them braise for a short hour

title: Vis in Schelpen (Fish in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g fish remains (without bones or skin)
- 2½ dl milk
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine or 18 g Duryea cornstarch
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- fine parsley
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Chop the fish, after removing the bones and skin, finely.
2. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102.
3. Mix it with the fish and the seasonings.
4. Fill the dampened shells with this mixture to about 3/8 full.
5. Finish them as indicated in recipe 102.

title: Visbeignets (Fish Fritters - First Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g cod or turbot
- 5 dl fish stock
- salt and pepper
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 2 egg yolks
- splash of cream
- breadcrumbs for coating
- 2 egg whites
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Use firm, flat fish such as turbot or cod
2. Cook the fish in the usual manner
3. Remove the skin and take the fish from the bones in large pieces
4. Make a thick sauce in the known manner but use no vinegar, using butter or margarine, flour, fish water, cream and egg yolks, ensuring the sauce is so thick that it adheres to the fish
5. Roll the fish pieces through the sauce on all sides and let them cool
6. Double coat them (breading as in recipe 116) and fry them in hot frying fat or salad oil until brown
7. Let them drain on gray paper

title: Visbeignets (Fish Fritters - Second Recipe)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- fish prepared as in first recipe
- 125 g flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 dl water
- salt
- frying fat or salad oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish as indicated in the first recipe
2. Let the pieces cool after rolling them through the sauce
3. Then roll them through a batter (pate a frire) consisting of flour, eggs, water and salt
4. Fry the beignets in frying fat or salad oil from which blue vapor rises, until brown and crispy
5. Let them drain on gray paper

title: Viscroquetjes (Fish Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g fish leftovers (without bones or skin)
- 1 1/2 dl milk, cream, or half milk, half cream
- 20 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- 1/2 egg or one egg white for breading
- bread crumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Make the fish, after removing the bones and skin, fine.
2. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102.
3. Mix it with the fish and spices and let the mixture, spread on a board, become stiff.
4. Form the croquettes in the manner as indicated for meat croquettes (see recipe 116), bread them double, and treat them further as meat croquettes.

title: Viskoekjes (Fish Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g fish scraps (without bones or skin)
- 30 g old bread
- 1/2 egg
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- fine parsley
- 30 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Chop the leftover fish, after carefully removing the small bones and skins, finely.
2. Mix it with the bread, which has been soaked in water or milk and squeezed between two spoons, the egg and the seasonings.
3. Form 4 flat cakes from it and fry them in the butter or margarine on both sides until light brown.

title: Viskoekjes (Fish Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g fish remains (without bones or skin)
- 30 g old bread
- 1/2 egg
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- fine parsley
- 30 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Chop the remaining fish, after carefully removing the small bones and skin, finely.
2. Mix it with the bread, which has been soaked in water or milk and squeezed out between two spoons, the egg and the seasonings.
3. Form 4 flat cakes from it and fry them in butter or margarine on both sides until light brown.
4. Note: The cakes can also be rolled in beaten egg with water and then in breadcrumbs before frying.

title: Vislever Koken (Cooked Fish Liver)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- Livers of haddock or cod
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- slices of rye bread

## Directions

1. Clean the livers by carefully cutting out the gallbladder, rinse them
2. Cook them gently in some fish water until done (approximately 5 to 10 minutes)
3. Serve with slices of rye bread, salt, pepper and mustard

title: Visschotel met Aardappelen (Fish Dish with Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g leftover cooked fish
- 3/4 kg cooked potatoes
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 lemon
- leftover butter sauce or milk
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. FIRST METHOD: Remove the fish (as warm as possible) from the bone, mash it finely, mix it with the finely mashed potatoes, moisten the mixture with some leftover butter sauce or milk, add salt if needed, put everything in a fireproof dish that has been rinsed with water, sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place a few small pieces of butter and a few notched lemon slices on it and let the dish brown in the oven.
2. SECOND METHOD: Remove the fish (as warm as possible) from the bone, lay it in pieces in the middle of a fireproof dish, pour some water with salt and lemon juice over it, place a few small pieces of butter and a few notched lemon slices on it. Lay a border of mashed potatoes around it, made from the cooked potatoes, approximately 3 dl milk, 20 g butter or margarine, some pepper, salt, nutmeg. Sprinkle breadcrumbs over the dish and let it brown in the oven with a few small pieces of butter on it.
3. THIRD METHOD: Slice the potatoes and lay them layer by layer with the fish in a fireproof dish. Moisten each layer with some sauce or milk, drizzle some lemon juice here and there over the fish. Ensure the top layer consists of potatoes. Sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place some butter or margarine and a few notched lemon slices on it and let the dish turn light brown in the oven.

title: Vissla (Fish Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g cooked, deboned fish
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Debone and skin cooked fish leftovers while still warm as much as possible.
2. Let them cool and mix them with a sauce of oil, vinegar and salt, and treat the salad as described in recipe 9 or cover them with mayonnaise and garnish them with parsley, radish, cucumbers and tomato.

title: Vissoep (Fish Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l fish water
- 1/2 l milk
- mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- parsley
- 50 g flour
- 50 g butter or margarine
- Maggi's Aroma if desired
- 1 egg (optional)
- leftovers of cooked, boned fish

## Directions

1. Let the spices steep in the fish water. Sieve this liquid and gradually add it, while stirring, to the melted butter or margarine, which has then been brought to a boil together with the flour and from which the lumps have been worked out.
2. Let the soup cook while stirring (approximately 10 minutes). Add the leftovers of cooked, boned fish. Stir in the milk and finely chopped parsley and if desired carefully pour it into the egg that has been beaten in the soup tureen, stirring vigorously.

title: Vlees in Schelpen (Meat in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g meat remains
- 2½ dl milk, broth or jus with water
- 30 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine or 18 g Duryea cornstarch
- fine parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Chop or grind the meat finely.
2. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring to a boil while stirring, add the liquid in small portions while stirring and keep stirring until the sauce is well cooked and done (10 min.) or make a cornstarch sauce (see recipe 329).
3. Mix it then with the meat and the seasonings.
4. Fill the dampened shells with this mixture to about 3/4 full.
5. Sprinkle breadcrumbs over it, place a few pieces of butter or margarine on it, clean the edge of the shells with a damp cloth or brush and let them become light brown in a moderately warm oven.

title: Vleeschpudding (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted meat
- ham
- saucis de boulogne
- onion
- anchovies
- 1.5 ounces bacon
- milk
- eggs
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Take roasted meat of any kind
2. Bake it finely with a little ham, saucis de boulogne, an onion, some anchovies and 1.5 ounces bacon
3. Mix milk, eggs and grated bread under it
4. Put it in a mold and cook it well
5. Serve with an anchovy sauce

title: Vleescroquetjes (Meat Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g meat leftovers
- 1 1/2 dl milk, bouillon, or jus with water
- 20 g flour
- 20 g butter or margarine
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- chopped parsley
- 1/2 egg or one egg white for breading
- 2 sheets gelatin or 4 g powdered gelatin
- bread crumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Make the meat fine.
2. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102.
3. Mix it with the meat and spices and let the mixture, spread on a board, become cold and almost stiff.
4. Take on a spoon approximately one eighth of this dough, make it as smooth as possible with another spoon, roll it through fine bread crumbs, form it into a croquette, but ensure there are no cracks in it.
5. Roll this through the egg that has been beaten with water (for one egg count 2 spoons water, for one egg white one spoon water), but ensure it reaches the croquette everywhere.
6. Roll it once more through bread crumbs and fry 3 to 4 croquettes at a time in hot frying fat, brown and crispy.
7. Ensure especially that the croquettes are completely under the fat.
8. Remove them with a skimming spoon and let them drain on gray paper for a moment.
9. Stack them pyramidically on a small dish on which a cloth napkin lies and garnish them with sprigs of fried parsley.

title: Vleeskoekjes (Meat Patties)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g meat leftovers
- 100 g stale bread
- 1 egg
- 1 small onion or leek
- Some finely chopped parsley
- Maggi's Aroma to taste
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 30 g fat from gravy, butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Mince or grind the meat finely, soak the bread in some water or milk, squeeze it out between two spoons, mix it with the beaten egg, the meat, the finely chopped onion or leek, parsley, spices as well as a few drops of Maggi's aroma.
2. Form 8 flat patties and fry these in the frying pan with some remaining fat from the gravy on both sides until brown.
3. Alternatively, the patties can be rolled in beaten egg mixed with water and then in breadcrumbs before frying.

title: Vleespannekoekjes (Meat Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eieren (eggs)
- 100 g bloem (flour)
- 2 dl melk (milk)
- wat zout (salt)
- 40 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)
- voor het vulsel: 200 g koud vlees (for the filling: cold meat, preferably veal or half veal, half pork)
- wat melk (milk)
- jus met water of bouillon (gravy with water or bouillon)
- peper (pepper)
- zout (salt)
- nootmuskaat (nutmeg)
- peterselie (parsley)
- 25 g ui (onion)
- 15 g boter of margarine (butter or margarine)

## Directions

1. Cut or grind the meat finely.
2. Make the chopped onion tender in the butter or margarine, but ensure that it remains light in color.
3. Mix the meat with the braised onion and the fat (optionally rubbed through a sieve), the liquid and the spices and let this mixture

title: Vleespannekoekjes (Meat Pancakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 100 g flour
- 2 dl milk
- salt
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 200 g cold meat (preferably veal or half veal, half pork)
- milk
- meat jus with water or bouillon
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- 25 g onion
- 15 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Mince or grind the meat finely.
2. Cook the chopped onion in butter or margarine until done, ensuring it remains light in color.
3. Mix the meat with the stewed onion and fat (if desired, pressed through a sieve), the liquid, and the spices, and let this mixture stew slowly on a low heat.
4. Beat the eggs with some salt, add the flour and the smallest half of the milk.
5. Stir out the lumps from this batter and gradually add the remaining milk.
6. Bake eight small pancakes from this batter in a pancake pan on both sides until light brown and cooked through.
7. Place a little of the meat mixture on half of each pancake and fold the other half over it.
8. Arrange the pancakes in a star pattern on a round plate with some fresh parsley sprigs between them.

title: Vleestimbal (Meat Timbale)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g cold meat
- 2 eggs
- 60 g old bread
- 1/2 dl lean gravy with water or broth
- 50 g onion
- 1 spoon finely chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- Maggi's Aroma
- trimmed lemon slices
- gherkins
- capers

## Directions

1. Mince the meat, cleaned onion and washed parsley
2. Beat the egg yolks with some Maggi's Aroma, add the soaked bread squeezed between two spoons, the herbs, the broth and the meat and stir everything together vigorously
3. Mix this mass as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white and place it in a butter or margarine-greased pudding or timbale mold sprinkled with breadcrumbs
4. Close it with a lid greased with butter or margarine and cook for 45 minutes in a pan of boiling water, ensuring the water is refilled to just below the rim of the mold
5. Turn the meat onto a warm, moist plate, pour curry or fricassée sauce (see recipe 339 or 349), or optionally béchamel sauce (see recipe 346) over it and garnish with trimmed lemon slices, gherkins and capers
6. Alternatively replace meat with boneless fish

title: Vlugkokende Gort (Quick-Cooking Barley)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g quick-cooking barley
- salt
- cold water (twice the amount of barley)

## Directions

1. Wash the barley, set it with twice as much cold water as barley and some salt on a low fire.
2. Let it cook done in just over 1/2 hour and treat the barley further as described in the other recipes.

title: Vogelnestjes (Bird's Nests)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 200 g cold meat
- finely chopped sour onions
- finely chopped parsley
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 60 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Chop the meat, onions and washed parsley finely
2. Mix this mixture with 2 small beaten eggs, salt and nutmeg to taste
3. Melt the butter or margarine in a frying pan, form 4 rings from the meat mixture off the heat
4. Break one egg into the opening of each ring (like a fried egg), but ensure the white does not run over the meat mixture
5. Bake the bird's nests on a gentle heat, until white and yolk are set, or (which is easier) place the pan in the oven until the white is set
6. Slide the bird's nests carefully onto a warmed dish and serve with pepper and salt alongside bread or instead of meat at the midday meal

title: Volkorenbrood (Whole Grain Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g whole grain flour
- 15 g yeast
- 2½ dl water
- 7½ g salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread as recipe 835.

title: Volkorenbrood (Whole Grain Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g volkorenmeel
- 15 g gist
- + 21/2 dl water
- 71/2 g zout

## Directions

1. Bereid het brood als recept 835.

title: Vruchtenbrood (Fruit Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g dried apricots
- 50 g figs
- 50 g dates
- 50 g raisins

## Directions

1. Pit the dates and wash the fruits well. Grind them two or three times through the mill. Make a large croquette of it; let it dry and cut it into slices.

title: Vruchtengelei (Fruit Gelée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fruit juice (oranges with mandarins, pineapple juice, apricot juice, etc.)
- 35 g white gelatin or 30 g powdered gelatin
- sugar to taste
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Prepare the fruit juice with sugar, soaked and squeezed gelatin and egg whites in the known manner (see recipe 555).
2. Fill small molds with the obtained gelée.
3. Turn the small puddings out after cooling and present them with custard sauce or with whipped cream and sugar or pour the gelée into a ring mold, see illustration.

title: Vruchtenpudding (Fruit Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- thin slices of the best sour apples
- sugar
- butter
- cinnamon
- snippets
- sukade

## Directions

1. Line the mold on the inside with puff pastry
2. Fill it with thin slices of the best sour apples, adding sugar, butter, cinnamon, snippets and sukade
3. Cover everything with puff pastry
4. Bind the mold in a cloth and let it cook for 3 hours
5. One can use any other fruit instead of apples

title: Vruchtenschoteltje met Beschuit en Geslagen Room (Fruit Dish with Rusk and Whipped Cream)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g berries
- 250 g raspberries
- 8 large rusks
- Approximately 100 g brown sugar
- 75 g butter or margarine
- ¼ l whipping cream
- 25 g powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the berries, drain them; select the raspberries, wash them and let them cook quickly with the berries and part of the sugar into a puree.
2. Place this in a fireproof dish.
3. Crush the rusks finely, mix them with the remaining sugar and sprinkle them over the fruit puree.
4. Place the butter or margarine in small pieces on top and let the dish brown in a moderately warm oven.
5. Let it cool afterwards.
6. Beat the cream stiff, mix it with the sifted powdered sugar and place it on the dish.

title: Vruchtensla (Fruit Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- fruits according to season
- sugar
- lemon juice
- vanilla or custard cream (optional)
- whipped cream with superfine sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel and pit the fruits if necessary and divide them into pieces or slices if they are large.
2. Make the pieces thin if the fruits are not soft.
3. Add lemon juice to give the dish a fresh taste.
4. Arrange the fruits sprinkled with sugar and sprinkled with lemon juice in a glass bowl and let them stand for about 1.5 hours.
5. Stir the mass from time to time.
6. Cool the fruit salad.
7. Serve the dish, if desired, with vanilla or custard cream or with whipped cream, which has been beaten with some superfine sugar.

title: Warm Bier (Warm Beer)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cans brown beer
- citrus peel
- cinnamon
- cloves
- 2 cans milk
- 4 to 6 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon potato flour
- 2 ounces sugar
- cold milk

## Directions

1. While you cook 2 cans brown beer with some citrus peel, cinnamon and cloves, and two cans milk, each separately, stir in another pan 4 to 6 egg yolks, 1 tablespoon potato flour, two ounces sugar and a large bowl of cold milk, well together
2. Now pour while continuously stirring first the boiling milk and then the boiling hot beer through a sieve into it
3. Stir the whole over a gentle fire until the eggs are cooked and you can remove it from the fire

title: Warme Bitterkoekjespudding (Warm Almond Macaroon Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g almond macaroons
- 200 g old bread without crust
- 3 dl milk
- 50 g sugar
- 3 eggs
- 50 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Soak the almond macaroons and the bread in the warm milk, rub both completely fine
2. Add the butter or margarine
3. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar
4. Add the finely made mass to this and mix it as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white
5. Grease a warm pudding mold with some butter or margarine (also the lid), sprinkle some fine breadcrumbs in it and add the mixture, but ensure the mold is filled only 3/4 full
6. Close it firmly and cook the pudding 1.5 hours au bain-marie, but ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold
7. Let the pudding dry out a little without the lid and serve with warm vanilla, foam or rum sauce

title: Warme Broodpudding (Warm Bread Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- 3.5 dl milk
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 50 g butter or margarine
- 35 g currants
- 35 g raisins
- 25 g candied citrus peel
- 50 g sugar
- 50 g almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Make the bread, carefully freed of crusts, fine in the boiling milk, ensuring there are no lumps at all
2. Add the butter or margarine
3. Stir the egg yolks with the sugar
4. Add to this the bread with the butter or margarine, the well-washed currants and raisins, freed from their stems, the shredded candied citrus peel and if desired the peeled and chopped almonds
5. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible
6. Grease a warm pudding mold (also the lid) with some butter or margarine, sprinkle some fine breadcrumbs in it and add the mixture, but ensure the mold is filled only 3/4 full
7. Close the mold firmly and cook the pudding 1.5 hours au bain-marie; ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold
8. Let the pudding dry out a little without the lid and serve with foam sauce or warm vanilla sauce

title: Warme Gemberpudding (Warm Ginger Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g old bread without crust
- 100 g candied ginger
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 3.5 dl milk
- 35 g sugar
- zest of a lemon

## Directions

1. Make the bread fine in the boiling milk
2. Add the egg yolks, the sugar, the grated lemon zest and the ginger cut into pieces, and if desired some ginger syrup
3. Mix this mass as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white and fill a warm pudding mold greased with butter or margarine and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs 3/4 full
4. Close it with a lid greased with butter or margarine firmly and cook the pudding 1.5 hours au bain-marie, but ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold
5. Let the pudding dry out a little without the lid and serve with warm vanilla sauce

title: Warme Griesmeelpudding (Warm Semolina Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l milk
- 100 g semolina
- 80 g sugar
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 0.5 vanilla pod or vanilla sugar or the zest of a lemon
- 50 g almonds or raisins (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook stiff semolina porridge in the usual way
2. Stir in the butter or margarine on the heat and carefully from off the heat the beaten yolks and if desired the peeled and chopped almonds or the washed raisins
3. Mix the porridge as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white, after removing the flavoring
4. Grease a warm pudding mold with some butter or margarine (also the lid), sprinkle some fine breadcrumbs in it and add the mixture, but ensure the mold is filled only 3/4 full
5. Close the mold firmly and cook the pudding 2 hours au bain-marie, but ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold, without it coming to a boil
6. Let the pudding dry out a little without the lid and serve with foam sauce or warm fruit juice sauce

title: Warme Hampudding (Warm Ham Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g coarse vermicelli or noodles
- 250 g lean ham
- 100 g Gouda cheese
- 2 eggs
- 2 dl bouillon made from a cube
- salt as needed
- 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley
- some spicy sauce
- margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the vermicelli or noodles until done in plenty of salted water.
2. Rinse them briefly with cold water so the vermicelli does not stick together.
3. Let them drain as dry as possible.
4. Cut the ham into small pieces and grate the cheese (or buy it already grated).
5. Grease a warm pudding mold with some margarine and fill it layer by layer with vermicelli, ham, cheese and parsley.
6. Beat the eggs with bouillon and some spicy sauce.
7. Pour the liquid over the contents of the mold.
8. Close the mold and cook it for approximately 2 hours in a water bath.
9. Turn the pudding out onto a warm plate and serve with parsley sauce.
10. Young green beans, peas or lettuce are suitable vegetables to serve with this.

title: Warme Kabinetpudding (Warm Cabinet Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g biscuits
- 100 g almond biscuits
- 3 eggs
- 30 g sugar
- 3 dl milk
- 50 g currants
- 50 g raisins
- 25 g candied citrus peel (or snippets)
- 1/2 grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Coat a warm pudding mold with butter or margarine.
2. Cut the biscuits straight on the sides, also cut them level on top and place them against the wall of the mold with the brown side outward.
3. Beat the eggs with the sugar and the grated lemon zest, add the milk.
4. Wash the currants and raisins and cook them briefly in water, chop the candied citrus peel (or the snippets).
5. Layer the mixture and almond biscuits in the mold, but make sure the top layer consists of almond biscuits.
6. Pour the beaten eggs over the mixture. Make sure the liquid just rises above the filling.
7. Close the mold in the usual manner and cook the pudding 1 1/2 hours in a bain-marie, but make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim of the mold.
8. Let the pudding dry without the lid and serve it with warm vanilla or fruit sauce.

title: Warme macaronipudding (Warm Macaroni Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g macaroni
- 250 g ham
- 75 g Parmesan or old Gouda cheese
- 1 1/2 to 2 dl milk
- 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the macaroni and cook the tubes whole in boiling salted water (approximately 1/2 hour to 45 minutes)
2. Place enough on the bottom of a butter or margarine-greased pudding mold to just cover the bottom, lay a layer of chopped ham and grated cheese mixed together on top
3. Add more macaroni again, but ensure it covers the sides of the mold as much as possible
4. Continue until all is used
5. Beat the eggs with salt and pepper, add the milk and pour this mixture into the mold
6. Close it in the usual manner and cook the pudding 2 hours in a bain-marie, ensuring the water is refilled to just below the rim of the mold
7. Turn the pudding onto a warm, moist plate and serve it with a savory cream sauce (recipe 337)

title: Warme mayonnaise (Warm Mayonnaise)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 dl cooking cream
- 1/2 dl salad oil
- 1/2 dl vinegar
- mustard
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with salt, pepper and mustard in a good enameled pan using a tinned whisk (whip).
2. Gradually add the oil and cream and let the sauce bind au bain marie while stirring vigorously.
3. Remove from heat and add vinegar.
4. Serve with warm salmon.

title: Warme Rijstpudding (Warm Rice Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 l milk
- 100 g rice
- 0.5 vanilla pod or vanilla sugar or the zest of a lemon
- 80 g sugar
- 2 to 3 eggs
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 50 g almonds

## Directions

1. Cook stiff rice porridge in the usual way, but let vanilla or lemon zest steep in it
2. Mix this then, after removing the flavoring, with the butter or margarine, the egg yolks, the sugar and the chopped almonds, fold in the stiffly beaten egg white as lightly as possible
3. Grease a warm pudding mold with some butter or margarine (also the lid), sprinkle some fine breadcrumbs in it and fill it 3/4 with the mixture
4. Close it firmly and cook the pudding 2 hours au bain-marie, but ensure the water is always refilled to just below the rim of the mold
5. Let the pudding dry out a little and serve with fruit or foam sauce

title: Warme Wijn (Hot Wine)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle red wine
- 1 bottle berry wine
- approximately 100 g sugar
- 1 orange
- 20 cloves
- a few pieces of cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Wash the orange, stick the cloves and a few pieces of cinnamon stick into it and put it in the wine, which is poured into the wine kettle.
2. Close the spout and lid tightly and warm the liquid gradually on a low fire.
3. Let it steep for some time, add the sugar and serve as warm as possible.

title: Warme Wijn (Warm Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound sugar
- 1 stick cinnamon
- 6 cloves
- water
- 1 bottle red wine

## Directions

1. Cook half a pound sugar, a stick of cinnamon, 6 cloves in half a can of water for a quarter hour, then add a bottle of red wine and now heat well and drink

title: Waterbaars (Perch)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 kg perch
- water
- salt
- 6 slices of white bread
- 4 slices of rye bread
- parsley
- parsley roots

## Directions

1. Clean the fish by removing the entrails, scraping off the scales and washing it well (if the fish is slimy, gently rub it with a little salt)
2. Cook it until done in boiling water with salt (not as much as for other fish) and allow a few peeled parsley roots to cook along with it (approximately 4-5 minutes)
3. Immediately place the fish with as much cooking liquid as possible in the bottom part of the fish plate, surround it with a few fresh sprigs of parsley
4. Serve with very thin square slices of white bread, trimmed of crust and thickly spread, topped with an equally large and similarly thickly spread piece of rye bread

title: Waterchocolade (Water Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 and one-half lood chocolate per cup water
- water
- egg yolk
- sugar

## Directions

1. For each cup of water take one and one-half lood chocolate, make it fine and let it stew with almost a cup of water on gentle heat, stirring strongly; when the chocolate is done, stir an egg yolk into it, with sugar to taste

title: Waterglazuur (Water Glaze)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g powdered sugar
- about one tablespoon water
- liqueur essence

## Directions

1. Stir boiling water into sifted powdered sugar until a stiff mass is obtained that does not run off a spoon but stands firm.
2. Add a few drops of maraschino, kirsch or vanilla essence and if desired some carmine, and brush it on the cake.
3. Note: Cocoa can be stirred into the powdered sugar to make a dark brown glaze. For this amount of glaze, 10 g of cocoa is needed.

title: Watergruel (Water Gruel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 l water
- 100 g pearl barley
- 2.5 dl berry juice
- 100 g currants (or half raisins, half currants)
- peel of one lemon
- 175 to 200 g sugar
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Soak the rinsed pearl barley for 24 hours in the cold water with salt.
2. Bring it to a boil in the same water with the lemon peel and let it cook gently for 1.5 hours.
3. Add the sugar, the well-rinsed currants without stems and the lemon juice and let the water gruel cook gently for another half hour.
4. Add the berry juice at the end.
5. Remove the lemon peel before serving.
6. Serve the water gruel warm or cold.
7. When using quick-cooking barley, omit the soaking.

title: Watersoep (Water Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots
- celery roots
- leeks
- leaf parsley
- white bread
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Let parsley and celery roots with leeks and leaf parsley, as well as cubes of white bread cook well done in a pan
2. Pour enough water over it now
3. Stir salt and butter through it
4. Let everything cook through thoroughly once more and serve it

title: Watersoep (Water Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots
- celery roots
- leeks
- leaf parsley
- white bread cut into cubes
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Let parsley roots, celery roots, leeks, and leaf parsley cook well done with cubes of white bread in a pan
2. Pour enough water into it
3. Stir salt and butter through
4. Let everything cook through properly once more and serve

title: Wellingtons (Wellington Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g almonds
- 500 g white sugar
- + 2 egg whites
- some lemon juice
- wafer papers
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Grind the peeled almonds twice with the sugar and egg white through an almond mill.
2. Add some lemon juice and if necessary more egg white until a fairly loose dough is obtained.
3. Put this in the piping bag fitted with a smooth funnel and pipe oblong strips diagonally onto a wafer paper.
4. Turn the wafer paper over onto white paper sprinkled with chopped almonds, press the cookies in slightly, turn the wafer paper back over, lay it on a baking sheet and bake the Wellingtons in a moderately warm oven until light brown (++ 20 minutes).
5. Break the wafer paper away along the side of the cookies.

title: Wener gebakjes met glazuur (Viennese Pastries with Icing - Muscovite Dough)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 100 g flour
- 100 g almonds
- 6 eggs
- 2 or 3 kinds of jam
- 150 g icing in different colors
- French candied fruits
- nutmeg
- petit four cases

## Directions

1. Make the batter in the known way (see recipe 833).
2. Spread the dough on a greased baking sheet, which is covered with a greased piece of grease-free paper so that the paper sticks out a little above the edges on all sides, and bake the sheet of dough as described in recipe 864.
3. Remove the paper and divide the pastry into oblong, square or round pieces.
4. Halve the pieces and put some jam between both halves.
5. Drizzle the pastries with thick icing; let them drip off and garnish them with French candied fruits or nutmeg and place them in the petit four cases.

title: Wentelteefjes (French Toast)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small slices of old bread without crust
- 2 eggs
- 4 dl milk
- 40 g sugar
- 75 g butter or margarine
- some fine cinnamon, grated lemon zest or scraped vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar and the flavoring.
2. Add the warm milk and soak the slices of bread, stripped of the crust and cut evenly, in this sauce.
3. Ensure the slices, although completely soaked through, do not break.
4. Let them drain on a plate for a moment and fry the wentelteefjes in the butter or margarine that has turned light brown on both sides until brown.
5. Sprinkle with some crushed sugar or powdered sugar.

title: Wentelteefjes van Beschuit (French Toast with Rusk)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small, round rusks
- 2 eggs
- 4 dl milk
- 40 g sugar
- 60 g butter or margarine
- some fine cinnamon, grated lemon zest or scraped vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare the wentelteefjes as described in the previous recipe, but soak the rusks briefly as they break easily.

title: Wentelteefjes van beschuit (French Toast with Rusks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small round rusks
- 2 eggs
- 4 dl milk
- 40 g sugar
- 60 g butter or margarine
- fine cinnamon
- grated lemon zest or scraped vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare the French toast as described in the previous recipe, but soak the rusks briefly, as they break easily.

title: Wentelteefles (French Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- milk
- salt
- rusks (beschuiten)
- butter
- bread sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 6 eggs fine
2. Add 1 tablespoon milk and some salt per egg
3. Soak rusks in this mixture
4. Fry in butter
5. When serving, sprinkle with bread sugar
6. May also use slices of stale white bread instead of rusks

title: Wespenesten (Wasp Nests)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- sugar
- cinnamon
- currants
- raisins
- sukade (candied fruit)
- grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Roll sugar, cinnamon, currants, raisins, sukade and grated lemon peel in puff pastry
2. Cut the dough into pieces the length of half a finger
3. Stand them upright in a tart pan
4. Bake until done

title: Wiener Pudding (Vienna Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g old bread without crust
- 150 g sugar
- 3 1/2 dl milk
- 3 large eggs
- 100 g raisins
- 50 g candied citrus peel
- juice and zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the bread into small cubes and mix it with the chopped candied citrus peel, the raisins washed with lukewarm water, the grated lemon zest and the lemon juice.
2. Beat the eggs with 100 g sugar.
3. Place the remaining sugar in a fairly large pan, let it stand on a moderate heat until it becomes caramel and begins to foam over the entire surface.
4. Pour the boiling milk slowly but in one stream over the caramel off the heat and, if necessary, let the caramel that is not yet dissolved dissolve further on the heat.
5. Pour this liquid, stirring well, into the beaten eggs, add the bread with the remaining ingredients and let it soak for 1 1/2 hours.
6. Coat a warm pudding mold with some butter or margarine.
7. Add the mixture to it, close it firmly and cook the pudding 1 1/2 to 2 hours in a bain-marie, but make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim of the mold.
8. Let the pudding dry slightly and serve it with warm vanilla or caramel sauce.

title: Wiener Schnitzel (Breaded Veal Cutlets with Garnish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beautiful, flat, lean veal cutlets of at least 100 g
- Salt
- 1 egg for breading
- Breadcrumbs
- 75 g butter or margarine
- 6 gherkins
- 4 anchovies
- 1 hard-boiled egg
- Lemon
- Capers

## Directions

1. Treat the cutlets as for kalfsoesters.
2. Place them on a warmed plate and garnish with slices of hard-boiled egg and anchovy that has been cleaned in the usual way, gherkins, halved seeded lemon slices or capers.
3. Fill the space between the cutlets with scrambled eggs or pipe potato purée alongside.

title: Wijngelei (Wine Gelée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 liter red wine
- 1/2 liter water
- 8 cloves
- 1 lemon
- cinnamon stick (10 cm long)
- 150 g sugar
- 30 g sugar
- 30 g white gelatin
- 2 to 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Treat the wine gelée as described in recipe 555, after the wine has first been infused with lemon, cloves and cinnamon stick.
2. Pour the liquid through a doubled thick cloth into a pudding mold rinsed with cold water and let it cool.
3. Serve whipped cream with this pudding.

title: Wijnsoep (Wine Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle Rhine or red wine
- one and a half spoon flour
- five egg yolks
- juice of 3 to 4 lemons
- rind of one lemon
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Let one bottle of Rhine or red wine cook gently with one and a half spoon of flour and five well-beaten egg yolks
2. Now add the juice of 3 to 4 lemons, grate the rind of one lemon over it
3. Add sugar and cinnamon to taste
4. While it is on the fire, stir carefully

title: Wijting (Whiting)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 whitings (just over 1 kg)
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the fish after cleaning them as indicated in recipe 177
2. Preferably have whiting filleted

title: Windbuidel (Wind Puff)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound butter
- 1 pint milk
- Flour
- 8 to 10 eggs
- Half ounce sugar

## Directions

1. Let half a pound of butter boil in a pint of milk over a high fire
2. Stir in as much flour as needed until it is bound
3. Let it cool and then add 8 to 10 eggs and half an ounce sugar

title: Windbuidel (Wind Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound butter
- One pint milk
- Flour, as needed to bind
- 8 to 10 eggs
- Half an ounce sugar
- Sugar for garnish

## Directions

1. Heat butter in milk over high heat until boiling
2. Stir in flour until bound
3. Let cool
4. Add eggs and half an ounce sugar
5. On a buttered baking sheet, make small heaps the size of a hazelnut, not too close together
6. Sprinkle each heap with sugar for shine
7. Bake in a good oven

title: Wine Punch (Wijnpunsch)
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven and a half ounces sugar
- One beer glass lemon juice
- Two cans red wine
- Two cans red wine
- Half a can arak

## Directions

1. Cook seven and a half ounces sugar, one beer glass lemon juice, and two cans of red wine, well skimmed
2. When it has slightly cooled, add two more cans of wine and half a can of arak
3. Stir everything well

title: Winterwortelen (Winter Carrots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg winter carrots
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt
- finely chopped parsley
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the carrots, cut them into equal, elongated pieces, but do not place these in water as they will otherwise lose their sweet flavor.
2. Wash them quickly once they are all ready and place them in boiling water so that the bottom of the pan is well covered.
3. Let them cook until tender and dry, stirring occasionally (approximately 45 minutes).
4. Add water during cooking if needed to prevent burning.
5. Stew them for 5 minutes with the butter or margarine and shake in the fine parsley away from the heat.

title: Witbrood (White Bread)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g flour
- 10 g yeast
- 7.5 g salt
- 2.5 dl milk

## Directions

1. Put the flour in a deep bowl, make a well in the middle, crumble the yeast into it and mix with about half of the lukewarm milk.
2. Stir in enough of the surrounding flour to form a thick dough (so-called starter).
3. Let this rise covered on a lukewarm spot for 20 to 30 minutes.
4. Then add the remaining milk and salt and knead this dough until it leaves the hands.
5. Let it rise covered on a lukewarm spot (about 1 hour).
6. Shape it so that a greased bread pan is filled about 2/3.
7. Let the dough rise here for another 1/2 hour and bake the bread in a hot oven, placed approximately in the middle, brown and done (1/2 to 3/4 hour).
8. Brush the top as soon as it becomes brown with water to get a shiny crust.
9. Tap on the bread to hear if it is done and turn it out of the pan.
10. Note: Replace the milk with water if desired.

title: Witte Bonen (White Beans)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 g white beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare them as brown beans (recipe 491), and reckon as cooking time 1 hour. Serve them with sour sauce.

title: Witte Kool (White Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- fat or pickled bacon (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop finely and cook
2. Then stew with butter, salt and pepper
3. Can also be stewed with fat or a piece of pickled bacon

title: Witte Pikante Saus (White Piquant Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l bouillon
- small carrot
- sprig of parsley
- pinch of thyme
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 dl Madeira or white wine

## Directions

1. Let the herbs steep in the bouillon, strain and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Remove from heat and blend with the egg yolk beaten with the wine or Madeira and serve with calf's meat, timbal, etc.

title: Witte Ragout (White Ragout) (for 1 large pastry)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l bouillon
- piece of mace
- thyme
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 45 g flour
- 45 g butter or margarine
- 50 g ground veal
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms
- approximately 450 g pieces of veal tongue and sweetbread
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 dl cream

## Directions

1. Draw bouillon in the usual way with the spices.
2. Sieve it and add it stirring to the butter or margarine, which has been melted and then brought to a boil together with the flour and from which the lumps have been rubbed out.
3. Let the sauce simmer stirring until cooked (10 minutes).
4. Warm in it the cooked meat balls, pieces of veal tongue and pieces of sweetbread and pour the ragout stirring to the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream.
5. Add the mushrooms and if desired some of the liquid.
6. Fill the pastries, fried items etc. with it.

title: Witte Ragoutsaus (White Ragout Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l bouillon
- pinch of thyme
- Spanish pepper
- parsley
- piece of onion
- 30 to 40 g flour
- 30 to 40 g butter or margarine
- 50 g ground veal
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms
- 1/2 dl cream

## Directions

1. Let the herbs steep in the bouillon, strain and prepare sauce from it in the usual way (see cauliflower sauce)
2. Warm the cooked ground meat balls in it and while beating vigorously, pour the sauce onto the egg yolk beaten with the cream
3. Stir in the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, and add some of the mushroom liquid if desired
4. Serve with calf's tongue and light meat

title: Witte Ragoutsoep (White Ragout Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon made from veal shank meat
- piece of mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- pinch of thyme
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 50 g veal mince
- small pieces of sweetbread and calf's tongue
- 3/4 tin of mushrooms with liquid
- 1 egg yolk
- some cream
- some lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make bouillon from the veal meat with the spices in the usual way.
2. Let it cool, skim off the fat and strain it.
3. Cut a small portion of calf's tongue and sweetbread, which have been cooked in the usual way and carefully freed of fat and skin, into small pieces.
4. Mix the mince in the usual way.
5. Form very small balls from it and cook these for 10 minutes in some water.
6. Remove them from the liquid with a skimming spoon.
7. Melt the butter or margarine, add the flour and bring it to the boil while stirring, gradually add the strained bouillon and let the soup cook while stirring for 10 minutes.
8. Then briefly warm the meat in it, carefully pour the soup to the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream.
9. Add the mushrooms, either whole or cut in half, the liquid from the mushrooms and if desired some lemon juice.

title: Witte saus (White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- water
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix butter and flour in a pan
2. Bring it to a boil with water while stirring constantly
3. Remove from heat
4. Add a little salt, pepper and vinegar

title: Witte Soep (White Soup, Potage à la Crème)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter bouillon made from 500 g veal shank meat or veal pullet
- piece of mace
- piece of Spanish pepper
- some parsley
- 30 g tapioca
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 dl cream

## Directions

1. Make fragrant bouillon from the meat with the water and spices in the usual way.
2. Strain it several times through a flannel or double-folded linen cloth until it becomes completely clear.
3. Cook the washed and soaked tapioca in it until tender (approximately 3/4 to 1 hour) and carefully pour the soup while stirring vigorously to the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream, in the soup tureen.

title: Witte wijnsaus (White Wine Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 60 g bastard sugar
- 2 dl Rhine wine or berry wine
- juice of 1/2 lemon or orange
- zest of 1/4 lemon or orange

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks and sugar vigorously together.
2. Add the wine, fruit juice and zest and stir the sauce on a gentle fire or in a bain-marie until it is somewhat bound.
3. Remove the citrus zest, beat the egg white stiff and add the hot sauce to the egg white at the last moment.
4. Serve with warm bread pudding, almond cake pudding etc.

title: Witte- of Savoyekool met rijst en aardappelen (White or Savoy Cabbage with Rice and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium-sized cabbage
- ¾ kg potatoes
- 200 g rice
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage by cutting it into pieces and removing the stalk and hard ribs.
2. Rinse it several times and place it in a pot with enough boiling water so it is submerged three-quarters of the way, let it cook gently for half an hour.
3. Add the fat and washed rice, ensuring the rice is entirely covered by water.
4. Lay the peeled potatoes, cut into quarters, on top and let everything cook together on low heat until done and almost dry (30 to 35 minutes).
5. Add more water if needed, but ensure the stamppot does not become too moist.
6. Quickly mash everything together with a wooden spoon or masher.
7. Note: The stamppot can also be prepared without rice, but then use 1½ kg potatoes.

title: Worst van Kalfslever (Calf's Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound calf liver
- one pound bacon
- spices and salt as for pork sausage
- half pound bread crumbs
- fresh intestines

## Directions

1. Take one pound calf liver and an equal amount of bacon
2. Remove all membranes from the liver
3. Chop it together very finely
4. Add spices and salt as for pork sausage, along with half pound of bread crumbs
5. Stuff this sausage meat into fresh intestines
6. Roast it on the grill

title: Worst van Varkenslever (Pork Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- two good fatty livers
- onions
- bouillon
- two and a half ounces melted lard in cubes
- half a can of calf's blood
- a quarter can of cream
- salt
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Take two good fatty livers and chop them fine
2. Cook a little onions with bouillon and chop them with the liver, add to this two and a half ounces melted lard in cubes, half a can of calf's blood, a quarter can of cream, salt, pepper and spices, and cook it slowly together, always stirring
3. When cool, stuff the sausage, and roast them on the grill when you want to eat them

title: Worst van Varkenslever (Pork Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- two good fat livers
- onions
- bouillon
- two and a half ounces melted lard
- half a can pork blood
- a quarter can cream
- salt
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Chop the livers finely
2. Cook some onions with bouillon and chop them together with the liver
3. Add two and a half ounces melted lard cut into cubes, half a can pork blood, a quarter can cream, salt, pepper and spices
4. Cook slowly together, stirring constantly
5. When cool, stuff the sausage and roast on the grill when ready to eat

title: Worst van visch (Fish Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salmon
- spices as used in common sausage
- 1 cup melted butter
- 4 to 5 eggs depending on quantity
- white bread
- butter for coating paper

## Directions

1. Cut pike and salmon into small cubes
2. Add spices as in common sausage, 1 cup melted butter and 4 to 5 eggs according to quantity
3. Knead this together with some white bread
4. Stuff into the belly of a pike or roll it in butter-coated paper to the size of sausages
5. Cook or roast as desired

title: Wortelensoep (Carrot Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- bouillon
- white bread

## Directions

1. Slice the carrots into slices and cook them done in bouillon
2. While the soup cooks, add a few slices of white bread

title: Worteltjes (Carrots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large bunches of carrots
- sugar
- salt (optional)

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots, wash them carefully and arrange them neatly in the well-cleaned bottles.
2. Add some sugar and salt if desired.
3. Pour enough water until the carrots are just covered.
4. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize them for 1½ hours.
5. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.

title: Worteltjes (Carrots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large bunches carrots
- sugar
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots, wash them carefully and arrange them neatly in well-cleaned bottles.
2. Add some sugar and if desired some salt.
3. Pour on enough water so that the carrots are just covered.
4. Close the bottles with ring, lid and clip(s) and sterilize for 1½ hours.
5. Let them cool with the clips on, remove the clips and attach a label with name and date.
6. Note: The carrots can also be cooked for some minutes and then placed in the bottles.

title: Worteltjes (Young Carrots)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large bunch or 1 kg cleaned carrots
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt
- finely chopped parsley
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots and make a cross in the thick top part, but do not place the scraped carrots in cold water as they will lose their sweet flavor.
2. Wash them quickly once they are all ready and place them in boiling water so that the bottom of the pan is well covered.
3. Let them cook until tender and dry, stirring occasionally (young carrots - 20 minutes, older carrots - approximately 30 minutes).
4. Add water during cooking if needed to prevent burning.
5. Stew them for 5 minutes with the butter or margarine and shake in the fine parsley away from the heat.

title: Zaan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints sweet milk
- 1 beer glass buttermilk
- a little cold butter
- a little saffron
- a couple of egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook 3 pints sweet milk
2. Pour it into a pot and stir until it is lukewarm
3. Add 1 beer glass buttermilk and mix with a little cold butter, a little saffron, and a couple of egg yolks

title: Zaan (Zaan)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet milk
- Buttermilk
- Cold butter
- Saffron
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook 3 pints of sweet milk, then pour it into a pot and stir until it is lukewarm
2. Add a beer glass of buttermilk and stir in a little cold butter, some saffron and a couple of egg yolks

title: Zachte Wener taart (Soft Viennese Cake)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g butter or margarine
- 100 g sugar
- 100 g flour
- 100 g almonds
- 6 eggs
- 2 or 3 kinds of jam

## Directions

1. Prepare the cake like the German sand cake, but add the peeled and ground almonds to the yolks.
2. Cut the cake after cooling into 3 or more layers, spread each layer with a different kind of jam.
3. Lay the layers on top of each other again and garnish the top with cooking and egg white icing and French candied fruits.

title: Zakeieren (Poached Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Use the freshest possible eggs for this
2. Bring water to a boil in a wide pan, add some salt and a small splash of vinegar
3. Break the eggs as close as possible above the water surface, or break them first into a cup and let them slide into the water
4. Quickly wrap the white, which immediately begins to set, around the yolk using two spoons and let the eggs simmer gently for 3 minutes
5. Remove them from the pan with a skimming spoon and cut or trim off the loose flakes of white
6. Lay them on pieces of toasted bread or bread fried in butter or margarine, in a bowl of very strong broth for soup, or serve them with tomato, béchamel, curry sauce etc.

title: Zalm (Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon into pieces and cook with water and salt

title: Zalm (Salmon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pieces salmon of 400 g
- water
- 10 g salt per litre of water

## Directions

1. Scrape the scales off the salmon, wash the pieces and cook them in boiling water with not too much salt (to keep the color nice) 2 minutes per 100 grams.
2. Remove the pieces with the fish slice or a couple of skimming spoons from the pan, place them on the fish platter and set it for a moment in front of an open window, to make the color a brighter red.
3. With warm salmon serve: parsley sauce, warm mayonnaise or melted butter, with cold salmon: cold mayonnaise and salad.
4. Note: Shad can be treated as salmon, but can also be cooked in the manner of haddock.

title: Zalm in aspic (Salmon in Aspic)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large can salmon
- 1 l white aspic
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- parsley

## Directions

1. For preparation see lobster in aspic.

title: Zalm met Kappersaus (Salmon with Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece salmon
- oil
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- chopped onions
- butter
- flour
- capers

## Directions

1. Take a piece of salmon, place it in oil, parsley, salt, pepper and a little chopped onion
2. Let it cook afterwards, almost an hour, if it is very thick
3. Place it on the dish and pour over a sauce of butter with flour and capers

title: Zalm of Kreeft in Schelpen (Salmon or Lobster in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tin of salmon or lobster
- mayonnaise
- aspic in 2 colors
- some peas and small carrots from a tin

## Directions

1. Place some salmon or lobster in the shells
2. Pour enough mayonnaise over so that the fish is just covered
3. Further garnish the shells with finely chopped aspic in two colors (for example white and red), small carrots and peas
4. The mayonnaise can be lightly colored rose by carefully stirring in some melted red aspic
5. Then let the mayonnaise set, but especially not become too stiff
6. If the shells must be made some time in advance, it is best in any case to mix the mayonnaise with some white or red aspic, which will make it better cover the fish

title: Zalmbroodjes (Smoked Salmon Sandwich)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices stale casino bread
- 20 g butter or margarine
- 25 g smoked salmon
- mayonnaise or remoulade sauce

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of stale casino bread, remove the crusts and cut them into different shapes, e.g. triangles, diamonds, pear-shaped figures, etc.
2. Fry them in the frying pan light brown and crispy, let them cool and spread them on one side with mayonnaise (recipe 368) or remoulade sauce (recipe 371)
3. Lay a properly fitting piece of smoked salmon on top and place the sandwiches on the plate
4. If desired, replace the salmon with smoked colored pollock

title: Zalmsalade (Salmon Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked salmon
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- wine vinegar
- consommé
- salad oil
- tarragon
- pimpernel
- head lettuce
- hard-boiled eggs
- capers
- gherkins
- anchovies

## Directions

1. Once the salmon is cooked, place the pieces in a sauce of oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, and fine herbs.
2. Make a sauce of four tablespoons wine vinegar, two tablespoons consommé, and ten tablespoons salad oil, salt, pepper, chopped tarragon, and pimpernel.
3. Place the pieces in this sauce and arrange them on a platter.
4. Pour the sauce over them.
5. Arrange head lettuce cut into four parts, and on each piece place a quarter egg with capers, gherkins, and anchovies.

title: Zandkoekjes (Sand Cookies)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g flour or 50 g cornstarch and 250 g flour
- 200 g butter or margarine
- 150 g powdered sugar or brown sugar

## Directions

1. Sift the flour or flour mixture and the powdered or brown sugar
2. Knead both with the butter or margarine together
3. Roll out the dough on a floured board with a floured rolling pin to 1/2 cm thickness
4. Cut out cookies with cookie cutters, cookie stamps, glasses or cups
5. Place them on a greased baking sheet with some distance between them
6. Bake them in the upper part of a moderately warm oven until lightly brown and done (approximately 15 to 20 minutes)
7. Remove them immediately from the baking sheet and let them cool
8. If the dough becomes too soft from kneading to roll out, let it stiffen on a cool place for some time covered
9. Alternative method: form the dough into a thick roll after kneading with the diameter of a silver guilder, let it stiffen and cut slices from it

title: Zandkoekjes met glazuur en geconfijte vruchtjes (Sand Cookies with Glaze and Candied Fruit)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g flour or 50 g cornstarch and 250 g flour
- 200 g butter or margarine
