- 150 g powdered sugar or brown sugar
- raspberry and berry or apricot jam
- 150 g water glaze
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Bake thin sand cookies in the known manner (see recipe 772)
2. Let them cool
3. Spread half with jam and place the other half on top
4. Garnish the cookies with water glaze (see recipe 819) and candied fruit

title: Zandtaartjes en Hernhutters (Small Sand Cakes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g butter or margarine
- 300 g flour or 50 g cornstarch and 250 g flour
- 150 g brown sugar

## Directions

1. Sift the flour or flour mixture into a bowl
2. Add the butter or margarine and brown sugar
3. Knead all ingredients together until no sugar granules are felt
4. Coat small molds (for Hernhutters use very small fluted molds) with melted butter or margarine
5. Fill them 3/4 full with the dough
6. Place on a baking sheet and bake in a moderately warm oven in the middle position until very lightly brown and done (approximately 20 minutes)
7. Test for doneness by inserting a larding needle, meat fork or knitting needle, which should come out dry
8. If the cakes brown too much during baking, cover with a piece of gray paper brushed with butter
9. Let the cakes cool and turn the molds over
10. Dust the cakes with powdered sugar after cooling

title: Zeelt (Tench)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg tench
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare according to the method for carp (recipe 186)

title: Zeer Goede Wafelen (Very Good Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6¼ ounces fine flour
- 1¼ ounces buckwheat flour
- 12 eggs with whites, plus 3 more without whites
- 1½ cups butter
- 2 quarts sweet milk, lukewarm
- 1 tablespoon yeast mixed with brown sugar
- 1 good beer glass rainwater

## Directions

1. Mix flour, buckwheat flour, eggs, butter, milk, and yeast mixture to make a batter.
2. When baking, add 1 good beer glass of rainwater.

title: Zeewolf (Catfish)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 portions of 200-250g each (approximately 1 kg)
- water
- salt
- small splash of vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the fish as indicated in recipe 177

title: Zoete Appelen (Sweet Apples)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sweet apples
- 1/2 dl berry juice
- 45 g sugar
- peel of 1/2 lemon or piece of cinnamon stick
- some sago

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, cut them into quarters, remove the cores.
2. Wash them, set them on with enough boiling water so they are about 1/3 submerged with the flavoring agent.
3. Let them cook gently, shaking occasionally, for about 1/2 hour.
4. Add the berry juice and sugar, remove the flavoring agent and bind the liquid carefully with a little mixed sago.

title: Zoete fritures (Sweet Fritters)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 2½ dl milk
- some salt
- 50 g Duryea maizena
- 225 g flour
- 40 g sugar
- scraped vanilla or fine cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the salt and flavoring.
2. Add the flour, maizena and enough milk until the flour no longer dusts.
3. Stir out the lumps from this batter and add the remaining milk.
4. Treat the fritters further as sweet fritters (recipe 168).

title: Zoete gelei met vruchten (Macédoine aux fruits) (Sweet Fruit Jelly) (8 servings)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water
- 300 g sugar
- 55 g white gelatine or 50 g powdered gelatine
- juice of one lemon
- 2 to 3 egg whites
- 4 dl maraschino
- 200 g candied fruits or more

## Directions

1. Clarify the water, which is mixed with the sugar, the soaked gelatine, the lemon juice and the egg white, in the same way as given for aspic.
2. Pour the liquid through a doubled dense cloth.

title: Zoete Gelei met Vruchten (Sweet Fruit Gelée)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- 300 g sugar
- 55 g white gelatin or 50 g powdered gelatin
- juice of one lemon
- 2 to 3 egg whites
- 4 dl maraschino
- 200 g candied fruits or more

## Directions

1. Prepare the water mixed with the sugar, the soaked gelatin, the lemon juice and the egg white in the same manner as described for aspic.
2. Pour the liquid through a doubled thick cloth, add the maraschino and pour a thin layer into a mold rinsed with cold water.
3. Let this cool (preferably on ice), then arrange some candied fruits, alternating in shape and color, on it, glue them with a little lukewarm gelée, let the mass cool again, pour gelée over until everything is covered and let it set.
4. Continue this way until the entire mold is filled.
5. Turn the macédoine out onto a damp plate.
6. Note: If the pudding does not come out of the mold easily, it is best to dip it briefly in warm water.

title: Zoetzuur van Kwetsen (Sweet and Sour Plums)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg plums
- 2 bottles pickling vinegar
- 50 g cloves
- 50 g cinnamon stick
- 2 kg sugar
- 5 g mace (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the plums, dry them, prick them all over with a silver fork, place them in a Cologne pot and pour over the vinegar, which has been cooked with the spices (optionally also mace) and the sugar
2. Let the plums stand for a week and stir them gently from time to time
3. Cook the plums in small batches together in the liquid until they are completely smooth without wrinkles
4. Carefully remove each plum that has become smooth from the liquid, place them in the Cologne pot, and when all plums are ready, pour the liquid with the spices over them
5. Close the pot with damp parchment paper and label it with name and date
6. Let them stand for at least 3 weeks before use
7. If necessary, add more sweet and sour liquid later to replace what has evaporated

title: Zoetzuur van Meloen (Sweet and Sour Melon)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg melon flesh (the hard flesh of the melon, without the inedible skin, by weight)
- 20 g cinnamon stick
- 20 g cloves
- 1 kg sugar
- 1 bottle pickling vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the melon flesh without the outer skin into equal cubes and insert a clove and a small piece of cinnamon stick in each cube
2. Weigh the pieces and take the same weight of white sugar
3. Pour enough vinegar over the pieces so they are just covered, add the sugar and cook the melon until the pieces become translucent and glossy
4. Immediately place each piece that is done in a Cologne pot
5. When all pieces are removed, pour the liquid over them
6. If necessary, cook the liquid briefly
7. Close the pot with damp parchment paper and twine and label it with name and date
8. Let the pieces stand for at least 3 weeks before use

title: Zoetzuur van Morellen (Sweet and Sour Morello Cherries)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg morello cherries
- 1 bottle pickling vinegar
- 1 kg sugar
- 30 cloves
- 10 g cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Wash the morello cherries, place them in a Cologne pot, pour the vinegar over which has been cooked with the spices and sugar
2. Close the pot with damp parchment paper, label it with name and date
3. Let the sweet and sour stand for at least 3 weeks before use

title: Zoute Allumettes (Salted Sticks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g hard butter or calf kidney fat
- 100 g flour
- salt
- water (approximately 1/2 dl)
- 40 g old Gouda cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry (feuilletée) in the usual manner (see recipe 846), but mix it with the grated cheese
2. Roll it out the last time to a thickness of 1/4 cm, cut strips from it 10 cm long and 2 cm wide
3. Brush these with beaten egg yolk with water, sprinkle them with a little salt and bake them in a hot oven until light brown and cooked through (10 to 15 minutes)
4. Serve them with ragout

title: Zoute Bolletjes (Savory Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 130 g flour
- 1/2 dl milk
- 10 g yeast
- 90 g butter or margarine
- 10 g salt
- 1/4 egg
- 1 egg yolk for brushing

## Directions

1. Put the flour in a warmed bowl, make a small well in the middle, add the milk and yeast, stir in about half of the flour and let this starter rise for 15-20 minutes in a warm place.
2. Add the melted butter or margarine, the beaten egg and the salt and knead this dough until it releases from your hands.
3. Throw it on a flour-dusted board and work it back and forth for 5 minutes, let it rest in a cool place for half an hour.
4. Flatten it, divide it into 60-65 pieces and shape them into balls.
5. Lay them on a greased baking sheet, flatten them slightly with a damp cloth and brush them with egg yolk beaten with a little water.
6. Let the rolls rise for another 15-20 minutes in a warm place, bake them in a hot oven until light brown and done (15-20 minutes). Remove them from the sheet and let them cool.

title: Zoute stengels (Salty Sticks)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g hard butter or margarine
- 50 g flour
- 5 g salt
- some water
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry in the usual way (recipe 846), but mix it with some extra salt.
2. Roll it out the last time to 1/4 cm thickness and cut strips 10 cm in length and 1/2 cm in width.
3. Brush these strips with egg yolk that has been beaten with some water, lay them on a baking sheet and bake them in a hot oven light brown and cooked through (approximately 10 minutes).

title: Zuring (Sorrel)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg sorrel
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt

## Directions

1. Strip the sorrel and rinse it several times in abundant boiling water
2. Set it on with as little water as possible and let it cook until done, stirring occasionally (15 minutes)
3. Let it drain well in a colander
4. Stew it for 5 minutes with the salt and butter or margarine

title: Zuring met Krenten (Sorrel with Currants)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg sorrel
- 75 g currants
- 75 g syrup or sugar
- 1 egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the sorrel until done as described in the previous recipe
2. Let it drain in a colander and mix it with the sugar or syrup and the currants, which have been rinsed with lukewarm water and briefly heated in boiling water
3. If it is still very moist, add the egg that has been beaten in a bowl

title: Zuring met Spiegeleieren (Sorrel with Fried Eggs)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg sorrel
- 35 g butter or margarine
- salt
- 4 fried eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the sorrel until done as described in recipe 466
2. Let it drain in a colander and stew it for 5 minutes with the salt and butter or margarine
3. Place it in a dish and lay the fried eggs, which have been prepared in the ordinary manner (see recipe 375), on top

title: Zuringsoep (Sorrel Soup)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l salted water or bouillon
- 200 g sorrel
- 1 head of lettuce
- chervil
- piece of Spanish pepper
- 35 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg (optional)
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the vegetables, wash them, put them on to cook with the boiling water or bouillon, the salt and the Spanish pepper and let them cook until done (approximately 1/4 hour).
2. Pour the liquid through a horsehair sieve, rub the vegetables through. Melt the butter or margarine and bring it to a boil while stirring with the flour, pour the sieved liquid in while stirring and let the soup cook until done (approximately 10 minutes).
3. Finally add the very finely chopped parsley.

title: Zuur Gebraden Vleesch (Alternative) (Sour Roasted Meat - Alternative Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Beer or wine vinegar
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Shallots
- Gherkins
- Lemon peel
- Fat or butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place the meat for four to eight days in beer or wine vinegar with bay leaves, cloves, shallots, gherkins and lemon peel, turning it every day.
2. Remove it from this liquid, strain it, and roast the meat with this liquid and the necessary fat or butter and salt, basting it very frequently.

title: Zuur Gebraden Vleesch (Sour Roasted Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib roast
- Salt
- Bacon
- Pepper
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Lemon peel
- Shallots
- Wine and beer in equal parts
- Water
- Butter
- Wooden roasting rack

## Directions

1. Pound and salt the rib roast as needed, lard it with bacon and place it in an earthen pan with bones underneath.
2. Add pepper, onions, cloves, bay leaves, lemon peel and shallots.
3. Pour equal parts wine and beer over it, so the meat is completely covered, and let it cook until a layer of fat forms.
4. Remove it and let it cool in the cellar; thus prepared it can keep well even 14 days in summer.
5. When ready to roast, place the meat in the pan on a wooden roaster, add the cooking liquid and the herbs, along with some more butter, pour water over it and let it braise in the covered pan for an hour.
6. Remove the meat and strain the liquid through a sieve.
7. Return the meat to the pan, pour some of the strained liquid over it and continue roasting as needed.

title: Zuurkool (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- sauerkraut
- vinegar
- butter
- pickled bacon or sausage (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse once
2. If it becomes too mild, add a splash of vinegar
3. Cook, shaking from time to time, until nearly dry
4. Let a small piece of butter melt in it
5. Can also be stewed with pickled bacon or sausage; some use smoked bacon

title: Zuurkool (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- firm white or savoy cabbage
- salt
- black peppercorns

## Directions

1. Remove outer leaves from cabbage
2. Cut in half and remove the core
3. Slice very finely by hand or with a mill
4. Line the bottom of the barrel with cabbage leaves
5. Sprinkle with coarse salt
6. Layer in cabbage, tamping down until liquid rises
7. Sprinkle with salt and black peppercorns
8. Continue layering until barrel is full
9. Place a board with a stone on top
10. After a few days, pour off some of the top liquid and add a heavier stone

title: Zuurkool (Sauerkraut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sauerkraut
- 40 g butter, margarine or fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Wash the sauerkraut, place it in a pot with enough boiling water so it is about half covered.
2. Let it cook, stirring occasionally, until tender and dry (approximately 1 hour).
3. Braise it with the butter, margarine or fat (approximately 10 minutes).
4. Serve it with mashed potatoes, bacon or sausages.
5. Note: When braising the sauerkraut with lard or beef fat, it is best to let it cook together for half an hour. If you have made the sauerkraut yourself, washing it, which causes some of the sour taste to be lost, is naturally unnecessary.

title: Zuurkool (Sauerkraut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 white cabbages
- 2 Savoy cabbages
- 60 g salt
- 12 black peppercorns
- Cologne pot
- cloth
- board
- stone

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbages, cut them in half, remove the stalk and cut them with a cabbage knife or cabbage shaver into long, thin strips.
2. Knead the cabbage with the salt until brine has formed.
3. Add the peppercorns and press the cabbage as firmly as possible into a well-cleaned Cologne pot.
4. Cover it with a clean cloth, place a board and a stone on top.
5. Store it as indicated in the general instructions.
6. Use the sauerkraut only after 6 or 8 weeks.

title: Zuurkool met Aardappelpurée en Saucijsjes (Sauerkraut with Mashed Potatoes and Sausages)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sauerkraut
- 60 g fat, butter or margarine
- 1 kg mashed potatoes
- 400 g sausages
- 30 g fat or margarine

## Directions

1. Cook the sauerkraut until tender in the manner described in the previous recipe.
2. Make mashed potatoes in the usual way (see recipe 409).
3. Fry the sausages in the fat, butter or margarine until light brown and cooked (see recipe 246).
4. Make a border of mashed potatoes on a heated plate, place the sauerkraut in the middle, place the sausages on top and pour the fat over it.

title: Zuurkool Met Wijn (Sauerkraut with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- sauerkraut
- bouillon
- butter
- red wine

## Directions

1. After rinsing the sauerkraut
2. Cook with bouillon until tender
3. Drain the liquid
4. Stew with butter and red wine

title: Zuurkool met Witte Bonen en Aardappelen (Sauerkraut with White Beans and Potatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg sauerkraut
- 1 kg potatoes
- 350 g white beans
- 60 g fat
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cook the washed and soaked beans in the usual manner (see recipe 493).
2. Add the washed sauerkraut to them and after 1/2 hour add the peeled, washed potatoes.
3. Add the fat and salt if needed.
4. Let everything cook together on a gentle fire until tender and almost dry (30 to 40 minutes).
5. Mash the dish lightly together.

title: Zuurkool Royale (Royal Sauerkraut)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 600 g sauerkraut
- approximately 3/4 kg potato purée
- 400 g lean ham
- 1/2 l creamy cheese sauce
- 1 bouillon cube
- some grated cheese extra

## Directions

1. Cook the sauerkraut in 1/4 l bouillon made from the cube.
2. Let it drain dry.
3. Wrap a portion of sauerkraut in each slice of ham.
4. Spread a thin layer of potato purée in an ovenproof dish.
5. Arrange the ham and sauerkraut parcels on top.
6. Pipe or lay the rest of the purée between them.
7. Pour the cheese sauce over the sauerkraut and purée and let the dish heat through in the oven and lightly brown on top.

title: Zuurkoolsla (Sauerkraut Salad)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g sauerkraut
- ¼ beet
- ½ head of lettuce
- 1 egg yolk
- 2½ dl oil
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the sauerkraut, if one does not know who has pickled it, cut it fine. Peel the beet and cut it into small pieces. Clean the lettuce in the usual way and pluck it into small pieces. Mix the vegetables with approximately 2 tablespoons of the mayonnaise, which is prepared with the lemon juice as in recipe 367.

title: Zwampudding (Sponge Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dl milk
- 100 g flour
- 100 g sugar
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 vanilla pod

## Directions

1. Stir butter or margarine, flour and sugar together until all lumps are gone.
2. Add the milk gradually and cook the mixture on moderate heat, stirring with a whisk, until it is done and bound.
3. Let it cool slightly, add the egg yolks and the scraped vanilla and last as lightly as possible the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Fill a warm pudding mold coated with butter or margarine and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs 1/3 full with the mixture, close it in the usual manner and cook the pudding 2 to 2 1/2 hours in a bain-marie, but make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim of the mold.
5. Be especially careful with a light pudding like this so that the water does not boil over when topping up.
6. Let it dry slightly without the lid.
7. Turn the pudding, which sinks quickly, onto a warmed dish and place a warmed bowl over it for transport.
8. Serve with a maraschino or white wine sauce.

title: Zwampudding (Sponge Pudding)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dl milk
- 100 g flour
- 100 g sugar
- 100 g butter or margarine
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Stir butter or margarine, flour and sugar together until all lumps disappear.
2. Gradually add the milk and cook the mixture on a moderately warm fire, stirring with a whisk, until it is done and bound.
3. Let it cool somewhat, add the egg yolks and the scraped vanilla and lastly mix as lightly as possible with the stiffly beaten egg white.
4. Fill a warm pudding mold coated with butter or margarine and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs one-third full with the mixture, close it in the usual manner and cook the pudding 2 to 2 1/2 hours in a bain-marie, but make sure the water is continuously topped up to just below the rim of the mold.
5. Be especially careful with a light pudding like this to prevent the water from boiling over when topping up.
6. Let it dry slightly without the lid.
7. Turn the pudding, which sinks quickly, onto a warmed dish and place a warmed bowl over it for transport.
8. Serve with a maraschino or white wine sauce.

title: Zwezerik in Schelpen (Sweetbreads in Shells)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 sweetbread
- 1/2 can mushrooms
- 2 dl broth
- 25 g flour
- 25 g butter or margarine
- 1/2 dl cream or milk
- 1 egg yolk
- pepper and salt
- pieces of mace
- piece Spanish pepper
- pinch thyme
- breadcrumbs
- 10 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbread for half an hour in cold water, then place it in fresh cold water, drain this off as it boils and let the sweetbread simmer in plenty of boiling water with salt and the seasonings until done (20 to 30 min.)
2. Pour cold water over it, peel it and cut it into small pieces.
3. Chop the mushrooms finely.
4. Prepare the sauce as indicated in recipe 102, but in this case use the strained sweetbread broth.
5. Mix it with the egg yolk, which has been beaten with the cream or milk, the sweetbread and the mushrooms.
6. Stir in pepper and salt to taste.
7. Fill the dampened shells with this mixture for 3/4.
8. Finish them as described in recipe 102.

title: Zwezerik met gehakt (Ris de veau Prince Henri) (Sweetbreads with Ground Meat)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- 500 g veal
- 300 g fatted pork
- 2 egg whites
- 100 g butter or margarine
- mace
- thyme
- onion
- pieces of cayenne pepper
- a little cream
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the soaked sweetbreads in the usual way until done (see recipe 293)
2. Rinse them with cold water, peel them and slice them to a thickness of 3/4 cm
3. Grind the washed meat and pork twice through a meat grinder finely, mix it with 1 1/2 egg white, pepper, salt, nutmeg and a little cream
4. Coat the sweetbread slices on one side with egg white, spread a layer of the meat mixture on this, fry the sweetbread in the butter or margarine that has been lightly browned in the pan until brown and done (15 minutes), and arrange them in overlapping rows on a heated platter
5. Pour the gravy, finished with a little cream and sweetbread bouillon, over them and garnish the edge with young vegetables or fleurons (recipe 847)

title: Zwezerik met soubisesaus (Ris de veau l'excelsior) (Sweetbreads with Soubise Sauce)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- 500 g veal
- 500 g fresh fatted pork
- 2 egg whites
- 100 g smoked beef tongue
- 100 g ham
- truffles and mushrooms
- 60 g butter or margarine
- 3/4 l soubise sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the sweetbreads, which have been standing in cold water for half an hour, for 10 minutes in boiling water
2. Rinse them with cold water, remove the membranes and fat and press them between two plates with a weight for 15 minutes
3. Lard the sweetbreads with strips of truffle and braise them gently in melted butter or margarine until done (approximately 1/2 hour)
4. Grind the washed veal with the pork (washed in lukewarm water) twice through a meat grinder, mix it with pepper, salt, nutmeg and one egg white
5. Divide this mixture into 3 equal parts
6. Mix one part with some finely chopped ham, the second with some finely chopped tongue and leave the third part unmixed
7. Form these mixtures with 2 spoons into flat, pear-shaped little figures, brush them with egg white and garnish them in a diamond pattern with thin strips of tongue and ham
8. Cook 3 to 4 of these quenelles at a time for 10 minutes in boiling salted water, keep them warm over the steam of boiling water while the other quenelles are cooked
9. Slice the sweetbreads diagonally, arrange them on a platter, pour the soubise sauce (prepared in the usual way according to recipe 353) over them and arrange the chopped meat balls around them
10. Serve the remaining soubise sauce in a sauce bowl

title: Zwezerikcoteletten met asperges (Sweetbread Cutlets with Asparagus)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- approximately 5 dl bouillon infused with mace, thyme and cayenne pepper
- 40 g flour
- 40 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg
- breadcrumbs for coating
- salad oil for frying
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 dl cream
- 1 kg asparagus
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. After soaking the sweetbreads for half an hour in cold water, cook them in the usual way in plenty of boiling salted water with herbs (25 to 30 minutes)
2. Rinse them with cold water, remove the membrane and slice them diagonally about 1 cm thick
3. Make a thick, light sauce in the usual way from butter or margarine, flour and strained sweetbread bouillon, mix it away from the heat with the egg yolk beaten with the cream
4. Dip the sweetbread slices in this sauce and let them cool
5. Double-bread them and fry them in fairly hot frying fat until brown and crispy
6. Let them drain briefly on porous paper and insert a piece of macaroni in the point, around which a paper collar is tied
7. Arrange the cutlets in a ring on a heated platter
8. Place the cooked asparagus tips in the center, pour a little melted butter or margarine over them and grate some nutmeg over them
9. Note: stewed asparagus (see recipe 463) can also be placed in the center of the cutlets

title: Zwezerikcrouetten (Sweetbread Croquettes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 sweetbread
- 1/2 tin of mushrooms
- 2.5 dl stock
- 0.5 dl cream
- 35 g flour
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 1 egg yolk
- pepper
- salt
- 0.5 egg or an egg white for breading
- 3 sheets of gelatin
- mace
- thyme
- cayenne pepper
- breadcrumbs
- frying fat

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbread for half an hour in cold water, then place it in fresh cold water, drain when it comes to a boil and cook it until done (20 to 25 minutes) in salted water with herbs.
2. Cover it with cold water, peel it and cut it into small pieces.
3. Finely chop the mushrooms.
4. Prepare the sauce as described in recipe 102, but in this case use the sweetbread stock.
5. Dissolve the soaked gelatin in it if necessary, add it to the egg yolk that has been beaten with the cream, stir in the sweetbread, mushrooms, pepper and salt to taste and let the mixture, spread on a board, become almost stiff, but ensure it is still easy to handle.
6. Shape the croquettes in the manner indicated for meat croquettes (recipe 116), bread them double and treat them further as meat croquettes (recipe 116).

title: Zwezerikken pasteitjes (Sweetbread Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- sweetbreads
- salt
- butter
- nutmeg
- lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare as with veal pastries
2. Remove all membranes and skins from the sweetbreads
3. Cook them briefly
4. Chop them finely and stew them now with some salt, butter, nutmeg and lemon
5. When done, let them cool and fill the pastries with them

title: Zwezerikschotel met tomaten (Sweetbread Platter with Tomatoes)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 sweetbreads
- curry powder (optional)
- 9 tomatoes
- 400 g ground veal
- 50 g stale bread
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- 75 g rice
- 3 dl tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the sweetbreads in the usual way (see recipe 293)
2. Peel them, press them between 2 plates and slice them diagonally
3. Arrange them in the shape of a sweetbread on a base of cooked rice, which may be mixed with curry if desired
4. Cover with tomato sauce prepared in the usual way (see recipe 340)
5. Garnish with stuffed tomatoes (recipe 131)

title: Zwoordrolletjes (Smoked Beef Rolls)
author: C.J. Wannée
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ kg thin pork cutlets of about 75 g per piece
- 900 g thin smoked beef
- thyme
- Spanish pepper
- bay leaves
- cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the cutlets and rub them in with some pepper and salt.
2. Cut the smoked beef, which has been soaked in lukewarm water for several hours, into approximately the same shape as the cutlets, place the meat in it, roll it up together and tie the resulting rolls closed with a string.
3. Cook the rolls for 3 hours in 2/3 water and 1/3 vinegar with a piece of cloth in which the spices are tied.
4. Scoop them with the liquid into well-cleaned Cologne pots. Make sure they are under the liquid and close the pots with damp parchment paper. Serve the smoked beef rolls with the jelly cold with the sandwich.
